5. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/README.md"
}
Output
"     1\u2192> \u26a0\ufe0f **This is an automatically published [staged repository](https://git.k8s.io/kubernetes/staging#external-repository-staging-area) for Kubernetes**.   \n     2\u2192> Contributions, including issues and pull requests, should be made to the main Kubernetes repository: [https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes).  \n     3\u2192> This repository is read-only for importing, and not used for direct contributions.  \n     4\u2192> See [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for more details.\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192# apiserver\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192Generic library for building a Kubernetes aggregated API server.\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192## Purpose\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192This library contains code to create Kubernetes aggregation server complete with delegated authentication and authorization,\n    14\u2192`kubectl` compatible discovery information, optional admission chain, and versioned types.  It's first consumers are\n    15\u2192`k8s.io/kubernetes`, `k8s.io/kube-aggregator`, and `github.com/kubernetes-incubator/service-catalog`.\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192## Compatibility\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192There are *NO compatibility guarantees* for this repository, yet.  It is in direct support of Kubernetes, so branches\n    21\u2192will track Kubernetes and be compatible with that repo.  As we more cleanly separate the layers, we will review the\n    22\u2192compatibility guarantee. We have a goal to make this easier to use in the future.\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192## Where does it come from?\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192`apiserver` is synced from https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver.\n    28\u2192Code changes are made in that location, merged into `k8s.io/kubernetes` and later synced here.\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192## Things you should *NOT* do\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192 1. Directly modify any files under `pkg` in this repo.  Those are driven from `k8s.io/kubernetes/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver`.\n    34\u2192 2. Expect compatibility.  This repo is changing quickly in direct support of\n    35\u2192    Kubernetes and the API isn't yet stable enough for API guarantees.\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192"
6. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/ARCHITECTURE.md"
}
Output
"     1\u2192# apiserver Architecture\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192## 1. Server Composition\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192The `kube-apiserver` binary is not one server, but a **server chain** of three distinct\n     6\u2192`GenericAPIServer` instances: the core Kubernetes API, API extensions (CRDs), and the aggregation\n     7\u2192layer.\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192This composition is managed by a layered configuration system, starting with command-line\n    10\u2192flags parsed by `options` structs (e.g., `RecommendedOptions` in `pkg/server/options`), which\n    11\u2192populate a `Config` object that is then used to instantiate the `GenericAPIServer`\n    12\u2192instances. The construction of this delegation chain can be found in the `CreateServerChain`\n    13\u2192function in `cmd/kube-apiserver/app/server.go`.\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192```mermaid\n    16\u2192graph TD\n    17\u2192    subgraph Incoming Request\n    18\u2192        direction LR\n    19\u2192        A[User/Client] --> B{/apis/apps/v1/deployments};\n    20\u2192    end\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192    subgraph kube-apiserver process\n    23\u2192        direction LR\n    24\u2192        B --> C[Aggregator Server];\n    25\u2192        C -- Not an APIService --> D[Kube API Server];\n    26\u2192        D -- Handles Request --> E[REST Storage];\n    27\u2192        C -- Is an APIService --> F[Proxy to Extension API Server];\n    28\u2192        D -- Not a Core API --> G[API Extensions Server];\n    29\u2192        G -- Handles CRD --> E;\n    30\u2192    end\n    31\u2192```\n    32\u2192\n    33\u21921.  **Aggregator Server (`kube-aggregator`):**\n    34\u2192    *   **Purpose:** Handles the `apiregistration.k8s.io` API and acts as a reverse proxy for\n    35\u2192        extension API servers. This functionality was designed to allow third-party APIs to be\n    36\u2192        \"aggregated\" into the main Kubernetes API server seamlessly.\n    37\u2192    *   **Mechanism:** It watches `APIService` objects. When a request arrives (e.g., for\n    38\u2192        `/apis/mycompany.com/v1/myresources`), it checks if an `APIService` has \"claimed\"\n    39\u2192        that path. If so, it uses a `ServiceResolver` to find the IP of the backing\n    40\u2192        `Service` and proxies the request.\n    41\u2192    *   **Use Case:** This pattern is for programmatic, high-control extensions that require\n    42\u2192        custom business logic (e.g., non-CRUD subresources like `/logs`) or alternative\n    43\u2192        storage backends.\n    44\u2192    *   **Delegation:** If no `APIService` matches, it delegates the request to the next\n    45\u2192        server in the chain.\n    46\u2192\n    47\u21922.  **Kube API Server (Core):**\n    48\u2192    *   **Purpose:** Serves all the built-in Kubernetes APIs (`core/v1`, `apps/v1`, etc.).\n    49\u2192    *   **Mechanism:** This is the main server, configured with all the core REST storage\n    50\u2192        strategies.\n    51\u2192    *   **Delegation:** If a request is for a path that is not a core API (e.g., for a CRD),\n    52\u2192        it delegates the request to the next server in the chain.\n    53\u2192\n    54\u21923.  **API Extensions Server (`apiextensions-apiserver`):**\n    55\u2192    *   **Purpose:** Handles the `apiextensions.k8s.io` API, which manages\n    56\u2192        `CustomResourceDefinition` objects (CRDs). The evolution of CRDs from a simple extension\n    57\u2192        mechanism to a feature-rich system with validation, versioning, and defaulting is\n    58\u2192        documented in a series of KEPs, starting with the graduation to GA in Kubernetes v1.16.\n    59\u2192    *   **Mechanism:** When a CRD is created, this server dynamically creates and installs a\n    60\u2192        new REST storage handler for the new resource, making it immediately available.\n    61\u2192    *   **Use Case:** CRDs are the most common extension pattern, offering a declarative,\n    62\u2192        schema-based way to define new resource types that are stored in etcd and require no\n    63\u2192        custom API server code.\n    64\u2192    *   **Delegation:** It is the end of the chain. If it cannot handle a request, a `404 Not\n    65\u2192        Found` is returned.\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192## 2. Handler Chain\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192Every request flows through a standard chain of HTTP handlers (filters). The request body\n    70\u2192is not deserialized until it has passed authentication and authorization. The default handler\n    71\u2192chain is constructed by the `DefaultBuildHandlerChain` function in\n    72\u2192`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go`.\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192```mermaid\n    75\u2192sequenceDiagram\n    76\u2192    participant Client\n    77\u2192    participant Handler Chain\n    78\u2192    participant Authentication\n    79\u2192    participant Authorization\n    80\u2192    participant Priority and Fairness\n    81\u2192    participant Admission Control\n    82\u2192    participant REST Endpoint\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192    Client->>Handler Chain: Request\n    85\u2192    Handler Chain->>Authentication: Authenticate\n    86\u2192    Authentication-->>Handler Chain: User Info\n    87\u2192    Handler Chain->>Authorization: Authorize\n    88\u2192    Authorization-->>Handler Chain: Allowed/Denied\n    89\u2192    Handler Chain->>Priority and Fairness: Classify & Queue\n    90\u2192    Priority and Fairness-->>Handler Chain: Proceed\n    91\u2192    Handler Chain->>Admission Control: Mutate & Validate\n    92\u2192    Admission Control-->>Handler Chain: Object OK\n    93\u2192    Handler Chain->>REST Endpoint: Handle\n    94\u2192    REST Endpoint-->>Handler Chain: Response\n    95\u2192    Handler Chain-->>Client: Response\n    96\u2192```\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192The handler chain consists of the following stages:\n    99\u2192\n   100\u21921.  **Authentication (`pkg/authentication`):** This filter identifies the user. The system is\n   101\u2192    pluggable and composed of multiple authenticators (e.g., client certs, bearer tokens, OIDC).\n   102\u2192    The identity of the user is determined by the first authenticator in the chain that successfully\n   103\u2192    identifies the user.\n   104\u21922.  **Authorization (`pkg/authorization`):** This filter checks if the user is permitted to\n   105\u2192    perform the action. This system is also pluggable and composed of multiple authorizers\n   106\u2192    (e.g., RBAC, Node, Webhook). Each authorizer may respond with either allow, deny, or no opinion.\n   107\u2192    If the response is no opinion, the request is passed to the next authorizer in the chain.\n   108\u21923.  **Priority and Fairness (`pkg/util/flowcontrol`):** This subsystem manages request\n   109\u2192    concurrency, classifying requests into `FlowSchema`s and `PriorityLevel`s to prevent\n   110\u2192    overload. This feature was introduced to prevent high traffic from overwhelming the API\n   111\u2192    server and to ensure that critical cluster operations are not starved.\n   112\u21924.  **Admission Control (`pkg/admission`):** This is the primary mechanism for policy\n   113\u2192    enforcement. It is only at this stage that the request body is deserialized into an\n   114\u2192    object. It is a chain of plugins that can mutate or validate an object. The built-in Pod\n   115\u2192    Security admission controller is a key example of this, enforcing Pod Security Standards\n   116\u2192    at the namespace level.\n   117\u21925.  **REST Endpoint Handling (`pkg/endpoints`):** The request is finally dispatched to the\n   118\u2192    appropriate REST handler, which is installed by the `APIInstaller`.\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192## 3. API Group Registration\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192The high-level steps for introducing an API are:\n   123\u2192\n   124\u21921.  **Define Types:** Create or modify the Go structs in the `types.go` file for the API group.\n   125\u21922.  **Generate Code:** Use the code generators provided by the Kubernetes project to create the required\n   126\u2192    boilerplate methods for deep-copy, conversion, and defaulting.\n   127\u21923.  **Implement the `Strategy`:** Write the custom business logic and validation for the\n   128\u2192    resource in its `Strategy` object.\n   129\u21924.  **Register and Install:** Create the `APIGroupInfo` struct, bundling the `Scheme` and the\n   130\u2192    `Strategy`-configured storage, and pass it to the `GenericAPIServer`'s `InstallAPIGroup`\n   131\u2192    method.\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192### The API Group Registry\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192The `runtime.Scheme` acts as a central registry for an API group's type information. A single\n   136\u2192`Scheme` object is created for each API group and is responsible for the following key\n   137\u2192capabilities:\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192*   **Type Registration and Mapping:** The `Scheme`'s primary role is to map a GroupVersionKind\n   140\u2192    (GVK) to its corresponding Go type and back. This process also relies on the `deepcopy-gen`\n   141\u2192    tool to create `DeepCopy()` methods for each type, which is critical for ensuring that\n   142\u2192    objects returned from caches are never modified directly.\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192*   **API Conversion:** The `Scheme` stores the conversion functions that translate objects\n   145\u2192    between different API versions. These functions are typically generated by the\n   146\u2192    `conversion-gen` tool and enable the **hub-and-spoke** model.\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192*   **Defaulting:** The `Scheme` registers defaulting functions that populate optional fields in\n   149\u2192    an object. These are usually generated by the `defaulter-gen` tool.\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192*   **Declarative Validation:** The `Scheme` can store and execute code-generated validation\n   152\u2192    functions, providing a baseline level of validation. This is distinct from the primary,\n   153\u2192    handwritten business logic validation, which is handled by the `Strategy` object.\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192### The `APIGroupInfo` Struct and `Strategy` Object\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192With a populated `Scheme`, the API group is registered with the `GenericAPIServer` by bundling\n   158\u2192the `Scheme` with the storage backend and versioning information into an `APIGroupInfo` struct.\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192```mermaid\n   161\u2192graph TD\n   162\u2192    subgraph Server Configuration\n   163\u2192        A[APIGroupInfo for apps v1];\n   164\u2192        A --> B{Scheme: Knows Deployment v1};\n   165\u2192        A --> C{Storage: deployments RESTStorage};\n   166\u2192        A --> D{Version Priority: v1, v1beta1};\n   167\u2192    end\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192    subgraph RESTStorage Implementation\n   170\u2192        C --> E[genericregistry.Store];\n   171\u2192        E --> F[etcd client];\n   172\u2192        E --> G[Deployment Strategy];\n   173\u2192    end\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192    subgraph Server Runtime\n   176\u2192        H[GenericAPIServer] -- InstallAPIGroup --> I[APIInstaller];\n   177\u2192        I -- Uses --> A;\n   178\u2192        I --> J{Register /apis/apps/v1/deployments};\n   179\u2192        J --> K[HTTP Handler];\n   180\u2192        K -- On Request --> C;\n   181\u2192    end\n   182\u2192```\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192The registration process follows these steps:\n   185\u2192\n   186\u21921.  **`APIGroupInfo` Construction:** For each API group, an `APIGroupInfo` struct is created,\n   187\u2192    which contains the populated `Scheme`, a map of resources to their storage\n   188\u2192    implementations, and an ordered list of **Version Priority**.\n   189\u2192\n   190\u21922.  **REST Storage Instantiation:** For each resource, a `genericregistry.Store` is created. It\n   191\u2192    is configured with a resource-specific `Strategy` object that contains the core business\n   192\u2192    logic (e.g., handwritten validation).\n   193\u2192\n   194\u21923.  **API Group Installation:** The `GenericAPIServer`'s `InstallAPIGroup` method takes the\n   195\u2192    `APIGroupInfo` and uses an `APIInstaller` to expose the resources as HTTP endpoints.\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192## 4. Watch Cache\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192To handle the high volume of watch requests from controllers without overwhelming etcd, the\n   200\u2192apiserver uses a **watch cache**. The implementation can be found in\n   201\u2192`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/`.\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192*   **Initialization:** The cacher first performs a `LIST` to get the current state of all\n   204\u2192    objects and a `ResourceVersion` for that point-in-time. It then starts a `WATCH` from\n   205\u2192    that version to ensure a consistent stream of events.\n   206\u2192*   **Serving from Cache:** Most list and watch requests are served from this in-memory cache, which\n   207\u2192    dramatically reduces the load on etcd. Consistent reads are also served from the\n   208\u2192    cache. This is achieved by first fetching the revision number of the latest write from\n   209\u2192    etcd. The server then ensures the cache is at least that recent\u2014waiting for it to\n   210\u2192    refresh if necessary\u2014before serving the request.\n   211\u2192*   **Fallback to Storage:** If a client request cannot be served from the\n   212\u2192    cache's buffer, the request \"falls through\" to the underlying etcd storage.\n   213\u2192*   **Bookmarks:** The cacher uses bookmark events to track the latest `ResourceVersion` for\n   214\u2192    unchanged objects. This prevents the cache's `ResourceVersion` from becoming too old,\n   215\u2192    which avoids the need for expensive relist operations from etcd when the objects have\n   216\u2192    not been modified.\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192## 5. Conflict Resolution\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192*   **Optimistic Concurrency via `resourceVersion`:** Clients are expected to perform updates using a\n   221\u2192    read-modify-write workflow. The apiserver uses the `resourceVersion` field of every\n   222\u2192    object to enforce optimistic concurrency. This `resourceVersion` is not an arbitrary number;\n   223\u2192    it maps directly to etcd's globally consistent `mod_revision`. When a client submits an\n   224\u2192    update (`PUT` or `PATCH`), it must provide the `resourceVersion` of the object it based its\n   225\u2192    modifications on. If the `resourceVersion` on the server does not match the current\n   226\u2192    `mod_revision` in etcd, the server rejects the request with a `409 Conflict` error. This\n   227\u2192    forces the client to re-read the object, resolve the conflict, and resubmit with the new\n   228\u2192    `resourceVersion`.\n   229\u2192*   **Server-Side Apply:** A declarative, \"intent-based\" patch. The server maintains a\n   230\u2192    `managedFields` section in the object's metadata to track which \"manager\" (e.g., a\n   231\u2192    controller) owns each field. This allows multiple actors to manage different parts of the\n   232\u2192    same object without overwriting each other's changes.\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192## 6. Discovery and OpenAPI\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192Apiservers serve the `/apis` discovery endpoints and the `/openapi/v2` and `/openapi/v3`\n   237\u2192specifications. The generation of the OpenAPI specification is a multi-stage process.\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192*   **`openapi-gen`**: This tool reflects on Go structs, reads godoc comments, and looks at\n   240\u2192    validation struct tags to generate a map of all API definitions.\n   241\u2192*   **`zz_generated.openapi.go`**: The output is a large Go file containing a\n   242\u2192    `GetOpenAPIDefinitions` function.\n   243\u2192*   **Runtime**: The `GenericAPIServer` calls this generated function to build the final OpenAPI\n   244\u2192    JSON spec that it serves to clients.\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192## 7. Security & Observability\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192*   **Audit (`pkg/audit`):** The apiserver has a policy-driven event logging pipeline. The audit\n   249\u2192    policy controls what is logged and at which stage of a request.\n   250\u2192*   **Security:**\n   251\u2192    *   **mTLS:** The primary authentication mechanism for system components.\n   252\u2192    *   **Service Account Token Issuance:** The `kube-apiserver` acts as an OIDC provider,\n   253\u2192        issuing and validating JWTs for `ServiceAccount`s.\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192## 8. Streaming Protocols\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192*   **Websockets:** The apiserver uses websockets to upgrade HTTP\n   258\u2192    connections for interactive, streaming protocols like `exec`, `attach`, and\n   259\u2192    `port-forward`. The `UpgradeAwareProxyHandler` manages this process.\n   260\u2192"
7. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "find /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server -maxdepth 2 -type f -name \"*.go\" | head -30"
}

70\u2192// Info about an API group.\n    71\u2192type APIGroupInfo struct {\n    72\u2192\tPrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion\n    73\u2192\t// Info about the resources in this group. It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\n    74\u2192\tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\n    75\u2192\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\n    76\u2192\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\n    77\u2192\t// define a version \"v1beta1\" but want to use the Kubernetes \"v1\" internal objects.\n    78\u2192\t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\n    79\u2192\t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\n    80\u2192\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n    81\u2192\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \"meta.k8s.io/v1\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\n    82\u2192\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\n    83\u2192\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\n    84\u2192\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192\t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\n    87\u2192\t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\n    88\u2192\t// TODO: replace with interfaces\n    89\u2192\tScheme *runtime.Scheme\n    90\u2192\t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\n    91\u2192\tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n    92\u2192\t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\n    93\u2192\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\n    96\u2192\t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\n    97\u2192\tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192func (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {"

\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver/install\"\n    93\u2192)\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192// hostnameFunc is a function to set the hostnameFunc of this apiserver.\n    96\u2192// To be used for testing purpose only, to simulate scenarios where multiple apiservers\n    97\u2192// exist. In such cases we want to ensure unique apiserver IDs which are a hash of hostnameFunc.\n    98\u2192var (\n    99\u2192\thostnameFunc = os.Hostname\n   100\u2192)\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192const (\n   103\u2192\t// DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix is where the legacy APIs will be located.\n   104\u2192\tDefaultLegacyAPIPrefix = \"/api\"\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192\t// APIGroupPrefix is where non-legacy API group will be located.\n   107\u2192\tAPIGroupPrefix = \"/apis\"\n   108\u2192)\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192// Config is a structure used to configure a GenericAPIServer.\n   111\u2192// Its members are sorted roughly in order of importance for composers.\n   112\u2192type Config struct {\n   113\u2192\t// SecureServing is required to serve https\n   114\u2192\tSecureServing *SecureServingInfo\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192\t// Authentication is the configuration for authentication\n   117\u2192\tAuthentication AuthenticationInfo\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192\t// Authorization is the configuration for authorization\n   120\u2192\tAuthorization AuthorizationInfo\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n   123\u2192\t// This is required for proper functioning of the PostStartHooks on a GenericAPIServer\n   124\u2192\t// TODO: move into SecureServing(WithLoopback) as soon as insecure serving is gone\n   125\u2192\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192\t// EgressSelector provides a lookup mechanism for dialing outbound connections.\n   128\u2192\t// It does so based on a EgressSelectorConfiguration which was read at startup.\n   129\u2192\tEgressSelector *egressselector.EgressSelector\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192\t// RuleResolver is required to get the list of rules that apply to a given user\n   132\u2192\t// in a given namespace\n   133\u2192\tRuleResolver authorizer.RuleResolver\n   134\u2192\t// AdmissionControl performs deep inspection of a given request (including content)\n   135\u2192\t// to set values and determine whether its allowed\n   136\u2192\tAdmissionControl      admission.Interface\n   137\u2192\tCorsAllowedOriginList []string\n   138\u2192\tHSTSDirectives        []string\n   139\u2192\t// FlowControl, if not nil, gives priority and fairness to request handling\n   140\u2192\tFlowControl utilflowcontrol.Interface\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192\tEnableIndex     bool\n   143\u2192\tEnableProfiling bool\n   144\u2192\tDebugSocketPath string\n   145\u2192\tEnableDiscovery bool\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192\t// Requires generic profiling enabled\n   148\u2192\tEnableContentionProfiling bool\n   149\u2192\tEnableMetrics             bool\n   150\u2192"

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package server\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"bytes\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    23\u2192\trt \"runtime\"\n    24\u2192\t\"sort\"\n    25\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192\t\"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/mux\"\n    35\u2192)\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192// APIServerHandlers holds the different http.Handlers used by the API server.\n    38\u2192// This includes the full handler chain, the director (which chooses between gorestful and nonGoRestful,\n    39\u2192// the gorestful handler (used for the API) which falls through to the nonGoRestful handler on unregistered paths,\n    40\u2192// and the nonGoRestful handler (which can contain a fallthrough of its own)\n    41\u2192// FullHandlerChain -> Director -> {GoRestfulContainer,NonGoRestfulMux} based on inspection of registered web services\n    42\u2192type APIServerHandler struct {\n    43\u2192\t// FullHandlerChain is the one that is eventually served with.  It should include the full filter\n    44\u2192\t// chain and then call the Director.\n    45\u2192\tFullHandlerChain http.Handler\n    46\u2192\t// The registered APIs.  InstallAPIs uses this.  Other servers probably shouldn't access this directly.\n    47\u2192\tGoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n    48\u2192\t// NonGoRestfulMux is the final HTTP handler in the chain.\n    49\u2192\t// It comes after all filters and the API handling\n    50\u2192\t// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.\n    51\u2192\tNonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\t// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.\n    54\u2192\t// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have\n    55\u2192\t// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)\n    56\u2192\t// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to\n    57\u2192\t// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding\n    58\u2192\t// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints\n    59\u2192\t// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the\n    60\u2192\t//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.\n    61\u2192\t// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to\n    62\u2192\t// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in\n    63\u2192\t// order to handle \"normal\" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think\n    64\u2192\t// we should consider completely removing gorestful.\n    65\u2192\t// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.\n    66\u2192\tDirector http.Handler\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192// HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.\n    70\u2192// It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization\n    71\u2192type HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192func NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {\n    74\u2192\tnonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)\n    75\u2192\tif notFoundHandler != nil {\n    76\u2192\t\tnonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)\n    77\u2192\t}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192\tgorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()\n    80\u2192\tgorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}\n    81\u2192\tgorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {\n    82\u2192\t\tlogStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)\n    83\u2192\t})\n    84\u2192\tgorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {\n    85\u2192\t\tserviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)\n    86\u2192\t})\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192\tdirector := director{\n    89\u2192\t\tname:               name,\n    90\u2192\t\tgoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n    91\u2192\t\tnonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n    92\u2192\t}\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192\treturn &APIServerHandler{\n    95\u2192\t\tFullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),\n    96\u2192\t\tGoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n    97\u2192\t\tNonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n    98\u2192\t\tDirector:           director,\n    99\u2192\t}\n   100\u2192}"

In such cases we want to ensure unique apiserver IDs which are a hash of hostnameFunc.\n98-var (\n99-\thostnameFunc = os.Hostname\n100-)\n101-\n102-const (\n103-\t// DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix is where the legacy APIs will be located.\n104-\tDefaultLegacyAPIPrefix = \"/api\"\n105-\n106-\t// APIGroupPrefix is where non-legacy API group will be located.\n107-\tAPIGroupPrefix = \"/apis\"\n108-)\n109-\n110-// Config is a structure used to configure a GenericAPIServer.\n111-// Its members are sorted roughly in order of importance for composers.\n112:type Config struct {\n113-\t// SecureServing is required to serve https\n114-\tSecureServing *SecureServingInfo\n115-\n116-\t// Authentication is the configuration for authentication\n117-\tAuthentication AuthenticationInfo\n118-\n119-\t// Authorization is the configuration for authorization\n120-\tAuthorization AuthorizationInfo\n121-\n122-\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n123-\t// This is required for proper functioning of the PostStartHooks on a GenericAPIServer\n124-\t/ TODO: move into SecureServing(WithLoopback) as soon as insecure serving is gone\n125-\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n126-\n127-\t// EgressSelector provides a lookup mechanism for dialing outbound connections.\n128-\t// It does so based on a EgressSelectorConfiguration which was read at startup.\n129-\tEgressSelector *egressselector.EgressSelector\n130-\n131-\t// RuleResolver is required to get the list of rules that apply to a given user\n132-\t// in a given namespace\n133-\tRuleResolver authorizer.RuleResolver\n134-\t// AdmissionControl performs deep inspection of a given request (including content)\n135-\t// to set values and determine whether its allowed\n136-\tAdmissionControl      admission.Interface\n137-\tCorsAllowedOriginList []string\n138-\tHSTSDirectives        []string\n139-\t// FlowControl, if not nil, gives priority and fairness to request handling\n140-\tFlowControl utilflowcontrol.Interface\n141-\n142-\tEnableIndex     bool\n143-\tEnableProfiling bool\n144-\tDebugSocketPath string\n145-\tEnableDiscovery bool\n146-\n147-\t// Requires generic profiling enabled\n148-\tEnableContentionProfiling bool\n149-\tEnableMetrics             bool\n150-\n151-\tDisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n152-\t// done values in this values for this map are ignored.\n153-\tPostStartHooks map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry\n154-\n155-\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\n156-\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\n157-\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\n158-\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\n159-\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\n160-\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\n161-\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\n162-\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs."

This is nil by default. Use DefaultOpenAPIConfig for \"working\" defaults.\n   215\u2192\tOpenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n   216\u2192\t// OpenAPIV3Config will be used in generating OpenAPI V3 spec. This is nil by default. Use DefaultOpenAPIV3Config for \"working\" defaults.\n   217\u2192\tOpenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n   218\u2192\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation avoids installing the OpenAPI handler if set to true.\n   219\u2192\tSkipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192\t// ResourceTransformers are used to transform resources from and to etcd, e.g. encryption.\n   222\u2192\tResourceTransformers storagevalue.ResourceTransformers\n   223\u2192\t// RESTOptionsGetter is used to construct RESTStorage types via the generic registry.\n   224\u2192\tRESTOptionsGetter genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192\t// If specified, all requests except those which match the LongRunningFunc predicate will timeout\n   227\u2192\t// after this duration.\n   228\u2192\tRequestTimeout time.Duration\n   229\u2192\t// If specified, long running requests such as watch will be allocated a random timeout between this value, and\n   230\u2192\t// twice this value.  Note that it is up to the request handlers to ignore or honor this timeout. In seconds.\n   231\u2192\tMinRequestTimeout int\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192\t// StorageInitializationTimeout defines the maximum amount of time to wait for storage initialization\n   234\u2192\t// before declaring apiserver ready.\n   235\u2192\tStorageInitializationTimeout time.Duration\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192\t// This represents the maximum amount of time it should take for apiserver to complete its startup\n   238\u2192\t// sequence and become healthy. From apiserver's start time to when this amount of time has\n   239\u2192\t// elapsed, /livez will assume that unfinished post-start hooks will complete successfully and\n   240\u2192\t// therefore return true.\n   241\u2192\tLivezGracePeriod time.Duration\n   242\u2192\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\n   243\u2192\t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\n   244\u2192\t// but /readyz will return failure.\n   245\u2192\tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192\t// The limit on the total size increase all \"copy\" operations in a json\n   248\u2192\t// patch may cause.\n   249\u2192\t// This affects all places that applies json patch in the binary.\n   250\u2192\tJSONPatchMaxCopyBytes int64\n   251\u2192\t// The limit on the request size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request\n   252\u2192\t// 0 means no limit.\n   253\u2192\tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n   254\u2192\t// MaxRequestsInFlight is the maximum number of parallel non-long-running requests. Every further\n   255\u2192\t// request has to wait. Applies only to non-mutating requests.\n   256\u2192\tMaxRequestsInFlight int\n   257\u2192\t// MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight is the maximum number of parallel mutating requests. Every further\n   258\u2192\t// request has to wait.\n   259\u2192\tMaxMutatingRequestsInFlight int\n   260\u2192\t// Predicate which is true for paths of long-running http requests\n   261\u2192\tLongRunningFunc apirequest.LongRunningRequestCheck\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192\t// GoawayChance is the probability that send a GOAWAY to HTTP/2 clients. When client received\n   264\u2192\t// GOAWAY, the in-flight requests will not be affected and new requests will use\n   265\u2192\t// a new TCP connection to triggering re-balancing to another server behind the load balance.\n   266\u2192\t// Default to 0, means never send GOAWAY. Max is 0.02 to prevent break the apiserver.\n   267\u2192\tGoawayChance float64\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192\t// MergedResourceConfig indicates which groupVersion enabled and its resources enabled/disabled.\n   270\u2192\t// This is composed of genericapiserver defaultAPIResourceConfig and those parsed from flags.\n   271\u2192\t// If not specify any in flags, then genericapiserver will only enable defaultAPIResourceConfig.\n   272\u2192\tMergedResourceConfig *serverstore.ResourceConfig\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192\t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals\n   275\u2192\t// that happen during lifecycle of the apiserver.\n   276\u2192\t// it's intentionally marked private as it should never be overridden.\n   277\u2192\tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192\t// StorageObjectCountTracker is used to keep track of the total number of objects\n   280\u2192\t// in the storage per resource, so we can estimate width of incoming requests.\n   281\u2192\tStorageObjectCountTracker flowcontrolrequest.StorageObjectCountTracker\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\n   284\u2192\t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\n   285\u2192\t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\n   286\u2192\t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\n   287\u2192\t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\n   288\u2192\t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\n   289\u2192\t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\n   290\u2192\tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192\t//===========================================================================\n   293\u2192\t// values below here are targets for removal\n   294\u2192\t//===========================================================================\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192\t// PublicAddress is the IP address where members of the cluster (kubelet,\n   297\u2192\t// kube-proxy, services, etc.) can reach the GenericAPIServer.\n   298\u2192\t// If nil or 0.0.0.0, the host's default interface will be used.\n   299\u2192\tPublicAddress net.IP\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192\t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\n   302\u2192\t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\n   303\u2192\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192\t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\n   306\u2192\tAPIServerID string\n   307\u2192\n   308\u2192\t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\n   309\u2192\tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\n   312\u2192\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192\t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\n   315\u2192\t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\n   316\u2192\t// watch request(s) to drain.\n   317\u2192\t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\n   318\u2192\t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\n   319\u2192\t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\n   320\u2192\t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\n   321\u2192\t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\n   322\u2192\t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\n   323\u2192\t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\n   324\u2192\t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\n   325\u2192\t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\n   326\u2192\t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\n   327\u2192\t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\n   328\u2192\t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\n   329\u2192\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\n   330\u2192}\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192type RecommendedConfig struct {\n   333\u2192\tConfig\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192\t// SharedInformerFactory provides shared informers for Kubernetes resources. This value is set by\n   336\u2192\t// RecommendedOptions.CoreAPI.ApplyTo called by RecommendedOptions.ApplyTo. It uses an in-cluster client config\n   337\u2192\t// by default, or the kubeconfig given with kubeconfig command line flag.\n   338\u2192\tSharedInformerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192\t// ClientConfig holds the kubernetes client configuration.\n   341\u2192\t// This value is set by RecommendedOptions.CoreAPI.ApplyTo called by RecommendedOptions.ApplyTo.\n   342\u2192\t// By default in-cluster client config is used.\n   343\u2192\tClientConfig *restclient.Config\n   344\u2192}\n   345\u2192\n   346\u2192type SecureServingInfo struct {\n   347\u2192\t// Listener is the secure server network listener.\n   348\u2192\tListener net.Listener\n   349\u2192"

"  1009\u2192func BuildHandlerChainWithStorageVersionPrecondition(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) http.Handler {\n  1010\u2192\t// WithStorageVersionPrecondition needs the WithRequestInfo to run first\n  1011\u2192\thandler := genericapifilters.WithStorageVersionPrecondition(apiHandler, c.StorageVersionManager, c.Serializer)\n  1012\u2192\treturn DefaultBuildHandlerChain(handler, c)\n  1013\u2192}\n  1014\u2192\n  1015\u2192func DefaultBuildHandlerChain(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) http.Handler {\n  1016\u2192\thandler := apiHandler\n  1017\u2192\n  1018\u2192\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n  1019\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuthorization(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)\n  1020\u2192\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"authorization\")\n  1021\u2192\n  1022\u2192\tif c.FlowControl != nil {\n  1023\u2192\t\tworkEstimatorCfg := flowcontrolrequest.DefaultWorkEstimatorConfig()\n  1024\u2192\t\trequestWorkEstimator := flowcontrolrequest.NewWorkEstimator(\n  1025\u2192\t\t\tc.StorageObjectCountTracker.Get, c.FlowControl.GetInterestedWatchCount, workEstimatorCfg, c.FlowControl.GetMaxSeats)\n  1026\u2192\t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n  1027\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithPriorityAndFairness(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.FlowControl, requestWorkEstimator, c.RequestTimeout/4)\n  1028\u2192\t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"priorityandfairness\")\n  1029\u2192\t} else {\n  1030\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithMaxInFlightLimit(handler, c.MaxRequestsInFlight, c.MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight, c.LongRunningFunc)\n  1031\u2192\t}\n  1032\u2192\n  1033\u2192\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n  1034\u2192\tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.ConstrainedImpersonation) {\n  1035\u2192\t\thandler = impersonation.WithConstrainedImpersonation(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)\n  1036\u2192\t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"constrainedimpersonation\")\n  1037\u2192\t} else {\n  1038\u2192\t\thandler = impersonation.WithImpersonation(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)\n  1039\u2192\t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"impersonation\")\n  1040\u2192\t}\n  1041\u2192\n  1042\u2192\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n  1043\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAudit(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator, c.LongRunningFunc)\n  1044\u2192\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"audit\")\n  1045\u2192\n  1046\u2192\tfailedHandler := genericapifilters.Unauthorized(c.Serializer)\n  1047\u2192\tfailedHandler = genericapifilters.WithFailedAuthenticationAudit(failedHandler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator)\n  1048\u2192\n  1049\u2192\t// WithTracing comes after authentication so we can allow authenticated\n  1050\u2192\t// clients to influence sampling.\n  1051\u2192\tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerTracing) {\n  1052\u2192\t\thandler = genericapifilters.WithTracing(handler, c.TracerProvider)\n  1053\u2192\t}\n  1054\u2192\tfailedHandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(failedHandler)\n  1055\u2192\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n  1056\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuthentication(handler, c.Authentication.Authenticator, failedHandler, c.Authentication.APIAudiences, c.Authentication.RequestHeaderConfig)\n  1057\u2192\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"authentication\")\n  1058\u2192\n  1059\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithCORS(handler, c.CorsAllowedOriginList, nil, nil, nil, \"true\")\n  1060\u2192\n  1061\u2192\t// WithWarningRecorder must be wrapped by the timeout handler\n  1062\u2192\t// to make the addition of warning headers threadsafe\n  1063\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithWarningRecorder(handler)\n  1064\u2192\n  1065\u2192\t// WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests will call the rest of the request handling in a go-routine with the\n  1066\u2192\t// context with deadline. The go-routine can keep running, while the timeout logic will return a timeout to the client.\n  1067\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\n  1068\u2192\n  1069\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestDeadline(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator,\n  1070\u2192\t\tc.LongRunningFunc, c.Serializer, c.RequestTimeout)\n  1071\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithWaitGroup(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup)\n  1072\u2192\tif c.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod > 0 {\n  1073\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown(handler, c.lifecycleSignals, c.WatchRequestWaitGroup)\n  1074\u2192\t}\n  1075\u2192\tif c.SecureServing != nil && !c.SecureServing.DisableHTTP2 && c.GoawayChance > 0 {\n  1076\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithProbabilisticGoaway(handler, c.GoawayChance)\n  1077\u2192\t}\n  1078\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithCacheControl(handler)\n  1079\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithHSTS(handler, c.HSTSDirectives)\n  1080\u2192\tif c.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n  1081\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithRetryAfter(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest.Signaled())\n  1082\u2192\t}\n  1083\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithHTTPLogging(handler)\n  1084\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithLatencyTrackers(handler)\n  1085\u2192\t// WithRoutine will execute future handlers in a separate goroutine and serving\n  1086\u2192\t// handler in current goroutine to minimize the stack memory usage. It must be\n  1087\u2192\t// after WithPanicRecover() to be protected from panics.\n  1088\u2192\tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServingWithRoutine) {\n  1089\u2192\t\thandler = routine.WithRoutine(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\n  1090\u2192\t}\n  1091\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestInfo(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)\n  1092\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestReceivedTimestamp(handler)\n  1093\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signaled())\n  1094\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithPanicRecovery(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)\n  1095\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuditInit(handler)\n  1096\u2192\treturn handler\n  1097\u2192}\n  1098\u2192\n  1099\u2192func installAPI(name string, s *GenericAPIServer, c *Config) {\n  1100\u2192\tif c.EnableIndex {\n  1101\u2192\t\troutes.Index{}.Install(s.listedPathProvider, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n  1102\u2192\t}\n  1103\u2192\tif c.EnableProfiling {\n  1104\u2192\t\troutes.Profiling{}.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n  1105\u2192\t\tif c.EnableContentionProfiling {\n  1106\u2192\t\t\tgoruntime.SetBlockProfileRate(1)\n  1107\u2192\t\t}\n  1108\u2192\t\t// so far, only logging related endpoints are considered valid to add for these debug flags."

"   376\u2192type AuthenticationInfo struct {\n   377\u2192\t// APIAudiences is a list of identifier that the API identifies as. This is\n   378\u2192\t// used by some authenticators to validate audience bound credentials.\n   379\u2192\tAPIAudiences authenticator.Audiences\n   380\u2192\t// Authenticator determines which subject is making the request\n   381\u2192\tAuthenticator authenticator.Request\n   382\u2192\n   383\u2192\tRequestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig\n   384\u2192}\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192type AuthorizationInfo struct {\n   387\u2192\t// Authorizer determines whether the subject is allowed to make the request based only\n   388\u2192\t// on the RequestURI\n   389\u2192\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\n   390\u2192}\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192func init() {\n   393\u2192\tutilruntime.Must(features.AddFeatureGates(utilfeature.DefaultMutableFeatureGate))\n   394\u2192}\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192// NewConfig returns a Config struct with the default values\n   397\u2192func NewConfig(codecs serializer.CodecFactory) *Config {\n   398\u2192\tdefaultHealthChecks := []healthz.HealthChecker{healthz.PingHealthz, healthz.LogHealthz}\n   399\u2192\tvar id string\n   400\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerIdentity) {\n   401\u2192\t\thostname, err := hostnameFunc()\n   402\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   403\u2192\t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"error getting hostname for apiserver identity: %v\", err)\n   404\u2192\t\t}\n   405\u2192\n   406\u2192\t\t// Since the hash needs to be unique across each kube-apiserver and aggregated apiservers,\n   407\u2192\t\t// the hash used for the identity should include both the hostname and the identity value.\n   408\u2192\t\t// TODO: receive the identity value as a parameter once the apiserver identity lease controller\n   409\u2192\t\t// post start hook is moved to generic apiserver.\n   410\u2192\t\tb := cryptobyte.NewBuilder(nil)\n   411\u2192\t\tb.AddUint16LengthPrefixed(func(b *cryptobyte.Builder) {\n   412\u2192\t\t\tb.AddBytes([]byte(hostname))\n   413\u2192\t\t})\n   414\u2192\t\tb.AddUint16LengthPrefixed(func(b *cryptobyte.Builder) {\n   415\u2192\t\t\tb.AddBytes([]byte(\"kube-apiserver\"))\n   416\u2192\t\t})\n   417\u2192\t\thashData, err := b.Bytes()\n   418\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   419\u2192\t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"error building hash data for apiserver identity: %v\", err)\n   420\u2192\t\t}\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192\t\thash := sha256.Sum256(hashData)\n   423\u2192\t\tid = \"apiserver-\" + strings.ToLower(base32.StdEncoding.WithPadding(base32.NoPadding).EncodeToString(hash[:16]))\n   424\u2192\t}\n   425\u2192\tlifecycleSignals := newLifecycleSignals()"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package admission\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"io\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    25\u2192\tauditinternal \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n    27\u2192)\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192// Attributes is an interface used by AdmissionController to get information about a request\n    30\u2192// that is used to make an admission decision.\n    31\u2192type Attributes interface {\n    32\u2192\t// GetName returns the name of the object as presented in the request.  On a CREATE operation, the client\n    33\u2192\t// may omit name and rely on the server to generate the name.  If that is the case, this method will return\n    34\u2192\t// the empty string\n    35\u2192\tGetName() string\n    36\u2192\t// GetNamespace is the namespace associated with the request (if any)\n    37\u2192\tGetNamespace() string\n    38\u2192\t// GetResource is the name of the resource being requested.  This is not the kind.  For example: pods\n    39\u2192\tGetResource() schema.GroupVersionResource\n    40\u2192\t// GetSubresource is the name of the subresource being requested.  This is a different resource, scoped to the parent resource, but it may have a different kind.\n    41\u2192\t// For instance, /pods has the resource \"pods\" and the kind \"Pod\", while /pods/foo/status has the resource \"pods\", the sub resource \"status\", and the kind \"Pod\"\n    42\u2192\t// (because status operates on pods). The binding resource for a pod though may be /pods/foo/binding, which has resource \"pods\", subresource \"binding\", and kind \"Binding\".\n    43\u2192\tGetSubresource() string\n    44\u2192\t// GetOperation is the operation being performed\n    45\u2192\tGetOperation() Operation\n    46\u2192\t// GetOperationOptions is the options for the operation being performed\n    47\u2192\tGetOperationOptions() runtime.Object\n    48\u2192\t// IsDryRun indicates that modifications will definitely not be persisted for this request. This is to prevent\n    49\u2192\t// admission controllers with side effects and a method of reconciliation from being overwhelmed.\n    50\u2192\t// However, a value of false for this does not mean that the modification will be persisted, because it\n    51\u2192\t// could still be rejected by a subsequent validation step.\n    52\u2192\tIsDryRun() bool\n    53\u2192\t// GetObject is the object from the incoming request prior to default values being applied\n    54\u2192\tGetObject() runtime.Object\n    55\u2192\t// GetOldObject is the existing object. Only populated for UPDATE and DELETE requests.\n    56\u2192\tGetOldObject() runtime.Object\n    57\u2192\t// GetKind is the type of object being manipulated.  For example: Pod\n    58\u2192\tGetKind() schema.GroupVersionKind\n    59\u2192\t// GetUserInfo is information about the requesting user\n    60\u2192\tGetUserInfo() user.Info\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\t// AddAnnotation sets annotation according to key-value pair. The key should be qualified, e.g., podsecuritypolicy.admission.k8s.io/admit-policy, where\n    63\u2192\t// \"podsecuritypolicy\" is the name of the plugin, \"admission.k8s.io\" is the name of the organization, \"admit-policy\" is the key name.\n    64\u2192\t// An error is returned if the format of key is invalid. When trying to overwrite annotation with a new value, an error is returned.\n    65\u2192\t// Both ValidationInterface and MutationInterface are allowed to add Annotations.\n    66\u2192\t// By default, an annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or\n    67\u2192\t// equal to Metadata.\n    68\u2192\tAddAnnotation(key, value string) error\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192\t// AddAnnotationWithLevel sets annotation according to key-value pair with additional intended audit level.\n    71\u2192\t// An Annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or equal to the\n    72\u2192\t// intended audit level.\n    73\u2192\tAddAnnotationWithLevel(key, value string, level auditinternal.Level) error\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192\t// GetReinvocationContext tracks the admission request information relevant to the re-invocation policy.\n    76\u2192\tGetReinvocationContext() ReinvocationContext\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192// ObjectInterfaces is an interface used by AdmissionController to get object interfaces\n    80\u2192// such as Converter or Defaulter. These interfaces are normally coming from Request Scope\n    81\u2192// to handle special cases like CRDs.\n    82\u2192type ObjectInterfaces interface {\n    83\u2192\t// GetObjectCreater is the ObjectCreator appropriate for the requested object.\n    84\u2192\tGetObjectCreater() runtime.ObjectCreater\n    85\u2192\t// GetObjectTyper is the ObjectTyper appropriate for the requested object.\n    86\u2192\tGetObjectTyper() runtime.ObjectTyper\n    87\u2192\t// GetObjectDefaulter is the ObjectDefaulter appropriate for the requested object.\n    88\u2192\tGetObjectDefaulter() runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n    89\u2192\t// GetObjectConvertor is the ObjectConvertor appropriate for the requested object.\n    90\u2192\tGetObjectConvertor() runtime.ObjectConvertor\n    91\u2192\t// GetEquivalentResourceMapper is the EquivalentResourceMapper appropriate for finding equivalent resources and expected kind for the requested object.\n    92\u2192\tGetEquivalentResourceMapper() runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\n    93\u2192}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192// privateAnnotationsGetter is a private interface which allows users to get annotations from Attributes.\n    96\u2192type privateAnnotationsGetter interface {\n    97\u2192\tgetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192// AnnotationsGetter allows users to get annotations from Attributes. An alternate Attribute should implement"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package admission\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import \"context\"\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192// chainAdmissionHandler is an instance of admission.NamedHandler that performs admission control using\n    22\u2192// a chain of admission handlers\n    23\u2192type chainAdmissionHandler []Interface\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192// NewChainHandler creates a new chain handler from an array of handlers. Used for testing.\n    26\u2192func NewChainHandler(handlers ...Interface) chainAdmissionHandler {\n    27\u2192\treturn chainAdmissionHandler(handlers)\n    28\u2192}\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192// Admit performs an admission control check using a chain of handlers, and returns immediately on first error\n    31\u2192func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Admit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {\n    32\u2192\tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\n    33\u2192\t\tif !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) {\n    34\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    35\u2192\t\t}\n    36\u2192\t\tif mutator, ok := handler.(MutationInterface); ok {\n    37\u2192\t\t\terr := mutator.Admit(ctx, a, o)\n    38\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n    39\u2192\t\t\t\treturn err\n    40\u2192\t\t\t}\n    41\u2192\t\t}\n    42\u2192\t}\n    43\u2192\treturn nil\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192// Validate performs an admission control check using a chain of handlers, and returns immediately on first error\n    47\u2192func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Validate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {\n    48\u2192\tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\n    49\u2192\t\tif !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) {\n    50\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    51\u2192\t\t}\n    52\u2192\t\tif validator, ok := handler.(ValidationInterface); ok {\n    53\u2192\t\t\terr := validator.Validate(ctx, a, o)\n    54\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n    55\u2192\t\t\t\treturn err\n    56\u2192\t\t\t}\n    57\u2192\t\t}\n    58\u2192\t}\n    59\u2192\treturn nil\n    60\u2192}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192// Handles will return true if any of the handlers handles the given operation\n    63\u2192func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Handles(operation Operation) bool {\n    64\u2192\tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\n    65\u2192\t\tif handler.Handles(operation) {\n    66\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n    67\u2192\t\t}\n    68\u2192\t}\n    69\u2192\treturn false\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192"

except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package admission\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"bytes\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"io\"\n    23\u2192\t\"os\"\n    24\u2192\t\"path\"\n    25\u2192\t\"path/filepath\"\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    28\u2192\t\"sigs.k8s.io/yaml\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver\"\n    34\u2192\tapiserverv1 \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver/v1\"\n    35\u2192)\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192func makeAbs(path, base string) (string, error) {\n    38\u2192\tif filepath.IsAbs(path) {\n    39\u2192\t\treturn path, nil\n    40\u2192\t}\n    41\u2192\tif len(base) == 0 || base == \".\" {\n    42\u2192\t\tcwd, err := os.Getwd()\n    43\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    44\u2192\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n    45\u2192\t\t}\n    46\u2192\t\tbase = cwd\n    47\u2192\t}\n    48\u2192\treturn filepath.Join(base, path), nil\n    49\u2192}\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192// ReadAdmissionConfiguration reads the admission configuration at the specified path.\n    52\u2192// It returns the loaded admission configuration if the input file aligns with the required syntax.\n    53\u2192// If it does not align with the provided syntax, it returns a default configuration for the enumerated\n    54\u2192// set of pluginNames whose config location references the specified configFilePath.\n    55\u2192// It does this to preserve backward compatibility when admission control files were opaque.\n    56\u2192// It returns an error if the file did not exist.\n    57\u2192func ReadAdmissionConfiguration(pluginNames []string, configFilePath string, configScheme *runtime.Scheme) (ConfigProvider, error) {\n    58\u2192\tif configFilePath == \"\" {\n    59\u2192\t\treturn configProvider{config: &apiserver.AdmissionConfiguration{}}, nil\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192\t// a file was provided, so we just read it.\n    62\u2192\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(configFilePath)\n    63\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    64\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unable to read admission control configuration from %q [%v]\", configFilePath, err)\n    65\u2192\t}\n    66\u2192\tcodecs := serializer.NewCodecFactory(configScheme, serializer.EnableStrict)\n    67\u2192\tdecoder := codecs.UniversalDecoder()\n    68\u2192\tdecodedObj, err := runtime.Decode(decoder, data)\n    69\u2192\t// we were able to decode the file successfully\n    70\u2192\tif err == nil {\n    71\u2192\t\tdecodedConfig, ok := decodedObj.(*apiserver.AdmissionConfiguration)\n    72\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n    73\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unexpected type: %T\", decodedObj)\n    74\u2192\t\t}\n    75\u2192\t\tbaseDir := path.Dir(configFilePath)\n    76\u2192\t\tfor i := range decodedConfig.Plugins {\n    77\u2192\t\t\tif decodedConfig.Plugins[i].Path == \"\" {\n    78\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n    79\u2192\t\t\t}\n    80\u2192\t\t\t// we update relative file paths to absolute paths\n    81\u2192\t\t\tabsPath, err := makeAbs(decodedConfig.Plugins[i].Path, baseDir)\n    82\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n    83\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n    84\u2192\t\t\t}\n    85\u2192\t\t\tdecodedConfig.Plugins[i].Path = absPath\n    86\u2192\t\t}\n    87\u2192\t\treturn configProvider{\n    88\u2192\t\t\tconfig: decodedConfig,\n    89\u2192\t\t}, nil\n    90\u2192\t}\n    91\u2192\t// we got an error where the decode wasn't related to a missing type\n    92\u2192\tif !(runtime.IsMissingVersion(err) || runtime.IsMissingKind(err) || runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(err)) {\n    93\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n    94\u2192\t}\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192\t// Only tolerate load errors if the file appears to be one of the two legacy plugin configs\n    97\u2192\tunstructuredData := map[string]interface{}{}\n    98\u2192\tif err2 := yaml.Unmarshal(data, &unstructuredData); err2 != nil {\n    99\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n   100\u2192\t}\n   101\u2192\t_, isLegacyImagePolicy := unstructuredData[\"imagePolicy\"]\n   102\u2192\t_, isLegacyPodNodeSelector := unstructuredData[\"podNodeSelectorPluginConfig\"]\n   103\u2192\tif !isLegacyImagePolicy && !isLegacyPodNodeSelector {\n   104\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n   105\u2192\t}\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192\t// convert the legacy format to the new admission control format\n   108\u2192\t// in order to preserve backwards compatibility, we set plugins that\n   109\u2192\t// previously read input from a non-versioned file configuration to the\n   110\u2192\t// current input file.\n   111\u2192\tlegacyPluginsWithUnversionedConfig := sets.NewString(\"ImagePolicyWebhook\", \"PodNodeSelector\")\n   112\u2192\texternalConfig := &apiserverv1.AdmissionConfiguration{}\n   113\u2192\tfor _, pluginName := range pluginNames {\n   114\u2192\t\tif legacyPluginsWithUnversionedConfig.Has(pluginName) {\n   115\u2192\t\t\texternalConfig.Plugins = append(externalConfig.Plugins,\n   116\u2192\t\t\t\tapiserverv1.AdmissionPluginConfiguration{\n   117\u2192\t\t\t\t\tName: pluginName,\n   118\u2192\t\t\t\t\tPath: configFilePath})\n   119\u2192\t\t}\n   120\u2192\t}\n   121\u2192\tconfigScheme.Default(externalConfig)\n   122\u2192\tinternalConfig := &apiserver.AdmissionConfiguration{}\n   123\u2192\tif err := configScheme.Convert(externalConfig, internalConfig, nil); err != nil {\n   124\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n   125\u2192\t}\n   126\u2192\treturn configProvider{\n   127\u2192\t\tconfig: internalConfig,\n   128\u2192\t}, nil\n   129\u2192}\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192type configProvider struct {\n   132\u2192\tconfig *apiserver.AdmissionConfiguration\n   133\u2192}\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192// GetAdmissionPluginConfigurationFor returns a reader that holds the admission plugin configuration.\n   136\u2192func GetAdmissionPluginConfigurationFor(pluginCfg apiserver.AdmissionPluginConfiguration) (io.Reader, error) {\n   137\u2192\t// if there is a nest object, return it directly\n   138\u2192\tif pluginCfg.Configuration != nil {\n   139\u2192\t\treturn bytes.NewBuffer(pluginCfg.Configuration.Raw), nil\n   140\u2192\t}\n   141\u2192\t// there is nothing nested, so we delegate to path\n   142\u2192\tif pluginCfg.Path != \"\" {\n   143\u2192\t\tcontent, err := os.ReadFile(pluginCfg.Path)\n   144\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   145\u2192\t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"Couldn't open admission plugin configuration %s: %#v\", pluginCfg.Path, err)\n   146\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   147\u2192\t\t}\n   148\u2192\t\treturn bytes.NewBuffer(content), nil\n   149\u2192\t}\n   150\u2192\t// there is no special config at all\n   151\u2192\treturn nil, nil\n   152\u2192}\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192// ConfigFor returns a reader for the specified plugin.\n   155\u2192// If no specific configuration is present, we return a nil reader.\n   156\u2192func (p configProvider) ConfigFor(pluginName string) (io.Reader, error) {\n   157\u2192\t// there is no config, so there is no potential config\n   158\u2192\tif p.config == nil {\n   159\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil\n   160\u2192\t}\n   161\u2192\t// look for matching plugin and get configuration\n   162\u2192\tfor _, pluginCfg := range p.config.Plugins {\n   163\u2192\t\tif pluginName != pluginCfg.Name {\n   164\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   165\u2192\t\t}\n   166\u2192\t\tpluginConfig, err := GetAdmissionPluginConfigurationFor(pluginCfg)\n   167\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   168\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   169\u2192\t\t}\n   170\u2192\t\treturn pluginConfig, nil\n   171\u2192\t}\n   172\u2192\t// there is no registered config that matches on plugin name.\n   173\u2192\treturn nil, nil\n   174\u2192}\n   175\u2192"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package storage\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    26\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n    32\u2192)\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192// Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.\n    35\u2192type Feature = string\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192// RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.\n    38\u2192var RequestWatchProgress Feature = \"RequestWatchProgress\"\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192// Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response\n    41\u2192// onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an\n    42\u2192// object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be\n    43\u2192// a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,\n    44\u2192// intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of\n    45\u2192// type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.\n    46\u2192type Versioner interface {\n    47\u2192\t// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object\n    48\u2192\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata\n    49\u2192\t// from database.\n    50\u2192\tUpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error\n    51\u2192\t// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object\n    52\u2192\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from\n    53\u2192\t// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client\n    54\u2192\t// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number\n    55\u2192\t// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during\n    56\u2192\t// serialization if it is set to nil.\n    57\u2192\tUpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error\n    58\u2192\t// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should\n    59\u2192\t// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.\n    60\u2192\tPrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error\n    61\u2192\t// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.\n    62\u2192\t// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.\n    63\u2192\tObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192\t// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and\n    66\u2192\t// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().\n    67\u2192\t// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch\n    68\u2192\t// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass\n    69\u2192\t// \"1\", you will see updates from \"2\" onwards).\n    70\u2192\tParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192// ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with\n    74\u2192// an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete\n    75\u2192// database and to improve testability.\n    76\u2192type ResponseMeta struct {\n    77\u2192\t// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be\n    78\u2192\t// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested\n    79\u2192\t// expiration time due to server lag).\n    80\u2192\tTTL int64\n    81\u2192\t// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.\n    82\u2192\tResourceVersion uint64\n    83\u2192}\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192// IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes\n    86\u2192// `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.\n    87\u2192type IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192// IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that\n    90\u2192// for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.\n    91\u2192type IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192// Everything accepts all objects.\n    94\u2192var Everything = SelectionPredicate{\n    95\u2192\tLabel: labels.Everything(),\n    96\u2192\tField: fields.Everything(),\n    97\u2192}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192// MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).\n   100\u2192type MatchValue struct {\n   101\u2192\tIndexName string\n   102\u2192\tValue     string\n   103\u2192}\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192// Pass an UpdateFunc to Interface.GuaranteedUpdate to make an update\n   106\u2192// that is guaranteed to succeed.\n   107\u2192// See the comment for GuaranteedUpdate for more details.\n   108\u2192type UpdateFunc func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (output runtime.Object, ttl *uint64, err error)\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192// ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\n   111\u2192// if the hook cannot be completed. The function may NOT transform the provided\n   112\u2192// object.\n   113\u2192// NOTE: the object in obj may be nil if it cannot be read from the\n   114\u2192// storage, due to transformation or decode error.\n   115\u2192type ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192// ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \"admit everything\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\n   118\u2192func ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\n   119\u2192\treturn nil\n   120\u2192}\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192// Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.\n   123\u2192type Preconditions struct {\n   124\u2192\t// Specifies the target UID.\n   125\u2192\t// +optional\n   126\u2192\tUID *types.UID `json:\"uid,omitempty\"`\n   127\u2192\t// Specifies the target ResourceVersion\n   128\u2192\t// +optional\n   129\u2192\tResourceVersion *string `json:\"resourceVersion,omitempty\"`\n   130\u2192}\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192// NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.\n   133\u2192func NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {\n   134\u2192\tu := types.UID(uid)\n   135\u2192\treturn &Preconditions{UID: &u}\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192func (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {\n   139\u2192\tif p == nil {\n   140\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   141\u2192\t}\n   142\u2192\tobjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\n   143\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   144\u2192\t\treturn NewInternalError(\n   145\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Errorf(\"can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w\",\n   146\u2192\t\t\t\t*p,\n   147\u2192\t\t\t\tobj,\n   148\u2192\t\t\t\terr))\n   149\u2192\t}\n   150\u2192\tif p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {"

36. Read @ -
