"   700\u2192// Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\n   701\u2192// from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\n   702\u2192func (c *Config) Complete(informers informers.SharedInformerFactory) CompletedConfig {\n   703\u2192\tif c.FeatureGate == nil {\n   704\u2192\t\tc.FeatureGate = utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate\n   705\u2192\t}\n   706\u2192\tif len(c.ExternalAddress) == 0 && c.PublicAddress != nil {\n   707\u2192\t\tc.ExternalAddress = c.PublicAddress.String()\n   708\u2192\t}\n   709\u2192\n   710\u2192\t// if there is no port, and we listen on one securely, use that one\n   711\u2192\tif _, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(c.ExternalAddress); err != nil {\n   712\u2192\t\tif c.SecureServing == nil {\n   713\u2192\t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"cannot derive external address port without listening on a secure port.\")\n   714\u2192\t\t}\n   715\u2192\t\t_, port, err := c.SecureServing.HostPort()\n   716\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   717\u2192\t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"cannot derive external address from the secure port: %v\", err)\n   718\u2192\t\t}\n   719\u2192\t\tc.ExternalAddress = net.JoinHostPort(c.ExternalAddress, strconv.Itoa(port))\n   720\u2192\t}\n   721\u2192\tcompleteOpenAPI(c.OpenAPIConfig, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\n   722\u2192\tcompleteOpenAPIV3(c.OpenAPIV3Config, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\n   723\u2192\n   724\u2192\tif c.DiscoveryAddresses == nil {\n   725\u2192\t\tc.DiscoveryAddresses = discovery.DefaultAddresses{DefaultAddress: c.ExternalAddress}\n   726\u2192\t}\n   727\u2192\n   728\u2192\tAuthorizeClientBearerToken(c.LoopbackClientConfig, &c.Authentication, &c.Authorization)\n   729\u2192\n   730\u2192\tif c.RequestInfoResolver == nil {\n   731\u2192\t\tc.RequestInfoResolver = NewRequestInfoResolver(c)\n   732\u2192\t}\n   733\u2192\n   734\u2192\tif c.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil {\n   735\u2192\t\tif c.RESTOptionsGetter == nil {\n   736\u2192\t\t\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistry()\n   737\u2192\t\t} else {\n   738\u2192\t\t\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistryWithIdentity(func(groupResource schema.GroupResource) string {\n   739\u2192\t\t\t\t// use the storage prefix as the key if possible\n   740\u2192\t\t\t\tif opts, err := c.RESTOptionsGetter.GetRESTOptions(groupResource, nil); err == nil {\n   741\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn opts.ResourcePrefix\n   742\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   743\u2192\t\t\t\t// otherwise return \"\" to use the default key (parent GV name)\n   744\u2192\t\t\t\treturn \"\"\n   745\u2192\t\t\t})\n   746\u2192\t\t}\n   747\u2192\t}\n   748\u2192\n   749\u2192\treturn CompletedConfig{&completedConfig{c, informers}}\n   750\u2192}\n   751\u2192\n   752\u2192// Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\n   753\u2192// from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\n   754\u2192func (c *RecommendedConfig) Complete() CompletedConfig {\n   755\u2192\treturn c.Config.Complete(c.SharedInformerFactory)\n   756\u2192}\n   757\u2192\n   758\u2192var defaultAllowedMediaTypes = []string{\n   759\u2192\truntime.ContentTypeJSON,\n   760\u2192\truntime.ContentTypeYAML,\n   761\u2192\truntime.ContentTypeProtobuf,\n   762\u2192}\n   763\u2192\n   764\u2192// New creates a new server which logically combines the handling chain with the passed server.\n   765\u2192// name is used to differentiate for logging. The handler chain in particular can be difficult as it starts delegating.\n   766\u2192// delegationTarget may not be nil.\n   767\u2192func (c completedConfig) New(name string, delegationTarget DelegationTarget) (*GenericAPIServer, error) {\n   768\u2192\tif c.Serializer == nil {\n   769\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.Serializer == nil\")\n   770\u2192\t}\n   771\u2192\tallowedMediaTypes := defaultAllowedMediaTypes\n   772\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.CBORServingAndStorage) {\n   773\u2192\t\tallowedMediaTypes = append(allowedMediaTypes, runtime.ContentTypeCBOR)\n   774\u2192\t}\n   775\u2192\tfor _, info := range c.Serializer.SupportedMediaTypes() {\n   776\u2192\t\tvar ok bool\n   777\u2192\t\tfor _, mt := range allowedMediaTypes {\n   778\u2192\t\t\tif info.MediaType == mt {\n   779\u2192\t\t\t\tok = true\n   780\u2192\t\t\t\tbreak\n   781\u2192\t\t\t}\n   782\u2192\t\t}\n   783\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   784\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"refusing to create new apiserver %q with support for media type %q (allowed media types are: %s)\", name, info.MediaType, strings.Join(allowedMediaTypes, \", \"))\n   785\u2192\t\t}\n   786\u2192\t}\n   787\u2192\tif c.LoopbackClientConfig == nil {\n   788\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.LoopbackClientConfig == nil\")\n   789\u2192\t}\n   790\u2192\tif c.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil {\n   791\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil\")\n   792\u2192\t}\n   793\u2192\n   794\u2192\thandlerChainBuilder := func(handler http.Handler) http.Handler {\n   795\u2192\t\treturn c.BuildHandlerChainFunc(handler, c.Config)\n   796\u2192\t}\n   797\u2192\n   798\u2192\tvar debugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\n   799\u2192\tif c.DebugSocketPath != \"\" {"

"   109\u2192type GenericAPIServer struct {\n   110\u2192\t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\n   111\u2192\tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n   114\u2192\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192\t// minRequestTimeout is how short the request timeout can be.  This is used to build the RESTHandler\n   117\u2192\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192\t// ShutdownTimeout is the timeout used for server shutdown. This specifies the timeout before server\n   120\u2192\t// gracefully shutdown returns.\n   121\u2192\tShutdownTimeout time.Duration\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192\t// legacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\n   124\u2192\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup\n   125\u2192\tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192\t// admissionControl is used to build the RESTStorage that backs an API Group.\n   128\u2192\tadmissionControl admission.Interface\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192\t// SecureServingInfo holds configuration of the TLS server.\n   131\u2192\tSecureServingInfo *SecureServingInfo\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192\t// ExternalAddress is the address (hostname or IP and port) that should be used in\n   134\u2192\t// external (public internet) URLs for this GenericAPIServer.\n   135\u2192\tExternalAddress string\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192\t// Serializer controls how common API objects not in a group/version prefix are serialized for this server.\n   138\u2192\t// Individual APIGroups may define their own serializers.\n   139\u2192\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192\t// \"Outputs\"\n   142\u2192\t// Handler holds the handlers being used by this API server\n   143\u2192\tHandler *APIServerHandler\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192\t// UnprotectedDebugSocket is used to serve pprof information in a unix-domain socket. This socket is\n   146\u2192\t// not protected by authentication/authorization.\n   147\u2192\tUnprotectedDebugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192\t// listedPathProvider is a lister which provides the set of paths to show at /\n   150\u2192\tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192\t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\n   153\u2192\tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\n   156\u2192\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192\t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\n   159\u2192\tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192\t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\n   162\u2192\tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\n   165\u2192\topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\n   168\u2192\topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\n   171\u2192\t// during PrepareRun.\n   172\u2192\t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\n   173\u2192\t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\n   174\u2192\tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192\t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\n   177\u2192\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n   178\u2192\tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192\t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\n   181\u2192\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n   182\u2192\tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\n   185\u2192\t// It is set during PrepareRun.\n   186\u2192\tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192\t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\n   189\u2192\t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\n   190\u2192\t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\n   191\u2192\tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\n   192\u2192\tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\n   193\u2192\tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\n   194\u2192\tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192\tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\n   197\u2192\tpreShutdownHooks       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n   198\u2192\tpreShutdownHooksCalled bool\n   199\u2192\n   200\u2192\t// healthz checks\n   201\u2192\thealthzRegistry healthCheckRegistry\n   202\u2192\treadyzRegistry  healthCheckRegistry\n   203\u2192\tlivezRegistry   healthCheckRegistry\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192\tlivezGracePeriod time.Duration\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192\t// auditing. The backend is started before the server starts listening.\n   208\u2192\tAuditBackend audit.Backend"

"   848\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallLegacyAPIGroup(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) error {\n   849\u2192\tif !s.legacyAPIGroupPrefixes.Has(apiPrefix) {\n   850\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%q is not in the allowed legacy API prefixes: %v\", apiPrefix, s.legacyAPIGroupPrefixes.List())\n   851\u2192\t}\n   852\u2192\n   853\u2192\topenAPIModels, err := s.getOpenAPIModels(apiPrefix, apiGroupInfo)\n   854\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   855\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to get openapi models: %v\", err)\n   856\u2192\t}\n   857\u2192\n   858\u2192\tif err := s.installAPIResources(apiPrefix, apiGroupInfo, openAPIModels); err != nil {\n   859\u2192\t\treturn err\n   860\u2192\t}\n   861\u2192\n   862\u2192\t// Install the version handler.\n   863\u2192\t// Add a handler at /<apiPrefix> to enumerate the supported api versions.\n   864\u2192\tlegacyRootAPIHandler := discovery.NewLegacyRootAPIHandler(s.discoveryAddresses, s.Serializer, apiPrefix)\n   865\u2192\t// No peer-to-peer discovery for legacy API group.\n   866\u2192\twrapped := discoveryendpoint.WrapAggregatedDiscoveryToHandler(legacyRootAPIHandler, s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager, s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager)\n   867\u2192\ts.Handler.GoRestfulContainer.Add(wrapped.GenerateWebService(\"/api\", metav1.APIVersions{}))\n   868\u2192\ts.registerStorageReadinessCheck(\"\", apiGroupInfo)\n   869\u2192\n   870\u2192\treturn nil\n   871\u2192}\n   872\u2192\n   873\u2192// InstallAPIGroups exposes given api groups in the API.\n   874\u2192// The <apiGroupInfos> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the\n   875\u2192// underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.\n   876\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfos ...*APIGroupInfo) error {\n   877\u2192\tfor _, apiGroupInfo := range apiGroupInfos {\n   878\u2192\t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions) == 0 {\n   879\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no version priority set for %#v\", *apiGroupInfo)\n   880\u2192\t\t}\n   881\u2192\t\t// Do not register empty group or empty version.  Doing so claims /apis/ for the wrong entity to be returned.\n   882\u2192\t\t// Catching these here places the error  much closer to its origin\n   883\u2192\t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group) == 0 {\n   884\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"cannot register handler with an empty group for %#v\", *apiGroupInfo)\n   885\u2192\t\t}\n   886\u2192\t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Version) == 0 {\n   887\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"cannot register handler with an empty version for %#v\", *apiGroupInfo)\n   888\u2192\t\t}\n   889\u2192\t}\n   890\u2192\n   891\u2192\topenAPIModels, err := s.getOpenAPIModels(APIGroupPrefix, apiGroupInfos...)\n   892\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   893\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to get openapi models: %v\", err)\n   894\u2192\t}\n   895\u2192\n   896\u2192\tfor _, apiGroupInfo := range apiGroupInfos {\n   897\u2192\t\tif err := s.installAPIResources(APIGroupPrefix, apiGroupInfo, openAPIModels); err != nil {\n   898\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to install api resources: %v\", err)\n   899\u2192\t\t}\n   900\u2192\n   901\u2192\t\t// setup discovery\n   902\u2192\t\t// Install the version handler.\n   903\u2192\t\t// Add a handler at /apis/<groupName> to enumerate all versions supported by this group.\n   904\u2192\t\tapiVersionsForDiscovery := []metav1.GroupVersionForDiscovery{}\n   905\u2192\t\tfor _, groupVersion := range apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions {\n   906\u2192\t\t\t// Check the config to make sure that we elide versions that don't have any resources\n   907\u2192\t\t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap[groupVersion.Version]) == 0 {\n   908\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   909\u2192\t\t\t}\n   910\u2192\t\t\tapiVersionsForDiscovery = append(apiVersionsForDiscovery, metav1.GroupVersionForDiscovery{\n   911\u2192\t\t\t\tGroupVersion: groupVersion.String(),\n   912\u2192\t\t\t\tVersion:      groupVersion.Version,\n   913\u2192\t\t\t})\n   914\u2192\t\t}\n   915\u2192\t\tpreferredVersionForDiscovery := metav1.GroupVersionForDiscovery{\n   916\u2192\t\t\tGroupVersion: apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].String(),\n   917\u2192\t\t\tVersion:      apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Version,\n   918\u2192\t\t}\n   919\u2192\t\tapiGroup := metav1.APIGroup{\n   920\u2192\t\t\tName:             apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group,\n   921\u2192\t\t\tVersions:         apiVersionsForDiscovery,\n   922\u2192\t\t\tPreferredVersion: preferredVersionForDiscovery,\n   923\u2192\t\t}\n   924\u2192\n   925\u2192\t\ts.DiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroup(apiGroup)\n   926\u2192\t\ts.Handler.GoRestfulContainer.Add(discovery.NewAPIGroupHandler(s.Serializer, apiGroup).WebService())\n   927\u2192\t\ts.registerStorageReadinessCheck(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group, apiGroupInfo)\n   928\u2192\t}\n   929\u2192\treturn nil\n   930\u2192}\n   931\u2192\n   932\u2192// registerStorageReadinessCheck registers the readiness checks for all underlying storages\n   933\u2192// for a given APIGroup.\n   934\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) registerStorageReadinessCheck(groupName string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) {\n   935\u2192\tfor version, storageMap := range apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n   936\u2192\t\tfor resource, storage := range storageMap {\n   937\u2192\t\t\tif withReadiness, ok := storage.(rest.StorageWithReadiness); ok {\n   938\u2192\t\t\t\tgvr := metav1.GroupVersionResource{\n   939\u2192\t\t\t\t\tGroup:    groupName,\n   940\u2192\t\t\t\t\tVersion:  version,\n   941\u2192\t\t\t\t\tResource: resource,\n   942\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   943\u2192\t\t\t\ts.StorageReadinessHook.RegisterStorage(gvr, withReadiness)\n   944\u2192\t\t\t}\n   945\u2192\t\t}\n   946\u2192\t}\n   947\u2192}\n   948\u2192\n   949\u2192// InstallAPIGroup exposes the given api group in the API.\n   950\u2192// The <apiGroupInfo> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the\n   951\u2192// underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.\n   952\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroup(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) error {\n   953\u2192\treturn s.InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfo)\n   954\u2192}\n   955\u2192\n   956\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) getAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, groupVersion schema.GroupVersion, apiPrefix string) (*genericapi.APIGroupVersion, error) {\n   957\u2192\tstorage := make(map[string]rest.Storage)\n   958\u2192\tfor k, v := range apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap[groupVersion.Version] {\n   959\u2192\t\tif strings.ToLower(k) != k {\n   960\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"resource names must be lowercase only, not %q\", k)\n   961\u2192\t\t}\n   962\u2192\t\tstorage[k] = v\n   963\u2192\t}\n   964\u2192\tversion := s.newAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo, groupVersion)\n   965\u2192\tversion.Root = apiPrefix\n   966\u2192\tversion.Storage = storage\n   967\u2192\treturn version, nil\n   968\u2192}\n   969\u2192\n   970\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) newAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, groupVersion schema.GroupVersion) *genericapi.APIGroupVersion {\n   971\u2192\n   972\u2192\tallServedVersionsByResource := map[string][]string{}\n   973\u2192\tfor version, resourcesInVersion := range apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n   974\u2192\t\tfor resource := range resourcesInVersion {\n   975\u2192\t\t\tif len(groupVersion.Group) == 0 {\n   976\u2192\t\t\t\tallServedVersionsByResource[resource] = append(allServedVersionsByResource[resource], version)\n   977\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   978\u2192\t\t\t\tallServedVersionsByResource[resource] = append(allServedVersionsByResource[resource], fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", groupVersion.Group, version))\n   979\u2192\t\t\t}\n   980\u2192\t\t}\n   981\u2192\t}\n   982\u2192\n   983\u2192\treturn &genericapi.APIGroupVersion{\n   984\u2192\t\tGroupVersion:                groupVersion,\n   985\u2192\t\tAllServedVersionsByResource: allServedVersionsByResource,\n   986\u2192\t\tMetaGroupVersion:            apiGroupInfo.MetaGroupVersion,\n   987\u2192\n   988\u2192\t\tParameterCodec:        apiGroupInfo.ParameterCodec,\n   989\u2192\t\tSerializer:            apiGroupInfo.NegotiatedSerializer,\n   990\u2192\t\tCreater:               apiGroupInfo.Scheme,\n   991\u2192\t\tConvertor:             apiGroupInfo.Scheme,\n   992\u2192\t\tConvertabilityChecker: apiGroupInfo.Scheme,\n   993\u2192\t\tUnsafeConvertor:       runtime.UnsafeObjectConvertor(apiGroupInfo.Scheme),\n   994\u2192\t\tDefaulter:             apiGroupInfo.Scheme,\n   995\u2192\t\tTyper:                 apiGroupInfo.Scheme,\n   996\u2192\t\tNamer:                 runtime.Namer(meta.NewAccessor()),\n   997\u2192"

\"x-kubernetes-group-version-kind\"\n    58\u2192\tRouteMetaSelectableFields = \"x-kubernetes-selectable-fields\"\n    59\u2192\tRouteMetaAction           = \"x-kubernetes-action\"\n    60\u2192)\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192type APIInstaller struct {\n    63\u2192\tgroup             *APIGroupVersion\n    64\u2192\tprefix            string // Path prefix where API resources are to be registered.\n    65\u2192\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n    66\u2192}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// Struct capturing information about an action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\n    69\u2192type action struct {\n    70\u2192\tVerb          string               // Verb identifying the action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\n    71\u2192\tPath          string               // The path of the action\n    72\u2192\tParams        []*restful.Parameter // List of parameters associated with the action.\n    73\u2192\tNamer         handlers.ScopeNamer\n    74\u2192\tAllNamespaces bool // true iff the action is namespaced but works on aggregate result for all namespaces\n    75\u2192}\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192func ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(list []metav1.APIResource) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, error) {\n    78\u2192\tvar apiResourceList []apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery\n    79\u2192\tparentResources := make(map[string]int)\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t// Loop through all top-level resources\n    82\u2192\tfor _, r := range list {\n    83\u2192\t\tif strings.Contains(r.Name, \"/\") {\n    84\u2192\t\t\t// Skip subresources for now so we can get the list of resources\n    85\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    86\u2192\t\t}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192\t\tvar scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope\n    89\u2192\t\tif r.Namespaced {\n    90\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace\n    91\u2192\t\t} else {\n    92\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\n    93\u2192\t\t}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\t\tresource := apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{\n    96\u2192\t\t\tResource: r.Name,\n    97\u2192\t\t\tScope:    scope,\n    98\u2192\t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{\n    99\u2192\t\t\t\tGroup:   r.Group,\n   100\u2192\t\t\t\tVersion: r.Version,\n   101\u2192\t\t\t\tKind:    r.Kind,\n   102\u2192\t\t\t},\n   103\u2192\t\t\tVerbs:            r.Verbs,\n   104\u2192\t\t\tShortNames:       r.ShortNames,\n   105\u2192\t\t\tCategories:       r.Categories,\n   106\u2192\t\t\tSingularResource: r.SingularName,\n   107\u2192\t\t}\n   108\u2192\t\tapiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, resource)\n   109\u2192\t\tparentResources[r.Name] = len(apiResourceList) - 1\n   110\u2192\t}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192\t// Loop through all subresources\n   113\u2192\tfor _, r := range list {\n   114\u2192\t\t// Split resource name and subresource name\n   115\u2192\t\tsplit := strings.SplitN(r.Name, \"/\", 2)\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192\t\tif len(split) != 2 {\n   118\u2192\t\t\t// Skip parent resources\n   119\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   120\u2192\t\t}\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192\t\tvar scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope\n   123\u2192\t\tif r.Namespaced {\n   124\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace\n   125\u2192\t\t} else {\n   126\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\n   127\u2192\t\t}\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192\t\tparentidx, exists := parentResources[split[0]]\n   130\u2192\t\tif !exists {\n   131\u2192\t\t\t// If a subresource exists without a parent, create a parent\n   132\u2192\t\t\tapiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{\n   133\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: split[0],\n   134\u2192\t\t\t\tScope:    scope,\n   135\u2192\t\t\t\t// avoid nil panics in v0.26.0-v0.26.3 client-go clients\n   136\u2192\t\t\t\t// see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/118361\n   137\u2192\t\t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{},\n   138\u2192\t\t\t})\n   139\u2192\t\t\tparentidx = len(apiResourceList) - 1\n   140\u2192\t\t\tparentResources[split[0]] = parentidx\n   141\u2192\t\t}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192\t\tif apiResourceList[parentidx].Scope != scope {\n   144\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Error: Parent %s (scope: %s) and subresource %s (scope: %s) scope do not match\", split[0], apiResourceList[parentidx].Scope, split[1], scope)\n   145\u2192\t\t\t//\n   146\u2192\t\t}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192\t\tsubresource := apidiscoveryv2.APISubresourceDiscovery{\n   149\u2192\t\t\tSubresource: split[1],\n   150\u2192\t\t\tVerbs:       r.Verbs,"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package endpoints\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"path\"\n    21\u2192\t\"time\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\trestful \"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\tapidiscoveryv2 \"k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2\"\n    26\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    29\u2192\tutilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n    36\u2192)\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192// ConvertabilityChecker indicates what versions a GroupKind is available in.\n    39\u2192type ConvertabilityChecker interface {\n    40\u2192\t// VersionsForGroupKind indicates what versions are available to convert a group kind. This determines\n    41\u2192\t// what our decoding abilities are.\n    42\u2192\tVersionsForGroupKind(gk schema.GroupKind) []schema.GroupVersion\n    43\u2192}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192// APIGroupVersion is a helper for exposing rest.Storage objects as http.Handlers via go-restful\n    46\u2192// It handles URLs of the form:\n    47\u2192// /${storage_key}[/${object_name}]\n    48\u2192// Where 'storage_key' points to a rest.Storage object stored in storage.\n    49\u2192// This object should contain all parameterization necessary for running a particular API version\n    50\u2192type APIGroupVersion struct {\n    51\u2192\tStorage map[string]rest.Storage\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\tRoot string\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192\t// GroupVersion is the external group version\n    56\u2192\tGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\t// AllServedVersionsByResource is indexed by resource and maps to a list of versions that resource exists in.\n    59\u2192\t// This was created so that StorageVersion for APIs can include a list of all version that are served for each\n    60\u2192\t// GroupResource tuple.\n    61\u2192\tAllServedVersionsByResource map[string][]string\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the Kubernetes APIVersion used for common objects in the apiserver\n    64\u2192\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\n    65\u2192\t// define a version \"v1beta1\" but want to use the Kubernetes \"v1\" internal objects. If\n    66\u2192\t// empty, defaults to GroupVersion.\n    67\u2192\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n    68\u2192\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \"meta.k8s.io/v1\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\n    69\u2192\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\n    70\u2192\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\n    71\u2192\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\t// Serializer is used to determine how to convert responses from API methods into bytes to send over\n    74\u2192\t// the wire.\n    75\u2192\tSerializer     runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n    76\u2192\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\tTyper                 runtime.ObjectTyper\n    79\u2192\tCreater               runtime.ObjectCreater\n    80\u2192\tConvertor             runtime.ObjectConvertor\n    81\u2192\tConvertabilityChecker ConvertabilityChecker\n    82\u2192\tDefaulter             runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n    83\u2192\tNamer                 runtime.Namer\n    84\u2192\tUnsafeConvertor       runtime.ObjectConvertor\n    85\u2192\tTypeConverter         managedfields.TypeConverter\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192\t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\n    90\u2192\t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\n    91\u2192\t// the create-on-update case\n    92\u2192\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192\tAdmit admission.Interface\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192\tMinRequestTimeout time.Duration\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192\t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\n    99\u2192\t// 0 means no limit.\n   100\u2192\tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n   101\u2192}\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192// InstallREST registers the REST handlers (storage, watch, proxy and redirect) into a restful Container.\n   104\u2192// It is expected that the provided path root prefix will serve all operations. Root MUST NOT end\n   105\u2192// in a slash.\n   106\u2192func (g *APIGroupVersion) InstallREST(container *restful.Container) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, []*storageversion.ResourceInfo, error) {\n   107\u2192\tprefix := path.Join(g.Root, g.GroupVersion.Group, g.GroupVersion.Version)\n   108\u2192\tinstaller := &APIInstaller{\n   109\u2192\t\tgroup:             g,\n   110\u2192\t\tprefix:            prefix,\n   111\u2192\t\tminRequestTimeout: g.MinRequestTimeout,\n   112\u2192\t}\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192\tapiResources, resourceInfos, ws, registrationErrors := installer.Install()\n   115\u2192\tversionDiscoveryHandler := discovery.NewAPIVersionHandler(g.Serializer, g.GroupVersion, staticLister{apiResources})\n   116\u2192\tversionDiscoveryHandler.AddToWebService(ws)\n   117\u2192\tcontainer.Add(ws)\n   118\u2192\taggregatedDiscoveryResources, err := ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(apiResources)\n   119\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   120\u2192\t\tregistrationErrors = append(registrationErrors, err)\n   121\u2192\t}\n   122\u2192\treturn aggregatedDiscoveryResources, removeNonPersistedResources(resourceInfos), utilerrors.NewAggregate(registrationErrors)\n   123\u2192}\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192func removeNonPersistedResources(infos []*storageversion.ResourceInfo) []*storageversion.ResourceInfo {\n   126\u2192\tvar filtered []*storageversion.ResourceInfo\n   127\u2192\tfor _, info := range infos {\n   128\u2192\t\t// if EncodingVersion is empty, then the apiserver does not\n   129\u2192\t\t// need to register this resource via the storage version API,\n   130\u2192\t\t// thus we can remove it.\n   131\u2192\t\tif info != nil && len(info.EncodingVersion) > 0 {\n   132\u2192\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, info)\n   133\u2192\t\t}\n   134\u2192\t}\n   135\u2192\treturn filtered\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192// staticLister implements the APIResourceLister interface\n   139\u2192type staticLister struct {\n   140\u2192\tlist []metav1.APIResource\n   141\u2192}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192func (s staticLister) ListAPIResources() []metav1.APIResource {\n   144\u2192\treturn s.list\n   145\u2192}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192var _ discovery.APIResourceLister = &staticLister{}\n   148\u2192"

either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package authenticatorfactory\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    21\u2192\t\"time\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/group\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/anonymous\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/bearertoken\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/headerrequest\"\n    30\u2192\tunionauth \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/union\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/websocket\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/x509\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/token/cache\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/dynamiccertificates\"\n    35\u2192\twebhooktoken \"k8s.io/apiserver/plugin/pkg/authenticator/token/webhook\"\n    36\u2192\tauthenticationclient \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed/authentication/v1\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\"\n    38\u2192)\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192// DelegatingAuthenticatorConfig is the minimal configuration needed to create an authenticator\n    41\u2192// built to delegate authentication to a kube API server\n    42\u2192type DelegatingAuthenticatorConfig struct {\n    43\u2192\tAnonymous *apiserver.AnonymousAuthConfig\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192\t// TokenAccessReviewClient is a client to do token review. It can be nil. Then every token is ignored.\n    46\u2192\tTokenAccessReviewClient authenticationclient.AuthenticationV1Interface\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192\t// TokenAccessReviewTimeout specifies a time limit for requests made by the authorization webhook client.\n    49\u2192\tTokenAccessReviewTimeout time.Duration\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192\t// WebhookRetryBackoff specifies the backoff parameters for the authentication webhook retry logic.\n    52\u2192\t// This allows us to configure the sleep time at each iteration and the maximum number of retries allowed\n    53\u2192\t// before we fail the webhook call in order to limit the fan out that ensues when the system is degraded.\n    54\u2192\tWebhookRetryBackoff *wait.Backoff\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192\t// CacheTTL is the length of time that a token authentication answer will be cached.\n    57\u2192\tCacheTTL time.Duration\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192\t// CAContentProvider are the options for verifying incoming connections using mTLS and directly assigning to users.\n    60\u2192\t// Generally this is the CA bundle file used to authenticate client certificates\n    61\u2192\t// If this is nil, then mTLS will not be used.\n    62\u2192\tClientCertificateCAContentProvider dynamiccertificates.CAContentProvider\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192\tAPIAudiences authenticator.Audiences\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192\tRequestHeaderConfig *RequestHeaderConfig\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192func (c DelegatingAuthenticatorConfig) New() (authenticator.Request, *spec.SecurityDefinitions, error) {\n    70\u2192\tauthenticators := []authenticator.Request{}\n    71\u2192\tsecurityDefinitions := spec.SecurityDefinitions{}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\t// front-proxy first, then remote\n    74\u2192\t// Add the front proxy authenticator if requested\n    75\u2192\tif c.RequestHeaderConfig != nil {\n    76\u2192\t\trequestHeaderAuthenticator := headerrequest.NewDynamicVerifyOptionsSecure(\n    77\u2192\t\t\tc.RequestHeaderConfig.CAContentProvider.VerifyOptions,\n    78\u2192\t\t\tc.RequestHeaderConfig.AllowedClientNames,\n    79\u2192\t\t\tc.RequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,\n    80\u2192\t\t\tc.RequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,\n    81\u2192\t\t\tc.RequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,\n    82\u2192\t\t\tc.RequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,\n    83\u2192\t\t)\n    84\u2192\t\tauthenticators = append(authenticators, requestHeaderAuthenticator)\n    85\u2192\t}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\t// x509 client cert auth\n    88\u2192\tif c.ClientCertificateCAContentProvider != nil {\n    89\u2192\t\tauthenticators = append(authenticators, x509.NewDynamic(c.ClientCertificateCAContentProvider.VerifyOptions, x509.CommonNameUserConversion))\n    90\u2192\t}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\tif c.TokenAccessReviewClient != nil {\n    93\u2192\t\tif c.WebhookRetryBackoff == nil {\n    94\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, errors.New(\"retry backoff parameters for delegating authentication webhook has not been specified\")\n    95\u2192\t\t}\n    96\u2192\t\ttokenAuth, err := webhooktoken.NewFromInterface(c.TokenAccessReviewClient, c.APIAudiences, *c.WebhookRetryBackoff, c.TokenAccessReviewTimeout, webhooktoken.AuthenticatorMetrics{\n    97\u2192\t\t\tRecordRequestTotal:   RecordRequestTotal,\n    98\u2192\t\t\tRecordRequestLatency: RecordRequestLatency,\n    99\u2192\t\t})\n   100\u2192\t\tif err != nil {"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package authorizerfactory\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    21\u2192\t\"time\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n    25\u2192\tauthorizationcel \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/cel\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/plugin/pkg/authorizer/webhook\"\n    27\u2192\tauthorizationclient \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/typed/authorization/v1\"\n    28\u2192)\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192// DelegatingAuthorizerConfig is the minimal configuration needed to create an authorizer\n    31\u2192// built to delegate authorization to a kube API server\n    32\u2192type DelegatingAuthorizerConfig struct {\n    33\u2192\tSubjectAccessReviewClient authorizationclient.AuthorizationV1Interface\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\t// Compiler is the CEL compiler to use for evaluating policies. If nil, a default compiler will be used.\n    36\u2192\tCompiler authorizationcel.Compiler\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192\t// AllowCacheTTL is the length of time that a successful authorization response will be cached\n    39\u2192\tAllowCacheTTL time.Duration\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192\t// DenyCacheTTL is the length of time that an unsuccessful authorization response will be cached.\n    42\u2192\t// You generally want more responsive, \"deny, try again\" flows.\n    43\u2192\tDenyCacheTTL time.Duration\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192\t// WebhookRetryBackoff specifies the backoff parameters for the authorization webhook retry logic.\n    46\u2192\t// This allows us to configure the sleep time at each iteration and the maximum number of retries allowed\n    47\u2192\t// before we fail the webhook call in order to limit the fan out that ensues when the system is degraded.\n    48\u2192\tWebhookRetryBackoff *wait.Backoff\n    49\u2192}\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192func (c DelegatingAuthorizerConfig) New() (authorizer.Authorizer, error) {\n    52\u2192\tif c.WebhookRetryBackoff == nil {\n    53\u2192\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"retry backoff parameters for delegating authorization webhook has not been specified\")\n    54\u2192\t}\n    55\u2192\tcompiler := c.Compiler\n    56\u2192\tif compiler == nil {\n    57\u2192\t\tcompiler = authorizationcel.NewDefaultCompiler()\n    58\u2192\t}\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192\treturn webhook.NewFromInterface(\n    61\u2192\t\tc.SubjectAccessReviewClient,\n    62\u2192\t\tc.AllowCacheTTL,\n    63\u2192\t\tc.DenyCacheTTL,\n    64\u2192\t\t*c.WebhookRetryBackoff,\n    65\u2192\t\tauthorizer.DecisionNoOpinion,\n    66\u2192\t\tNewDelegatingAuthorizerMetrics(),\n    67\u2192\t\tcompiler,\n    68\u2192\t)\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192"

58\u2192\n    59\u2192// FinishFunc is a function returned by Begin hooks to complete an operation.\n    60\u2192type FinishFunc func(ctx context.Context, success bool)\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192// AfterDeleteFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterDelete hook.\n    63\u2192type AfterDeleteFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.DeleteOptions)\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192// BeginCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginCreate hook.\n    66\u2192type BeginCreateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (FinishFunc, error)\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// AfterCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterCreate hook.\n    69\u2192type AfterCreateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions)\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192// error)\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192// AfterUpdateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterUpdate hook.\n    75\u2192type AfterUpdateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.UpdateOptions)\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192// GenericStore interface can be used for type assertions when we need to access the underlying strategies.\n    78\u2192type GenericStore interface {\n    79\u2192\tGetCreateStrategy() rest.RESTCreateStrategy\n    80\u2192\tGetUpdateStrategy() rest.RESTUpdateStrategy\n    81\u2192\tGetDeleteStrategy() rest.RESTDeleteStrategy\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192// Store implements k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest.StandardStorage. It's\n    85\u2192// intended to be embeddable and allows the consumer to implement any\n    86\u2192// non-generic functions that are required. This object is intended to be\n    87\u2192// copyable so that it can be used in different ways but share the same\n    88\u2192// underlying behavior.\n    89\u2192//\n    90\u2192// All fields are required unless specified.\n    91\u2192//\n    92\u2192// The intended use of this type is embedding within a Kind specific\n    93\u2192// RESTStorage implementation. This type provides CRUD semantics on a Kubelike\n    94\u2192// resource, handling details like conflict detection with ResourceVersion and\n    95\u2192// semantics. The RESTCreateStrategy, RESTUpdateStrategy, and\n    96\u2192// RESTDeleteStrategy are generic across all backends, and encapsulate logic\n    97\u2192// specific to the API.\n    98\u2192//\n    99\u2192// TODO: make the default exposed methods exactly match a generic RESTStorage\n   100\u2192type Store struct {\n   101\u2192\t// NewFunc returns a new instance of the type this registry returns for a\n   102\u2192\t// GET of a single object, e.g.:\n   103\u2192\t//\n   104\u2192\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource/name-of-object\n   105\u2192\tNewFunc func() runtime.Object\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192\t// NewListFunc returns a new list of the type this registry; it is the\n   108\u2192\t// type returned when the resource is listed, e.g.:\n   109\u2192\t//\n   110\u2192\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource\n   111\u2192\tNewListFunc func() runtime.Object\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192\t// DefaultQualifiedResource is the pluralized name of the resource.\n   114\u2192\t// This field is used if there is no request info present in the context.\n   115\u2192\t// See qualifiedResourceFromContext for details.\n   116\u2192\tDefaultQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192\t// SingularQualifiedResource is the singular name of the resource.\n   119\u2192\tSingularQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192\t// KeyRootFunc returns the root etcd key for this resource; should not\n   122\u2192\t// include trailing \"/\".  This is used for operations that work on the\n   123\u2192\t// entire collection (listing and watching).\n   124\u2192\t//\n   125\u2192\t// KeyRootFunc and KeyFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\n   126\u2192\tKeyRootFunc func(ctx context.Context) string\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192\t// KeyFunc returns the key for a specific object in the collection.\n   129\u2192\t// KeyFunc is called for Create/Update/Get/Delete. Note that 'namespace'\n   130\u2192\t// can be gotten from ctx.\n   131\u2192\t//\n   132\u2192\t// KeyFunc and KeyRootFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\n   133\u2192\tKeyFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string) (string, error)\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192\t// ObjectNameFunc returns the name of an object or an error.\n   136\u2192\tObjectNameFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error)\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192\t// TTLFunc returns the TTL (time to live) that objects should be persisted\n   139\u2192\t// with. The existing parameter is the current TTL or the default for this\n   140\u2192\t// operation. The update parameter indicates whether this is an operation\n   141\u2192\t// against an existing object.\n   142\u2192\t//\n   143\u2192\t// Objects that are persisted with a TTL are evicted once the TTL expires.\n   144\u2192\tTTLFunc func(obj runtime.Object, existing uint64, update bool) (uint64, error)\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192\t// PredicateFunc returns a matcher corresponding to the provided labels\n   147\u2192\t// and fields. The SelectionPredicate returned should return true if the\n   148\u2192\t// object matches the given field and label selectors.\n   149\u2192\tPredicateFunc func(label labels.Selector, field fields.Selector) storage.SelectionPredicate\n   150\u2192"

either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package handlers\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"encoding/hex\"\n    22\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    23\u2192\t\"io\"\n    24\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n \"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192\tapiequality \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    35\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    36\u2192\tmetav1beta1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1beta1\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n    43\u2192\trequestmetrics \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/metrics\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n    46\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning\"\n    49\u2192)\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192const (\n    52\u2192\t// 34 chose as a number close to 30 that is likely to be unique enough to jump out at me the next time I see a timeout.\n    53\u2192\t// Everyone chooses 30.\n    54\u2192\trequestTimeoutUpperBound = 34 * time.Second\n    55\u2192\t// DuplicateOwnerReferencesWarningFormat is the warning that a client receives when a create/update request contains\n    56\u2192\t// duplicate owner reference entries.\n    57\u2192\tDuplicateOwnerReferencesWarningFormat = \".metadata.ownerReferences contains duplicate entries; API server dedups owner references in 1.20+, and may reject such requests as early as 1.24; please fix your requests; duplicate UID(s) observed: %v\"\n    58\u2192\t// DuplicateOwnerReferencesAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat indicates the duplication was observed\n    59\u2192\t// after mutating admission.\n    60\u2192\t// NOTE: For CREATE and UPDATE requests the API server dedups both before and after mutating admission.\n    61\u2192\t// For PATCH request the API server only dedups after mutating admission.\n    62\u2192\tDuplicateOwnerReferencesAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat = \".metadata.ownerReferences contains duplicate entries after mutating admission happens; API server dedups owner references in 1.20+, and may reject such requests as early as 1.24; please fix your requests; duplicate UID(s) observed: %v\"\n    63\u2192\t// shortPrefix is one possible beginning of yaml unmarshal strict errors.\n    64\u2192\tshortPrefix = \"yaml: unmarshal errors:\\n\"\n    65\u2192\t// longPrefix is the other possible beginning of yaml unmarshal strict errors.\n    66\u2192\tlongPrefix = \"error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: unmarshal errors:\\n\"\n    67\u2192)\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192// RequestScope encapsulates common fields across all RESTful handler methods.\n    70\u2192type RequestScope struct {\n    71\u2192\tNamer ScopeNamer\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n    74\u2192\truntime.ParameterCodec\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192\t// StandardSerializers, if set, restricts which serializers can be used when\n    77\u2192\t// we aren't transforming the output (into Table or PartialObjectMetadata).\n    78\u2192\t// Used only by CRDs which do not yet support Protobuf.\n    79\u2192\tStandardSerializers []runtime.SerializerInfo\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\tCreater         runtime.ObjectCreater\n    82\u2192\tConvertor       runtime.ObjectConvertor\n    83\u2192\tDefaulter       runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n    84\u2192\tTyper           runtime.ObjectTyper\n    85\u2192\tUnsafeConvertor runtime.ObjectConvertor\n    86\u2192\tAuthorizer      authorizer.Authorizer\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192\tEquivalentResourceMapper runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192\tTableConvertor rest.TableConvertor\n    91\u2192\tFieldManager   *managedfields.FieldManager\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192\tResource schema.GroupVersionResource\n    94\u2192\tKind     schema.GroupVersionKind\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192\t// AcceptsGroupVersionDelegate is an optional delegate that can be queried about whether a given GVK\n    97\u2192\t// can be accepted in create or update requests. If nil, only scope.Kind is accepted.\n    98\u2192\t// Note that this does not enable multi-version support for reads from a single endpoint.\n    99\u2192\tAcceptsGroupVersionDelegate rest.GroupVersionAcceptor\n   100\u2192"

Input
{
  "description": "Explore request filters and chain",
  "max_turns": 30,
  "prompt": "Explore the Kubernetes apiserver request filter chain and handler flow thoroughly. The codebase is at /workspace, apiserver code at staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/.\n\nI need you to investigate:\n\n1. The filter chain in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/ \u2014 list all filter files and explain what each does\n2. The handler setup in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler*.go \u2014 look for how the handler chain is built (DefaultBuildHandlerChain or similar)\n3. The endpoint/request handlers in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/ \u2014 especially handlers/ subdirectory. How do REST verbs (GET, PUT, DELETE, etc.) get dispatched?\n4. The APIGroupInfo registration flow \u2014 how do API groups get installed? Look for InstallAPIGroup, InstallLegacyAPIGroup, etc.\n5. The content negotiation/serialization in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation/ or similar\n6. How watches are handled \u2014 look for watch handler, watch multiplexer, or cacher\n\nGive me specific file paths, function names, and line numbers for all key components.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "aa37b0f",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report with all the key findings:\n\n## Comprehensive Report: Kubernetes APIServer Request Filter Chain and Handler Flow\n\nBased on my thorough exploration of the Kubernetes apiserver codebase, here is a detailed analysis of all key components:\n\n---\n\n### 1. FILTER CHAIN IN `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/`\n\n#### All Filter Files and Their Purpose:\n\n| Filter File | Location | Key Functions | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **audit_init.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/` | `WithAuditInit()` | Initializes audit context for request processing |\n| **priority-and-fairness.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/priority-and-fairness.go` | `WithPriorityAndFairness()` (line ~250+), `priorityAndFairnessHandler.Handle()` (line 88-150+) | Manages API Priority and Fairness (APF) - classifies requests into flow schemas and priority levels, implements queuing and execution control |\n| **maxinflight.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/maxinflight.go` | `WithMaxInFlightLimit()` (line 126-216), `startWatermarkMaintenance()` (line 92-104) | Limits concurrent in-flight requests separately for mutating vs read-only operations; tracks watermarks |\n| **timeout.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/timeout.go` | `WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests()` (line 38-62), `WithTimeout()` (line 75-149) | Enforces request timeouts for non-long-running requests; identifies long-running requests via callback; manages timeout context |\n| **longrunning.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/longrunning.go` | `BasicLongRunningRequestCheck()` (line 28-41) | Factory function that creates long-running request checker based on verb and subresource lists |\n| **cors.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/cors.go` | `WithCORS()` (line 36-) | Implements CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) with configurable origins, methods, and headers |\n| **hsts.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/hsts.go` | `WithHSTS()` (line 26-40) | Sets HTTP Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) headers for HTTPS enforcement |\n| **content_type.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/content_type.go` | `WithContentType()` (line 22-28) | Sets Content-Type and X-Content-Type-Options headers |\n| **goaway.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/goaway.go` | `WithProbabilisticGoaway()` (line 44-57) | Sends GOAWAY frames probabilistically on HTTP/2 for load balancing and connection rebalancing |\n| **watch_termination.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/watch_termination.go` | `WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown()` (line 28-62) | Terminates watch connections gracefully during server shutdown; tracks wait group |\n| **with_retry_after.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/with_retry_after.go` | `WithRetryAfter()` | Sets Retry-After header on responses during server shutdown |\n| **waitgroup.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/waitgroup.go` | `WithWaitGroup()` | Tracks and coordinates request completion via wait groups |\n| **wrap.go** | `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/wrap.go` | `WithPanicRecovery()` (line 31-59), `WithHTTPLogging()` (line 62-64) | Panic recovery wrapper and HTTP logging filter |\n\n---\n\n### 2. HANDLER CHAIN BUILDING - `DefaultBuildHandlerChain`\n\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go` (lines 1015-1097)\n\n**Type Definition:** `HandlerChainBuilderFn` (line 71) - a function type that wraps handlers with filtering\n\n**Key Function:** `DefaultBuildHandlerChain(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) http.Handler` (line 1015)\n\n**Filter Chain Order (Bottom-to-Top - outermost first):**\n\n```\n1. WithAuditInit                        (line 1095) - Initialize audit context\n2. WithPanicRecovery                    (line 1094) - Panic recovery wrapper\n3. WithRoutine (conditional)            (line 1089) - Execute in separate goroutine\n4. WithLatencyTrackers                  (line 1084) - Track latency\n5. WithHTTPLogging                      (line 1083) - HTTP request logging\n6. WithRetryAfter (conditional)         (line 1081) - Set Retry-After headers on shutdown\n7. WithHSTS                             (line 1079) - HSTS header\n8. WithCacheControl                     (line 1078) - Cache-Control header\n9. WithProbabilisticGoaway (conditional)(line 1076) - HTTP/2 GOAWAY\n10. WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown (line 1073) - Graceful watch termination on shutdown\n11. WithWaitGroup                       (line 1071) - Request completion tracking\n12. WithRequestDeadline                 (line 1069) - Request deadline context\n13. WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests(line 1067) - Request timeout enforcement\n14. WithWarningRecorder                 (line 1063) - Warning header support\n15. WithCORS                            (line 1059) - CORS headers\n16. WithAuthentication                  (line 1056) - Authentication + failedHandler\n17. WithTracing (conditional)           (line 1052) - OpenTelemetry tracing\n18. WithFailedAuthenticationAudit       (failedHandler wrapping)\n19. WithAudit                           (line 1043) - Audit logging\n20. WithImpersonation / WithConstrainedImpersonation (line 1035-1039) - User impersonation\n21. WithMaxInFlightLimit / WithPriorityAndFairness (line 1027-1030) - Request concurrency control\n22. WithAuthorization                   (line 1019) - Authorization check\n23. (innermost) apiHandler              - Director/GoRestful/NonGoRestful handler\n```\n\n**Related Config Struct:** `Config` (line ~185-186) has `BuildHandlerChainFunc` field\n\n**API Handler Wrapper:** `NewAPIServerHandler()` (line 73-100) creates the APIServerHandler with:\n- `FullHandlerChain` - final handler with all filters\n- `GoRestfulContainer` - go-restful WebService container for API routes\n- `NonGoRestfulMux` - fallback mux for non-API routes\n- `Director` - routes between GoRestful and NonGoRestfulMux\n\n---\n\n### 3. HANDLER SETUP - `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go`\n\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go`\n\n**Key Types:**\n\n```go\ntype APIServerHandler struct {\n    FullHandlerChain http.Handler              // Final handler with all filters\n    GoRestfulContainer *restful.Container      // REST API handler\n    NonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux       // Non-REST routes\n    Director http.Handler                      // Router between GoRestful and NonGoRestful\n}\n\ntype director struct {\n    name string\n    goRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n    nonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\n}\n```\n\n**Key Functions:**\n\n- `NewAPIServerHandler()` (line 73) - Creates APIServerHandler with WebService container and mux\n- `director.ServeHTTP()` (line 121-154) - Routes incoming requests to appropriate handler:\n  - Checks if path matches registered WebServices (line 125-149)\n  - If no match, delegates to NonGoRestfulMux (line 151-153)\n- `APIServerHandler.ServeHTTP()` (line 187-189) - Entry point, delegates to FullHandlerChain\n\n---\n\n### 4. ENDPOINT/REQUEST HANDLERS - REST VERB DISPATCHING\n\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/`\n\n#### Handler Functions by REST Verb:\n\n| Verb | Handler File | Function Name | Key Features |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **GET** | `get.go` | `GetResource()` (line 90), `getResourceHandler()` (line 57) | Single resource fetch; calls getter function |\n| **GET** (w/ options) | `get.go` | `GetResourceWithOptions()` (line 118) | GET with custom options object |\n| **LIST** | `get.go` | `ListResource()` (line 171) | List resources; delegates to watch handler if `?watch=true` |\n| **CREATE** | `create.go` | `CreateResource()` (line 245), `CreateNamedResource()` | Create with POST; handles named creation variant |\n| **UPDATE** | `update.go` | `UpdateResource()` (line 51) | Replace resource with PUT |\n| **PATCH** | handlers/ | `PatchResource()` | Partial update with PATCH; supports JSON/Merge/Strategic merge patches |\n| **DELETE** | `delete.go` | `DeleteResource()` (line 58) | Graceful or immediate deletion |\n| **DELETE (collection)** | handlers/ | `DeleteCollection()` | Bulk delete with field selectors |\n| **WATCH** | `watch.go` | `WatchServer` (line 188), `serveWatchHandler()` (line 70) | Long-running watch stream |\n| **CONNECT** | `rest.go` | `ConnectResource()` (line 177) | CONNECT-style tunneling/proxying |\n\n#### Handler Wrappers (from installer.go):\n\n- `restfulListResource()` (line 1294) - Wraps ListResource for go-restful\n- `restfulCreateResource()` (line 1306)\n- `restfulDeleteResource()` (line 1312)\n- `restfulUpdateResource()` (line 1324)\n- `restfulPatchResource()` (line 1330)\n- `restfulGetResource()` (line 1336)\n- etc.\n\n#### Request Scope Type (rest.go, line 69-109):\n\n```go\ntype RequestScope struct {\n    Namer                    ScopeNamer           // Extracts namespace/name from request\n    Serializer               runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n    ParameterCodec           runtime.ParameterCodec\n    StandardSerializers      []runtime.SerializerInfo\n    Creater, Convertor, Defaulter, Typer runtime.Object*\n    Authorizer               authorizer.Authorizer\n    EquivalentResourceMapper runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\n    TableConvertor           rest.TableConvertor\n    FieldManager             *managedfields.FieldManager\n    Resource, Kind           schema.GroupVersion*\n    Subresource              string\n    MetaGroupVersion         schema.GroupVersion\n    HubGroupVersion          schema.GroupVersion\n    MaxRequestBodyBytes      int64\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. ACTION AND ROUTE REGISTRATION\n\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go`\n\n#### Action Struct (line 69-75):\n```go\ntype action struct {\n    Verb string               // MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, etc.\n    Path string               // /api/v1/pods, /namespaces/{namespace}/pods/{name}\n    Params []*restful.Parameter\n    Namer handlers.ScopeNamer\n    AllNamespaces bool\n}\n```\n\n#### Verb Constants (lines 175-189):\n- `request.MethodGet` \u2192 \"get\"\n- `request.MethodList` \u2192 \"list\"\n- `request.MethodPost` \u2192 \"create\"\n- `request.MethodPut` \u2192 \"update\"\n- `request.MethodPatch` \u2192 \"patch\"\n- `request.MethodDelete` \u2192 \"delete\"\n- `request.MethodDeleteCollection` \u2192 \"deletecollection\"\n- `request.MethodWatch` \u2192 \"watch\"\n- `request.MethodWatchList` \u2192 \"watch\"\n- `request.MethodConnect` \u2192 \"connect\"\n\n#### Action Building (lines 500-597):\n\n**For cluster-scoped resources:**\n- List: `/{resource}` with MethodList\n- Get: `/{resource}/{name}` with MethodGet\n- Create: `/{resource}` with MethodPost\n- Update: `/{resource}/{name}` with MethodPut\n- Delete: `/{resource}/{name}` with MethodDelete\n- Watch: `watch/{resource}/{name}` with MethodWatch\n\n**For namespace-scoped resources:**\n- List: `/namespaces/{namespace}/{resource}` with MethodList\n- Get: `/namespaces/{namespace}/{resource}/{name}` with MethodGet\n- Create: `/namespaces/{namespace}/{resource}` with MethodPost\n- Update: `/namespaces/{namespace}/{resource}/{name}` with MethodPut\n- Delete: `/namespaces/{namespace}/{resource}/{name}` with MethodDelete\n- Watch: `watch/namespaces/{namespace}/{resource}/{name}` with MethodWatch\n- Bulk operations: `/{resource}` for AllNamespaces list\n\n#### Route Registration (lines 799-1049):\n\nRoutes are registered in a switch statement on `action.Verb`:\n\n```go\ncase request.MethodGet:        // handler = restfulGetResource()\ncase request.MethodList:       // handler = restfulListResource()\ncase request.MethodPut:        // handler = restfulUpdateResource()\ncase request.MethodPatch:      // handler = restfulPatchResource()\ncase request.MethodPost:       // handler = restfulCreateResource()\ncase request.MethodDelete:     // handler = restfulDeleteResource()\ncase request.MethodDeleteCollection:  // handler = restfulDeleteCollection()\ncase request.MethodWatch:      // handler = restfulListResource(forceWatch=true)\n```\n\nEach route is wrapped with:\n- `metrics.InstrumentRouteFunc()` (line 812, etc.) - Metric recording\n- `utilwarning.AddWarningsHandler()` - Deprecation warnings\n- `ws.GET(path).To(handler)` - go-restful route building\n\n---\n\n### 6. API GROUP INSTALLATION FLOW\n\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go`\n\n#### Main Entry Points:\n\n- `InstallAPIGroups()` (line 876-930) - Install multiple API groups\n- `InstallAPIGroup()` (line 952-954) - Single group wrapper\n- `installAPIResources()` (called by InstallAPIGroups at line 897) - Install resources for a group\n\n#### APIGroupInfo Structure (lines 956-1004):\n\n```go\ntype APIGroupInfo struct {\n    PrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion\n    VersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\n    MetaGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\n    ParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\n    NegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n    Scheme *runtime.Scheme\n    // ...and many more fields\n}\n```\n\n#### Flow:\n\n1. `InstallAPIGroups()` validates group/version info\n2. Gets OpenAPI models via `getOpenAPIModels()`\n3. For each group, calls `installAPIResources(APIGroupPrefix, apiGroupInfo, openAPIModels)`\n4. Sets up discovery handlers via `DiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroup()`\n5. Registers readiness checks via `registerStorageReadinessCheck()`\n\n#### Resource Installation:\n\n- `getAPIGroupVersion()` (line 956) - Extracts storage map for a specific GroupVersion\n- `newAPIGroupVersion()` (line 970) - Creates APIGroupVersion with serializers, convertor, authorizer, etc.\n- Calls installer (from endpoints package) to register routes\n\n---\n\n### 7. INSTALLER AND ENDPOINT REGISTRATION\n\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go`\n\n#### APIInstaller Type (lines 62-66):\n\n```go\ntype APIInstaller struct {\n    group *APIGroupVersion\n    prefix string\n    minRequestTimeout time.Duration\n}\n```\n\n#### Main Function: `registerResourceHandlers()` (line 287+)\n\n1. Splits resource and subresource (line 295)\n2. Determines if resource is namespaced (line 316-334)\n3. Checks storage interface implementations:\n   - `rest.Creater` \u2192 POST support\n   - `rest.Lister` \u2192 GET support for lists\n   - `rest.Getter` \u2192 GET support for single\n   - `rest.Updater` \u2192 PUT support\n   - `rest.Patcher` \u2192 PATCH support\n   - `rest.GracefulDeleter` \u2192 DELETE support\n   - `rest.Watcher` \u2192 WATCH support\n   - `rest.Connecter` \u2192 CONNECT support\n4. Builds action list based on capabilities (lines 500-597)\n5. For each action, creates route with appropriate handler (lines 728-1049)\n\n#### Field Manager Setup (lines 714-726):\n\nCreates `managedfields.FieldManager` for server-side apply and field tracking\n\n---\n\n### 8. CONTENT NEGOTIATION AND SERIALIZATION\n\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation/negotiate.go`\n\n#### Key Functions:\n\n- `NegotiateOutputMediaType()` (line 51-63) - Negotiates response media type from Accept header\n- `NegotiateOutputMediaTypeStream()` (line 66-73) - Negotiates for streaming (watch) responses\n- `NegotiateInputSerializer()` (line 76-79) - Negotiates input serializer from Content-Type\n- `NegotiateInputSerializerForMediaType()` (line 82-98) - Specific media type handling\n- `MediaTypesForSerializer()` (line 34-47) - Lists supported media types\n- `isPrettyPrint()` (line 102-120) - Detects pretty-print requests\n\n#### EndpointRestrictions Interface (lines 124-135):\n\n```go\ntype EndpointRestrictions interface {\n    AllowsMediaTypeTransform(mimeType, mimeSubType string, target *schema.GroupVersionKind) bool\n    AllowsServerVersion(version string) bool\n    AllowsStreamSchema(schema string) bool\n}\n```\n\n#### Supported Media Types:\n\n- JSON: `application/json`\n- YAML: `application/yaml`\n- Protobuf: `application/vnd.kubernetes.protobuf`\n- CBOR: `application/cbor` (with feature gate)\n- Streaming: `{mediaType};stream=watch`\n\n---\n\n### 9. WATCH HANDLER FLOW\n\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/watch.go`\n\n#### Entry Point: `ListResource()` (get.go, line 171)\n\nAt line 250-297, if `opts.Watch` or `forceWatch`:\n1. Checks if watcher is available (line 251-254)\n2. Extracts timeout from query params or uses minRequestTimeout (lines 256-262)\n3. **Creates context with timeout** (line 265)\n4. **Calls `rw.Watch(ctx, &opts)`** (line 267) - calls underlying storage watcher\n5. **Calls `serveWatchHandler()`** (line 272) - wraps watch in HTTP handler\n6. Wraps serving in metrics recording and goroutine execution (lines 281-296)\n\n#### `serveWatchHandler()` (line 70-185):\n\n1. Negotiates output media type (line 77)\n2. Gets stream framer and encoder (lines 81-87)\n3. Creates memory allocator for encoder (lines 131-138)\n4. Creates embedded encoder for object transforms (line 147)\n5. **Builds WatchServer** (lines 163-178) with:\n   - Watcher interface (from storage)\n   - Serializers and framer\n   - Timeout factory\n   - Server shutdown signal channel\n6. Returns either WebSocket handler (line 182) or HTTP handler (line 184)\n\n#### WatchServer Type (lines 188-208):\n\n```go\ntype WatchServer struct {\n    Watching watch.Interface\n    Scope *RequestScope\n    UseTextFraming bool\n    MediaType string\n    Framer runtime.Framer\n    Encoder, EmbeddedEncoder runtime.Encoder\n    MemoryAllocator runtime.MemoryAllocator\n    TimeoutFactory TimeoutFactory\n    ServerShuttingDownCh <-chan struct{}\n    metricsScope string\n}\n```\n\n#### `WatchServer.HandleHTTP()` (lines 235-360+):\n\n1. Sets up HTTP response headers (lines 264-267)\n2. Creates frame writer from framer (line 250)\n3. Sets up timeout (line 260)\n4. Creates watch encoder (line 277)\n5. **Main event loop** (lines 281-318):\n   - Listens on watch result channel\n   - Checks for server shutdown signal (line 283)\n   - Checks for request context cancellation (line 292)\n   - Checks for timeout (line 294)\n   - **Encodes and sends events** to client (line 303)\n   - Flushes periodically (line 311)\n   - Records metrics (line 308)\n\n#### Watch Event Processing:\n\n- Watch events come from `watcher.ResultChan()` (line 278)\n- Each event is encoded via `watchEncoder.Encode(event)` (line 303)\n- Events are framed and flushed to HTTP response\n\n---\n\n### 10. WATCH IN STORAGE/CACHER\n\n**File:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cache_watcher.go`\n\n#### CacheWatcher Type (lines 53-88):\n\n```go\ntype cacheWatcher struct {\n    input chan *watchCacheEvent\n    result chan watch.Event\n    done chan struct{}\n    filter filterWithAttrsFunc\n    stopped bool\n    forget func(bool)\n    versioner storage.Versioner\n    deadline time.Time\n    allowWatchBookmarks bool\n    groupResource schema.GroupResource\n    identifier string\n    drainInputBuffer bool\n    bookmarkAfterResourceVersion uint64\n    stateMutex sync.Mutex\n    state int // Bookmark state machine\n}\n```\n\n#### States (lines 37-49):\n\n- `cacheWatcherWaitingForBookmark` - Waiting for specific resource version\n- `cacheWatcherBookmarkReceived` - Got required RV\n- `cacheWatcherBookmarkSent` - Already sent bookmark\n\n#### Event Buffering:\n\n- Input channel receives watchCacheEvent objects\n- Watcher filters and sends to result channel\n- Result channel is what HTTP handler reads from\n\n---\n\n### 11. REQUEST FLOW SUMMARY\n\nComplete request path:\n\n```\nClient Request\n    \u2193\nHTTP Server (port 6443)\n    \u2193\nFullHandlerChain.ServeHTTP()  [All filters applied]\n    \u2193\nAPIServerHandler.ServeHTTP()\n    \u2193\nDirector.ServeHTTP()  [Routes to GoRestful or NonGoRestfulMux]\n    \u2193\nGoRestfulContainer.Dispatch() [For /api/v1/* and /apis/group/version/*]\n    \u2193\nWebService.Route matching\n    \u2193\nRoute.To(handler) - One of:\n    \u251c\u2500 GetResource()\n    \u251c\u2500 ListResource()  \u2192[if watch=true]\u2192 serveWatchHandler() \u2192 WatchServer.HandleHTTP()\n    \u251c\u2500 CreateResource()\n    \u251c\u2500 UpdateResource()\n    \u251c\u2500 DeleteResource()\n    \u251c\u2500 PatchResource()\n    \u2514\u2500 ConnectResource()\n    \u2193\nStorage Interface (rest.Getter, rest.Lister, rest.Watcher, etc.)\n    \u2193\nUnderlying Storage (etcd3, cacher, etc.)\n```\n\n---\n\n### 12. KEY FILE LOCATIONS - ABSOLUTE PATHS\n\n**Filter Chain Files:**\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/` (all filters)\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go` (DefaultBuildHandlerChain)\n\n**Handler Setup:**\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go` (APIServerHandler)\n\n**Endpoint Handlers:**\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/get.go` (GET, LIST, WATCH)\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/watch.go` (WatchServer)\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/create.go` (CREATE)\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/update.go` (UPDATE)\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/patch.go` (PATCH)\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/delete.go` (DELETE)\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/rest.go` (ConnectResource, RequestScope)\n\n**API Group Installation:**\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go` (InstallAPIGroup*)\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go` (APIInstaller, registerResourceHandlers)\n\n**Content Negotiation:**\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation/negotiate.go` (NegotiateOutputMediaType*)\n\n**Watch/Cacher:**\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cacher.go` (Cacher implementation)\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cache_watcher.go` (CacheWatcher)\n- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/watcher.go` (etcd3 Watcher)\n\nThis comprehensive analysis covers all the key components of the Kubernetes apiserver request handling pipeline, from the initial filter chain through to storage and watch event delivery.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore the Kubernetes apiserver request filter chain and handler flow thoroughly. The codebase is at /workspace, apiserver code at staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/.\n\nI need you to investigate:\n\n1. The filter chain in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/ \u2014 list all filter files and explain what each does\n2. The handler setup in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler*.go \u2014 look for how the handler chain is built (DefaultBuildHandlerChain or similar)\n3. The endpoint/request handlers in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/ \u2014 especially handlers/ subdirectory. How do REST verbs (GET, PUT, DELETE, etc.) get dispatched?\n4. The APIGroupInfo registration flow \u2014 how do API groups get installed? Look for InstallAPIGroup, InstallLegacyAPIGroup, etc.\n5. The content negotiation/serialization in staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation/ or similar\n6. How watches are handled \u2014 look for watch handler, watch multiplexer, or cacher\n\nGive me specific file paths, function names, and line numbers for all key components.",

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file
