required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package server\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"bytes\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    23\u2192\trt \"runtime\"\n    24\u2192\t\"sort\"\n    25\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192\t\"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/mux\"\n    35\u2192)\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192// APIServerHandlers holds the different http.Handlers used by the API server.\n    38\u2192// This includes the full handler chain, the director (which chooses between gorestful and nonGoRestful,\n    39\u2192// the gorestful handler (used for the API) which falls through to the nonGoRestful handler on unregistered paths,\n    40\u2192// and the nonGoRestful handler (which can contain a fallthrough of its own)\n    41\u2192// FullHandlerChain -> Director -> {GoRestfulContainer,NonGoRestfulMux} based on inspection of registered web services\n    42\u2192type APIServerHandler struct {\n    43\u2192\t// FullHandlerChain is the one that is eventually served with.  It should include the full filter\n    44\u2192\t// chain and then call the Director.\n    45\u2192\tFullHandlerChain http.Handler\n    46\u2192\t// The registered APIs.  InstallAPIs uses this.  Other servers probably shouldn't access this directly.\n    47\u2192\tGoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n    48\u2192\t// NonGoRestfulMux is the final HTTP handler in the chain.\n    49\u2192\t// It comes after all filters and the API handling\n    50\u2192\t// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.\n    51\u2192\tNonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\t// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.\n    54\u2192\t// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have\n    55\u2192\t// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)\n    56\u2192\t// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to\n    57\u2192\t// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding\n    58\u2192\t// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints\n    59\u2192\t// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the\n    60\u2192\t//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.\n    61\u2192\t// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to\n    62\u2192\t// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in\n    63\u2192\t// order to handle \"normal\" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think\n    64\u2192\t// we should consider completely removing gorestful.\n    65\u2192\t// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.\n    66\u2192\tDirector http.Handler\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192// HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.\n    70\u2192// It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization\n    71\u2192type HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192func NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {\n    74\u2192\tnonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)\n    75\u2192\tif notFoundHandler != nil {\n    76\u2192\t\tnonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)\n    77\u2192\t}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192\tgorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()\n    80\u2192\tgorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}\n    81\u2192\tgorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {\n    82\u2192\t\tlogStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)\n    83\u2192\t})\n    84\u2192\tgorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {\n    85\u2192\t\tserviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)\n    86\u2192\t})\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192\tdirector := director{\n    89\u2192\t\tname:               name,\n    90\u2192\t\tgoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n    91\u2192\t\tnonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n    92\u2192\t}\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192\treturn &APIServerHandler{\n    95\u2192\t\tFullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),\n    96\u2192\t\tGoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n    97\u2192\t\tNonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n    98\u2192\t\tDirector:           director,\n    99\u2192\t}\n   100\u2192}\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192// ListedPaths returns the paths that should be shown under /\n   103\u2192func (a *APIServerHandler) ListedPaths() []string {\n   104\u2192\tvar handledPaths []string\n   105\u2192\t// Extract the paths handled using restful.WebService\n   106\u2192\tfor _, ws := range a.GoRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n   107\u2192\t\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, ws.RootPath())\n   108\u2192\t}\n   109\u2192\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, a.NonGoRestfulMux.ListedPaths()...)\n   110\u2192\tsort.Strings(handledPaths)\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192\treturn handledPaths\n   113\u2192}\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192type director struct {\n   116\u2192\tname               string\n   117\u2192\tgoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n   118\u2192\tnonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux\n   119\u2192}\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192func (d director) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n   122\u2192\tpath := req.URL.Path\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192\t// check to see if our webservices want to claim this path\n   125\u2192\tfor _, ws := range d.goRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n   126\u2192\t\tswitch {\n   127\u2192\t\tcase ws.RootPath() == \"/apis\":\n   128\u2192\t\t\t// if we are exactly /apis or /apis/, then we need special handling in loop.\n   129\u2192\t\t\t// normally these are passed to the nonGoRestfulMux, but if discovery is enabled, it will go directly.\n   130\u2192\t\t\t// We can't rely on a prefix match since /apis matches everything (see the big comment on Director above)\n   131\u2192\t\t\tif path == \"/apis\" || path == \"/apis/\" {\n   132\u2192\t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n   133\u2192\t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n   134\u2192\t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n   135\u2192\t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n   136\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   137\u2192\t\t\t}\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192\t\tcase strings.HasPrefix(path, ws.RootPath()):\n   140\u2192\t\t\t// ensure an exact match or a path boundary match\n   141\u2192\t\t\tif len(path) == len(ws.RootPath()) || path[len(ws.RootPath())] == '/' {\n   142\u2192\t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n   143\u2192\t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n   144\u2192\t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n   145\u2192\t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n   146\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   147\u2192\t\t\t}\n   148\u2192\t\t}\n   149\u2192\t}\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192\t// if we didn't find a match, then we just skip gorestful altogether\n   152\u2192\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by nonGoRestful\", d.name, req.Method, path)\n   153\u2192\td.nonGoRestfulMux.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n   154\u2192}\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192// TODO: Unify with RecoverPanics?\n   157\u2192func logStackOnRecover(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, panicReason interface{}, w http.ResponseWriter) {\n   158\u2192\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\n   159\u2192\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"recover from panic situation: - %v\\r\\n\", panicReason))\n   160\u2192\tfor i := 2; ; i++ {\n   161\u2192\t\t_, file, line, ok := rt.Caller(i)\n   162\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   163\u2192\t\t\tbreak\n   164\u2192\t\t}\n   165\u2192\t\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    %s:%d\\r\\n\", file, line))\n   166\u2192\t}\n   167\u2192\tklog.Errorln(buffer.String())\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192\theaders := http.Header{}\n   170\u2192\tif ct := w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\"); len(ct) > 0 {\n   171\u2192\t\theaders.Set(\"Accept\", ct)\n   172\u2192\t}\n   173\u2192\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusInternalServerError, \"\", schema.GroupResource{}, \"\", \"\", 0, false), s, schema.GroupVersion{}, w, &http.Request{Header: headers})\n   174\u2192}\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192func serviceErrorHandler(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, resp *restful.Response) {\n   177\u2192\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(\n   178\u2192\t\tapierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(serviceErr.Code, \"\", schema.GroupResource{}, \"\", serviceErr.Message, 0, false),\n   179\u2192\t\ts,\n   180\u2192\t\tschema.GroupVersion{},\n   181\u2192\t\tresp,\n   182\u2192\t\trequest.Request,\n   183\u2192\t)\n   184\u2192}\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192// ServeHTTP makes it an http.Handler\n   187\u2192func (a *APIServerHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n   188\u2192\ta.FullHandlerChain.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n   189\u2192}\n   190\u2192"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    21\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    24\u2192\tapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    25\u2192)\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192// BasicLongRunningRequestCheck returns true if the given request has one of the specified verbs or one of the specified subresources, or is a profiler request.\n    28\u2192func BasicLongRunningRequestCheck(longRunningVerbs, longRunningSubresources sets.String) apirequest.LongRunningRequestCheck {\n    29\u2192\treturn func(r *http.Request, requestInfo *apirequest.RequestInfo) bool {\n    30\u2192\t\tif longRunningVerbs.Has(requestInfo.Verb) {\n    31\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n    32\u2192\t\t}\n    33\u2192\t\tif requestInfo.IsResourceRequest && longRunningSubresources.Has(requestInfo.Subresource) {\n    34\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n    35\u2192\t\t}\n    36\u2192\t\tif !requestInfo.IsResourceRequest && strings.HasPrefix(requestInfo.Path, \"/debug/pprof/\") {\n    37\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n    38\u2192\t\t}\n    39\u2192\t\treturn false\n    40\u2192\t}\n    41\u2192}\n    42\u2192"
70. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/maxinflight.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    21\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    22\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    23\u2192\t\"time\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n    29\u2192\tapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    30\u2192\tfcmetrics \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/metrics\"\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    33\u2192)\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192const (\n    36\u2192\t// Constant for the retry-after interval on rate limiting.\n    37\u2192\tretryAfter = \"1\"\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192\t// How often inflight usage metric should be updated. Because\n    40\u2192\t// the metrics tracks maximal value over period making this\n    41\u2192\t// longer will increase the metric value.\n    42\u2192\tinflightUsageMetricUpdatePeriod = time.Second\n    43\u2192)\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192var (\n    46\u2192\tnonMutatingRequestVerbs = sets.NewString(\"get\", \"list\", \"watch\")\n    47\u2192\twatchVerbs              = sets.NewString(\"watch\")\n    48\u2192)\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192func handleError(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, err error) {\n    51\u2192\terrorMsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Internal Server Error: %#v\", r.RequestURI)\n    52\u2192\thttp.Error(w, errorMsg, http.StatusInternalServerError)\n    53\u2192\tklog.Error(err.Error())\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192// requestWatermark is used to track maximal numbers of requests in a particular phase of handling\n    57\u2192type requestWatermark struct {\n    58\u2192\tphase                                string\n    59\u2192\treadOnlyObserver, mutatingObserver   fcmetrics.RatioedGauge\n    60\u2192\tlock                                 sync.Mutex\n    61\u2192\treadOnlyWatermark, mutatingWatermark int\n    62\u2192}\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192func (w *requestWatermark) recordMutating(mutatingVal int) {\n    65\u2192\tw.mutatingObserver.Set(float64(mutatingVal))\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\tw.lock.Lock()\n    68\u2192\tdefer w.lock.Unlock()\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192\tif w.mutatingWatermark < mutatingVal {\n    71\u2192\t\tw.mutatingWatermark = mutatingVal\n    72\u2192\t}\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192func (w *requestWatermark) recordReadOnly(readOnlyVal int) {\n    76\u2192\tw.readOnlyObserver.Set(float64(readOnlyVal))\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\tw.lock.Lock()\n    79\u2192\tdefer w.lock.Unlock()\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\tif w.readOnlyWatermark < readOnlyVal {\n    82\u2192\t\tw.readOnlyWatermark = readOnlyVal\n    83\u2192\t}\n    84\u2192}\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192// watermark tracks requests being executed (not waiting in a queue)\n    87\u2192var watermark = &requestWatermark{\n    88\u2192\tphase: metrics.ExecutingPhase,\n    89\u2192}\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192// startWatermarkMaintenance starts the goroutines to observe and maintain the specified watermark.\n    92\u2192func startWatermarkMaintenance(watermark *requestWatermark, stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n    93\u2192\t// Periodically update the inflight usage metric.\n    94\u2192\tgo wait.Until(func() {\n    95\u2192\t\twatermark.lock.Lock()\n    96\u2192\t\treadOnlyWatermark := watermark.readOnlyWatermark\n    97\u2192\t\tmutatingWatermark := watermark.mutatingWatermark\n    98\u2192\t\twatermark.readOnlyWatermark = 0\n    99\u2192\t\twatermark.mutatingWatermark = 0\n   100\u2192\t\twatermark.lock.Unlock()\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192\t\tmetrics.UpdateInflightRequestMetrics(watermark.phase, readOnlyWatermark, mutatingWatermark)\n   103\u2192\t}, inflightUsageMetricUpdatePeriod, stopCh)\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192var initMaxInFlightOnce sync.Once\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192func initMaxInFlight(nonMutatingLimit, mutatingLimit int) {\n   109\u2192\tinitMaxInFlightOnce.Do(func() {\n   110\u2192\t\t// Fetching these gauges is delayed until after their underlying metric has been registered\n   111\u2192\t\t// so that this latches onto the efficient implementation.\n   112\u2192\t\twatermark.readOnlyObserver = fcmetrics.GetExecutingReadonlyConcurrency()\n   113\u2192\t\twatermark.mutatingObserver = fcmetrics.GetExecutingMutatingConcurrency()\n   114\u2192\t\tif nonMutatingLimit != 0 {\n   115\u2192\t\t\twatermark.readOnlyObserver.SetDenominator(float64(nonMutatingLimit))\n   116\u2192\t\t\tklog.V(2).InfoS(\"Set denominator for readonly requests\", \"limit\", nonMutatingLimit)\n   117\u2192\t\t}\n   118\u2192\t\tif mutatingLimit != 0 {\n   119\u2192\t\t\twatermark.mutatingObserver.SetDenominator(float64(mutatingLimit))\n   120\u2192\t\t\tklog.V(2).InfoS(\"Set denominator for mutating requests\", \"limit\", mutatingLimit)\n   121\u2192\t\t}\n   122\u2192\t})\n   123\u2192}\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192// WithMaxInFlightLimit limits the number of in-flight requests to buffer size of the passed in channel.\n   126\u2192func WithMaxInFlightLimit(\n   127\u2192\thandler http.Handler,\n   128\u2192\tnonMutatingLimit int,\n   129\u2192\tmutatingLimit int,\n   130\u2192\tlongRunningRequestCheck apirequest.LongRunningRequestCheck,\n   131\u2192) http.Handler {\n   132\u2192\tif nonMutatingLimit == 0 && mutatingLimit == 0 {\n   133\u2192\t\treturn handler\n   134\u2192\t}\n   135\u2192\tvar nonMutatingChan chan bool\n   136\u2192\tvar mutatingChan chan bool\n   137\u2192\tif nonMutatingLimit != 0 {\n   138\u2192\t\tnonMutatingChan = make(chan bool, nonMutatingLimit)\n   139\u2192\t\tklog.V(2).InfoS(\"Initialized nonMutatingChan\", \"len\", nonMutatingLimit)\n   140\u2192\t} else {\n   141\u2192\t\tklog.V(2).InfoS(\"Running with nil nonMutatingChan\")\n   142\u2192\t}\n   143\u2192\tif mutatingLimit != 0 {\n   144\u2192\t\tmutatingChan = make(chan bool, mutatingLimit)\n   145\u2192\t\tklog.V(2).InfoS(\"Initialized mutatingChan\", \"len\", mutatingLimit)\n   146\u2192\t} else {\n   147\u2192\t\tklog.V(2).InfoS(\"Running with nil mutatingChan\")\n   148\u2192\t}\n   149\u2192\tinitMaxInFlight(nonMutatingLimit, mutatingLimit)\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n   152\u2192\t\tctx := r.Context()\n   153\u2192\t\trequestInfo, ok := apirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n   154\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   155\u2192\t\t\thandleError(w, r, fmt.Errorf(\"no RequestInfo found in context, handler chain must be wrong\"))\n   156\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   157\u2192\t\t}\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192\t\t// Skip tracking long running events.\n   160\u2192\t\tif longRunningRequestCheck != nil && longRunningRequestCheck(r, requestInfo) {\n   161\u2192\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n   162\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   163\u2192\t\t}\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192\t\tvar c chan bool\n   166\u2192\t\tisMutatingRequest := !nonMutatingRequestVerbs.Has(requestInfo.Verb)\n   167\u2192\t\tif isMutatingRequest {\n   168\u2192\t\t\tc = mutatingChan\n   169\u2192\t\t} else {\n   170\u2192\t\t\tc = nonMutatingChan\n   171\u2192\t\t}\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192\t\tif c == nil {\n   174\u2192\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n   175\u2192\t\t} else {\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192\t\t\tselect {\n   178\u2192\t\t\tcase c <- true:\n   179\u2192\t\t\t\t// We note the concurrency level both while the\n   180\u2192\t\t\t\t// request is being served and after it is done being\n   181\u2192\t\t\t\t// served, because both states contribute to the\n   182\u2192\t\t\t\t// sampled stats on concurrency.\n   183\u2192\t\t\t\tif isMutatingRequest {\n   184\u2192\t\t\t\t\twatermark.recordMutating(len(c))\n   185\u2192\t\t\t\t} else {\n   186\u2192\t\t\t\t\twatermark.recordReadOnly(len(c))\n   187\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   188\u2192\t\t\t\tdefer func() {\n   189\u2192\t\t\t\t\t<-c\n   190\u2192\t\t\t\t\tif isMutatingRequest {\n   191\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\twatermark.recordMutating(len(c))\n   192\u2192\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n   193\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\twatermark.recordReadOnly(len(c))\n   194\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n   195\u2192\t\t\t\t}()\n   196\u2192\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192\t\t\tdefault:\n   199\u2192\t\t\t\t// at this point we're about to return a 429, BUT not all actors should be rate limited.  A system:master is so powerful\n   200\u2192\t\t\t\t// that they should always get an answer.  It's a super-admin or a loopback connection.\n   201\u2192\t\t\t\tif currUser, ok := apirequest.UserFrom(ctx); ok {\n   202\u2192\t\t\t\t\tfor _, group := range currUser.GetGroups() {\n   203\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tif group == user.SystemPrivilegedGroup {\n   204\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n   205\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n   206\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n   207\u2192\t\t\t\t\t}\n   208\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   209\u2192\t\t\t\t// We need to split this data between buckets used for throttling.\n   210\u2192\t\t\t\tmetrics.RecordDroppedRequest(r, requestInfo, metrics.APIServerComponent, isMutatingRequest)\n   211\u2192\t\t\t\tmetrics.RecordRequestTermination(r, requestInfo, metrics.APIServerComponent, http.StatusTooManyRequests)\n   212\u2192\t\t\t\ttooManyRequests(r, w, retryAfter)\n   213\u2192\t\t\t}\n   214\u2192\t\t}\n   215\u2192\t})\n   216\u2192}\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192// StartMaxInFlightWatermarkMaintenance starts the goroutines to observe and maintain watermarks for max-in-flight\n   219\u2192// requests.\n   220\u2192func StartMaxInFlightWatermarkMaintenance(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n   221\u2192\tstartWatermarkMaintenance(watermark, stopCh)\n   222\u2192}\n   223\u2192"
71. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/timeout.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"bufio\"\n    21\u2192\t\"encoding/json\"\n    22\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    23\u2192\t\"net\"\n    24\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    25\u2192\t\"runtime\"\n    26\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    27\u2192\t\"time\"\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    30\u2192\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n    32\u2192\tapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/responsewriter\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/httplog\"\n    35\u2192)\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192// WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests times out non-long-running requests after the time given by timeout.\n    38\u2192func WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests(handler http.Handler, longRunning apirequest.LongRunningRequestCheck) http.Handler {\n    39\u2192\tif longRunning == nil {\n    40\u2192\t\treturn handler\n    41\u2192\t}\n    42\u2192\ttimeoutFunc := func(req *http.Request) (*http.Request, bool, func(), *apierrors.StatusError) {\n    43\u2192\t\t// TODO unify this with apiserver.MaxInFlightLimit\n    44\u2192\t\tctx := req.Context()\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\t\trequestInfo, ok := apirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n    47\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n    48\u2192\t\t\t// if this happens, the handler chain isn't setup correctly because there is no request info\n    49\u2192\t\t\treturn req, false, func() {}, apierrors.NewInternalError(fmt.Errorf(\"no request info found for request during timeout\"))\n    50\u2192\t\t}\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192\t\tif longRunning(req, requestInfo) {\n    53\u2192\t\t\treturn req, true, nil, nil\n    54\u2192\t\t}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192\t\tpostTimeoutFn := func() {\n    57\u2192\t\t\tmetrics.RecordRequestTermination(req, requestInfo, metrics.APIServerComponent, http.StatusGatewayTimeout)\n    58\u2192\t\t}\n    59\u2192\t\treturn req, false, postTimeoutFn, apierrors.NewTimeoutError(\"request did not complete within the allotted timeout\", 0)\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192\treturn WithTimeout(handler, timeoutFunc)\n    62\u2192}\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192type timeoutFunc = func(*http.Request) (req *http.Request, longRunning bool, postTimeoutFunc func(), err *apierrors.StatusError)\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192// WithTimeout returns an http.Handler that runs h with a timeout\n    67\u2192// determined by timeoutFunc. The new http.Handler calls h.ServeHTTP to handle\n    68\u2192// each request, but if a call runs for longer than its time limit, the\n    69\u2192// handler responds with a 504 Gateway Timeout error and the message\n    70\u2192// provided. (If msg is empty, a suitable default message will be sent.) After\n    71\u2192// the handler times out, writes by h to its http.ResponseWriter will return\n    72\u2192// http.ErrHandlerTimeout. If timeoutFunc returns a nil timeout channel, no\n    73\u2192// timeout will be enforced. recordFn is a function that will be invoked whenever\n    74\u2192// a timeout happens.\n    75\u2192func WithTimeout(h http.Handler, timeoutFunc timeoutFunc) http.Handler {\n    76\u2192\treturn &timeoutHandler{h, timeoutFunc}\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192type timeoutHandler struct {\n    80\u2192\thandler http.Handler\n    81\u2192\ttimeout timeoutFunc\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192func (t *timeoutHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    85\u2192\tr, longRunning, postTimeoutFn, err := t.timeout(r)\n    86\u2192\tif longRunning {\n    87\u2192\t\tt.handler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n    88\u2192\t\treturn\n    89\u2192\t}\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192\ttimeoutCh := r.Context().Done()\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192\t// resultCh is used as both errCh and stopCh\n    94\u2192\tresultCh := make(chan interface{})\n    95\u2192\tvar tw timeoutWriter\n    96\u2192\ttw, w = newTimeoutWriter(w)\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192\t// Make a copy of request and work on it in new goroutine\n    99\u2192\t// to avoid race condition when accessing/modifying request (e.g. headers)\n   100\u2192\trCopy := r.Clone(r.Context())\n   101\u2192\tgo func() {\n   102\u2192\t\tdefer func() {\n   103\u2192\t\t\terr := recover()\n   104\u2192\t\t\t// do not wrap the sentinel ErrAbortHandler panic value\n   105\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil && err != http.ErrAbortHandler {\n   106\u2192\t\t\t\t// Same as stdlib http server code. Manually allocate stack\n   107\u2192\t\t\t\t// trace buffer size to prevent excessively large logs\n   108\u2192\t\t\t\tconst size = 64 << 10\n   109\u2192\t\t\t\tbuf := make([]byte, size)\n   110\u2192\t\t\t\tbuf = buf[:runtime.Stack(buf, false)]\n   111\u2192\t\t\t\terr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\\n%s\", err, buf)\n   112\u2192\t\t\t}\n   113\u2192\t\t\tresultCh <- err\n   114\u2192\t\t}()\n   115\u2192\t\tt.handler.ServeHTTP(w, rCopy)\n   116\u2192\t}()\n   117\u2192\tselect {\n   118\u2192\tcase err := <-resultCh:\n   119\u2192\t\t// panic if error occurs; stop otherwise\n   120\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   121\u2192\t\t\tpanic(err)\n   122\u2192\t\t}\n   123\u2192\t\treturn\n   124\u2192\tcase <-timeoutCh:\n   125\u2192\t\tdefer func() {\n   126\u2192\t\t\t// resultCh needs to have a reader, since the function doing\n   127\u2192\t\t\t// the work needs to send to it. This is defer'd to ensure it runs\n   128\u2192\t\t\t// ever if the post timeout work itself panics.\n   129\u2192\t\t\tgo func() {\n   130\u2192\t\t\t\ttimedOutAt := time.Now()\n   131\u2192\t\t\t\tres := <-resultCh\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192\t\t\t\tstatus := metrics.PostTimeoutHandlerOK\n   134\u2192\t\t\t\tif res != nil {\n   135\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// a non nil res indicates that there was a panic.\n   136\u2192\t\t\t\t\tstatus = metrics.PostTimeoutHandlerPanic\n   137\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192\t\t\t\tmetrics.RecordRequestPostTimeout(metrics.PostTimeoutSourceTimeoutHandler, status)\n   140\u2192\t\t\t\tutilruntime.HandleErrorWithContext(r.Context(), nil, \"Post-timeout activity\", \"timeElapsed\", time.Since(timedOutAt), \"method\", r.Method, \"path\", r.URL.Path, \"result\", res)\n   141\u2192\t\t\t}()\n   142\u2192\t\t}()\n   143\u2192\t\thttplog.SetStacktracePredicate(r.Context(), func(status int) bool {\n   144\u2192\t\t\treturn false\n   145\u2192\t\t})\n   146\u2192\t\tdefer postTimeoutFn()\n   147\u2192\t\ttw.timeout(err)\n   148\u2192\t}\n   149\u2192}\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192type timeoutWriter interface {\n   152\u2192\thttp.ResponseWriter\n   153\u2192\ttimeout(*apierrors.StatusError)\n   154\u2192}\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192func newTimeoutWriter(w http.ResponseWriter) (timeoutWriter, http.ResponseWriter) {\n   157\u2192\tbase := &baseTimeoutWriter{w: w, handlerHeaders: w.Header().Clone()}\n   158\u2192\twrapped := responsewriter.WrapForHTTP1Or2(base)\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192\treturn base, wrapped\n   161\u2192}\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192var _ http.ResponseWriter = &baseTimeoutWriter{}\n   164\u2192var _ responsewriter.UserProvidedDecorator = &baseTimeoutWriter{}\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192type baseTimeoutWriter struct {\n   167\u2192\tw http.ResponseWriter\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192\t// headers written by the normal handler\n   170\u2192\thandlerHeaders http.Header\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192\tmu sync.Mutex\n   173\u2192\t// if the timeout handler has timeout\n   174\u2192\ttimedOut bool\n   175\u2192\t// if this timeout writer has wrote header\n   176\u2192\twroteHeader bool\n   177\u2192\t// if this timeout writer has been hijacked\n   178\u2192\thijacked bool\n   179\u2192}\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192func (tw *baseTimeoutWriter) Unwrap() http.ResponseWriter {\n   182\u2192\treturn tw.w\n   183\u2192}\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192func (tw *baseTimeoutWriter) Header() http.Header {\n   186\u2192\ttw.mu.Lock()\n   187\u2192\tdefer tw.mu.Unlock()\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192\tif tw.timedOut {\n   190\u2192\t\treturn http.Header{}\n   191\u2192\t}\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192\treturn tw.handlerHeaders\n   194\u2192}\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192func (tw *baseTimeoutWriter) Write(p []byte) (int, error) {\n   197\u2192\ttw.mu.Lock()\n   198\u2192\tdefer tw.mu.Unlock()\n   199\u2192\n   200\u2192\tif tw.timedOut {\n   201\u2192\t\treturn 0, http.ErrHandlerTimeout\n   202\u2192\t}\n   203\u2192\tif tw.hijacked {\n   204\u2192\t\treturn 0, http.ErrHijacked\n   205\u2192\t}\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192\tif !tw.wroteHeader {\n   208\u2192\t\tcopyHeaders(tw.w.Header(), tw.handlerHeaders)\n   209\u2192\t\ttw.wroteHeader = true\n   210\u2192\t}\n   211\u2192\treturn tw.w.Write(p)\n   212\u2192}\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192func (tw *baseTimeoutWriter) Flush() {\n   215\u2192\ttw.mu.Lock()\n   216\u2192\tdefer tw.mu.Unlock()\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192\tif tw.timedOut {\n   219\u2192\t\treturn\n   220\u2192\t}\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192\t// the outer ResponseWriter object returned by WrapForHTTP1Or2 implements\n   223\u2192\t// http.Flusher if the inner object (tw.w) implements http.Flusher.\n   224\u2192\ttw.w.(http.Flusher).Flush()\n   225\u2192}\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192func (tw *baseTimeoutWriter) WriteHeader(code int) {\n   228\u2192\ttw.mu.Lock()\n   229\u2192\tdefer tw.mu.Unlock()\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192\tif tw.timedOut || tw.wroteHeader || tw.hijacked {\n   232\u2192\t\treturn\n   233\u2192\t}\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192\tcopyHeaders(tw.w.Header(), tw.handlerHeaders)\n   236\u2192\ttw.wroteHeader = true\n   237\u2192\ttw.w.WriteHeader(code)\n   238\u2192}\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192func copyHeaders(dst, src http.Header) {\n   241\u2192\tfor k, v := range src {\n   242\u2192\t\tdst[k] = v\n   243\u2192\t}\n   244\u2192}\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192func (tw *baseTimeoutWriter) timeout(err *apierrors.StatusError) {\n   247\u2192\ttw.mu.Lock()\n   248\u2192\tdefer tw.mu.Unlock()\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192\ttw.timedOut = true\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192\t// The timeout writer has not been used by the inner handler.\n   253\u2192\t// We can safely timeout the HTTP request by sending by a timeout\n   254\u2192\t// handler\n   255\u2192\tif !tw.wroteHeader && !tw.hijacked {\n   256\u2192\t\ttw.w.WriteHeader(http.StatusGatewayTimeout)\n   257\u2192\t\tenc := json.NewEncoder(tw.w)\n   258\u2192\t\tenc.Encode(&err.ErrStatus)\n   259\u2192\t} else {\n   260\u2192\t\t// The timeout writer has been used by the inner handler. There is\n   261\u2192\t\t// no way to timeout the HTTP request at the point. We have to shutdown\n   262\u2192\t\t// the connection for HTTP1 or reset stream for HTTP2.\n   263\u2192\t\t//\n   264\u2192\t\t// Note from the golang's docs:\n   265\u2192\t\t// If ServeHTTP panics, the server (the caller of ServeHTTP) assumes\n   266\u2192\t\t// that the effect of the panic was isolated to the active request.\n   267\u2192\t\t// It recovers the panic, logs a stack trace to the server error log,\n   268\u2192\t\t// and either closes the network connection or sends an HTTP/2\n   269\u2192\t\t// RST_STREAM, depending on the HTTP protocol. To abort a handler so\n   270\u2192\t\t// the client sees an interrupted response but the server doesn't log\n   271\u2192\t\t// an error, panic with the value ErrAbortHandler.\n   272\u2192\t\t//\n   273\u2192\t\t// We are throwing http.ErrAbortHandler deliberately so that a client is notified and to suppress a not helpful stacktrace in the logs\n   274\u2192\t\tpanic(http.ErrAbortHandler)\n   275\u2192\t}\n   276\u2192}\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192func (tw *baseTimeoutWriter) CloseNotify() <-chan bool {\n   279\u2192\ttw.mu.Lock()\n   280\u2192\tdefer tw.mu.Unlock()\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192\tif tw.timedOut {\n   283\u2192\t\tdone := make(chan bool)\n   284\u2192\t\tclose(done)\n   285\u2192\t\treturn done\n   286\u2192\t}\n   287\u2192\n   288\u2192\t// the outer ResponseWriter object returned by WrapForHTTP1Or2 implements\n   289\u2192\t// http.CloseNotifier if the inner object (tw.w) implements http.CloseNotifier.\n   290\u2192\treturn tw.w.(http.CloseNotifier).CloseNotify()\n   291\u2192}\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192func (tw *baseTimeoutWriter) Hijack() (net.Conn, *bufio.ReadWriter, error) {\n   294\u2192\ttw.mu.Lock()\n   295\u2192\tdefer tw.mu.Unlock()\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192\tif tw.timedOut {\n   298\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, http.ErrHandlerTimeout\n   299\u2192\t}\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192\t// the outer ResponseWriter object returned by WrapForHTTP1Or2 implements\n   302\u2192\t// http.Hijacker if the inner object (tw.w) implements http.Hijacker.\n   303\u2192\tconn, rw, err := tw.w.(http.Hijacker).Hijack()\n   304\u2192\tif err == nil {\n   305\u2192\t\ttw.hijacked = true\n   306\u2192\t}\n   307\u2192\treturn conn, rw, err\n   308\u2192}\n   309\u2192"

except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    23\u2192\t\"runtime\"\n    24\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n    25\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    26\u2192\t\"sync/atomic\"\n    27\u2192\t\"time\"\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\tflowcontrol \"k8s.io/api/flowcontrol/v1\"\n    30\u2192\tapitypes \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    31\u2192\tepmetrics \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n    32\u2192\tapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/httplog\"\n    34\u2192\tutilflowcontrol \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol\"\n    35\u2192\tfcmetrics \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/metrics\"\n    36\u2192\tflowcontrolrequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/request\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    38\u2192\tutilsclock \"k8s.io/utils/clock\"\n    39\u2192)\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192// PriorityAndFairnessClassification identifies the results of\n    42\u2192// classification for API Priority and Fairness\n    43\u2192type PriorityAndFairnessClassification struct {\n    44\u2192\tFlowSchemaName    string\n    45\u2192\tFlowSchemaUID     apitypes.UID\n    46\u2192\tPriorityLevelName string\n    47\u2192\tPriorityLevelUID  apitypes.UID\n    48\u2192}\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192// waitingMark tracks requests waiting rather than being executed\n    51\u2192var waitingMark = &requestWatermark{\n    52\u2192\tphase: epmetrics.WaitingPhase,\n    53\u2192}\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192var atomicMutatingExecuting, atomicReadOnlyExecuting atomic.Int32\n    56\u2192var atomicMutatingWaiting, atomicReadOnlyWaiting atomic.Int32\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192// newInitializationSignal is defined for testing purposes.\n    59\u2192var newInitializationSignal = utilflowcontrol.NewInitializationSignal\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192func truncateLogField(s string) string {\n    62\u2192\tconst maxFieldLogLength = 64\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192\tif len(s) > maxFieldLogLength {\n    65\u2192\t\ts = s[0:maxFieldLogLength]\n    66\u2192\t}\n    67\u2192\treturn s\n    68\u2192}\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192var initAPFOnce sync.Once\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192type priorityAndFairnessHandler struct {\n    73\u2192\thandler                 http.Handler\n    74\u2192\tlongRunningRequestCheck apirequest.LongRunningRequestCheck\n    75\u2192\tfcIfc                   utilflowcontrol.Interface\n    76\u2192\tworkEstimator           flowcontrolrequest.WorkEstimatorFunc\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\t// droppedRequests tracks the history of dropped requests for\n    79\u2192\t// the purpose of computing RetryAfter header to avoid system\n    80\u2192\t// overload.\n    81\u2192\tdroppedRequests utilflowcontrol.DroppedRequestsTracker\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192\t// newReqWaitCtxFn creates a derived context with a deadline\n    84\u2192\t// of how long a given request can wait in its queue.\n    85\u2192\tnewReqWaitCtxFn func(context.Context) (context.Context, context.CancelFunc)\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192func (h *priorityAndFairnessHandler) Handle(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    89\u2192\tctx := r.Context()\n    90\u2192\trequestInfo, ok := apirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n    91\u2192\tif !ok {\n    92\u2192\t\thandleError(w, r, fmt.Errorf(\"no RequestInfo found in context\"))\n    93\u2192\t\treturn\n    94\u2192\t}\n    95\u2192\tuser, ok := apirequest.UserFrom(ctx)\n    96\u2192\tif !ok {\n    97\u2192\t\thandleError(w, r, fmt.Errorf(\"no User found in context\"))\n    98\u2192\t\treturn\n    99\u2192\t}\n   100\u2192"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package handlers\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"encoding/hex\"\n    22\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    23\u2192\t\"io\"\n    24\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    25\u2192\t\"net/url\"\n    26\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    27\u2192\t\"time\"\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\tgrpccodes \"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n    30\u2192\tgrpcstatus \"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192\tapiequality \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    35\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    36\u2192\tmetav1beta1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1beta1\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n    43\u2192\trequestmetrics \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/metrics\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n    46\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning\"\n    49\u2192)\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192const (\n    52\u2192\t// 34 chose as a number close to 30 that is likely to be unique enough to jump out at me the next time I see a timeout.\n    53\u2192\t// Everyone chooses 30.\n    54\u2192\trequestTimeoutUpperBound = 34 * time.Second\n    55\u2192\t// DuplicateOwnerReferencesWarningFormat is the warning that a client receives when a create/update request contains\n    56\u2192\t// duplicate owner reference entries.\n    57\u2192\tDuplicateOwnerReferencesWarningFormat = \".metadata.ownerReferences contains duplicate entries; API server dedups owner references in 1.20+, and may reject such requests as early as 1.24; please fix your requests; duplicate UID(s) observed: %v\"\n    58\u2192\t// DuplicateOwnerReferencesAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat indicates the duplication was observed\n    59\u2192\t// after mutating admission.\n    60\u2192\t// NOTE: For CREATE and UPDATE requests the API server dedups both before and after mutating admission.\n    61\u2192\t// For PATCH request the API server only dedups after mutating admission.\n    62\u2192\tDuplicateOwnerReferencesAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat = \".metadata.ownerReferences contains duplicate entries after mutating admission happens; API server dedups owner references in 1.20+, and may reject such requests as early as 1.24; please fix your requests; duplicate UID(s) observed: %v\"\n    63\u2192\t// shortPrefix is one possible beginning of yaml unmarshal strict errors.\n    64\u2192\tshortPrefix = \"yaml: unmarshal errors:\\n\"\n    65\u2192\t// longPrefix is the other possible beginning of yaml unmarshal strict errors.\n    66\u2192\tlongPrefix = \"error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: unmarshal errors:\\n\"\n    67\u2192)\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192// RequestScope encapsulates common fields across all RESTful handler methods.\n    70\u2192type RequestScope struct {\n    71\u2192\tNamer ScopeNamer\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n    74\u2192\truntime.ParameterCodec\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192\t// StandardSerializers, if set, restricts which serializers can be used when\n    77\u2192\t// we aren't transforming the output (into Table or PartialObjectMetadata).\n    78\u2192\t// Used only by CRDs which do not yet support Protobuf.\n    79\u2192\tStandardSerializers []runtime.SerializerInfo\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\tCreater         runtime.ObjectCreater\n    82\u2192\tConvertor       runtime.ObjectConvertor\n    83\u2192\tDefaulter       runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n    84\u2192\tTyper           runtime.ObjectTyper\n    85\u2192\tUnsafeConvertor runtime.ObjectConvertor\n    86\u2192\tAuthorizer      authorizer.Authorizer\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192\tEquivalentResourceMapper runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192\tTableConvertor rest.TableConvertor\n    91\u2192\tFieldManager   *managedfields.FieldManager\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192\tResource schema.GroupVersionResource\n    94\u2192\tKind     schema.GroupVersionKind\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192\t// AcceptsGroupVersionDelegate is an optional delegate that can be queried about whether a given GVK\n    97\u2192\t// can be accepted in create or update requests. If nil, only scope.Kind is accepted.\n    98\u2192\t// Note that this does not enable multi-version support for reads from a single endpoint.\n    99\u2192\tAcceptsGroupVersionDelegate rest.GroupVersionAcceptor\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192\tSubresource string\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192\tMetaGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192\t// HubGroupVersion indicates what version objects read from etcd or incoming requests should be converted to for in-memory handling.\n   106\u2192\tHubGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192\tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n   109\u2192}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192func (scope *RequestScope) err(err error, w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n   112\u2192\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(err, scope.Serializer, scope.Kind.GroupVersion(), w, req)\n   113\u2192}\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192// AcceptsGroupVersion returns true if the specified GroupVersion is allowed\n   116\u2192// in create and update requests.\n   117\u2192func (scope *RequestScope) AcceptsGroupVersion(gv schema.GroupVersion) bool {\n   118\u2192\t// If there's a custom acceptor, delegate to it. This is extremely rare.\n   119\u2192\tif scope.AcceptsGroupVersionDelegate != nil {\n   120\u2192\t\treturn scope.AcceptsGroupVersionDelegate.AcceptsGroupVersion(gv)\n   121\u2192\t}\n   122\u2192\t// Fall back to only allowing the singular Kind. This is the typical behavior.\n   123\u2192\treturn gv == scope.Kind.GroupVersion()\n   124\u2192}\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192func (scope *RequestScope) AllowsMediaTypeTransform(mimeType, mimeSubType string, gvk *schema.GroupVersionKind) bool {\n   127\u2192\t// some handlers like CRDs can't serve all the mime types that PartialObjectMetadata or Table can - if\n   128\u2192\t// gvk is nil (no conversion) allow StandardSerializers to further restrict the set of mime types.\n   129\u2192\tif gvk == nil {\n   130\u2192\t\tif len(scope.StandardSerializers) == 0 {\n   131\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n   132\u2192\t\t}\n   133\u2192\t\tfor _, info := range scope.StandardSerializers {\n   134\u2192\t\t\tif info.MediaTypeType == mimeType && info.MediaTypeSubType == mimeSubType {\n   135\u2192\t\t\t\treturn true\n   136\u2192\t\t\t}\n   137\u2192\t\t}\n   138\u2192\t\treturn false\n   139\u2192\t}\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192\t// TODO: this is temporary, replace with an abstraction calculated at endpoint installation time\n   142\u2192\tif gvk.GroupVersion() == metav1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion || gvk.GroupVersion() == metav1.SchemeGroupVersion {\n   143\u2192\t\tswitch gvk.Kind {\n   144\u2192\t\tcase \"Table\":\n   145\u2192\t\t\treturn scope.TableConvertor != nil &&\n   146\u2192\t\t\t\tmimeType == \"application\" &&\n   147\u2192\t\t\t\t(mimeSubType == \"json\" || mimeSubType == \"yaml\")\n   148\u2192\t\tcase \"PartialObjectMetadata\", \"PartialObjectMetadataList\":\n   149\u2192\t\t\t// TODO: should delineate between lists and non-list endpoints\n   150\u2192\t\t\treturn true"

either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package handlers\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"math/rand\"\n    23\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    24\u2192\t\"net/url\"\n    25\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    26\u2192\t\"time\"\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    32\u2192\tmetainternalversion \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\"\n    33\u2192\tmetainternalversionscheme \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion/scheme\"\n    34\u2192\tmetainternalversionvalidation \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion/validation\"\n    35\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    43\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routine\"\n    46\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    49\u2192)\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192// getterFunc performs a get request with the given context and object name. The request\n    52\u2192// may be used to deserialize an options object to pass to the getter.\n    53\u2192type getterFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string, req *http.Request) (runtime.Object, error)\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192// getResourceHandler is an HTTP handler function for get requests. It delegates to the\n    56\u2192// passed-in getterFunc to perform the actual get.\n    57\u2192func getResourceHandler(scope *RequestScope, getter getterFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n    58\u2192\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n    59\u2192\t\tctx := req.Context()\n    60\u2192\t\tctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, \"Get\", traceFields(req)...)\n    61\u2192\t\treq = req.WithContext(ctx)\n    62\u2192\t\tdefer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192\t\tnamespace, name, err := scope.Namer.Name(req)\n    65\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    66\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n    67\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    68\u2192\t\t}\n    69\u2192\t\tctx = request.WithNamespace(ctx, namespace)\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192\t\toutputMediaType, _, err := negotiation.NegotiateOutputMediaType(req, scope.Serializer, scope)\n    72\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    73\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n    74\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    75\u2192\t\t}\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192\t\tresult, err := getter(ctx, name, req)\n    78\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    79\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n    80\u2192\t\t\treturn"

required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package handlers\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"io\"\n    23\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    24\u2192\t\"sync/atomic\"\n    25\u2192\t\"time\"\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192\t\"golang.org/x/net/websocket\"\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    30\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/httpstream/wsstream\"\n    33\u2192\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n    38\u2192\tapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/httplog\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\"\n    42\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    43\u2192\tcompbasemetrics \"k8s.io/component-base/metrics\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    45\u2192)\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192// timeoutFactory abstracts watch timeout logic for testing\n    48\u2192type TimeoutFactory interface {\n    49\u2192\tTimeoutCh() (<-chan time.Time, func() bool)\n    50\u2192}\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192// realTimeoutFactory implements timeoutFactory\n    53\u2192type realTimeoutFactory struct {\n    54\u2192\ttimeout time.Duration\n    55\u2192}\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192// TimeoutCh returns a channel which will receive something when the watch times out,\n    58\u2192// and a cleanup function to call when this happens.\n    59\u2192func (w *realTimeoutFactory) TimeoutCh() (<-chan time.Time, func() bool) {\n    60\u2192\tif w.timeout == 0 {\n    61\u2192\t\t// nothing will ever be sent down this channel\n    62\u2192\t\treturn nil, func() bool { return false }\n    63\u2192\t}\n    64\u2192\tt := time.NewTimer(w.timeout)\n    65\u2192\treturn t.C, t.Stop\n    66\u2192}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// serveWatchHandler returns a handle to serve a watch response.\n    69\u2192// TODO: the functionality in this method and in WatchServer.Serve is not cleanly decoupled.\n    70\u2192func serveWatchHandler(watcher watch.Interface, scope *RequestScope, mediaTypeOptions negotiation.MediaTypeOptions, req *http.Request, w http.ResponseWriter, timeout time.Duration, metricsScope string) (http.Handler, error) {\n    71\u2192\toptions, err := optionsForTransform(mediaTypeOptions, req)\n    72\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    73\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n    74\u2192\t}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192\t// negotiate for the stream serializer from the scope's serializer\n    77\u2192\tserializer, err := negotiation.NegotiateOutputMediaTypeStream(req, scope.Serializer, scope)\n    78\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    79\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n    80\u2192\t}\n    81\u2192\tframer := serializer.StreamSerializer.Framer\n    82\u2192\tvar encoder runtime.Encoder\n    83\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\n    84\u2192\t\tencoder = scope.Serializer.EncoderForVersion(runtime.UseNondeterministicEncoding(serializer.StreamSerializer.Serializer), scope.Kind.GroupVersion())\n    85\u2192\t} else {\n    86\u2192\t\tencoder = scope.Serializer.EncoderForVersion(serializer.StreamSerializer.Serializer, scope.Kind.GroupVersion())\n    87\u2192\t}\n    88\u2192\tuseTextFraming := serializer.EncodesAsText\n    89\u2192\tif framer == nil {\n    90\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"no framer defined for %q available for embedded encoding\", serializer.MediaType)\n    91\u2192\t}\n    92\u2192\t// TODO: next step, get back mediaTypeOptions from negotiate and return the exact value here\n    93\u2192\tmediaType := serializer.MediaType\n    94\u2192\tswitch mediaType {\n    95\u2192\tcase runtime.ContentTypeJSON:\n    96\u2192\t\t// as-is\n    97\u2192\tcase runtime.ContentTypeCBOR:\n    98\u2192\t\t// If a client indicated it accepts application/cbor (exactly one data item) on a\n    99\u2192\t\t// watch request, set the conformant application/cbor-seq media type the watch\n   100\u2192\t\t// response. RFC 9110 allows an origin server to deviate from the indicated"

required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package endpoints\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    21\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    22\u2192\t\"reflect\"\n    23\u2192\t\"sort\"\n    24\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    25\u2192\t\"time\"\n    26\u2192\t\"unicode\"\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\trestful \"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n    29\u2192\t\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\"\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\t\"golang.org/x/text/cases\"\n    32\u2192\t\"golang.org/x/text/language\"\n    33\u2192\tapidiscoveryv2 \"k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2\"\n    34\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/deprecation\"\n    43\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation\"\n    46\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    48\u2192\tutilwarning \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/warning\"\n    49\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    50\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n    51\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n    52\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    53\u2192\tversioninfo \"k8s.io/component-base/version\"\n    54\u2192)\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192const (\n    57\u2192\tRouteMetaGVK              = \"x-kubernetes-group-version-kind\"\n    58\u2192\tRouteMetaSelectableFields = \"x-kubernetes-selectable-fields\"\n    59\u2192\tRouteMetaAction           = \"x-kubernetes-action\"\n    60\u2192)\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192type APIInstaller struct {\n    63\u2192\tgroup             *APIGroupVersion\n    64\u2192\tprefix            string // Path prefix where API resources are to be registered.\n    65\u2192\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n    66\u2192}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// Struct capturing information about an action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\n    69\u2192type action struct {\n    70\u2192\tVerb          string               // Verb identifying the action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\n    71\u2192\tPath          string               // The path of the action\n    72\u2192\tParams        []*restful.Parameter // List of parameters associated with the action.\n    73\u2192\tNamer         handlers.ScopeNamer\n    74\u2192\tAllNamespaces bool // true iff the action is namespaced but works on aggregate result for all namespaces\n    75\u2192}\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192func ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(list []metav1.APIResource) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, error) {\n    78\u2192\tvar apiResourceList []apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery\n    79\u2192\tparentResources := make(map[string]int)\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t// Loop through all top-level resources\n    82\u2192\tfor _, r := range list {\n    83\u2192\t\tif strings.Contains(r.Name, \"/\") {\n    84\u2192\t\t\t// Skip subresources for now so we can get the list of resources\n    85\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    86\u2192\t\t}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192\t\tvar scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope\n    89\u2192\t\tif r.Namespaced {\n    90\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace\n    91\u2192\t\t} else {\n    92\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\n    93\u2192\t\t}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\t\tresource := apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{\n    96\u2192\t\t\tResource: r.Name,\n    97\u2192\t\t\tScope:    scope,\n    98\u2192\t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{\n    99\u2192\t\t\t\tGroup:   r.Group,\n   100\u2192\t\t\t\tVersion: r.Version,\n   101\u2192\t\t\t\tKind:    r.Kind,\n   102\u2192\t\t\t},\n   103\u2192\t\t\tVerbs:            r.Verbs,\n   104\u2192\t\t\tShortNames:       r.ShortNames,\n   105\u2192\t\t\tCategories:       r.Categories,\n   106\u2192\t\t\tSingularResource: r.SingularName,\n   107\u2192\t\t}\n   108\u2192\t\tapiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, resource)\n   109\u2192\t\tparentResources[r.Name] = len(apiResourceList) - 1\n   110\u2192\t}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192\t// Loop through all subresources\n   113\u2192\tfor _, r := range list {\n   114\u2192\t\t// Split resource name and subresource name\n   115\u2192\t\tsplit := strings.SplitN(r.Name, \"/\", 2)\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192\t\tif len(split) != 2 {\n   118\u2192\t\t\t// Skip parent resources\n   119\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   120\u2192\t\t}\n 126\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\n   127\u2192\t\t}\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192\t\tparentidx, exists := parentResources[split[0]]\n   130\u2192\t\tif !exists {\n   131\u2192\t\t\t// 137\u2192\t\t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{},\n   138\u2192\t\t\t})\n   139\u2192\t\t\tparentidx = len(apiResourceList) - 1\n   140\u2192\t\t\tparentResources[split[0]] = parentidx\n 1065\u2192\t// WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests will call the rest of the request handling in a go-routine with the\n  1066\u2192\t// context with deadline. The go-routine can keep running, while the timeout logic will return a timeout to the client.\n  1067\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\n  1068\u2192\n  1069\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestDeadline(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator,\n  1070\u2192\t\tc.LongRunningFunc, c.Serializer, c.RequestTimeout)\n  1071\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithWaitGroup(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup)\n  1072\u2192\tif c.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod > 0 {\n  1073\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown(handler, c.lifecycleSignals, c.WatchRequestWaitGroup)\n  1074\u2192\t}\n  1075\u2192\tif c.SecureServing != nil && !c.SecureServing.DisableHTTP2 && c.GoawayChance > 0 {\n  1076\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithProbabilisticGoaway(handler, c.GoawayChance)\n  1077\u2192\t}\n  1078\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithCacheControl(handler)\n  1079\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithHSTS(handler, c.HSTSDirectives)\n  1080\u2192\tif c.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n  1081\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithRetryAfter(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest.Signaled())\n  1082\u2192\t}\n  1083\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithHTTPLogging(handler)\n  1084\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithLatencyTrackers(handler)\n  1085\u2192\t// WithRoutine will execute future handlers in a separate goroutine and serving\n  1086\u2192\t// handler in current goroutine to minimize the stack memory usage. 1155\u2192\tif s == nil || s.Listener == nil {\n  1156\u2192\t\treturn \"\", 0, fmt.Errorf(\"no listener found\")\n  1157\u2192\t}\n  1158\u2192\taddr := s.Listener.Addr().String()\n  1159\u2192\thost, portStr, err := net.SplitHostPort(addr)\n  1160\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1161\u2192\t\treturn \"\", 0, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get port from listener address %q: %v\", addr, err)\n 1166\u2192\t}\n  1167\u2192\treturn host, port, nil\n  1168\u2192}\n  1169\u2192\n  1170\u2192// AuthorizeClientBearerToken wraps the authenticator and authorizer in loopback authentication logic\n  1171\u2192// if the loopback client config is specified AND it has a bearer token. Note that if either authn or\n  1172\u2192// authz is nil, this function won't add a token authenticator or authorizer.\n  1173\u2192func AuthorizeClientBearerToken(loopback *restclient.Config, authn *AuthenticationInfo, authz *AuthorizationInfo) {\n  1174\u2192\tif loopback == nil || len(loopback.BearerToken) == 0 {\n  1175\u2192\t\treturn\n  1176\u2192\t}\n  1177\u2192\tif authn == nil || authz == nil {\n  1178\u2192\t\t// prevent nil pointer panic\n  1179\u2192\t\treturn\n  1180\u2192\t}\n  1181\u2192\tif authn.Authenticator == nil || authz.Authorizer == nil {\n  1182\u2192\t\t// authenticator or authorizer might be nil if we want to bypass authz/authn\n  1183\u2192\t\t// and we also do nothing in this case.\n  1184\u2192\t\treturn\n  1185\u2192\t}\n  1186\u2192\n  1187\u2192\tprivilegedLoopbackToken := loopback.BearerToken\n  1188\u2192\tvar uid = uuid.New().String()\n  1189\u2192\ttokens := make(map[string]*user.DefaultInfo)\n  1190\u2192\ttokens[privilegedLoopbackToken] = &user.DefaultInfo{\n  1191\u2192\t\tName:   user.APIServerUser,\n  1192\u2192\t\tUID:    uid,\n  1193\u2192\t\tGroups: []string{user.AllAuthenticated, user.SystemPrivilegedGroup},\n  1194\u2192\t}\n  1195\u2192\n  1196\u2192\ttokenAuthenticator := authenticatorfactory.NewFromTokens(tokens, authn.APIAudiences)\n  1197\u2192\tauthn.Authenticator = authenticatorunion.New(tokenAuthenticator, authn.Authenticator)\n  1198\u2192}\n  1199\u2192\n  1200\u2192// For testing purpose only\n  1201\u2192func SetHostnameFuncForTests(name string) {\n  1202\u2192\thostnameFunc = func() (host string, err error) {\n  1203\u2192\t\thost = name\n  1204\u2192\t\terr = nil\n  1205\u2192\t\treturn\n  1206\u2192\t}\n  1207\u2192}\n  1208\u2192"

"   876\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfos ...*APIGroupInfo) error {\n   877\u2192\tfor _, apiGroupInfo := range apiGroupInfos {\n   878\u2192\t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions) == 0 {\n   879\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no version priority set for %#v\", *apiGroupInfo)\n   880\u2192\t\t}\n   881\u2192\t\t// Do not register empty group or empty version. s.installAPIResources(APIGroupPrefix, apiGroupInfo, openAPIModels); err != nil {\n   898\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to install api resources: %v\", err)\n   899\u2192\t\t}\n   900\u2192\n   901\u2192\t\t// setup discovery\n   902\u2192\t\t// Install the version handler.\n   903\u2192\t\t// Add a handler at /apis/<groupName> to enumerate all versions supported by this group.\n   904\u2192\t\tapiVersionsForDiscovery := []metav1.GroupVersionForDiscovery{}\n   905\u2192\t\tfor _, groupVersion := range apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions {\n   906\u2192\t\t\t// Check the config to 909\u2192\t\t\t}\n   910\u2192\t\t\tapiVersionsForDiscovery = append(apiVersionsForDiscovery, metav1.GroupVersionForDiscovery{\n   911\u2192\t\t\t\tGroupVersion: groupVersion.String(),\n   912\u2192\t\t\t\tVersion:      groupVersion.Version,\n   913\u2192\t\t\t})\n   914\u2192\t\t}\n   915\u2192\t\tpreferredVersionForDiscovery := metav1.GroupVersionForDiscovery{\n   916\u2192\t\t\tGroupVersion: apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].String(),\n   917\u2192\t\t\tVersion:      apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Version,\n   918\u2192\t\t}\n   919\u2192\t\tapiGroup := metav1.APIGroup{\n   920\u2192\t\t\tName:             apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group,\n   921\u2192\t\t\tVersions:         apiVersionsForDiscovery,\n   922\u2192\t\t\tPreferredVersion: preferredVersionForDiscovery,\n   923\u2192\t\t}\n   924\u2192\n   925\u2192\t\ts.DiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroup(apiGroup)\n   926\u2192\t\ts.Handler.GoRestfulContainer.Add(discovery.NewAPIGroupHandler(s.Serializer, apiGroup).WebService())\n   927\u2192\t\ts.registerStorageReadinessCheck(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group, apiGroupInfo)\n   928\u2192\t}\n   929\u2192\treturn nil\n   930\u2192}\n   931\u2192\n   932\u2192// registerStorageReadinessCheck registers the readiness checks for all underlying storages\n   933\u2192// for a given APIGroup.\n   934\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) registerStorageReadinessCheck(groupName string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) {\n   935\u2192\tfor version, storageMap := range apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n   936\u2192\t\tfor resource, storage := range storageMap {\n   937\u2192\t\t\tif withReadiness, ok := storage.(rest.StorageWithReadiness); ok {\n   938\u2192\t\t\t\tgvr := metav1.GroupVersionResource{\n   939\u2192\t\t\t\t\tGroup:    groupName,\n   940\u2192\t\t\t\t\tVersion:  version,\n   941\u2192\t\t\t\t\tResource: resource,\n   942\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   943\u2192\t\t\t\ts.StorageReadinessHook.RegisterStorage(gvr, withReadiness)\n   944\u2192\t\t\t}\n   945\u2192\t\t}\n   946\u2192\t}\n   947\u2192}\n   948\u2192\n   949\u2192// InstallAPIGroup exposes the given api group in the API.\n   950\u2192// The <apiGroupInfo> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the\n   951\u2192// underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.\n   952\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroup(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) error {\n   953\u2192\treturn s.InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfo)\n   954\u2192}\n   955\u2192\n   956\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) getAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, groupVersion schema.GroupVersion, apiPrefix string) (*genericapi.APIGroupVersion, error) {\n   957\u2192\tstorage := make(map[string]rest.Storage)\n   958\u2192\tfor k, v := range apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap[groupVersion.Version] {\n   959\u2192\t\tif strings.ToLower(k) != k {\n   960\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"resource names must be lowercase only, not %q\", k)\n   961\u2192\t\t}\n   962\u2192\t\tstorage[k] = v\n   963\u2192\t}\n   964\u2192\tversion := s.newAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo, groupVersion)\n   965\u2192\tversion.Root = apiPrefix\n   966\u2192\tversion.Storage = storage\n   967\u2192\treturn version, nil\n   968\u2192}\n   969\u2192\n   970\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) newAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, groupVersion schema.GroupVersion) *genericapi.APIGroupVersion {\n   971\u2192\n   972\u2192\tallServedVersionsByResource := map[string][]string{}\n   973\u2192\tfor version, resourcesInVersion := range apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n   974\u2192\t\tfor resource := range resourcesInVersion {\n   975\u2192\t\t\tif len(groupVersion.Group) == 0 {\n   976\u2192\t\t\t\tallServedVersionsByResource[resource] = append(allServedVersionsByResource[resource], version)\n   977\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   978\u2192\t\t\t\tallServedVersionsByResource[resource] = append(allServedVersionsByResource[resource], fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", groupVersion.Group, version))\n   979\u2192\t\t\t}\n   980\u2192\t\t}\n   981\u2192\t}\n   982\u2192\n   983\u2192\treturn &genericapi.APIGroupVersion{\n   984\u2192\t\tGroupVersion:                groupVersion,\n   985\u2192\t\tAllServedVersionsByResource: allServedVersionsByResource,\n   986\u2192\t\tMetaGroupVersion:            apiGroupInfo.MetaGroupVersion,\n   987\u2192\n   988\u2192\t\tParameterCodec:        apiGroupInfo.ParameterCodec,\n   989\u2192\t\tSerializer:            apiGroupInfo.NegotiatedSerializer,\n   990\u2192\t\tCreater:               apiGroupInfo.Scheme,\n   991\u2192\t\tConvertor:             apiGroupInfo.Scheme,\n   992\u2192\t\tConvertabilityChecker: apiGroupInfo.Scheme,\n   993\u2192\t\tUnsafeConvertor:       runtime.UnsafeObjectConvertor(apiGroupInfo.Scheme),\n   994\u2192\t\tDefaulter:             apiGroupInfo.Scheme,\n   995\u2192\t\tTyper:                 apiGroupInfo.Scheme,\n   996\u2192\t\tNamer:                 runtime.Namer(meta.NewAccessor()),\n   997\u2192\n   998\u2192\t\tEquivalentResourceRegistry: s.EquivalentResourceRegistry,\n   999\u2192\n  1000\u2192\t\tAdmit:             s.admissionControl,\n  1001\u2192\t\tMinRequestTimeout: s.minRequestTimeout,\n  1002\u2192\t\tAuthorizer:        s.Authorizer,\n  1003\u2192\t}\n  1004\u2192}\n  1005\u2192\n  1006\u2192// NewDefaultAPIGroupInfo returns an APIGroupInfo stubbed with \"normal\" values\n  1007\u2192// exposed for easier composition from other packages\n  1008\u2192func NewDefaultAPIGroupInfo(group string, scheme *runtime.Scheme, parameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec, codecs serializer.CodecFactory) APIGroupInfo {\n  1009\u2192\topts := []serializer.CodecFactoryOptionsMutator{}\n  1010\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\n  1011\u2192\t\topts = append(opts, serializer.WithSerializer(cbor.NewSerializerInfo))\n  1012\u2192\t}\n  1013\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.StreamingCollectionEncodingToJSON) {\n  1014\u2192\t\topts = append(opts, serializer.WithStreamingCollectionEncodingToJSON())\n  1015\u2192\t}\n  1016\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.StreamingCollectionEncodingToProtobuf) {\n  1017\u2192\t\topts = append(opts, serializer.WithStreamingCollectionEncodingToProtobuf())\n  1018\u2192\t}\n  1019\u2192\tif len(opts) != 0 {\n  1020\u2192\t\tcodecs = serializer.NewCodecFactory(scheme, opts...)\n  1021\u2192\t}\n  1022\u2192\treturn APIGroupInfo{\n  1023\u2192\t\tPrioritizedVersions:          scheme.PrioritizedVersionsForGroup(group),\n  1024\u2192\t\tVersionedResourcesStorageMap: map[string]map[string]rest.Storage{},\n  1025\u2192\t\t// TODO unhardcode this.  It was hardcoded before, but we need to re-evaluate"
