"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package negotiation\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"mime\"\n    21\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    22\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n    23\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\t\"github.com/munnerz/goautoneg\"\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    30\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    31\u2192)\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192// MediaTypesForSerializer returns a list of media and stream media types for the server.\n    34\u2192func MediaTypesForSerializer(ns runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) (mediaTypes, streamMediaTypes []string) {\n    35\u2192\tfor _, info := range ns.SupportedMediaTypes() {\n    36\u2192\t\tmediaTypes = append(mediaTypes, info.MediaType)\n    37\u2192\t\tif info.StreamSerializer != nil {\n    38\u2192\t\t\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) && info.MediaType == runtime.ContentTypeCBOR {\n    39\u2192\t\t\t\tstreamMediaTypes = append(streamMediaTypes, runtime.ContentTypeCBORSequence)\n    40\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n    41\u2192\t\t\t}\n    42\u2192\t\t\t// stream=watch is the existing mime-type parameter for watch\n    43\u2192\t\t\tstreamMediaTypes = append(streamMediaTypes, info.MediaType+\";stream=watch\")\n    44\u2192\t\t}\n    45\u2192\t}\n    46\u2192\treturn mediaTypes, streamMediaTypes\n    47\u2192}\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192// NegotiateOutputMediaType negotiates the output structured media type and a serializer, or\n    50\u2192// returns an error.\n    51\u2192func NegotiateOutputMediaType(req *http.Request, ns runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, restrictions EndpointRestrictions) (MediaTypeOptions, runtime.SerializerInfo, error) {\n    52\u2192\tmediaType, ok := NegotiateMediaTypeOptions(req.Header.Get(\"Accept\"), ns.SupportedMediaTypes(), restrictions)\n    53\u2192\tif !ok {\n    54\u2192\t\tsupported, _ := MediaTypesForSerializer(ns)\n    55\u2192\t\treturn mediaType, runtime.SerializerInfo{}, NewNotAcceptableError(supported)\n    56\u2192\t}\n    57\u2192\t// TODO: move into resthandler\n    58\u2192\tinfo := mediaType.Accepted\n    59\u2192\tif (mediaType.Pretty || isPrettyPrint(req)) && info.PrettySerializer != nil {\n    60\u2192\t\tinfo.Serializer = info.PrettySerializer\n    61\u2192\t}\n    62\u2192\treturn mediaType, info, nil\n    63\u2192}\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192// NegotiateOutputMediaTypeStream returns a stream serializer for the given request.\n    66\u2192func NegotiateOutputMediaTypeStream(req *http.Request, ns runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, restrictions EndpointRestrictions) (runtime.SerializerInfo, error) {\n    67\u2192\tmediaType, ok := NegotiateMediaTypeOptions(req.Header.Get(\"Accept\"), ns.SupportedMediaTypes(), restrictions)\n    68\u2192\tif !ok || mediaType.Accepted.StreamSerializer == nil {\n    69\u2192\t\t_, supported := MediaTypesForSerializer(ns)\n    70\u2192\t\treturn runtime.SerializerInfo{}, NewNotAcceptableError(supported)\n    71\u2192\t}\n    72\u2192\treturn mediaType.Accepted, nil\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192// NegotiateInputSerializer returns the input serializer for the provided request.\n    76\u2192func NegotiateInputSerializer(req *http.Request, streaming bool, ns runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) (runtime.SerializerInfo, error) {\n    77\u2192\tmediaType := req.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n    78\u2192\treturn NegotiateInputSerializerForMediaType(mediaType, streaming, ns)\n    79\u2192}\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192// NegotiateInputSerializerForMediaType returns the appropriate serializer for the given media type or an error.\n    82\u2192func NegotiateInputSerializerForMediaType(mediaType string, streaming bool, ns runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) (runtime.SerializerInfo, error) {\n    83\u2192\tmediaTypes := ns.SupportedMediaTypes()\n    84\u2192\tif len(mediaType) == 0 {\n    85\u2192\t\tmediaType = mediaTypes[0].MediaType\n    86\u2192\t}\n    87\u2192\tif mediaType, _, err := mime.ParseMediaType(mediaType); err == nil {\n    88\u2192\t\tif info, ok := runtime.SerializerInfoForMediaType(mediaTypes, mediaType); ok {\n    89\u2192\t\t\treturn info, nil\n    90\u2192\t\t}\n    91\u2192\t}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192\tsupported, streamingSupported := MediaTypesForSerializer(ns)\n    94\u2192\tif streaming {\n    95\u2192\t\treturn runtime.SerializerInfo{}, NewUnsupportedMediaTypeError(streamingSupported)\n    96\u2192\t}\n    97\u2192\treturn runtime.SerializerInfo{}, NewUnsupportedMediaTypeError(supported)\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192// isPrettyPrint returns true if the \"pretty\" query parameter is true or if the User-Agent\n   101\u2192// matches known \"human\" clients.\n   102\u2192func isPrettyPrint(req *http.Request) bool {\n   103\u2192\t// DEPRECATED: should be part of the content type\n   104\u2192\tif req.URL != nil {\n   105\u2192\t\t// avoid an allocation caused by parsing the URL query\n   106\u2192\t\tif strings.Contains(req.URL.RawQuery, \"pretty\") {\n   107\u2192\t\t\tpp := req.URL.Query().Get(\"pretty\")\n   108\u2192\t\t\tif len(pp) > 0 {\n   109\u2192\t\t\t\tpretty, _ := strconv.ParseBool(pp)\n   110\u2192\t\t\t\treturn pretty\n   111\u2192\t\t\t}\n   112\u2192\t\t}\n   113\u2192\t}\n   114\u2192\tuserAgent := req.UserAgent()\n   115\u2192\t// This covers basic all browsers and cli http tools\n   116\u2192\tif strings.HasPrefix(userAgent, \"curl\") || strings.HasPrefix(userAgent, \"Wget\") || strings.HasPrefix(userAgent, \"Mozilla/5.0\") {\n   117\u2192\t\treturn true\n   118\u2192\t}\n   119\u2192\treturn false\n   120\u2192}\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192// EndpointRestrictions is an interface that allows content-type negotiation\n   123\u2192// to verify server support for specific options\n   124\u2192type EndpointRestrictions interface {\n   125\u2192\t// AllowsMediaTypeTransform returns true if the endpoint allows either the requested mime type\n   126\u2192\t// or the requested transformation. If false, the caller should ignore this mime type. If the\n   127\u2192\t// target is nil, the client is not requesting a transformation.\n   128\u2192\tAllowsMediaTypeTransform(mimeType, mimeSubType string, target *schema.GroupVersionKind) bool\n   129\u2192\t// AllowsServerVersion should return true if the specified version is valid\n   130\u2192\t// for the server group.\n   131\u2192\tAllowsServerVersion(version string) bool\n   132\u2192\t// AllowsStreamSchema should return true if the specified stream schema is\n   133\u2192\t// valid for the server group.\n   134\u2192\tAllowsStreamSchema(schema string) bool\n   135\u2192}\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192// DefaultEndpointRestrictions is the default EndpointRestrictions which allows\n   138\u2192// content-type negotiation to verify server support for specific options\n   139\u2192var DefaultEndpointRestrictions = emptyEndpointRestrictions{}\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192type emptyEndpointRestrictions struct{}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192func (emptyEndpointRestrictions) AllowsMediaTypeTransform(mimeType string, mimeSubType string, gvk *schema.GroupVersionKind) bool {\n   144\u2192\treturn gvk == nil\n   145\u2192}\n   146\u2192func (emptyEndpointRestrictions) AllowsServerVersion(string) bool  { return false }\n   147\u2192func (emptyEndpointRestrictions) AllowsStreamSchema(s string) bool { return s == \"watch\" }\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192// MediaTypeOptions describes information for a given media type that may alter\n   150\u2192// the server response"

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go",
  "limit": 200,
  "offset": 200

Output
"   200\u2192\n   201\u2192\t// Register the paths in a deterministic (sorted) order to get a deterministic swagger spec.\n   202\u2192\tpaths := make([]string, len(a.group.Storage))\n   203\u2192\tvar i int = 0\n   204\u2192\tfor path := range a.group.Storage {\n   205\u2192\t\tpaths[i] = path\n   206\u2192\t\ti++\n   207\u2192\t}\n   208\u2192\tsort.Strings(paths)\n   209\u2192\tfor _, path := range paths {\n   210\u2192\t\tapiResource, resourceInfo, err := a.registerResourceHandlers(path, a.group.Storage[path], ws)\n   211\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   212\u2192\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Errorf(\"error in registering resource: %s, %v\", path, err))\n   213\u2192\t\t}\n   214\u2192\t\tif apiResource != nil {\n   215\u2192\t\t\tapiResources = append(apiResources, *apiResource)\n   216\u2192\t\t}\n   217\u2192\t\tif resourceInfo != nil {\n   218\u2192\t\t\tresourceInfos = append(resourceInfos, resourceInfo)\n   219\u2192\t\t}\n   220\u2192\t}\n   221\u2192\treturn apiResources, resourceInfos, ws, errors\n   222\u2192}\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192// newWebService creates a new restful webservice with the api installer's prefix and version.\n   225\u2192func (a *APIInstaller) newWebService() *restful.WebService {\n   226\u2192\tws := new(restful.WebService)\n   227\u2192\tws.Path(a.prefix)\n   228\u2192\t// a.prefix contains \"prefix/group/version\"\n   229\u2192\tws.Doc(\"API at \" + a.prefix)\n   230\u2192\t// Backwards compatibility, we accepted objects with empty content-type at V1.\n   231\u2192\t// If we stop using go-restful, we can default empty content-type to application/json on an\n   232\u2192\t// endpoint by endpoint basis\n   233\u2192\tws.Consumes(\"*/*\")\n   234\u2192\tmediaTypes, streamMediaTypes := negotiation.MediaTypesForSerializer(a.group.Serializer)\n   235\u2192\tws.Produces(append(mediaTypes, streamMediaTypes...)...)\n   236\u2192\tws.ApiVersion(a.group.GroupVersion.String())\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192\treturn ws\n   239\u2192}\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192// calculate the storage gvk, the gvk objects are converted to before persisted to the etcd.\n   242\u2192func getStorageVersionKind(storageVersioner runtime.GroupVersioner, storage rest.Storage, typer runtime.ObjectTyper) (schema.GroupVersionKind, error) {\n   243\u2192\tobject := storage.New()\n   244\u2192\tfqKinds, _, err := typer.ObjectKinds(object)\n   245\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   246\u2192\t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, err\n   247\u2192\t}\n   248\u2192\tgvk, ok := storageVersioner.KindForGroupVersionKinds(fqKinds)\n   249\u2192\tif !ok {\n   250\u2192\t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot find the storage version kind for %v\", reflect.TypeOf(object))\n   251\u2192\t}\n   252\u2192\treturn gvk, nil\n   253\u2192}\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192// GetResourceKind returns the external group version kind registered for the given storage\n   256\u2192// object. If the storage object is a subresource and has an override supplied for it, it returns\n   257\u2192// the group version kind supplied in the override.\n   258\u2192func GetResourceKind(groupVersion schema.GroupVersion, storage rest.Storage, typer runtime.ObjectTyper) (schema.GroupVersionKind, error) {\n   259\u2192\t// Let the storage tell us exactly what GVK it has\n   260\u2192\tif gvkProvider, ok := storage.(rest.GroupVersionKindProvider); ok {\n   261\u2192\t\treturn gvkProvider.GroupVersionKind(groupVersion), nil\n   262\u2192\t}\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192\tobject := storage.New()\n   265\u2192\tfqKinds, _, err := typer.ObjectKinds(object)\n   266\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   267\u2192\t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, err\n   268\u2192\t}\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192\t// a given go type can have multiple potential fully qualified kinds.  Find the one that corresponds with the group\n   271\u2192\t// we're trying to register here\n   272\u2192\tfqKindToRegister := schema.GroupVersionKind{}\n   273\u2192\tfor _, fqKind := range fqKinds {\n   274\u2192\t\tif fqKind.Group == groupVersion.Group {\n   275\u2192\t\t\tfqKindToRegister = groupVersion.WithKind(fqKind.Kind)\n   276\u2192\t\t\tbreak\n   277\u2192\t\t}\n   278\u2192\t}\n   279\u2192\tif fqKindToRegister.Empty() {\n   280\u2192\t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(\"unable to locate fully qualified kind for %v: found %v when registering for %v\", reflect.TypeOf(object), fqKinds, groupVersion)\n   281\u2192\t}\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192\t// group is guaranteed to match based on the check above\n   284\u2192\treturn fqKindToRegister, nil\n   285\u2192}\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192func (a *APIInstaller) registerResourceHandlers(path string, storage rest.Storage, ws *restful.WebService) (*metav1.APIResource, *storageversion.ResourceInfo, error) {\n   288\u2192\tadmit := a.group.Admit\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192\toptionsExternalVersion := a.group.GroupVersion\n   291\u2192\tif a.group.OptionsExternalVersion != nil {\n   292\u2192\t\toptionsExternalVersion = *a.group.OptionsExternalVersion\n   293\u2192\t}\n   294\u2192\n   295\u2192\tresource, subresource, err := splitSubresource(path)\n   296\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   297\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   298\u2192\t}\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192\tgroup, version := a.group.GroupVersion.Group, a.group.GroupVersion.Version\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192\tfqKindToRegister, err := GetResourceKind(a.group.GroupVersion, storage, a.group.Typer)\n   303\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   304\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   305\u2192\t}\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192\tversionedPtr, err := a.group.Creater.New(fqKindToRegister)\n   308\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   309\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   310\u2192\t}\n   311\u2192\tdefaultVersionedObject := indirectArbitraryPointer(versionedPtr)\n   312\u2192\tkind := fqKindToRegister.Kind\n   313\u2192\tisSubresource := len(subresource) > 0\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192\t// If there is a subresource, namespace scoping is defined by the parent resource\n   316\u2192\tvar namespaceScoped bool\n   317\u2192\tif isSubresource {\n   318\u2192\t\tparentStorage, ok := a.group.Storage[resource]\n   319\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   320\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"missing parent storage: %q\", resource)\n   321\u2192\t\t}\n   322\u2192\t\tscoper, ok := parentStorage.(rest.Scoper)\n   323\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   324\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%q must implement scoper\", resource)\n   325\u2192\t\t}\n   326\u2192\t\tnamespaceScoped = scoper.NamespaceScoped()\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192\t} else {\n   329\u2192\t\tscoper, ok := storage.(rest.Scoper)\n   330\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   331\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%q must implement scoper\", resource)\n   332\u2192\t\t}\n   333\u2192\t\tnamespaceScoped = scoper.NamespaceScoped()\n   334\u2192\t}\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192\t// what verbs are supported by the storage, used to know what verbs we support per path\n   337\u2192\tcreater, isCreater := storage.(rest.Creater)\n   338\u2192\tnamedCreater, isNamedCreater := storage.(rest.NamedCreater)\n   339\u2192\tlister, isLister := storage.(rest.Lister)\n   340\u2192\tgetter, isGetter := storage.(rest.Getter)\n   341\u2192\tgetterWithOptions, isGetterWithOptions := storage.(rest.GetterWithOptions)\n   342\u2192\tgracefulDeleter, isGracefulDeleter := storage.(rest.GracefulDeleter)\n   343\u2192\tcollectionDeleter, isCollectionDeleter := storage.(rest.CollectionDeleter)\n   344\u2192\tupdater, isUpdater := storage.(rest.Updater)\n   345\u2192\tpatcher, isPatcher := storage.(rest.Patcher)\n   346\u2192\twatcher, isWatcher := storage.(rest.Watcher)\n   347\u2192\tconnecter, isConnecter := storage.(rest.Connecter)\n   348\u2192\tstorageMeta, isMetadata := storage.(rest.StorageMetadata)\n   349\u2192\tstorageVersionProvider, isStorageVersionProvider := storage.(rest.StorageVersionProvider)\n   350\u2192\tgvAcceptor, _ := storage.(rest.GroupVersionAcceptor)\n   351\u2192\tif !isMetadata {\n   352\u2192\t\tstorageMeta = defaultStorageMetadata{}\n   353\u2192\t}\n   354\u2192\n   355\u2192\tif isNamedCreater {\n   356\u2192\t\tisCreater = true\n   357\u2192\t}\n   358\u2192\n   359\u2192\tvar versionedList interface{}\n   360\u2192\tif isLister {\n   361\u2192\t\tlist := lister.NewList()\n   362\u2192\t\tlistGVKs, _, err := a.group.Typer.ObjectKinds(list)\n   363\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   364\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   365\u2192\t\t}\n   366\u2192\t\tversionedListPtr, err := a.group.Creater.New(a.group.GroupVersion.WithKind(listGVKs[0].Kind))\n   367\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   368\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   369\u2192\t\t}\n   370\u2192\t\tversionedList = indirectArbitraryPointer(versionedListPtr)\n   371\u2192\t}\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192\tversionedListOptions, err := a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"ListOptions\"))\n   374\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   375\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   376\u2192\t}\n   377\u2192\tversionedCreateOptions, err := a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"CreateOptions\"))\n   378\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   379\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   380\u2192\t}\n   381\u2192\tversionedPatchOptions, err := a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"PatchOptions\"))\n   382\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   383\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   384\u2192\t}\n   385\u2192\tversionedUpdateOptions, err := a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"UpdateOptions\"))\n   386\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   387\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   388\u2192\t}\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192\tvar versionedDeleteOptions runtime.Object\n   391\u2192\tvar versionedDeleterObject interface{}\n   392\u2192\tdeleteReturnsDeletedObject := false\n   393\u2192\tif isGracefulDeleter {\n   394\u2192\t\tversionedDeleteOptions, err = a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"DeleteOptions\"))\n   395\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   396\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   397\u2192\t\t}\n   398\u2192\t\tversionedDeleterObject = indirectArbitraryPointer(versionedDeleteOptions)\n   399\u2192"

449\u2192\t\tconnectOptions, connectSubpath, _ = connecter.NewConnectOptions()\n   450\u2192\t\tif connectOptions != nil {\n   451\u2192\t\t\tconnectOptionsInternalKinds, _, err := a.group.Typer.ObjectKinds(connectOptions)\n   452\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   453\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   454\u2192\t\t\t}\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192\t\t\tconnectOptionsInternalKind = connectOptionsInternalKinds[0]\n   457\u2192\t\t\tversionedConnectOptions, err = a.group.Creater.New(a.group.GroupVersion.WithKind(connectOptionsInternalKind.Kind))\n   458\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   459\u2192\t\t\t\tversionedConnectOptions, err = a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(connectOptionsInternalKind.Kind))\n   460\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   461\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   462\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   463\u2192\t\t\t}\n   464\u2192\t\t}\n   465\u2192\t}\n   466\u2192\n   467\u2192\tallowWatchList := isWatcher && isLister // watching on lists is allowed only for kinds that support both watch and list.\n   468\u2192\tnameParam := ws.PathParameter(\"name\", \"name of the \"+kind).DataType(\"string\")\n   469\u2192\tpathParam := ws.PathParameter(\"path\", \"path to the resource\").DataType(\"string\")\n   470\u2192\n   471\u2192\tparams := []*restful.Parameter{}\n   472\u2192\tactions := []action{}\n   473\u2192\n   474\u2192\tvar resourceKind string\n   475\u2192\tkindProvider, ok := storage.(rest.KindProvider)\n   476\u2192\tif ok {\n   477\u2192\t\tresourceKind = kindProvider.Kind()\n   478\u2192\t} else {\n   479\u2192\t\tresourceKind = kind\n   480\u2192\t}\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192\ttableProvider, isTableProvider := storage.(rest.TableConvertor)\n   483\u2192\tif isLister && !isTableProvider {\n   484\u2192\t\t// All listers must implement TableProvider\n   485\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%q must implement TableConvertor\", resource)\n   486\u2192\t}\n   487\u2192\n   488\u2192\tvar apiResource metav1.APIResource\n   489\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.StorageVersionHash) &&\n   490\u2192\t\tisStorageVersionProvider &&\n   491\u2192\t\tstorageVersionProvider.StorageVersion() != nil {\n   492\u2192\t\tversioner := storageVersionProvider.StorageVersion()\n   493\u2192\t\tgvk, err := getStorageVersionKind(versioner, storage, a.group.Typer)\n   494\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   495\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   496\u2192\t\t}\n   497\u2192\t\tapiResource.StorageVersionHash = discovery.StorageVersionHash(gvk.Group, gvk.Version, gvk.Kind)\n   498\u2192\t}\n   499\u2192\n   500\u2192\t// Get the list of actions for the given scope.\n   501\u2192\tswitch {\n   502\u2192\tcase !namespaceScoped:\n   503\u2192\t\t// Handle non-namespace scoped resources like nodes.\n   504\u2192\t\tresourcePath := resource\n   505\u2192\t\tresourceParams := params\n   506\u2192\t\titemPath := resourcePath + \"/{name}\"\n   507\u2192\t\tnameParams := append(params, nameParam)\n   508\u2192\t\tproxyParams := append(nameParams, pathParam)\n   509\u2192\t\tsuffix := \"\"\n   510\u2192\t\tif isSubresource {\n   511\u2192\t\t\tsuffix = \"/\" + subresource\n   512\u2192\t\t\titemPath = itemPath + suffix\n   513\u2192\t\t\tresourcePath = itemPath\n   514\u2192\t\t\tresourceParams = nameParams\n   515\u2192\t\t}\n   516\u2192\t\tapiResource.Name = path\n   517\u2192\t\tapiResource.Namespaced = false\n   518\u2192\t\tapiResource.Kind = resourceKind\n   519\u2192\t\tnamer := handlers.ContextBasedNaming{\n   520\u2192\t\t\tNamer:         a.group.Namer,\n   521\u2192\t\t\tClusterScoped: true,\n   522\u2192\t\t}\n   523\u2192\n   524\u2192\t\t// Handler for standard REST verbs (GET, PUT, POST and DELETE).\n   525\u2192\t\t// Add actions at the resource path: /api/apiVersion/resource\n   526\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodList, resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, isLister)\n   527\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodPost, resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, isCreater)\n   528\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodDeleteCollection, resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, isCollectionDeleter)\n   529\u2192\t\t// DEPRECATED in 1.11\n   530\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodWatchList, \"watch/\" + resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, allowWatchList)\n   531\u2192\n   532\u2192\t\t// Add actions at the item path: /api/apiVersion/resource/{name}\n   533\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodGet, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isGetter)\n   534\u2192\t\tif getSubpath {\n   535\u2192\t\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodGet, itemPath + \"/{path:*}\", proxyParams, namer, false}, isGetter)\n   536\u2192\t\t}\n   537\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodPut, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isUpdater)\n   538\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodPatch, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isPatcher)\n   539\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodDelete, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isGracefulDeleter)\n   540\u2192\t\t// DEPRECATED in 1.11\n   541\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodWatch, \"watch/\" + itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isWatcher)\n   542\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodConnect, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isConnecter)\n   543\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodConnect, itemPath + \"/{path:*}\", proxyParams, namer, false}, isConnecter && connectSubpath)\n   544\u2192\tdefault:\n   545\u2192\t\tnamespaceParamName := \"namespaces\"\n   546\u2192\t\t// Handler for standard REST verbs (GET, PUT, POST and DELETE).\n   547\u2192\t\tnamespaceParam := ws.PathParameter(\"namespace\", \"object name and auth scope, such as for teams and projects\").DataType(\"string\")\n   548\u2192\t\tnamespacedPath := namespaceParamName + \"/{namespace}/\" + resource\n   549\u2192\t\tnamespaceParams := []*restful.Parameter{namespaceParam}\n   550\u2192\n   551\u2192\t\tresourcePath := namespacedPath\n   552\u2192\t\tresourceParams := namespaceParams\n   553\u2192\t\titemPath := namespacedPath + \"/{name}\"\n   554\u2192\t\tnameParams := append(namespaceParams, nameParam)\n   555\u2192\t\tproxyParams := append(nameParams, pathParam)\n   556\u2192\t\titemPathSuffix := \"\"\n   557\u2192\t\tif isSubresource {\n   558\u2192\t\t\titemPathSuffix = \"/\" + subresource\n   559\u2192\t\t\titemPath = itemPath + itemPathSuffix\n   560\u2192\t\t\tresourcePath = itemPath\n   561\u2192\t\t\tresourceParams = nameParams\n   562\u2192\t\t}\n   563\u2192\t\tapiResource.Name = path\n   564\u2192\t\tapiResource.Namespaced = true\n   565\u2192\t\tapiResource.Kind = resourceKind\n   566\u2192\t\tnamer := handlers.ContextBasedNaming{\n   567\u2192\t\t\tNamer:         a.group.Namer,\n   568\u2192\t\t\tClusterScoped: false,\n   569\u2192\t\t}\n   570\u2192\n   571\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodList, resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, isLister)\n   572\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodPost, resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, isCreater)\n   573\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodDeleteCollection, resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, isCollectionDeleter)\n   574\u2192\t\t// DEPRECATED in 1.11\n   575\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodWatchList, \"watch/\" + resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, allowWatchList)\n   576\u2192\n   577\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodGet, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isGetter)\n   578\u2192\t\tif getSubpath {\n   579\u2192\t\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodGet, itemPath + \"/{path:*}\", proxyParams, namer, false}, isGetter)\n   580\u2192\t\t}\n   581\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodPut, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isUpdater)\n   582\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodPatch, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isPatcher)\n   583\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodDelete, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isGracefulDeleter)\n   584\u2192\t\t// DEPRECATED in 1.11\n   585\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodWatch, \"watch/\" + itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isWatcher)\n   586\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodConnect, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isConnecter)\n   587\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodConnect, itemPath + \"/{path:*}\", proxyParams, namer, false}, isConnecter && connectSubpath)\n   588\u2192\n   589\u2192\t\t// list or post across namespace.\n   590\u2192\t\t// For ex: LIST all pods in all namespaces by sending a LIST request at /api/apiVersion/pods.\n   591\u2192\t\t// TODO: more strongly type whether a resource allows these actions on \"all namespaces\" (bulk delete)\n   592\u2192\t\tif !isSubresource {\n   593\u2192\t\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodList, resource, params, namer, true}, isLister)\n   594\u2192\t\t\t// DEPRECATED in 1.11\n   595\u2192\t\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodWatchList, \"watch/\" + resource, params, namer, true}, allowWatchList)\n   596\u2192\t\t}\n   597\u2192\t}\n   598\u2192\n   599\u2192\tvar resourceInfo *storageversion.ResourceInfo\n   600\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.StorageVersionAPI) &&\n   601\u2192\t\tutilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.APIServerIdentity) &&\n   602\u2192\t\tisStorageVersionProvider &&\n   603\u2192\t\tstorageVersionProvider.StorageVersion() != nil {\n   604\u2192\n   605\u2192\t\tversioner := storageVersionProvider.StorageVersion()\n   606\u2192\t\tencodingGVK, err := getStorageVersionKind(versioner, storage, a.group.Typer)\n   607\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   608\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   609\u2192\t\t}\n   610\u2192\t\tdecodableVersions := []schema.GroupVersion{}\n   611\u2192\t\tif a.group.ConvertabilityChecker != nil {\n   612\u2192\t\t\tdecodableVersions = a.group.ConvertabilityChecker.VersionsForGroupKind(fqKindToRegister.GroupKind())\n   613\u2192\t\t}\n   614\u2192\n   615\u2192\t\tresourceInfo = &storageversion.ResourceInfo{\n   616\u2192\t\t\tGroupResource: schema.GroupResource{\n   617\u2192\t\t\t\tGroup:    a.group.GroupVersion.Group,\n   618\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: apiResource.Name,\n   619\u2192\t\t\t},\n   620\u2192\t\t\tEncodingVersion: encodingGVK.GroupVersion().String(),\n   621\u2192\t\t\t// We record EquivalentResourceMapper first instead of calculate\n   622\u2192\t\t\t// DecodableVersions immediately because API installation must\n   623\u2192\t\t\t// be completed first for us to know equivalent APIs\n   624\u2192\t\t\tEquivalentResourceMapper: a.group.EquivalentResourceRegistry,\n   625\u2192\n   626\u2192\t\t\tDirectlyDecodableVersions: decodableVersions,\n   627\u2192\n   628\u2192\t\t\tServedVersions: a.group.AllServedVersionsByResource[path],\n   629\u2192\t\t}\n   630\u2192\t}\n   631\u2192\n   632\u2192\t// Create Routes for the actions.\n   633\u2192\t// TODO: Add status documentation using Returns()\n   634\u2192\t// Errors (see api/errors/errors.go as well as go-restful router):\n   635\u2192\t// http.StatusNotFound, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed,\n   636\u2192\t// http.StatusUnsupportedMediaType, http.StatusNotAcceptable,\n   637\u2192\t// http.StatusBadRequest, http.StatusUnauthorized, http.StatusForbidden,\n   638\u2192\t// http.StatusRequestTimeout, http.StatusConflict, http.StatusPreconditionFailed,\n   639\u2192\t// http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, http.StatusInternalServerError,\n   640\u2192\t// http.StatusServiceUnavailable\n   641\u2192\t// and api error codes\n   642\u2192\t// Note that if we specify a versioned Status object here, we may need to\n   643\u2192\t// create one for the tests, also\n   644\u2192\t// Success:\n   645\u2192\t// http.StatusOK, http.StatusCreated, http.StatusAccepted, http.StatusNoContent\n   646\u2192\t//\n   647\u2192\t// test/integration/auth_test.go is currently the most comprehensive status code test\n   648\u2192\n   649\u2192\tfor _, s := range a.group.Serializer.SupportedMediaTypes() {"

either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package cacher\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    23\u2192\t\"reflect\"\n    24\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    25\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    26\u2192\t\"time\"\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\"\n    29\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/metadata\"\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    33\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    43\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/delegator\"\n    46\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/metrics\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/progress\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/store\"\n    49\u2192\tetcdfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/feature\"\n    50\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    51\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n    52\u2192\t\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\"\n    53\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    54\u2192\t\"k8s.io/utils/clock\"\n    55\u2192)\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192var (\n    58\u2192\temptyFunc             = func(bool) {}\n    59\u2192\tcoreNamespaceResource = schema.GroupResource{Group: \"\", Resource: \"namespaces\"}\n    60\u2192)\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192const (\n    63\u2192\t// storageWatchListPageSize is the cacher's request chunk size of\n    64\u2192\t// initial and resync watch lists to storage.\n    65\u2192\tstorageWatchListPageSize = int64(10000)\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\t// DefaultEventFreshDuration is the default time duration of events\n    68\u2192\t// we want to keep.\n    69\u2192\t// We set it to defaultBookmarkFrequency plus epsilon to maximize\n    70\u2192\t// chances that last bookmark was sent within kept history, at the\n    71\u2192\t// same time, minimizing the needed memory usage.\n    72\u2192\tDefaultEventFreshDuration = defaultBookmarkFrequency + 15*time.Second\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192\t// defaultBookmarkFrequency defines how frequently watch bookmarks should be send\n    75\u2192\t// in addition to sending a bookmark right before watch deadline.\n    76\u2192\tdefaultBookmarkFrequency = time.Minute\n    77\u2192)\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192// Config contains the configuration for a given Cache.\n    80\u2192type Config struct {\n    81\u2192\t// An underlying storage.Interface.\n    82\u2192\tStorage storage.Interface\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192\t// An underlying storage.Versioner.\n    85\u2192\tVersioner storage.Versioner\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\t// The GroupResource the cacher is caching. Used for disambiguating *unstructured.Unstructured (CRDs) in logging\n    88\u2192\t// and metrics.\n    89\u2192\tGroupResource schema.GroupResource\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192\t// EventsHistoryWindow specifies minimum history duration that storage is keeping.\n    92\u2192\t// If lower than DefaultEventFreshDuration, the cache creation will fail.\n    93\u2192\tEventsHistoryWindow time.Duration\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\t// The Cache will be caching objects of a given Type and assumes that they\n    96\u2192\t// are all stored under ResourcePrefix directory in the underlying database.\n    97\u2192\tResourcePrefix string\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192\t// KeyFunc is used to get a key in the underlying storage for a given object.\n   100\u2192\tKeyFunc func(runtime.Object) (string, error)"
89. Bash @ -
Input

  "command": "grep -n \"func register\\|func.*action\\|MethodList\\|MethodGet\\|MethodPut\\|MethodDelete\" /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go | head -30"
}

err != nil {\n   886\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   887\u2192\t\t\t}\n   888\u2192\t\t\taddParams(route, action.Params)\n   889\u2192\t\t\troutes = append(routes, route)\n   890\u2192\t\tcase request.MethodPatch: // Partially update a resource\n   891\u2192\t\t\tdoc := \"partially update the specified \" + kind\n   892\u2192\t\t\tif isSubresource {\n   893\u2192\t\t\t\tdoc = \"partially update \" + subresource + \" of the specified \" + kind\n   894\u2192\t\t\t}\n   895\u2192\t\t\tsupportedTypes := []string{\n   896\u2192\t\t\t\tstring(types.JSONPatchType),\n   897\u2192\t\t\t\tstring(types.MergePatchType),\n   898\u2192\t\t\t\tstring(types.StrategicMergePatchType),\n   899\u2192\t\t\t\tstring(types.ApplyYAMLPatchType),\n   900\u2192\t\t\t}\n   901\u2192\t\t\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\n   902\u2192\t\t\t\tsupportedTypes = append(supportedTypes, string(types.ApplyCBORPatchType))\n   903\u2192\t\t\t}\n   904\u2192\t\t\thandler := metrics.InstrumentRouteFunc(action.Verb, group, version, resource, subresource, requestScope, metrics.APIServerComponent, deprecated, removedRelease, restfulPatchResource(patcher, reqScope, admit, supportedTypes))\n   905\u2192\t\t\thandler = utilwarning.AddWarningsHandler(handler, warnings)\n   906\u2192\t\t\troute := ws.PATCH(action.Path).To(handler).\n   907\u2192\t\t\t\tDoc(doc).\n   908\u2192\t\t\t\tParam(ws.QueryParameter(\"pretty\", \"If 'true', then the output is pretty printed. Defaults to 'false' unless the user-agent indicates a browser or command-line HTTP tool (curl and wget).\")).\n   909\u2192\t\t\t\tConsumes(supportedTypes...).\n   910\u2192\t\t\t\tOperation(\"patch\"+namespaced+kind+cases.Title(language.English).String(subresource)+operationSuffix).\n   911\u2192\t\t\t\tProduces(append(storageMeta.ProducesMIMETypes(action.Verb), mediaTypes...)...).\n   912\u2192\t\t\t\tReturns(http.StatusOK, \"OK\", producedObject).\n   913\u2192\t\t\t\t// Patch can return 201 when a server side apply is requested\n   914\u2192\t\t\t\tReturns(http.StatusCreated, \"Created\", producedObject).\n   915\u2192\t\t\t\tReads(metav1.Patch{}).\n   916\u2192\t\t\t\tWrites(producedObject)\n   917\u2192\t\t\tif err := AddObjectParams(ws, route, versionedPatchOptions); err != nil {\n   918\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   919\u2192\t\t\t}\n   920\u2192\t\t\taddParams(route, action.Params)\n   921\u2192\t\t\troutes = append(routes, route)\n   922\u2192\t\tcase request.MethodPost: // Create a resource.\n   923\u2192\t\t\tvar handler restful.RouteFunction\n   924\u2192\t\t\tif isNamedCreater {\n   925\u2192\t\t\t\thandler = restfulCreateNamedResource(namedCreater, reqScope, admit)\n   926\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   927\u2192\t\t\t\thandler = restfulCreateResource(creater, reqScope, admit)\n   928\u2192\t\t\t}\n   929\u2192\t\t\thandler = metrics.InstrumentRouteFunc(action.Verb, group, version, resource, subresource, requestScope, metrics.APIServerComponent, deprecated, removedRelease, handler)\n   930\u2192\t\t\thandler = utilwarning.AddWarningsHandler(handler, warnings)\n   931\u2192\t\t\tarticle := GetArticleForNoun(kind, \" \")\n   932\u2192\t\t\tdoc := \"create\" + article + kind\n   933\u2192\t\t\tif isSubresource {\n   934\u2192\t\t\t\tdoc = \"create \" + subresource + \" of\" + article + kind\n   935\u2192\t\t\t}\n   936\u2192\t\t\troute := ws.POST(action.Path).To(handler).\n   937\u2192\t\t\t\tDoc(doc).\n   938\u2192\t\t\t\tParam(ws.QueryParameter(\"pretty\", \"If 'true', then the output is pretty printed. Defaults to 'false' unless the user-agent indicates a browser or command-line HTTP tool (curl and wget).\")).\n   939\u2192\t\t\t\tOperation(\"create\"+namespaced+kind+cases.Title(language.English).String(subresource)+operationSuffix).\n   940\u2192\t\t\t\tProduces(append(storageMeta.ProducesMIMETypes(action.Verb), mediaTypes...)...).\n   941\u2192\t\t\t\tReturns(http.StatusOK, \"OK\", producedObject).\n   942\u2192\t\t\t\t// TODO: in some cases, the API may return a v1.Status instead of the versioned object\n   943\u2192\t\t\t\t// but currently go-restful can't handle multiple different objects being returned.\n   944\u2192\t\t\t\tReturns(http.StatusCreated, \"Created\", producedObject).\n   945\u2192\t\t\t\tReturns(http.StatusAccepted, \"Accepted\", producedObject).\n   946\u2192\t\t\t\tReads(defaultVersionedObject).\n   947\u2192\t\t\t\tWrites(producedObject)\n   948\u2192\t\t\tif err := AddObjectParams(ws, route, versionedCreateOptions); err != nil {\n   949\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   950\u2192\t\t\t}\n   951\u2192\t\t\taddParams(route, action.Params)\n   952\u2192\t\t\troutes = append(routes, route)\n   953\u2192\t\tcase request.MethodDelete: // Delete a resource.\n   954\u2192\t\t\tarticle := GetArticleForNoun(kind, \" \")\n   955\u2192\t\t\tdoc := \"delete\" + article + kind\n   956\u2192\t\t\tif isSubresource {\n   957\u2192\t\t\t\tdoc = \"delete \" + subresource + \" of\" + article + kind\n   958\u2192\t\t\t}\n   959\u2192\t\t\tdeleteReturnType := versionedStatus\n   960\u2192\t\t\tif deleteReturnsDeletedObject {\n   961\u2192\t\t\t\tdeleteReturnType = producedObject\n   962\u2192\t\t\t}\n   963\u2192\t\t\thandler := metrics.InstrumentRouteFunc(action.Verb, group, version, resource, subresource, requestScope, metrics.APIServerComponent, deprecated, removedRelease, restfulDeleteResource(gracefulDeleter, isGracefulDeleter, reqScope, admit))\n   964\u2192\t\t\thandler = utilwarning.AddWarningsHandler(handler, warnings)\n   965\u2192\t\t\troute := ws.DELETE(action.Path).To(handler).\n   966\u2192\t\t\t\tDoc(doc).\n   967\u2192\t\t\t\tParam(ws.QueryParameter(\"pretty\", \"If 'true', then the output is pretty printed. Defaults to 'false' unless the user-agent indicates a browser or command-line HTTP tool (curl and wget).\")).\n   968\u2192\t\t\t\tOperation(\"delete\"+namespaced+kind+cases.Title(language.English).String(subresource)+operationSuffix).\n   969\u2192\t\t\t\tProduces(append(storageMeta.ProducesMIMETypes(action.Verb), mediaTypes...)...).\n   970\u2192\t\t\t\tWrites(deleteReturnType).\n   971\u2192\t\t\t\tReturns(http.StatusOK, \"OK\", deleteReturnType).\n   972\u2192\t\t\t\tReturns(http.StatusAccepted, \"Accepted\", deleteReturnType)\n   973\u2192\t\t\tif isGracefulDeleter {\n   974\u2192\t\t\t\troute.Reads(versionedDeleterObject)\n   975\u2192\t\t\t\troute.ParameterNamed(\"body\").Required(false)\n   976\u2192\t\t\t\tif err := AddObjectParams(ws, route, versionedDeleteOptions); err != nil {\n   977\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   978\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   979\u2192\t\t\t}\n   980\u2192\t\t\taddParams(route, action.Params)\n   981\u2192\t\t\troutes = append(routes, route)\n   982\u2192\t\tcase request.MethodDeleteCollection:\n   983\u2192\t\t\tdoc := \"delete collection of \" + kind\n   984\u2192\t\t\tif isSubresource {\n   985\u2192\t\t\t\tdoc = \"delete collection of \" + subresource + \" of a \" + kind\n   986\u2192\t\t\t}\n   987\u2192\t\t\thandler := metrics.InstrumentRouteFunc(action.Verb, group, version, resource, subresource, requestScope, metrics.APIServerComponent, deprecated, removedRelease, restfulDeleteCollection(collectionDeleter, isCollectionDeleter, reqScope, admit))\n   988\u2192\t\t\thandler = utilwarning.AddWarningsHandler(handler, warnings)\n   989\u2192\t\t\troute := ws.DELETE(action.Path).To(handler).\n   990\u2192\t\t\t\tDoc(doc).\n   991\u2192\t\t\t\tParam(ws.QueryParameter(\"pretty\", \"If 'true', then the output is pretty printed. Defaults to 'false' unless the user-agent indicates a browser or command-line HTTP tool (curl and wget).\")).\n   992\u2192\t\t\t\tOperation(\"deletecollection\"+namespaced+kind+cases.Title(language.English).String(subresource)+operationSuffix).\n   993\u2192\t\t\t\tProduces(append(storageMeta.ProducesMIMETypes(action.Verb), mediaTypes...)...).\n   994\u2192\t\t\t\tWrites(versionedStatus).\n   995\u2192\t\t\t\tReturns(http.StatusOK, \"OK\", versionedStatus)\n   996\u2192\t\t\tif isCollectionDeleter {\n   997\u2192\t\t\t\troute.Reads(versionedDeleterObject)\n   998\u2192\t\t\t\troute.ParameterNamed(\"body\").Required(false)\n   999\u2192\t\t\t\tif err := AddObjectParams(ws, route, versionedDeleteOptions); err != nil {\n  1000\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n  1001\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n  1002\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1003\u2192\t\t\tif err := AddObjectParams(ws, route, versionedListOptions, \"watch\", \"allowWatchBookmarks\"); err != nil {\n  1004\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n  1005\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1006\u2192\t\t\taddParams(route, action.Params)\n  1007\u2192\t\t\troutes = append(routes, route)\n  1008\u2192\t\t// deprecated in 1.11\n  1009\u2192\t\tcase request.MethodWatch: // Watch a resource.\n  1010\u2192\t\t\tdoc := \"watch changes to an object of kind \" + kind\n  1011\u2192\t\t\tif isSubresource {\n  1012\u2192\t\t\t\tdoc = \"watch changes to \" + subresource + \" of an object of kind \" + kind\n  1013\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1014\u2192\t\t\tdoc += \". deprecated: use the 'watch' parameter with a list operation instead, filtered to a single item with the 'fieldSelector' parameter.\"\n  1015\u2192\t\t\thandler := metrics.InstrumentRouteFunc(action.Verb, group, version, resource, subresource, requestScope, metrics.APIServerComponent, deprecated, removedRelease, restfulListResource(lister, watcher, reqScope, true, a.minRequestTimeout))\n  1016\u2192\t\t\thandler = utilwarning.AddWarningsHandler(handler, warnings)\n  1017\u2192\t\t\troute := ws.GET(action.Path).To(handler).\n  1018\u2192\t\t\t\tDoc(doc).\n  1019\u2192\t\t\t\tParam(ws.QueryParameter(\"pretty\", \"If 'true', then the output is pretty printed. Defaults to 'false' unless the user-agent indicates a browser or command-line HTTP tool (curl and wget).\")).\n  1020\u2192\t\t\t\tOperation(\"watch\"+namespaced+kind+cases.Title(language.English).String(subresource)+operationSuffix).\n  1021\u2192\t\t\t\tProduces(allMediaTypes...).\n  1022\u2192\t\t\t\tReturns(http.StatusOK, \"OK\", versionedWatchEvent).\n  1023\u2192\t\t\t\tWrites(versionedWatchEvent)\n  1024\u2192\t\t\tif err := AddObjectParams(ws, route, versionedListOptions); err != nil {\n  1025\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n  1026\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1027\u2192\t\t\taddParams(route, action.Params)\n  1028\u2192\t\t\troutes = append(routes, route)\n  1029\u2192\t\t// deprecated in 1.11\n  1030\u2192\t\tcase request.MethodWatchList: // Watch all resources of a kind.\n  1031\u2192\t\t\tdoc := \"watch individual changes to a list of \" + kind\n  1032\u2192\t\t\tif isSubresource {\n  1033\u2192\t\t\t\tdoc = \"watch individual changes to a list of \" + subresource + \" of \" + kind\n  1034\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1035\u2192\t\t\tdoc += \". deprecated: use the 'watch' parameter with a list operation instead.\"\n  1036\u2192\t\t\thandler := metrics.InstrumentRouteFunc(action.Verb, group, version, resource, subresource, requestScope, metrics.APIServerComponent, deprecated, removedRelease, restfulListResource(lister, watcher, reqScope, true, a.minRequestTimeout))\n  1037\u2192\t\t\thandler = utilwarning.AddWarningsHandler(handler, warnings)\n  1038\u2192\t\t\troute := ws.GET(action.Path).To(handler).\n  1039\u2192\t\t\t\tDoc(doc).\n  1040\u2192\t\t\t\tParam(ws.QueryParameter(\"pretty\", \"If 'true', then the output is pretty printed. Defaults to 'false' unless the user-agent indicates a browser or command-line HTTP tool (curl and wget).\")).\n  1041\u2192\t\t\t\tOperation(\"watch\"+namespaced+kind+cases.Title(language.English).String(subresource)+\"List\"+operationSuffix).\n  1042\u2192\t\t\t\tProduces(allMediaTypes...).\n  1043\u2192\t\t\t\tReturns(http.StatusOK, \"OK\", versionedWatchEvent).\n  1044\u2192\t\t\t\tWrites(versionedWatchEvent)\n  1045\u2192\t\t\tif err := AddObjectParams(ws, route, versionedListOptions); err != nil {\n  1046\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n  1047\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1048\u2192\t\t\taddParams(route, action.Params)\n  1049\u2192\t\t\troutes = append(routes, route)"

Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    21\u2192\t\"regexp\"\n    22\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    25\u2192)\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192// TODO: use restful.CrossOriginResourceSharing\n    28\u2192// See github.com/emicklei/go-restful/blob/master/examples/cors/restful-CORS-filter.go, and\n    29\u2192// github.com/emicklei/go-restful/blob/master/examples/basicauth/restful-basic-authentication.go\n    30\u2192// Or, for a more detailed implementation use https://github.com/martini-contrib/cors\n    31\u2192// or implement CORS at your proxy layer.\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192// WithCORS is a simple CORS implementation that wraps an http Handler.\n    34\u2192// Pass nil for allowedMethods and allowedHeaders to use the defaults. If allowedOriginPatterns\n    35\u2192// is empty or nil, no CORS support is installed.\n    36\u2192func WithCORS(handler http.Handler, allowedOriginPatterns []string, allowedMethods []string, allowedHeaders []string, exposedHeaders []string, allowCredentials string) http.Handler {\n    37\u2192\tif len(allowedOriginPatterns) == 0 {\n    38\u2192\t\treturn handler\n    39\u2192\t}\n    40\u2192\tallowedOriginPatternsREs := allowedOriginRegexps(allowedOriginPatterns)\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192\t// Set defaults for methods and headers if nothing was passed\n    43\u2192\tif allowedMethods == nil {\n    44\u2192\t\tallowedMethods = []string{\"POST\", \"GET\", \"OPTIONS\", \"PUT\", \"DELETE\", \"PATCH\"}\n    45\u2192\t}\n    46\u2192\tallowMethodsResponseHeader := strings.Join(allowedMethods, \", \")\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192\tif allowedHeaders == nil {\n    49\u2192\t\tallowedHeaders = []string{\"Content-Type\", \"Content-Length\", \"Accept-Encoding\", \"X-CSRF-Token\", \"Authorization\", \"X-Requested-With\", \"If-Modified-Since\"}\n    50\u2192\t}\n    51\u2192\tallowHeadersResponseHeader := strings.Join(allowedHeaders, \", \")\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\tif exposedHeaders == nil {\n    54\u2192\t\texposedHeaders = []string{\"Date\"}\n    55\u2192\t}\n    56\u2192\texposeHeadersResponseHeader := strings.Join(exposedHeaders, \", \")\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n    59\u2192\t\torigin := req.Header.Get(\"Origin\")\n    60\u2192\t\tif origin == \"\" {"
92. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/goaway.go",
  "limit": 60
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2020 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"math/rand\"\n    21\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    22\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    23\u2192)\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192// GoawayDecider decides if server should send a GOAWAY\n    26\u2192type GoawayDecider interface {\n    27\u2192\tGoaway(r *http.Request) bool\n    28\u2192}\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192var (\n    31\u2192\t// randPool used to get a rand.Rand and generate a random number thread-safely,\n    32\u2192\t// which improve the performance of using rand.Rand with a locker\n    33\u2192\trandPool = &sync.Pool{\n    34\u2192\t\tNew: func() interface{} {\n    35\u2192\t\t\treturn rand.New(rand.NewSource(rand.Int63()))\n    36\u2192\t\t},\n    37\u2192\t}\n    38\u2192)\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192// WithProbabilisticGoaway returns an http.Handler that send GOAWAY probabilistically\n    41\u2192// according to the given chance for HTTP2 requests. After client receive GOAWAY,\n    42\u2192// the in-flight long-running requests will not be influenced, and the new requests\n    43\u2192// will use a new TCP connection to re-balancing to another server behind the load balance.\n    44\u2192func WithProbabilisticGoaway(inner http.Handler, chance float64) http.Handler {\n    45\u2192\treturn &goaway{\n    46\u2192\t\thandler: inner,\n    47\u2192\t\tdecider: &probabilisticGoawayDecider{\n    48\u2192\t\t\tchance: chance,\n    49\u2192\t\t\tnext: func() float64 {\n    50\u2192\t\t\t\trnd := randPool.Get().(*rand.Rand)\n    51\u2192\t\t\t\tret := rnd.Float64()\n    52\u2192\t\t\t\trandPool.Put(rnd)\n    53\u2192\t\t\t\treturn ret\n    54\u2192\t\t\t},\n    55\u2192\t\t},\n    56\u2192\t}\n    57\u2192}\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192// goaway send a GOAWAY to client according to decider for HTTP2 requests\n    60\u2192type goaway struct {"

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/content_type.go",
  "limit": 60
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import \"net/http\"\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192// WithContentType sets both the Content-Type and the X-Content-Type-Options (nosniff) header\n    22\u2192func WithContentType(handler http.Handler, contentType string) http.Handler {\n    23\u2192\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    24\u2192\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", contentType)\n    25\u2192\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Content-Type-Options\", \"nosniff\")\n    26\u2192\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n    27\u2192\t})\n    28\u2192}\n    29\u2192"
94. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/watch_termination.go",
  "limit": 80
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2023 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    21\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n    24\u2192\tapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    26\u2192)\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192func WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown(handler http.Handler, termination apirequest.ServerShutdownSignal, wg RequestWaitGroup) http.Handler {\n    29\u2192\tif termination == nil || wg == nil {\n    30\u2192\t\tklog.Warningf(\"watch termination during shutdown not attached to the handler chain\")\n    31\u2192\t\treturn handler\n    32\u2192\t}\n    33\u2192\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n    34\u2192\t\tctx := req.Context()\n    35\u2192\t\trequestInfo, ok := apirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n    36\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n    37\u2192\t\t\t// if this happens, the handler chain isn't setup correctly because there is no request info\n    38\u2192\t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New(\"no RequestInfo found in the context\"))\n    39\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    40\u2192\t\t}\n    41\u2192\t\tif !watchVerbs.Has(requestInfo.Verb) {\n    42\u2192\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    43\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    44\u2192\t\t}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\t\tif err := wg.Add(1); err != nil {\n    47\u2192\t\t\t// When apiserver is shutting down, signal clients to retry\n    48\u2192\t\t\t// There is a good chance the client hit a different server, so a tight retry is good for client responsiveness.\n    49\u2192\t\t\twaitGroupWriteRetryAfterToResponse(w)\n    50\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    51\u2192\t\t}\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\t\t// attach ServerShutdownSignal to the watch request so that the\n    54\u2192\t\t// watch handler loop can return as soon as the server signals\n    55\u2192\t\t// that it is shutting down.\n    56\u2192\t\tctx = apirequest.WithServerShutdownSignal(req.Context(), termination)\n    57\u2192\t\treq = req.WithContext(ctx)\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n    60\u2192\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    61\u2192\t})\n    62\u2192}\n    63\u2192"

180\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(errors.NewBadRequest(\"dryRun is not supported\"), w, req)\n   181\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   182\u2192\t\t}\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192\t\tnamespace, name, err := 194\u2192\t\tif err := getRequestOptions(req, scope, opts, subpath, subpathKey, isSubresource); err != nil {\n   195\u2192\t\t\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\n   196\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   197\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   198\u2192\t\t}\n   199\u2192\t\tif admit != nil && admit.Handles(admission.Connect) {\n   200\u2192\t\t\tuserInfo, _ := request.UserFrom(ctx)\n   201\u2192\t\t\t// TODO: remove the mutating admission here as soon as we have ported all plugin that handle CONNECT\n   202\u2192\t\t\tif mutatingAdmission, ok := admit.(admission.MutationInterface); ok {\n   203\u2192\t\t\t\terr = mutatingAdmission.Admit(ctx, admission.NewAttributesRecord(opts, nil, scope.Kind, namespace, name, scope.Resource, scope.Subresource, admission.Connect, nil, false, userInfo), scope)\n   204\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   205\u2192\t\t\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   206\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn\n admission.NewAttributesRecord(opts, nil, scope.Kind, namespace, name, scope.Resource, scope.Subresource, admission.Connect, nil, false, userInfo), scope)\n   211\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   212\u2192\t\t\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   213\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn\n   214\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   215\u2192\t\t\t}\n   216\u2192\t\t}\n   217\u2192\t\trequestInfo, _ := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n   218\u2192\t\tmetrics.RecordLongRunning(req, requestInfo, metrics.APIServerComponent, func() {\n   219\u2192\t\t\thandler, err := connecter.Connect(ctx, name, opts, &responder{scope: scope, req: req, w: w})\n   220\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   221\u2192\t\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   222\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   223\u2192\t\t\t}\n   224\u2192\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n   225\u2192\t\t})\n   226\u2192\t}\n   227\u2192}\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192// responder implements rest.Responder for assisting a connector in writing objects or errors.\n   230\u2192type responder struct {\n   231\u2192\tscope *RequestScope\n   232\u2192\treq   *http.Request\n   233\u2192\tw     http.ResponseWriter\n   234\u2192}\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192func (r *responder) Object(statusCode int, obj runtime.Object) {\n   237\u2192\tresponsewriters.WriteObjectNegotiated(r.scope.Serializer, r.scope, r.scope.Kind.GroupVersion(), r.w, r.req, statusCode, obj, false)\n   238\u2192}\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192func (r *responder) Error(err error) {\n   241\u2192\tr.scope.err(err, r.w, r.req)\n   242\u2192}\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192// transformDecodeError adds additional information into a bad-request api error when a decode fails.\n   245\u2192func transformDecodeError(typer runtime.ObjectTyper, baseErr error, into runtime.Object, gvk *schema.GroupVersionKind, body []byte) error {\n   246\u2192\tobjGVKs, _, err := typer.ObjectKinds(into)\n   247\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   248\u2192\t\treturn errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\n   249\u2192\t}\n   250\u2192\tobjGVK := objGVKs[0]\n   251\u2192\tif gvk != nil && len(gvk.Kind) > 0 {\n   252\u2192\t\treturn errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s in version %q cannot be handled as a %s: %v\", gvk.Kind, gvk.Version, objGVK.Kind, baseErr))\n   253\u2192\t}\n   254\u2192\tsummary := summarizeData(body, 30)\n   255\u2192\treturn errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\"the object provided is unrecognized (must be of type %s): %v (%s)\", objGVK.Kind, baseErr, summary))\n   256\u2192}\n   257\u2192\n   258\u2192func hasUID(obj runtime.Object) (bool, error) {\n   259\u2192\tif obj == nil {\n   260\u2192\t\treturn false, nil\n   261\u2192\t}\n   262\u2192\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\n   263\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   264\u2192\t\treturn false, errors.NewInternalError(err)\n   265\u2192\t}\n   266\u2192\tif len(accessor.GetUID()) == 0 {\n   267\u2192\t\treturn false, nil\n   268\u2192\t}\n   269\u2192\treturn true, nil\n   270\u2192}\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192// checkName checks the provided name against the request\n   273\u2192func checkName(obj runtime.Object, name, namespace string, namer ScopeNamer) error {\n   274\u2192\tobjNamespace, objName, err := namer.ObjectName(obj)\n   275\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   276\u2192\t\treturn errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\n   277\u2192\t\t\t\"the name of the object (%s based on URL) was undeterminable: %v\", name, err))\n   278\u2192\t}\n   279\u2192\tif objName != name {\n   280\u2192\t\treturn errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\n   281\u2192\t\t\t\"the name of the object (%s) does not match the name on the URL (%s)\", objName, name))\n   282\u2192\t}\n   283\u2192\tif len(namespace) > 0 {\n   284\u2192\t\tif len(objNamespace) > 0 && objNamespace != namespace {\n   285\u2192\t\t\treturn errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\n   286\u2192\t\t\t\t\"the namespace of the object (%s) does not match the namespace on the request (%s)\", objNamespace, namespace))\n   287\u2192\t\t}\n   288\u2192\t}\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192\treturn nil\n   291\u2192}\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192// dedupOwnerReferences dedups owner references over the entire entry.\n   294\u2192// NOTE: We don't know enough about the existing cases of owner references\n   295\u2192// sharing the same UID but different fields. Nor do we know what might break.\n   296\u2192// In the future we may just dedup/reject owner references with the same UID.\n   297\u2192func dedupOwnerReferences(refs []metav1.OwnerReference) ([]metav1.OwnerReference, []string) {\n   298\u2192\tvar result []metav1.OwnerReference\n   299\u2192\tvar duplicates []string\n   300\u2192\tseen := make(map[types.UID]struct{})\n   301\u2192\tfor _, ref := range refs {\n   302\u2192\t\t_, ok := seen[ref.UID]\n   303\u2192\t\t// Short-circuit if we haven't seen the UID before. Otherwise\n   304\u2192\t\t// check the entire list we have so far.\n   305\u2192\t\tif !ok || !hasOwnerReference(result, ref) {\n   306\u2192\t\t\tseen[ref.UID] = struct{}{}\n   307\u2192\t\t\tresult = append(result, ref)\n   308\u2192\t\t} else {\n   309\u2192\t\t\tduplicates = append(duplicates, string(ref.UID))\n   310\u2192\t\t}\n   311\u2192\t}\n   312\u2192\treturn result, duplicates\n   313\u2192}\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192// hasOwnerReference returns true if refs has an item equal to ref. The function\n   316\u2192// focuses on semantic equality instead of memory equality, to catch duplicates\n   317\u2192// with different pointer addresses. The function uses apiequality.Semantic\n   318\u2192// instead of implementing its own comparison, to tolerate API changes to\n   319\u2192// metav1.OwnerReference.\n   320\u2192// NOTE: This is expensive, but we accept it because we've made sure it only\n   321\u2192// happens to owner references containing duplicate UIDs, plus typically the\n   322\u2192// number of items in the list should be small.\n   323\u2192func hasOwnerReference(refs []metav1.OwnerReference, ref metav1.OwnerReference) bool {\n   324\u2192\tfor _, r := range refs {\n   325\u2192\t\tif apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual(r, ref) {\n   326\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n   327\u2192\t\t}\n   328\u2192\t}\n   329\u2192\treturn false\n   330\u2192}\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192// dedupOwnerReferencesAndAddWarning dedups owner references in the object metadata.\n   333\u2192// If duplicates are found, the function records a warning to the provided context.\n   334\u2192func dedupOwnerReferencesAndAddWarning(obj runtime.Object, requestContext context.Context, afterMutatingAdmission bool) {\n   335\u2192\taccessor, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\n   336\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   337\u2192\t\t// The object doesn't have metadata. Nothing we need to do here.\n   338\u2192\t\treturn\n   339\u2192\t}\n   340\u2192\trefs := accessor.GetOwnerReferences()\n   341\u2192\tdeduped, duplicates := dedupOwnerReferences(refs)\n   342\u2192\tif len(duplicates) > 0 {\n   343\u2192\t\t// NOTE: For CREATE and UPDATE requests the API server dedups both before and after mutating admission.\n   344\u2192\t\t// For PATCH request the API server only dedups after mutating admission.\n   345\u2192\t\tformat := DuplicateOwnerReferencesWarningFormat\n   346\u2192\t\tif afterMutatingAdmission {\n   347\u2192\t\t\tformat = DuplicateOwnerReferencesAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat\n   348\u2192\t\t}\n   349\u2192\t\twarning.AddWarning(requestContext, \"\", fmt.Sprintf(format,"

utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.WatchList)); len(errs) > 0 {\n   202\u2192\t\t\terr := errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{Group: metav1.GroupName, Kind: \"ListOptions\"}, \"\", errs)\n   203\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   204\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   205\u2192\t\t}\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192\t\toutputMediaType, _, err := negotiation.NegotiateOutputMediaType(req, scope.Serializer, scope)\n   208\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   209\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   210\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   211\u2192\t\t}\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192\t\t// transform fields\n   214\u2192\t\t// TODO: DecodeParametersInto should do this.\n   215\u2192\t\tif opts.FieldSelector != nil {\n   216\u2192\t\t\tfn := func(label, value string) (newLabel, newValue string, err error) {\n   217\u2192\t\t\t\treturn scope.Convertor.ConvertFieldLabel(scope.Kind, label, value)\n   218\u2192\t\t\t}\n   219\u2192\t\t\tif opts.FieldSelector, err = opts.FieldSelector.Transform(fn); err != nil {\n   220\u2192\t\t\t\t// TODO: allow bad request to set field causes based on query parameters\n   221\u2192\t\t\t\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\n   222\u2192\t\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   223\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   224\u2192\t\t\t}\n   225\u2192\t\t}\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192\t\tif hasName {\n   228\u2192\t\t\t// metadata.name is the canonical internal name.\n   229\u2192\t\t\t// SelectionPredicate will notice that this is a request for\n   230\u2192\t\t\t// a single object and optimize the storage query accordingly.\n   231\u2192\t\t\tnameSelector := fields.OneTermEqualSelector(\"metadata.name\", name)\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192\t\t\t// Note that fieldSelector setting explicitly the \"metadata.name\"\n   234\u2192\t\t\t// will result in reaching this branch (as the value of that field\n   235\u2192\t\t\t// is propagated to requestInfo as the name parameter.\n   236\u2192\t\t\t// That said, the allowed field selectors in this branch are:\n   237\u2192\t\t\t// nil, fields.Everything and field selector matching metadata.name\n   238\u2192\t\t\t// for our name.\n   239\u2192\t\t\tif opts.FieldSelector != nil && !opts.FieldSelector.Empty() {\n   240\u2192\t\t\t\tselectedName, ok := opts.FieldSelector.RequiresExactMatch(\"metadata.name\")\n   241\u2192\t\t\t\tif !ok || name != selectedName {\n   242\u2192\t\t\t\t\tscope.err(errors.NewBadRequest(\"fieldSelector metadata.name doesn't match requested name\"), w, req)\n   243\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn\n   244\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   245\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   246\u2192\t\t\t\topts.FieldSelector = nameSelector\n   247\u2192\t\t\t}\n   248\u2192\t\t}\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192\t\tif opts.Watch || forceWatch {\n   251\u2192\t\t\tif rw == nil {\n   252\u2192\t\t\t\tscope.err(errors.NewMethodNotSupported(scope.Resource.GroupResource(), \"watch\"), w, req)\n   253\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   254\u2192\t\t\t}\n   255\u2192\t\t\t// TODO: Currently we explicitly ignore ?timeout= and use only ?timeoutSeconds=.\n   256\u2192\t\t\ttimeout := time.Duration(0)\n   257\u2192\t\t\tif opts.TimeoutSeconds != nil {\n   258\u2192\t\t\t\ttimeout = time.Duration(*opts.TimeoutSeconds) * time.Second\n   259\u2192\t\t\t}\n   260\u2192\t\t\tif timeout == 0 && minRequestTimeout > 0 {\n   261\u2192\t\t\t\ttimeout = time.Duration(float64(minRequestTimeout) * (rand.Float64() + 1.0))\n   262\u2192\t\t\t}\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192\t\t\tklog.V(3).InfoS(\"Starting watch\", \"path\", req.URL.Path, \"resourceVersion\", opts.ResourceVersion, \"labels\", opts.LabelSelector, \"fields\", opts.FieldSelector, \"sendInitialEvents\", opts.SendInitialEvents, \"timeout\", timeout, \"audit-ID\", audit.GetAuditIDTruncated(ctx))\n   265\u2192\t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)\n   266\u2192\t\t\tdefer func() { cancel() }()\n   267\u2192\t\t\twatcher, err := rw.Watch(ctx, &opts)\n   268\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   269\u2192\t\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   270\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   271\u2192\t\t\t}\n   272\u2192\t\t\thandler, err := serveWatchHandler(watcher, scope, outputMediaType, req, w, timeout, metrics.CleanListScope(ctx, &opts))\n   273\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   274\u2192\t\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   275\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   276\u2192\t\t\t}\n   277\u2192\t\t\t// Invalidate cancel() to defer until serve() is complete.\n   278\u2192\t\t\tdeferredCancel := cancel\n   279\u2192\t\t\tcancel = func() {}\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192\t\t\tserve := func() {\n   282\u2192\t\t\t\tdefer deferredCancel()\n   283\u2192\t\t\t\trequestInfo, _ := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n   284\u2192\t\t\t\tmetrics.RecordLongRunning(req, requestInfo, metrics.APIServerComponent, func() {\n   285\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdefer watcher.Stop()\n   286\u2192\t\t\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n   287\u2192\t\t\t\t})\n   288\u2192\t\t\t}\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192\t\t\t// Run watch serving in a separate goroutine to allow freeing current stack memory\n   291\u2192\t\t\tt := routine.TaskFrom(req.Context())\n   292\u2192\t\t\tif t != nil {\n   293\u2192\t\t\t\tt.Func = serve\n   294\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   295\u2192\t\t\t\tserve()\n   296\u2192\t\t\t}\n   297\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   298\u2192\t\t}\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192\t\t// Log only long List requests (ignore Watch).\n   301\u2192\t\tdefer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)\n   302\u2192\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"About to List from storage\")\n   303\u2192\t\tresult, err := r.List(ctx, &opts)\n   304\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   305\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   306\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   307\u2192\t\t}\n   308\u2192\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"Listing from storage done\")\n   309\u2192\t\tdefer span.AddEvent(\"Writing http response done\", attribute.Int(\"count\", meta.LenList(result)))\n   310\u2192\t\ttransformResponseObject(ctx, scope, req, w, http.StatusOK, outputMediaType, result)\n   311\u2192\t}\n   312\u2192}\n   313\u2192"
100. Read @ -
