{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/watch.go",
  "limit": 250,
  "offset": 68
}
Output
"    68\u2192// serveWatchHandler returns a handle to serve a watch response.\n    69\u2192// TODO: the functionality in this method and in WatchServer.Serve is not cleanly decoupled.\n    70\u2192func serveWatchHandler(watcher watch.Interface, scope *RequestScope, mediaTypeOptions negotiation.MediaTypeOptions, req *http.Request, w http.ResponseWriter, timeout time.Duration, metricsScope string) (http.Handler, error) {\n    71\u2192\toptions, err := optionsForTransform(mediaTypeOptions, req)\n    72\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    73\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n    74\u2192\t}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192\t// negotiate for the stream serializer from the scope's serializer\n    77\u2192\tserializer, err := negotiation.NegotiateOutputMediaTypeStream(req, scope.Serializer, scope)\n    78\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    79\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n    80\u2192\t}\n    81\u2192\tframer := serializer.StreamSerializer.Framer\n    82\u2192\tvar encoder runtime.Encoder\n    83\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\n    84\u2192\t\tencoder = scope.Serializer.EncoderForVersion(runtime.UseNondeterministicEncoding(serializer.StreamSerializer.Serializer), scope.Kind.GroupVersion())\n    85\u2192\t} else {\n    86\u2192\t\tencoder = scope.Serializer.EncoderForVersion(serializer.StreamSerializer.Serializer, scope.Kind.GroupVersion())\n    87\u2192\t}\n    88\u2192\tuseTextFraming := serializer.EncodesAsText\n    89\u2192\tif framer == nil {\n    90\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"no framer defined for %q available for embedded encoding\", serializer.MediaType)\n    91\u2192\t}\n    92\u2192\t// TODO: next step, get back mediaTypeOptions from negotiate and return the exact value here\n    93\u2192\tmediaType := serializer.MediaType\n    94\u2192\tswitch mediaType {\n    95\u2192\tcase runtime.ContentTypeJSON:\n    96\u2192\t\t// as-is\n    97\u2192\tcase runtime.ContentTypeCBOR:\n    98\u2192\t\t// If a client indicated it accepts application/cbor (exactly one data item) on a\n    99\u2192\t\t// watch request, set the conformant application/cbor-seq media type the watch\n   100\u2192\t\t// response. RFC 9110 allows an origin server to deviate from the indicated\n   101\u2192\t\t// preference rather than send a 406 (Not Acceptable) response (see\n   102\u2192\t\t// https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#section-12.1-5).\n   103\u2192\t\tmediaType = runtime.ContentTypeCBORSequence\n   104\u2192\tdefault:\n   105\u2192\t\tmediaType += \";stream=watch\"\n   106\u2192\t}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192\tctx := req.Context()\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\t// locate the appropriate embedded encoder based on the transform\n   111\u2192\tvar negotiatedEncoder runtime.Encoder\n   112\u2192\tcontentKind, contentSerializer, transform := targetEncodingForTransform(scope, mediaTypeOptions, req)\n   113\u2192\tif transform {\n   114\u2192\t\tinfo, ok := runtime.SerializerInfoForMediaType(contentSerializer.SupportedMediaTypes(), serializer.MediaType)\n   115\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   116\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"no encoder for %q exists in the requested target %#v\", serializer.MediaType, contentSerializer)\n   117\u2192\t\t}\n   118\u2192\t\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\n   119\u2192\t\t\tnegotiatedEncoder = contentSerializer.EncoderForVersion(runtime.UseNondeterministicEncoding(info.Serializer), contentKind.GroupVersion())\n   120\u2192\t\t} else {\n   121\u2192\t\t\tnegotiatedEncoder = contentSerializer.EncoderForVersion(info.Serializer, contentKind.GroupVersion())\n   122\u2192\t\t}\n   123\u2192\t} else {\n   124\u2192\t\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.CBORServingAndStorage) {\n   125\u2192\t\t\tnegotiatedEncoder = scope.Serializer.EncoderForVersion(runtime.UseNondeterministicEncoding(serializer.Serializer), contentKind.GroupVersion())\n   126\u2192\t\t} else {\n   127\u2192\t\t\tnegotiatedEncoder = scope.Serializer.EncoderForVersion(serializer.Serializer, contentKind.GroupVersion())\n   128\u2192\t\t}\n   129\u2192\t}\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192\tvar memoryAllocator runtime.MemoryAllocator\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192\tif encoderWithAllocator, supportsAllocator := negotiatedEncoder.(runtime.EncoderWithAllocator); supportsAllocator {\n   134\u2192\t\t// don't put the allocator inside the embeddedEncodeFn as that would allocate memory on every call.\n   135\u2192\t\t// instead, we allocate the buffer for the entire watch session and release it when we close the connection.\n   136\u2192\t\tmemoryAllocator = runtime.AllocatorPool.Get().(*runtime.Allocator)\n   137\u2192\t\tnegotiatedEncoder = runtime.NewEncoderWithAllocator(encoderWithAllocator, memoryAllocator)\n   138\u2192\t}\n   139\u2192\tvar tableOptions *metav1.TableOptions\n   140\u2192\tif options != nil {\n   141\u2192\t\tif passedOptions, ok := options.(*metav1.TableOptions); ok {\n   142\u2192\t\t\ttableOptions = passedOptions\n   143\u2192\t\t} else {\n   144\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unexpected options type: %T\", options)\n   145\u2192\t\t}\n   146\u2192\t}\n   147\u2192\tembeddedEncoder := newWatchEmbeddedEncoder(ctx, negotiatedEncoder, mediaTypeOptions.Convert, tableOptions, scope)\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192\tif encoderWithAllocator, supportsAllocator := encoder.(runtime.EncoderWithAllocator); supportsAllocator {\n   150\u2192\t\tif memoryAllocator == nil {\n   151\u2192\t\t\t// don't put the allocator inside the embeddedEncodeFn as that would allocate memory on every call.\n   152\u2192\t\t\t// instead, we allocate the buffer for the entire watch session and release it when we close the connection.\n   153\u2192\t\t\tmemoryAllocator = runtime.AllocatorPool.Get().(*runtime.Allocator)\n   154\u2192\t\t}\n   155\u2192\t\tencoder = runtime.NewEncoderWithAllocator(encoderWithAllocator, memoryAllocator)\n   156\u2192\t}\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192\tvar serverShuttingDownCh <-chan struct{}\n   159\u2192\tif signals := apirequest.ServerShutdownSignalFrom(req.Context()); signals != nil {\n   160\u2192\t\tserverShuttingDownCh = signals.ShuttingDown()\n   161\u2192\t}\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192\tserver := &WatchServer{\n   164\u2192\t\tWatching: watcher,\n   165\u2192\t\tScope:    scope,\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192\t\tUseTextFraming:  useTextFraming,\n   168\u2192\t\tMediaType:       mediaType,\n   169\u2192\t\tFramer:          framer,\n   170\u2192\t\tEncoder:         encoder,\n   171\u2192\t\tEmbeddedEncoder: embeddedEncoder,\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192\t\tMemoryAllocator:      memoryAllocator,\n   174\u2192\t\tTimeoutFactory:       &realTimeoutFactory{timeout},\n   175\u2192\t\tServerShuttingDownCh: serverShuttingDownCh,\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192\t\tmetricsScope: metricsScope,\n   178\u2192\t}\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192\tif wsstream.IsWebSocketRequest(req) {\n   181\u2192\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", server.MediaType)\n   182\u2192\t\treturn websocket.Handler(server.HandleWS), nil\n   183\u2192\t}\n   184\u2192\treturn http.HandlerFunc(server.HandleHTTP), nil\n   185\u2192}\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192// WatchServer serves a watch.Interface over a websocket or vanilla HTTP.\n   188\u2192type WatchServer struct {\n   189\u2192\tWatching watch.Interface\n   190\u2192\tScope    *RequestScope\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192\t// true if websocket messages should use text framing (as opposed to binary framing)\n   193\u2192\tUseTextFraming bool\n   194\u2192\t// the media type this watch is being served with\n   195\u2192\tMediaType string\n   196\u2192\t// used to frame the watch stream\n   197\u2192\tFramer runtime.Framer\n   198\u2192\t// used to encode the watch stream event itself\n   199\u2192\tEncoder runtime.Encoder\n   200\u2192\t// used to encode the nested object in the watch stream\n   201\u2192\tEmbeddedEncoder runtime.Encoder\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192\tMemoryAllocator      runtime.MemoryAllocator\n   204\u2192\tTimeoutFactory       TimeoutFactory\n   205\u2192\tServerShuttingDownCh <-chan struct{}\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192\tmetricsScope string\n   208\u2192}\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192// watchEventMetricsRecorder allows the caller to count bytes written and report the size of the event.\n   211\u2192// It is thread-safe, as long as underlying io.Writer is thread-safe.\n   212\u2192// Once all Write calls for a given watch event have finished, RecordEvent must be called.\n   213\u2192type watchEventMetricsRecorder struct {\n   214\u2192\twriter      io.Writer\n   215\u2192\tcountMetric compbasemetrics.CounterMetric\n   216\u2192\tsizeMetric  compbasemetrics.ObserverMetric\n   217\u2192\tbyteCount   atomic.Int64\n   218\u2192}\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192// Write implements io.Writer.\n   221\u2192func (c *watchEventMetricsRecorder) Write(p []byte) (n int, err error) {\n   222\u2192\tn, err = c.writer.Write(p)\n   223\u2192\tc.byteCount.Add(int64(n))\n   224\u2192\treturn\n   225\u2192}\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192// Record reports the metrics and resets the byte count.\n   228\u2192func (c *watchEventMetricsRecorder) RecordEvent() {\n   229\u2192\tc.countMetric.Inc()\n   230\u2192\tc.sizeMetric.Observe(float64(c.byteCount.Swap(0)))\n   231\u2192}\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192// HandleHTTP serves a series of encoded events via HTTP with Transfer-Encoding: chunked.\n   234\u2192// or over a websocket connection.\n   235\u2192func (s *WatchServer) HandleHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n   236\u2192\tdefer func() {\n   237\u2192\t\tif s.MemoryAllocator != nil {\n   238\u2192\t\t\truntime.AllocatorPool.Put(s.MemoryAllocator)\n   239\u2192\t\t}\n   240\u2192\t}()\n   241\u2192\n   242\u2192\tflusher, ok := w.(http.Flusher)\n   243\u2192\tif !ok {\n   244\u2192\t\terr := fmt.Errorf(\"unable to start watch - can't get http.Flusher: %#v\", w)\n   245\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(err)\n   246\u2192\t\ts.Scope.err(errors.NewInternalError(err), w, req)\n   247\u2192\t\treturn\n   248\u2192\t}\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192\tframer := s.Framer.NewFrameWriter(w)\n   251\u2192\tif framer == nil {\n   252\u2192\t\t// programmer error\n   253\u2192\t\terr := fmt.Errorf(\"no stream framing support is available for media type %q\", s.MediaType)\n   254\u2192\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(err)\n   255\u2192\t\ts.Scope.err(errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error()), w, req)\n   256\u2192\t\treturn\n   257\u2192\t}\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192\t// ensure the connection times out\n   260\u2192\ttimeoutCh, cleanup := s.TimeoutFactory.TimeoutCh()\n   261\u2192\tdefer cleanup()\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192\t// begin the stream\n   264\u2192\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", s.MediaType)\n   265\u2192\tw.Header().Set(\"Transfer-Encoding\", \"chunked\")\n   266\u2192\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n   267\u2192\tflusher.Flush()\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192\tgvr := s.Scope.Resource\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192\trecorder := &watchEventMetricsRecorder{\n   272\u2192\t\twriter:      framer,\n   273\u2192\t\tcountMetric: metrics.WatchEvents.WithContext(req.Context()).WithLabelValues(gvr.Group, gvr.Version, gvr.Resource),\n   274\u2192\t\tsizeMetric:  metrics.WatchEventsSizes.WithContext(req.Context()).WithLabelValues(gvr.Group, gvr.Version, gvr.Resource),\n   275\u2192\t}\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192\twatchEncoder := newWatchEncoder(req.Context(), gvr, s.EmbeddedEncoder, s.Encoder, recorder)\n   278\u2192\tch := s.Watching.ResultChan()\n   279\u2192\tdone := req.Context().Done()\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192\tfor {\n   282\u2192\t\tselect {\n   283\u2192\t\tcase <-s.ServerShuttingDownCh:\n   284\u2192\t\t\t// the server has signaled that it is shutting down (not accepting\n   285\u2192\t\t\t// any new request), all active watch request(s) should return\n   286\u2192\t\t\t// immediately here. The WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown server\n   287\u2192\t\t\t// filter will ensure that the response to the client is rate\n   288\u2192\t\t\t// limited in order to avoid any thundering herd issue when the\n   289\u2192\t\t\t// client(s) try to reestablish the WATCH on the other\n   290\u2192\t\t\t// available apiserver instance(s).\n   291\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   292\u2192\t\tcase <-done:\n   293\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   294\u2192\t\tcase <-timeoutCh:\n   295\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   296\u2192\t\tcase event, ok := <-ch:\n   297\u2192\t\t\tif !ok {\n   298\u2192\t\t\t\t// End of results.\n   299\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   300\u2192\t\t\t}\n   301\u2192\t\t\tisWatchListLatencyRecordingRequired := shouldRecordWatchListLatency(event)\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192\t\t\tif err := watchEncoder.Encode(event); err != nil {\n   304\u2192\t\t\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(err)\n   305\u2192\t\t\t\t// client disconnect.\n   306\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   307\u2192\t\t\t}\n   308\u2192\t\t\trecorder.RecordEvent()\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192\t\t\tif len(ch) == 0 {\n   311\u2192\t\t\t\tflusher.Flush()\n   312\u2192\t\t\t}\n   313\u2192\t\t\tif isWatchListLatencyRecordingRequired {\n   314\u2192\t\t\t\t// Record completion of initial listing phase for WatchList\n   315\u2192\t\t\t\treceivedTimestamp, ok := apirequest.ReceivedTimestampFrom(req.Context())\n   316\u2192\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n   317\u2192\t\t\t\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"unable to measure watchlist latency because no received ts found in the ctx, gvr: %s\", s.Scope.Resource))"
101. Read @ -

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cache_watcher.go",
  "limit": 100
}

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2023 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package cacher\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    23\u2192\t\"time\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    27\u2192\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/metrics\"\n    31\u2192\tutilflowcontrol \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol\"\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    34\u2192)\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192// possible states of the cache watcher\n    37\u2192const (\n    38\u2192\t// cacheWatcherWaitingForBookmark indicates the cacher\n    39\u2192\t// is waiting for a bookmark event with a specific RV set\n    40\u2192\tcacheWatcherWaitingForBookmark = iota\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192\t// cacheWatcherBookmarkReceived indicates that the cacher\n    43\u2192\t// has received a bookmark event with required RV\n    44\u2192\tcacheWatcherBookmarkReceived\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\t// cacheWatcherBookmarkSent indicates that the cacher\n    47\u2192\t// has already sent a bookmark event to a client\n    48\u2192\tcacheWatcherBookmarkSent\n    49\u2192)\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192// cacheWatcher implements watch.Interface\n    52\u2192// this is not thread-safe\n    53\u2192type cacheWatcher struct {\n    54\u2192\tinput     chan *watchCacheEvent\n    55\u2192\tresult    chan watch.Event\n    56\u2192\tdone      chan struct{}\n    57\u2192\tfilter    filterWithAttrsFunc\n    58\u2192\tstopped   bool\n    59\u2192\tforget    func(bool)\n    60\u2192\tversioner storage.Versioner\n    61\u2192\t// The watcher will be closed by server after the deadline,\n    62\u2192\t// save it here to send bookmark events before that.\n    63\u2192\tdeadline            time.Time\n    64\u2192\tallowWatchBookmarks bool\n    65\u2192\tgroupResource       schema.GroupResource\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\t// human readable identifier that helps assigning cacheWatcher\n    68\u2192\t// instance with request\n    69\u2192\tidentifier string\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192\t// drainInputBuffer indicates whether we should delay closing this watcher\n    72\u2192\t// and send all event in the input buffer.\n    73\u2192\tdrainInputBuffer bool\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192\t// bookmarkAfterResourceVersion holds an RV that indicates\n    76\u2192\t// when we should start delivering bookmark events.\n    77\u2192\t// If this field holds the value of 0 that means\n    78\u2192\t// we don't have any special preferences toward delivering bookmark events.\n    79\u2192\t// Note that this field is used in conjunction with the state field.\n    80\u2192\t// It should not be changed once the watcher has been started.\n    81\u2192\tbookmarkAfterResourceVersion uint64\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192\t// stateMutex protects state\n    84\u2192\tstateMutex sync.Mutex\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192\t// state holds a numeric value indicating the current state of the watcher\n    87\u2192\tstate int\n    88\u2192}\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192func newCacheWatcher(\n    91\u2192\tchanSize int,\n    92\u2192\tfilter filterWithAttrsFunc,\n    93\u2192\tforget func(bool),\n    94\u2192\tversioner storage.Versioner,\n    95\u2192\tdeadline time.Time,\n    96\u2192\tallowWatchBookmarks bool,\n    97\u2192\tgroupResource schema.GroupResource,\n    98\u2192\tidentifier string,\n    99\u2192) *cacheWatcher {\n   100\u2192\treturn &cacheWatcher{"

  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/wrap.go",

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/httplog\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    28\u2192)\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192// WithPanicRecovery wraps an http Handler to recover and log panics (except in the special case of http.ErrAbortHandler panics, which suppress logging).\n    31\u2192func WithPanicRecovery(handler http.Handler, resolver request.RequestInfoResolver) http.Handler {\n    32\u2192\treturn withPanicRecovery(handler, func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request, err interface{}) {\n    33\u2192\t\tif err == http.ErrAbortHandler {\n    34\u2192\t\t\t// Honor the http.ErrAbortHandler sentinel panic value\n    35\u2192\t\t\t//\n    36\u2192\t\t\t// If ServeHTTP panics, the server (the caller of ServeHTTP) assumes\n    37\u2192\t\t\t// that the effect of the panic was isolated to the active request.\n    38\u2192\t\t\t// It recovers the panic, logs a stack trace to the server error log,\n    39\u2192\t\t\t// and either closes the network connection or sends an HTTP/2\n    40\u2192\t\t\t// RST_STREAM, depending on the HTTP protocol. To abort a handler so\n    41\u2192\t\t\t// the client sees an interrupted response but the server doesn't log\n    42\u2192\t\t\t// an error, panic with the value ErrAbortHandler.\n    43\u2192\t\t\t//\n    44\u2192\t\t\t// Note that HandleCrash function is actually crashing, after calling the handlers\n    45\u2192\t\t\tif info, err := resolver.NewRequestInfo(req); err != nil {\n    46\u2192\t\t\t\tmetrics.RecordRequestAbort(req, nil)\n    47\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n    48\u2192\t\t\t\tmetrics.RecordRequestAbort(req, info)\n    49\u2192\t\t\t}\n    50\u2192\t\t\t// This call can have different handlers, but the default chain rate limits. Call it after the metrics are updated\n    51\u2192\t\t\t// in case the rate limit delays it.  If you outrun the rate for this one timed out requests, something has gone\n    52\u2192\t\t\t// seriously wrong with your server, but generally having a logging signal for timeouts is useful.\n    53\u2192\t\t\truntime.HandleErrorWithContext(req.Context(), nil, \"Timeout or abort while handling\", \"method\", req.Method, \"URI\", req.RequestURI, \"auditID\", audit.GetAuditIDTruncated(req.Context()))\n    54\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    55\u2192\t\t}\n    56\u2192\t\thttp.Error(w, \"This request caused apiserver to panic. Look in the logs for details.\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n    57\u2192\t\tklog.ErrorS(nil, \"apiserver panic'd\", \"method\", req.Method, \"URI\", req.RequestURI, \"auditID\", audit.GetAuditIDTruncated(req.Context()))\n    58\u2192\t})\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192// WithHTTPLogging enables logging of incoming requests.\n    62\u2192func WithHTTPLogging(handler http.Handler) http.Handler {\n    63\u2192\treturn httplog.WithLogging(handler, httplog.DefaultStacktracePred)\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192func withPanicRecovery(handler http.Handler, crashHandler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request, interface{})) http.Handler {\n    67\u2192\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n    68\u2192\t\tdefer runtime.HandleCrash(func(err interface{}) {\n    69\u2192\t\t\tcrashHandler(w, req, err)\n    70\u2192\t\t})\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192\t\t// Dispatch to the internal handler\n    73\u2192\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    74\u2192\t})\n    75\u2192}\n    76\u2192"
103. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/hsts.go",
  "limit": 50
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2020 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    21\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    22\u2192)\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192// WithHSTS is a simple HSTS implementation that wraps an http Handler.\n    25\u2192// If hstsDirectives is empty or nil, no HSTS support is installed.\n    26\u2192func WithHSTS(handler http.Handler, hstsDirectives []string) http.Handler {\n    27\u2192\tif len(hstsDirectives) == 0 {\n    28\u2192\t\treturn handler\n    29\u2192\t}\n    30\u2192\tallDirectives := strings.Join(hstsDirectives, \"; \")\n    31\u2192\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n    32\u2192\t\t// Chrome and Mozilla Firefox maintain an HSTS preload list\n    33\u2192\t\t// issue : golang.org/issue/26162\n    34\u2192\t\t// Set the Strict-Transport-Security header if it is not already set\n    35\u2192\t\tif _, ok := w.Header()[\"Strict-Transport-Security\"]; !ok {\n    36\u2192\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Strict-Transport-Security\", allDirectives)\n    37\u2192\t\t}\n    38\u2192\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    39\u2192\t})\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192"
104. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Explore storage and admission layers",
  "max_turns": 30,
  "prompt": "Explore the Kubernetes apiserver storage layer, admission control, and authentication/authorization subsystems thoroughly. Codebase at /workspace, apiserver at staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/.\n\nInvestigate:\n\n1. STORAGE LAYER:\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/ \u2014 the storage interface and implementations\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/ \u2014 the etcd3 storage backend\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/ \u2014 the caching layer (Cacher struct, watch cache)\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/ \u2014 the generic registry (Store struct, GenericStore)\n   - How does the REST storage layer (rest.Storage) connect to the etcd backend?\n\n2. ADMISSION CONTROL:\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/ \u2014 admission interfaces, chain, plugins\n   - How admission is invoked during create/update/delete (look in endpoint handlers)\n   - Webhook admission (look for webhook, mutating, validating dirs)\n\n3. AUTHENTICATION:\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/ \u2014 authenticator interfaces\n   - How authentication is wired in (look for WithAuthentication filter)\n\n4. AUTHORIZATION:\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/ \u2014 authorizer interfaces\n   - How authorization is wired in (look for WithAuthorization filter)\n\n5. EXTENSION POINTS:\n   - PostStartHooks in genericapiserver.go\n   - Aggregation layer (if present under staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/)\n   - Custom resource definitions hooks or extension patterns\n   - Decorator/StorageDecorator patterns\n\nGive me exact file paths, key struct/function names, and line numbers.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a9fa3ec",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Excellent! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the final report with all critical details:\n\n## COMPREHENSIVE KUBERNETES APISERVER ARCHITECTURE EXPLORATION\n\n### 1. STORAGE LAYER\n\n#### Core Storage Interface\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go` (lines 169-279)\n\n**Key Interface**: `Interface` (line 169)\n- Methods: `Create()`, `Delete()`, `Watch()`, `Get()`, `GetList()`, `GuaranteedUpdate()`, `Stats()`, `ReadinessCheck()`, `RequestWatchProgress()`, `GetCurrentResourceVersion()`, `EnableResourceSizeEstimation()`, `CompactRevision()`\n- Versioner interface (lines 46-71): Manages resource versions and object metadata\n- Preconditions struct (lines 123-165): Enforces optimistic concurrency control via UID/ResourceVersion\n\n#### Etcd3 Storage Backend\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/store.go` (lines 80-149)\n\n**Key Struct**: `store` (lines 80-98)\n- Fields: `client`, `codec`, `versioner`, `transformer`, `pathPrefix`, `groupResource`, `watcher`, `leaseManager`, `decoder`, `resourcePrefix`, `newListFunc`, `compactor`\n- Constructor: `New()` (line 147)\n- Implements `storage.Interface` (line 100)\n- Uses etcd3 Kubernetes client (`go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/kubernetes`)\n- Handles lease management, watching, and value transformation\n\n#### Caching Layer (Watch Cache)\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cacher.go` (lines 257-343, 348-415)\n\n**Key Struct**: `Cacher` (lines 262-343)\n- Maintains in-memory watch cache with event history\n- Fields: `incoming` channel, `watchCache`, `reflector`, `versioner`, `watchers` (indexed watchers map), `bookmarkWatchers`, `compactor`\n- Constructor: `NewCacherFromConfig()` (lines 348-415)\n- Uses `watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets` for bookmark scheduling (lines 198-248)\n- Delegates read-only operations to in-memory cache, mutations to underlying storage\n\n**Config struct** (lines 79-123):\n- `Storage`, `Versioner`, `GroupResource`, `EventsHistoryWindow`, `ResourcePrefix`, `KeyFunc`, `NewFunc`, `NewListFunc`, `GetAttrsFunc`, `IndexerFuncs`, `Indexers`, `Codec`, `Clock`\n\n#### Storage Decorator Pattern\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/storage_decorator.go` (lines 27-37)\n\n**Key Type**: `StorageDecorator` function signature\n```go\nfunc(\n    config *storagebackend.ConfigForResource,\n    resourcePrefix string,\n    keyFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error),\n    newFunc, newListFunc func() runtime.Object,\n    getAttrsFunc storage.AttrFunc,\n    trigger storage.IndexerFuncs,\n    indexers *cache.Indexers\n) (storage.Interface, factory.DestroyFunc, error)\n```\n\n#### Cacher Creation with Decorator\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/storage_factory.go` (lines 36-86)\n\n**Function**: `StorageWithCacher()` (lines 36-86)\n- Creates raw storage via `generic.NewRawStorage()` (line 47)\n- Wraps with `cacherstorage.NewCacherFromConfig()` (line 70)\n- Returns delegator via `cacherstorage.NewCacheDelegator()` (line 74)\n- Provides unified destroy function managing both cacher and underlying storage lifecycle\n\n### 2. REGISTRY & REST STORAGE\n\n#### Generic Store Implementation\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go` (lines 100-150)\n\n**Key Struct**: `Store` (lines 100-150)\n- Central CRUD implementation for resources\n- Fields: `NewFunc`, `NewListFunc`, `DefaultQualifiedResource`, `SingularQualifiedResource`, `KeyRootFunc`, `KeyFunc`, `ObjectNameFunc`, `TTLFunc`, `PredicateFunc`\n- Implements REST operations: Create, Get, List, Update, Delete, Watch\n\n#### REST Storage Interfaces\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go` (lines 53-200)\n\n**Key Interfaces**:\n- `Storage` (lines 58-67): Base interface with `New()`, `Destroy()`\n- `Lister` (lines 124-133): `List()` and table conversion\n- `Getter` (lines 135-141): `Get()` named resources\n- `GracefulDeleter` (lines 170-182): Delete with options and validation\n- `Creater`/`NamedCreater`: Create operations\n- `Updater`/`Patcher`: Modification operations\n- `Watcher`: Watch stream operations\n\n#### REST Options & Storage Configuration\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/options.go` (lines 30-60)\n\n**Key Struct**: `RESTOptions` (lines 31-40)\n- Fields: `StorageConfig`, `Decorator`, `EnableGarbageCollection`, `DeleteCollectionWorkers`, `ResourcePrefix`, `CountMetricPollPeriod`, `StorageObjectCountTracker`\n\n**Interface**: `RESTOptionsGetter` (lines 47-52)\n- `GetRESTOptions(resource schema.GroupResource, example runtime.Object) (RESTOptions, error)`\n\n### 3. ADMISSION CONTROL\n\n#### Admission Interfaces\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go` (lines 122-155)\n\n**Key Interfaces**:\n- `Interface` (lines 123-127): `Handles(operation Operation)` bool\n- `MutationInterface` (lines 129-135): `Admit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error`\n- `ValidationInterface` (lines 137-144): `Validate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error`\n- `Attributes` (lines 31-77): Request metadata (name, namespace, resource, operation, user, object, old object, annotations)\n- `ObjectInterfaces` (lines 79-93): Provides runtime interfaces (Creater, Typer, Defaulter, Convertor, EquivalentResourceMapper)\n\n**Operations**: CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, CONNECT (lines 151-155)\n\n#### Admission Chain\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go` (lines 23-70)\n\n**Type**: `chainAdmissionHandler` (line 23)\n- Implements both `MutationInterface` and `ValidationInterface`\n- `Admit()` (lines 31-44): Iterates handlers, calls mutation plugins\n- `Validate()` (lines 47-59): Iterates handlers, calls validation plugins\n- Short-circuits on first error\n\n#### Admission Invocation in Handlers\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/create.go` (lines 53-247)\n\n**CreateHandler** (lines 53-237):\n- Line 160: `admit = admission.WithAudit(admit)` - wraps with audit\n- Line 182: Creates `admissionAttributes := admission.NewAttributesRecord(obj, nil, scope.Kind, namespace, name, ...)`\n- Lines 188, 202-205: Invokes admission:\n  - Validating admission via `rest.AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc()` (line 188)\n  - Mutating admission via `mutatingAdmission.Admit()` (line 203)\n\n### 4. AUTHENTICATION\n\n#### Authenticator Interfaces\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/bearertoken/bearertoken.go` (lines 32-76)\n\n**Key Struct**: `Authenticator` (lines 32-38)\n- Extracts bearer tokens from Authorization header\n- `AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request)` returns `(*authenticator.Response, bool, error)` (line 42)\n- Removes Authorization header on success (line 67)\n\n#### Authentication Filter\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authentication.go` (lines 42-125)\n\n**Function**: `WithAuthentication()` (lines 46-124)\n- Wraps handler with authentication middleware\n- Parameters: `handler`, `auth authenticator.Request`, `failed http.Handler`, `apiAuds authenticator.Audiences`, `requestHeaderConfig`\n- Line 67: `resp, ok, err := auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)` - authenticates request\n- Lines 92-98: Clears authentication headers (standard + custom)\n- Line 122: `req = req.WithContext(genericapirequest.WithUser(req.Context(), resp.User))`\n- HTTP/2 DOS mitigation for unauthenticated clients (lines 116-121)\n\n### 5. AUTHORIZATION\n\n#### Authorizer Interfaces\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer/interfaces.go` (lines 83-198)\n\n**Key Interface**: `Authorizer` (lines 83-85)\n- `Authorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (authorized Decision, reason string, err error)`\n\n**Attributes Interface** (lines 31-78):\n- Methods: `GetUser()`, `GetVerb()`, `IsReadOnly()`, `GetNamespace()`, `GetResource()`, `GetSubresource()`, `GetName()`, `GetAPIGroup()`, `GetAPIVersion()`, `IsResourceRequest()`, `GetPath()`, `GetFieldSelector()`, `GetLabelSelector()`\n\n**Decision Type** (lines 175-198):\n- Values: `DecisionDeny`, `DecisionAllow`, `DecisionNoOpinion`\n\n#### Authorization Filter\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authorization.go` (lines 52-153)\n\n**Function**: `WithAuthorization()` (lines 53-98)\n- Line 66: `attributes, err := GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)` - builds authorization attributes\n- Line 71: `authorized, reason, err := a.Authorize(ctx, attributes)` - authorizes request\n- Lines 80-85: Allows if `DecisionAllow`, adds audit annotations\n- Lines 93-97: Denies with 403 Forbidden if not allowed\n\n**Function**: `GetAuthorizerAttributes()` (lines 101-153)\n- Constructs `attributesRecord` from request context and request info\n- Includes field/label selector parsing if enabled (lines 126-150)\n\n### 6. REQUEST PROCESSING PIPELINE\n\n#### Handler Chain Builder\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go` (lines 1015-1097)\n\n**Function**: `DefaultBuildHandlerChain()` (lines 1015-1097)\n\n**Filter Order** (innermost to outermost):\n1. Line 1018: `WithRequestInfo()` - parses request path info\n2. Line 1019: `WithAuthorization()` - authorization check\n3. Lines 1022-1031: Flow control (priority & fairness) or max in-flight limits\n4. Lines 1034-1040: Impersonation (constrained or standard)\n5. Line 1043: `WithAudit()` - audit logging\n6. Line 1056: `WithAuthentication()` - extracts user identity\n7. Line 1067: `WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests()`\n8. Line 1069: `WithRequestDeadline()`\n9. Lines 1072-1074: Watch termination during shutdown\n10. Lines 1078-1084: Cache control, HSTS, CORS, security headers\n\n#### Endpoint Installation\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go` (lines 287-543)\n\n**Function**: `registerResourceHandlers()` (lines 287-543)\n- Line 288: `admit := a.group.Admit` - gets admission controller\n- Lines 337-349: Inspects storage for supported operations (Creater, Lister, Updater, Deleter, Watcher, etc.)\n- Lines 525-542: Registers HTTP handlers for standard REST verbs\n\n### 7. GENERIC API SERVER & CONFIGURATION\n\n#### GenericAPIServer Structure\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go` (lines 109-250)\n\n**Key Fields**:\n- Line 128: `admissionControl admission.Interface` - admission controller\n- Line 213: `Authorizer authorizer.Authorizer` - authorizer\n- Line 188-194: PostStartHooks management (map, lock, called flag)\n- Line 207-208: `AuditBackend audit.Backend` - audit backend\n- Line 215-217: `EquivalentResourceRegistry`\n- Line 249: `StorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager`\n\n#### PostStartHooks\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/hooks.go` (lines 33-245)\n\n**PostStartHookFunc** (line 43):\n```go\ntype PostStartHookFunc func(context PostStartHookContext) error\n```\n\n**PostStartHookContext** (lines 49-54):\n- `LoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config` - privileged loopback connection\n- `Context context.Context` - cancellation context\n\n**Methods**:\n- `AddPostStartHook()` (lines 83-115): Register hook with deduplication\n- `RunPostStartHooks()` (lines 156-169): Run all hooks in goroutines with wait group\n- Line 167: `go runPostStartHook(hookName, hookEntry, context)` - async execution\n\n**Lifecycle**:\n- Lines 98-103: Check if hooks already called, prevent duplicate registration\n- Lines 106-109: Register healthz check for post-start hook completion\n- Lines 195-206: Execute hook with crash recovery, fatal on error\n\n#### Server Configuration\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go` (lines 110-330)\n\n**Config Struct** Key Fields:\n- Lines 116-120: Authentication/Authorization info\n- Line 134-136: `AdmissionControl admission.Interface`\n- Line 140: `FlowControl utilflowcontrol.Interface`\n- Line 186: `BuildHandlerChainFunc` - customizable handler chain\n- Line 224: `RESTOptionsGetter genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter` - storage configuration\n- Lines 152-153: `PostStartHooks map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry`\n- Line 215-217: OpenAPI configuration\n\n**AuthenticationInfo** (lines 376-384):\n- `APIAudiences authenticator.Audiences`\n- `Authenticator authenticator.Request`\n- `RequestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig`\n\n**AuthorizationInfo** (lines 386-390):\n- `Authorizer authorizer.Authorizer`\n\n### 8. API GROUP INSTALLATION\n\n#### APIInstaller\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/installer.go` (lines 62-66, 195-222, 287-543)\n\n**Struct**: `APIInstaller` (lines 62-66)\n- Fields: `group *APIGroupVersion`, `prefix string`, `minRequestTimeout time.Duration`\n\n**Install()** (lines 195-222):\n- Iterates through `a.group.Storage` map of resources\n- Calls `registerResourceHandlers()` for each resource\n- Returns `apiResources`, `resourceInfos`, `webService`, `errors`\n\n**registerResourceHandlers()** (lines 287-543):\n- Line 288: Gets admission controller from group\n- Lines 337-349: Inspects storage interfaces\n- Lines 501-543: Generates appropriate HTTP actions and routes\n- Handles both namespace-scoped and cluster-scoped resources\n- Registers Create, Get, List, Update, Delete, Watch, DeleteCollection, Connect\n\n#### APIGroupVersion\n**File**: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go` (lines 70-98)\n\n**APIGroupInfo** (lines 71-98):\n- `PrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion`\n- `VersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage` - version -> resource -> storage\n- `OptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion`\n- `MetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion`\n- `Scheme *runtime.Scheme`\n- `NegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer`\n- `ParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec`\n\n### 9. EXTENSION POINTS & AGGREGATION\n\n#### PostStartHooks as Extension Points\n- Registered in `Config.PostStartHooks` (line 153)\n- Executed after server starts listening (line 773)\n- Used for controller initialization, resource discovery population\n- Health-checked during startup (line 109)\n\n#### StorageDecorators for Custom Storage Logic\n- `StorageDecorator` function type (lines 29-37 in storage_decorator.go)\n- Passed in `RESTOptions.Decorator` (line 33)\n- Allows wrapping storage with caching, encryption, versioning\n- `StorageWithCacher()` is standard decorator (lines 36-86 in storage_factory.go)\n\n#### Aggregation Layer Reference\n- `AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager` (line 155 in genericapiserver.go)\n- `PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager` (line 158)\n- `AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager` (line 161)\n\n### 10. CRITICAL FLOW DIAGRAMS\n\n**CREATE REQUEST FLOW**:\n```\nHTTP Request\n  \u2193\nWithAuditInit (line 1095)\n  \u2193\nWithPanicRecovery (line 1094)\n  \u2193\nWithRequestInfo (line 1091) - extracts RequestInfo\n  \u2193\nWithAuthentication (line 1056) - calls bearertoken.AuthenticateRequest\n  \u2193\nWithAudit (line 1043) - logs audit events\n  \u2193\nWithImpersonation (line 1038)\n  \u2193\nWithAuthorization (line 1019) - calls authorizer.Authorize\n  \u2193\ncreateHandler (line 53 in create.go)\n  \u251c\u2500 admission.NewAttributesRecord (line 182)\n  \u251c\u2500 Validating admission via rest.AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc (line 188)\n  \u251c\u2500 FieldManager.UpdateNoErrors (line 199)\n  \u251c\u2500 Mutating admission: mutatingAdmission.Admit (line 203)\n  \u2514\u2500 rest.NamedCreater.Create (line 184)\n      \u2193\n      Store.Create \u2192 storage.Interface.GuaranteedUpdate\n        \u2193\n        etcd3.store or Cacher.GuaranteedUpdate\n          \u2193\n          etcd3.store - writes to etcd with transformer/encryptor\n          \u2193\n          Cacher - delegates to underlying storage, updates watch cache\n```\n\n**AUTHENTICATION & AUTHORIZATION STACKING**:\n```\nAuthorization Filter (line 1019 in config.go)\n  \u251c\u2500 Request flows in\n  \u251c\u2500 GetAuthorizerAttributes (line 66)\n  \u2502  \u2514\u2500 Builds AttributesRecord from RequestInfo and User\n  \u251c\u2500 authorizer.Authorize (line 71)\n  \u2502  \u2514\u2500 RBAC/ABAC/Webhook authorizers evaluate\n  \u2514\u2500 If DecisionAllow, pass to next handler\n\nAuthentication Filter (line 1056 in config.go)\n  \u251c\u2500 Request flows in\n  \u251c\u2500 authenticator.Request.AuthenticateRequest (line 67)\n  \u2502  \u2514\u2500 BearerToken extracts and validates token\n  \u251c\u2500 User stored in context (line 122)\n  \u2514\u2500 Authorization header removed (line 89)\n```\n\n### 11. KEY FILE PATHS (ABSOLUTE)\n\n1. Storage interfaces: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go`\n2. Etcd3 backend: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/store.go`\n3. Cacher: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cacher.go`\n4. Storage decorator: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/storage_decorator.go`\n5.   "prompt": "Explore the Kubernetes apiserver storage layer, admission control, and authentication/authorization subsystems thoroughly. Codebase at /workspace, apiserver at staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/.\n\nInvestigate:\n\n1. STORAGE LAYER:\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/ \u2014 the storage interface and implementations\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/ \u2014 the etcd3 storage backend\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/ \u2014 the caching layer (Cacher struct, watch cache)\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/ \u2014 the generic registry (Store struct, GenericStore)\n   - How does the REST storage layer (rest.Storage) connect to the etcd backend?\n\n2. ADMISSION CONTROL:\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/ \u2014 admission interfaces, chain, plugins\n   - How admission is invoked during create/update/delete (look in endpoint handlers)\n   - Webhook admission (look for webhook, mutating, validating dirs)\n\n3. AUTHENTICATION:\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/ \u2014 authenticator interfaces\n   - How authentication is wired in (look for WithAuthentication filter)\n\n4. AUTHORIZATION:\n   - staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/ \u2014 authorizer interfaces\n   - How authorization is wired in (look for WithAuthorization filter)\n\n5. EXTENSION POINTS:\n   - PostStartHooks in genericapiserver.go\n   - Aggregation layer (if present under staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/)\n   - Custom resource definitions hooks or extension patterns\n   - Decorator/StorageDecorator patterns\n\nGive me exact file paths, key struct/function names, and line numbers.",

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package storage\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    26\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n    32\u2192)\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192// Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.\n    35\u2192type Feature = string\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192// RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.\n    38\u2192var RequestWatchProgress Feature = \"RequestWatchProgress\"\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192// Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response\n    41\u2192// onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an\n    42\u2192// object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be\n    43\u2192// a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,\n    44\u2192// intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of\n    45\u2192// type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.\n    46\u2192type Versioner interface {\n    47\u2192\t// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object\n    48\u2192\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata\n    49\u2192\t// from database.\n    50\u2192\tUpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error\n    51\u2192\t// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object\n    52\u2192\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from\n    53\u2192\t// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client\n    54\u2192\t// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number\n    55\u2192\t// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during\n    56\u2192\t// serialization if it is set to nil.\n    57\u2192\tUpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error\n    58\u2192\t// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should\n    59\u2192\t// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.\n    60\u2192\tPrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error\n    61\u2192\t// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.\n    62\u2192\t// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.\n    63\u2192\tObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192\t// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and\n    66\u2192\t// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().\n    67\u2192\t// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch\n    68\u2192\t// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass\n    69\u2192\t// \"1\", you will see updates from \"2\" onwards).\n    70\u2192\tParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192// ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with\n    74\u2192// an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete\n    75\u2192// database and to improve testability.\n    76\u2192type ResponseMeta struct {\n    77\u2192\t// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be\n    78\u2192\t// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested\n    79\u2192\t// expiration time due to server lag).\n    80\u2192\tTTL int64\n    81\u2192\t// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.\n    82\u2192\tResourceVersion uint64\n    83\u2192}\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192// IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes\n    86\u2192// `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.\n    87\u2192type IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192// IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that\n    90\u2192// for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.\n    91\u2192type IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192// Everything accepts all objects.\n    94\u2192var Everything = SelectionPredicate{\n    95\u2192\tLabel: labels.Everything(),\n    96\u2192\tField: fields.Everything(),\n    97\u2192}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192// MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).\n   100\u2192type MatchValue struct {\n   101\u2192\tIndexName string\n   102\u2192\tValue     string\n   103\u2192}\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192// Pass an UpdateFunc to Interface.GuaranteedUpdate to make an update\n   106\u2192// that is guaranteed to succeed.\n   107\u2192// See the comment for GuaranteedUpdate for more details.\n   108\u2192type UpdateFunc func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (output runtime.Object, ttl *uint64, err error)\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192// ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\n   111\u2192// if the hook cannot be completed. The function may NOT transform the provided\n   112\u2192// object.\n   113\u2192// NOTE: the object in obj may be nil if it cannot be read from the\n   114\u2192// storage, due to transformation or decode error.\n   115\u2192type ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192// ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \"admit everything\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\n   118\u2192func ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\n   119\u2192\treturn nil\n   120\u2192}\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192// Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.\n   123\u2192type Preconditions struct {\n   124\u2192\t// Specifies the target UID.\n   125\u2192\t// +optional\n   126\u2192\tUID *types.UID `json:\"uid,omitempty\"`\n   127\u2192\t// Specifies the target ResourceVersion\n   128\u2192\t// +optional\n   129\u2192\tResourceVersion *string `json:\"resourceVersion,omitempty\"`\n   130\u2192}\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192// NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.\n   133\u2192func NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {\n   134\u2192\tu := types.UID(uid)\n   135\u2192\treturn &Preconditions{UID: &u}\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192func (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {\n   139\u2192\tif p == nil {\n   140\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   141\u2192\t}\n   142\u2192\tobjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\n   143\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   144\u2192\t\treturn NewInternalError(\n   145\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Errorf(\"can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w\",\n   146\u2192\t\t\t\t*p,\n   147\u2192\t\t\t\tobj,\n   148\u2192\t\t\t\terr))\n   149\u2192\t}\n   150\u2192\tif p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {\n   151\u2192\t\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\n   152\u2192\t\t\t\"Precondition failed: UID in precondition: %v, UID in object meta: %v\",\n   153\u2192\t\t\t*p.UID,\n   154\u2192\t\t\tobjMeta.GetUID())\n   155\u2192\t\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\n   156\u2192\t}\n   157\u2192\tif p.ResourceVersion != nil && *p.ResourceVersion != objMeta.GetResourceVersion() {\n   158\u2192\t\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\n   159\u2192\t\t\t\"Precondition failed: ResourceVersion in precondition: %v, ResourceVersion in object meta: %v\",\n   160\u2192\t\t\t*p.ResourceVersion,\n   161\u2192\t\t\tobjMeta.GetResourceVersion())\n   162\u2192\t\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\n   163\u2192\t}\n   164\u2192\treturn nil\n   165\u2192}\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192// Interface offers a common interface for object marshaling/unmarshaling operations and\n   168\u2192// hides all the storage-related operations behind it.\n   169\u2192type Interface interface {\n   170\u2192\t// Returns Versioner associated with this interface.\n   171\u2192\tVersioner() Versioner\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192\t// Create adds a new object at a key unless it already exists. 'ttl' is time-to-live\n   174\u2192\t// in seconds (0 means forever). If no error is returned and out is not nil, out will be\n   175\u2192\t// set to the read value from database.\n   176\u2192\tCreate(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192\t// Delete removes the specified key and returns the value that existed at that spot.\n   179\u2192\t// If key didn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error.\n   180\u2192\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\n   181\u2192\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\n   182\u2192\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\n   183\u2192\tDelete(\n   184\u2192\t\tctx context.Context, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions,\n   185\u2192\t\tvalidateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192\t// Watch begins watching the specified key. Events are decoded into API objects,\n   188\u2192\t// and any items selected by 'p' are sent down to returned watch.Interface.\n   189\u2192\t// resourceVersion may be used to specify what version to begin watching,\n   190\u2192\t// which should be the current resourceVersion, and no longer rv+1\n   191\u2192\t// (e.g. reconnecting without missing any updates).\n   192\u2192\t// If resource version is \"0\", this interface will get current object at given key\n   193\u2192\t// and send it in an \"ADDED\" event, before watch starts.\n   194\u2192\tWatch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192\t// Get unmarshals object found at key into objPtr. On a not found error, will either\n   197\u2192\t// return a zero object of the requested type, or an error, depending on 'opts.ignoreNotFound'.\n   198\u2192\t// Treats empty responses and nil response nodes exactly like a not found error.\n   199\u2192\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\n   200\u2192\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\n   201\u2192\tGet(ctx context.Context, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192\t// GetList unmarshalls objects found at key into a *List api object (an object\n   204\u2192\t// that satisfies runtime.IsList definition).\n   205\u2192\t// If 'opts.Recursive' is false, 'key' is used as an exact match. If `opts.Recursive'\n   206\u2192\t// is true, 'key' is used as a prefix.\n   207\u2192\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\n   208\u2192\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\n   209\u2192\tGetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error\n   210\u2192\n   211\u2192\t// GuaranteedUpdate keeps calling 'tryUpdate()' to update key 'key' (of type 'destination')\n   212\u2192\t// retrying the update until success if there is index conflict.\n   213\u2192\t// Note that object passed to tryUpdate may change across invocations of tryUpdate() if\n   214\u2192\t// other writers are simultaneously updating it, so tryUpdate() needs to take into account\n   215\u2192\t// the current contents of the object when deciding how the update object should look.\n   216\u2192\t// If the key doesn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error if ignoreNotFound=false\n   217\u2192\t// else `destination` will be set to the zero value of it's type.\n   218\u2192\t// If the eventual successful invocation of `tryUpdate` returns an output with the same serialized\n   219\u2192\t// contents as the input, it won't perform any update, but instead set `destination` to an object with those\n   220\u2192\t// contents.\n   221\u2192\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\n   222\u2192\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\n   223\u2192\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\n   224\u2192\t//\n   225\u2192\t// Example:\n   226\u2192\t//\n   227\u2192\t// s := /* implementation of Interface */\n   228\u2192\t// err := s.GuaranteedUpdate(\n   229\u2192\t//     \"myKey\", &MyType{}, true, preconditions,\n   230\u2192\t//     func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {\n   231\u2192\t//       // Before each invocation of the user defined function, \"input\" is reset to\n   232\u2192\t//       // current contents for \"myKey\" in database.\n   233\u2192\t//       curr := input.(*MyType)  // Guaranteed to succeed.\n   234\u2192\t//\n   235\u2192\t//       // Make the modification\n   236\u2192\t//       curr.Counter++\n   237\u2192\t//\n   238\u2192\t//       // Return the modified object - return an error to stop iterating. Return\n   239\u2192\t//       // a uint64 to alter the TTL on the object, or nil to keep it the same value.\n   240\u2192\t//       return cur, nil, nil\n   241\u2192\t//    }, cachedExistingObject\n   242\u2192\t// )\n   243\u2192\tGuaranteedUpdate(\n   244\u2192\t\tctx context.Context, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,\n   245\u2192\t\tpreconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192\t// Stats returns storage stats.\n   248\u2192\tStats(ctx context.Context) (Stats, error)\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192\t// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\n   251\u2192\tReadinessCheck() error\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192\t// RequestWatchProgress requests the a watch stream progress status be sent in the\n   254\u2192\t// watch response stream as soon as possible.\n   255\u2192\t// Used for monitor watch progress even if watching resources with no changes.\n   256\u2192\t//\n   257\u2192\t// If watch is lagging, progress status might:\n   258\u2192\t// * be pointing to stale resource version. Use etcd KV request to get linearizable resource version.\n   259\u2192\t// * not be delivered at all. It's recommended to poll request progress periodically.\n   260\u2192\t//\n   261\u2192\t// Note: Only watches with matching context grpc metadata will be notified.\n   262\u2192\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/9325a57125e8502941d1b0c7379c4bb80a678d5c/vendor/go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/watch.go#L1037-L1042\n   263\u2192\t//\n   264\u2192\t// TODO: Remove when storage.Interface will be separate from etc3.store.\n   265\u2192\t// Deprecated: Added temporarily to simplify exposing RequestProgress for watch cache.\n   266\u2192\tRequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192\t// GetCurrentResourceVersion gets the current resource version from etcd.\n   269\u2192\t// This method issues an empty list request and reads only the ResourceVersion from the object metadata\n   270\u2192\tGetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192\t// EnableResourceSizeEstimation enables estimating resource size by providing function get keys from storage.\n   273\u2192\tEnableResourceSizeEstimation(KeysFunc) error\n   274\u2192\n   275\u2192\t// CompactRevision returns latest observed revision that was compacted.\n   276\u2192\t// Without ListFromCacheSnapshot enabled only locally executed compaction will be observed.\n   277\u2192\t// Returns 0 if no compaction was yet observed.\n   278\u2192\tCompactRevision() int64\n   279\u2192}\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192// KeysFunc is a function prototype to fetch keys from storage.\n   282\u2192type KeysFunc func(context.Context) ([]string, error)\n   283\u2192\n   284\u2192// GetOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage get operations.\n   285\u2192type GetOptions struct {\n   286\u2192\t// IgnoreNotFound determines what is returned if the requested object is not found. If\n   287\u2192\t// true, a zero object is returned. If false, an error is returned.\n   288\u2192\tIgnoreNotFound bool\n   289\u2192\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the get operation\n   290\u2192\t// as a \"not older than\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\n   291\u2192\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\n   292\u2192\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\n   293\u2192\tResourceVersion string\n   294\u2192}\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192// ListOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage list operations.\n   297\u2192type ListOptions struct {\n   298\u2192\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the list operation\n   299\u2192\t// as a \"not older than\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\n   300\u2192\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\n   301\u2192\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\n   302\u2192\tResourceVersion string\n   303\u2192\t// ResourceVersionMatch provides the rule for how the resource version constraint applies. If set\n   304\u2192\t// to the default value \"\" the legacy resource version semantic apply.\n   305\u2192\tResourceVersionMatch metav1.ResourceVersionMatch\n   306\u2192\t// Predicate provides the selection rules for the list operation.\n   307\u2192\tPredicate SelectionPredicate\n   308\u2192\t// Recursive determines whether the list or watch is defined for a single object located at the\n   309\u2192\t// given key, or for the whole set of objects with the given key as a prefix.\n   310\u2192\tRecursive bool\n   311\u2192\t// ProgressNotify determines whether storage-originated bookmark (progress notify) events should\n   312\u2192\t// be delivered to the users. The option is ignored for non-watch requests.\n   313\u2192\t//\n   314\u2192\t// Firstly, note that this field is different from the Predicate.AllowWatchBookmarks field.\n   315\u2192\t// Secondly, this field is intended for internal clients only such as the watch cache.\n   316\u2192\t//\n   317\u2192\t// This means that external clients do not have the ability to set this field directly.\n   318\u2192\t// For example by setting the allowWatchBookmarks query parameter.\n   319\u2192\t//\n   320\u2192\t// The motivation for this approach is the fact that the frequency\n   321\u2192\t// of bookmark events from a storage like etcd might be very high.\n   322\u2192\t// As the number of watch requests increases, the server load would also increase.\n   323\u2192\t//\n   324\u2192\t// Furthermore, the server is not obligated to provide bookmark events at all,\n   325\u2192\t// as described in https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/956-watch-bookmark#proposal\n   326\u2192\tProgressNotify bool\n   327\u2192\t// SendInitialEvents, when set together with Watch option,\n   328\u2192\t// begin the watch stream with synthetic init events to build the\n   329\u2192\t// whole state of all resources followed by a synthetic \"Bookmark\"\n   330\u2192\t// event containing a ResourceVersion after which the server\n   331\u2192\t// continues streaming events.\n   332\u2192\tSendInitialEvents *bool\n   333\u2192}\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192// DeleteOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage delete operations.\n   336\u2192type DeleteOptions struct {\n   337\u2192\t// IgnoreStoreReadError, if enabled, will ignore store read error\n   338\u2192\t// such as transformation or decode failure and go ahead with the\n   339\u2192\t// deletion of the object.\n   340\u2192\t// NOTE: for normal deletion flow it should always be false, it may be\n   341\u2192\t// enabled by the caller only to facilitate unsafe deletion of corrupt\n   342\u2192\t// object which otherwise can not be deleted using the normal flow\n   343\u2192\tIgnoreStoreReadError bool\n   344\u2192}\n   345\u2192\n   346\u2192func ValidateListOptions(keyPrefix string, versioner Versioner, opts ListOptions) (withRev int64, continueKey string, err error) {\n   347\u2192\tif opts.Recursive && len(opts.Predicate.Continue) > 0 {\n   348\u2192\t\tcontinueKey, continueRV, err := DecodeContinue(opts.Predicate.Continue, keyPrefix)\n   349\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   350\u2192\t\t\treturn 0, \"\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid continue token: %v\", err))\n   351\u2192\t\t}\n   352\u2192\t\tif len(opts.ResourceVersion) > 0 && opts.ResourceVersion != \"0\" {\n   353\u2192\t\t\treturn 0, \"\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(\"specifying resource version is not allowed when using continue\")\n   354\u2192\t\t}\n   355\u2192\t\t// If continueRV > 0, the LIST request needs a specific resource version.\n   356\u2192\t\t// continueRV==0 is invalid.\n   357\u2192\t\t// If continueRV < 0, the request is for the latest resource version.\n   358\u2192\t\tif continueRV > 0 {\n   359\u2192\t\t\twithRev = continueRV\n   360\u2192\t\t}\n   361\u2192\t\treturn withRev, continueKey, nil\n   362\u2192\t}\n   363\u2192\tif len(opts.ResourceVersion) == 0 {\n   364\u2192\t\treturn withRev, \"\", nil\n   365\u2192\t}\n   366\u2192\tparsedRV, err := versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)\n   367\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   368\u2192\t\treturn withRev, \"\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid resource version: %v\", err))\n   369\u2192\t}\n   370\u2192\tswitch opts.ResourceVersionMatch {\n   371\u2192\tcase metav1.ResourceVersionMatchNotOlderThan:\n   372\u2192\t\t// The not older than constraint is checked after we get a response from etcd,\n   373\u2192\t\t// and returnedRV is then set to the revision we get from the etcd response.\n   374\u2192\tcase metav1.ResourceVersionMatchExact:\n   375\u2192\t\twithRev = int64(parsedRV)\n   376\u2192\tcase \"\": // legacy case\n   377\u2192\t\tif opts.Recursive && opts.Predicate.Limit > 0 && parsedRV > 0 {\n   378\u2192\t\t\twithRev = int64(parsedRV)\n   379\u2192\t\t}\n   380\u2192\tdefault:\n   381\u2192\t\treturn withRev, \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"unknown ResourceVersionMatch value: %v\", opts.ResourceVersionMatch)\n   382\u2192\t}\n   383\u2192\treturn withRev, \"\", nil\n   384\u2192}\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192// Stats provides statistics information about storage.\n   387\u2192type Stats struct {\n   388\u2192\t// ObjectCount informs about number of objects stored in the storage.\n   389\u2192\tObjectCount int64\n   390\u2192\t// EstimatedAverageObjectSizeBytes informs about size of objects stored in the storage, based on size of serialized values.\n   391\u2192\t// Value is an estimate, meaning it doesn't need to provide accurate nor consistent.\n   392\u2192\tEstimatedAverageObjectSizeBytes int64\n   393\u2192}\n   394\u2192\n   395\u2192func PrepareKey(resourcePrefix, key string, recursive bool) (string, error) {\n   396\u2192\tif key == \"..\" ||\n   397\u2192\t\tstrings.HasPrefix(key, \"../\") ||\n   398\u2192\t\tstrings.HasSuffix(key, \"/..\") ||\n   399\u2192\t\tstrings.Contains(key, \"/../\") {\n   400\u2192\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid key: %q\", key)\n   401\u2192\t}\n   402\u2192\tif key == \".\" ||\n   403\u2192\t\tstrings.HasPrefix(key, \"./\") ||\n   404\u2192\t\tstrings.HasSuffix(key, \"/.\") ||\n   405\u2192\t\tstrings.Contains(key, \"/./\") {\n   406\u2192\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid key: %q\", key)\n   407\u2192\t}\n   408\u2192\tif key == \"\" || key == \"/\" {\n   409\u2192\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"empty key: %q\", key)\n   410\u2192\t}\n   411\u2192\t// For recursive requests, we need to make sure the key ended with \"/\" so that we only\n   412\u2192\t// get children \"directories\". e.g. if we have key \"/a\", \"/a/b\", \"/ab\", getting keys\n   413\u2192\t// with prefix \"/a\" will return all three, while with prefix \"/a/\" will return only\n   414\u2192\t// \"/a/b\" which is the correct answer.\n   415\u2192\tif recursive && !strings.HasSuffix(key, \"/\") {\n   416\u2192\t\tkey += \"/\"\n   417\u2192\t}\n   418\u2192\tif !strings.HasPrefix(key, resourcePrefix) {\n   419\u2192\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid key: %q lacks resource prefix: %q\", key, resourcePrefix)\n   420\u2192\t}\n   421\u2192\treturn key, nil\n   422\u2192}\n   423\u2192"

required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package etcd3\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"bytes\"\n    21\u2192\t\"context\"\n    22\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    23\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    24\u2192\t\"path\"\n    25\u2192\t\"reflect\"\n    26\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n    27\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    28\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    29\u2192\t\"time\"\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\t\"go.etcd.io/etcd/api/v3/mvccpb\"\n    32\u2192\tclientv3 \"go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3\"\n    33\u2192\t\"go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/kubernetes\"\n    34\u2192\t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\"\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192\tetcdrpc \"go.etcd.io/etcd/api/v3/v3rpc/rpctypes\"\n    37\u2192\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n    43\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n    46\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\"\n    49\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/metrics\"\n    50\u2192\tetcdfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/feature\"\n    51\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/value\"\n    52\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    53\u2192\t\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\"\n    54\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    55\u2192)\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192const (\n    58\u2192\t// maxLimit is a maximum page limit increase used when fetching objects from etcd.\n    59\u2192\t// This limit is used only for increasing page size by kube-apiserver. If request\n    60\u2192\t// specifies larger limit initially, it won't be changed.\n    61\u2192\tmaxLimit = 10000\n    62\u2192)\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192// authenticatedDataString satisfies the value.Context interface. It uses the key to\n    65\u2192// authenticate the stored data. This does not defend against reuse of previously\n    66\u2192// encrypted values under the same key, but will prevent an attacker from using an\n    67\u2192// encrypted value from a different key. A stronger authenticated data segment would\n    68\u2192// include the etcd3 Version field (which is incremented on each write to a key and\n    69\u2192// reset when the key is deleted), but an attacker with write access to etcd can\n    70\u2192// force deletion and recreation of keys to weaken that angle.\n    71\u2192type authenticatedDataString string\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192// AuthenticatedData implements the value.Context interface.\n    74\u2192func (d authenticatedDataString) AuthenticatedData() []byte {\n    75\u2192\treturn []byte(string(d))\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192var _ value.Context = authenticatedDataString(\"\")\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192type store struct {\n    81\u2192\tclient             *kubernetes.Client\n    82\u2192\tcodec              runtime.Codec\n    83\u2192\tversioner          storage.Versioner\n    84\u2192\ttransformer        value.Transformer\n    85\u2192\tpathPrefix         string\n    86\u2192\tgroupResource      schema.GroupResource\n    87\u2192\twatcher            *watcher\n    88\u2192\tleaseManager       *leaseManager\n    89\u2192\tdecoder            Decoder\n    90\u2192\tlistErrAggrFactory func() ListErrorAggregator\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\tresourcePrefix string\n    93\u2192\tnewListFunc    func() runtime.Object\n    94\u2192\tcompactor      Compactor\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192\tcollectorMux          sync.RWMutex\n    97\u2192\tresourceSizeEstimator *resourceSizeEstimator\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192var _ storage.Interface = (*store)(nil)\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192func (s *store) RequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error {\n   103\u2192\t// Use watchContext to match ctx metadata provided when creating the watch.\n   104\u2192\t// In best case scenario we would use the same context that watch was created, but there is no way access it from watchCache.\n   105\u2192\treturn s.client.RequestProgress(s.watchContext(ctx))\n   106\u2192}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192type objState struct {\n   109\u2192\tobj   runtime.Object\n   110\u2192\tmeta  *storage.ResponseMeta\n   111\u2192\trev   int64\n   112\u2192\tdata  []byte\n   113\u2192\tstale bool\n   114\u2192}\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192// ListErrorAggregator aggregates the error(s) that the LIST operation\n   117\u2192// encounters while retrieving object(s) from the storage\n   118\u2192type ListErrorAggregator interface {\n   119\u2192\t// Aggregate aggregates the given error from list operation\n   120\u2192\t// key: it identifies the given object in the storage.\n   121\u2192\t// err: it represents the error the list operation encountered while\n   122\u2192\t// retrieving the given object from the storage.\n   123\u2192\t// done: true if the aggregation is done and the list operation should\n   124\u2192\t// abort, otherwise the list operation will continue\n   125\u2192\tAggregate(key string, err error) bool\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192\t// Err returns the aggregated error\n   128\u2192\tErr() error\n   129\u2192}\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192// defaultListErrorAggregatorFactory returns the default list error\n   132\u2192// aggregator that maintains backward compatibility, which is abort\n   133\u2192// the list operation as soon as it encounters the first error\n   134\u2192func defaultListErrorAggregatorFactory() ListErrorAggregator { return &abortOnFirstError{} }\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192// LIST aborts on the first error it encounters (backward compatible)\n   137\u2192type abortOnFirstError struct {\n   138\u2192\terr error\n   139\u2192}\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192func (a *abortOnFirstError) Aggregate(key string, err error) bool {\n   142\u2192\ta.err = err\n   143\u2192\treturn true\n   144\u2192}\n   145\u2192func (a *abortOnFirstError) Err() error { return a.err }\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192// New returns an etcd3 implementation of storage.Interface.\n   148\u2192func New(c *kubernetes.Client, compactor Compactor, codec runtime.Codec, newFunc, newListFunc func() runtime.Object, prefix, resourcePrefix string, groupResource schema.GroupResource, transformer value.Transformer, leaseManagerConfig LeaseManagerConfig, decoder Decoder, versioner storage.Versioner) (*store, error) {\n   149\u2192\t// for compatibility with etcd2 impl.\n   150\u2192\t// no-op for default prefix of '/registry'."

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file
