either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package cacher\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    23\u2192\t\"reflect\"\n    24\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    25\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    26\u2192\t\"time\"\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\"\n    29\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/metadata\"\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    33\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    43\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/delegator\"\n    46\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/metrics\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/progress\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/store\"\n    49\u2192\tetcdfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/feature\"\n    50\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    51\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n    52\u2192\t\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\"\n    53\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    54\u2192\t\"k8s.io/utils/clock\"\n    55\u2192)\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192var (\n    58\u2192\temptyFunc             = func(bool) {}\n    59\u2192\tcoreNamespaceResource = schema.GroupResource{Group: \"\", Resource: \"namespaces\"}\n    60\u2192)\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192const (\n    63\u2192\t// storageWatchListPageSize is the cacher's request chunk size of\n    64\u2192\t// initial and resync watch lists to storage.\n    65\u2192\tstorageWatchListPageSize = int64(10000)\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\t// DefaultEventFreshDuration is the default time duration of events\n    68\u2192\t// we want to keep.\n    69\u2192\t// We set it to defaultBookmarkFrequency plus epsilon to maximize\n    70\u2192\t// chances that last bookmark was sent within kept history, at the\n    71\u2192\t// same time, minimizing the needed memory usage.\n    72\u2192\tDefaultEventFreshDuration = defaultBookmarkFrequency + 15*time.Second\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192\t// defaultBookmarkFrequency defines how frequently watch bookmarks should be send\n    75\u2192\t// in addition to sending a bookmark right before watch deadline.\n    76\u2192\tdefaultBookmarkFrequency = time.Minute\n    77\u2192)\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192// Config contains the configuration for a given Cache.\n    80\u2192type Config struct {\n    81\u2192\t// An underlying storage.Interface.\n    82\u2192\tStorage storage.Interface\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192\t// An underlying storage.Versioner.\n    85\u2192\tVersioner storage.Versioner\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\t// The GroupResource the cacher is caching. Used for disambiguating *unstructured.Unstructured (CRDs) in logging\n    88\u2192\t// and metrics.\n    89\u2192\tGroupResource schema.GroupResource\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192\t// EventsHistoryWindow specifies minimum history duration that storage is keeping.\n    92\u2192\t// If lower than DefaultEventFreshDuration, the cache creation will fail.\n    93\u2192\tEventsHistoryWindow time.Duration\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\t// The Cache will be caching objects of a given Type and assumes that they\n    96\u2192\t// are all stored under ResourcePrefix directory in the underlying database.\n    97\u2192\tResourcePrefix string\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192\t// KeyFunc is used to get a key in the underlying storage for a given object.\n   100\u2192\tKeyFunc func(runtime.Object) (string, error)\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192\t// GetAttrsFunc is used to get object labels, fields\n   103\u2192\tGetAttrsFunc func(runtime.Object) (label labels.Set, field fields.Set, err error)\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192\t// IndexerFuncs is used for optimizing amount of watchers that\n   106\u2192\t// needs to process an incoming event.\n   107\u2192\tIndexerFuncs storage.IndexerFuncs\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192\t// Indexers is used to accelerate the list operation, falls back to regular list\n   110\u2192\t// operation if no indexer found.\n   111\u2192\tIndexers *cache.Indexers\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192\t// NewFunc is a function that creates new empty object storing a object of type Type.\n   114\u2192\tNewFunc func() runtime.Object\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192\t// NewList is a function that creates new empty object storing a list of\n   117\u2192\t// objects of type Type.\n   118\u2192\tNewListFunc func() runtime.Object\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192\tCodec runtime.Codec\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192\tClock clock.WithTicker\n   123\u2192}\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192type watchersMap map[int]*cacheWatcher\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192func (wm watchersMap) addWatcher(w *cacheWatcher, number int) {\n   128\u2192\twm[number] = w\n   129\u2192}\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192func (wm watchersMap) deleteWatcher(number int) {\n   132\u2192\tdelete(wm, number)\n   133\u2192}\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192func (wm watchersMap) terminateAll(done func(*cacheWatcher)) {\n   136\u2192\tfor key, watcher := range wm {\n   137\u2192\t\tdelete(wm, key)\n   138\u2192\t\tdone(watcher)\n   139\u2192\t}\n   140\u2192}\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192type indexedWatchers struct {\n   143\u2192\tallWatchers   map[namespacedName]watchersMap\n   144\u2192\tvalueWatchers map[string]watchersMap\n   145\u2192}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192func (i *indexedWatchers) addWatcher(w *cacheWatcher, number int, scope namespacedName, value string, supported bool) {\n   148\u2192\tif supported {\n   149\u2192\t\tif _, ok := i.valueWatchers[value]; !ok {\n   150\u2192\t\t\ti.valueWatchers[value] = watchersMap{}"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package admission\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import \"context\"\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192// chainAdmissionHandler is an instance of admission.NamedHandler that performs admission control using\n    22\u2192// a chain of admission handlers\n    23\u2192type chainAdmissionHandler []Interface\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192// NewChainHandler creates a new chain handler from an array of handlers. Used for testing.\n    26\u2192func NewChainHandler(handlers ...Interface) chainAdmissionHandler {\n    27\u2192\treturn chainAdmissionHandler(handlers)\n    28\u2192}\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192// Admit performs an admission control check using a chain of handlers, and returns immediately on first error\n    31\u2192func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Admit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {\n    32\u2192\tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\n    33\u2192\t\tif !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) {\n    34\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    35\u2192\t\t}\n    36\u2192\t\tif mutator, ok := handler.(MutationInterface); ok {\n    37\u2192\t\t\terr := mutator.Admit(ctx, a, o)\n    38\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n    39\u2192\t\t\t\treturn err\n    40\u2192\t\t\t}\n    41\u2192\t\t}\n    42\u2192\t}\n    43\u2192\treturn nil\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192// Validate performs an admission control check using a chain of handlers, and returns immediately on first error\n    47\u2192func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Validate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {\n    48\u2192\tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\n    49\u2192\t\tif !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) {\n    50\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    51\u2192\t\t}\n    52\u2192\t\tif validator, ok := handler.(ValidationInterface); ok {\n    53\u2192\t\t\terr := validator.Validate(ctx, a, o)\n    54\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n    55\u2192\t\t\t\treturn err\n    56\u2192\t\t\t}\n    57\u2192\t\t}\n    58\u2192\t}\n    59\u2192\treturn nil\n    60\u2192}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192// Handles will return true if any of the handlers handles the given operation\n    63\u2192func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Handles(operation Operation) bool {\n    64\u2192\tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\n    65\u2192\t\tif handler.Handles(operation) {\n    66\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n    67\u2192\t\t}\n    68\u2192\t}\n    69\u2192\treturn false\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package admission\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"io\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    25\u2192\tauditinternal \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n    27\u2192)\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192// Attributes is an interface used by AdmissionController to get information about a request\n    30\u2192// that is used to make an admission decision.\n    31\u2192type Attributes interface {\n    32\u2192\t// GetName returns the name of the object as presented in the request.  On a CREATE operation, the client\n    33\u2192\t// may omit name and rely on the server to generate the name.  If that is the case, this method will return\n    34\u2192\t// the empty string\n    35\u2192\tGetName() string\n    36\u2192\t// GetNamespace is the namespace associated with the request (if any)\n    37\u2192\tGetNamespace() string\n    38\u2192\t// GetResource is the name of the resource being requested.  This is not the kind.  For example: pods\n    39\u2192\tGetResource() schema.GroupVersionResource\n    40\u2192\t// GetSubresource is the name of the subresource being requested.  This is a different resource, scoped to the parent resource, but it may have a different kind.\n    41\u2192\t// For instance, /pods has the resource \"pods\" and the kind \"Pod\", while /pods/foo/status has the resource \"pods\", the sub resource \"status\", and the kind \"Pod\"\n    42\u2192\t// (because status operates on pods). The binding resource for a pod though may be /pods/foo/binding, which has resource \"pods\", subresource \"binding\", and kind \"Binding\".\n    43\u2192\tGetSubresource() string\n    44\u2192\t// GetOperation is the operation being performed\n    45\u2192\tGetOperation() Operation\n    46\u2192\t// GetOperationOptions is the options for the operation being performed\n    47\u2192\tGetOperationOptions() runtime.Object\n    48\u2192\t// IsDryRun indicates that modifications will definitely not be persisted for this request. This is to prevent\n    49\u2192\t// admission controllers with side effects and a method of reconciliation from being overwhelmed.\n    50\u2192\t// However, a value of false for this does not mean that the modification will be persisted, because it\n    51\u2192\t// could still be rejected by a subsequent validation step.\n    52\u2192\tIsDryRun() bool\n    53\u2192\t// GetObject is the object from the incoming request prior to default values being applied\n    54\u2192\tGetObject() runtime.Object\n    55\u2192\t// GetOldObject is the existing object. Only populated for UPDATE and DELETE requests.\n    56\u2192\tGetOldObject() runtime.Object\n    57\u2192\t// GetKind is the type of object being manipulated.  For example: Pod\n    58\u2192\tGetKind() schema.GroupVersionKind\n    59\u2192\t// GetUserInfo is information about the requesting user\n    60\u2192\tGetUserInfo() user.Info\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\t// AddAnnotation sets annotation according to key-value pair. The key should be qualified, e.g., podsecuritypolicy.admission.k8s.io/admit-policy, where\n    63\u2192\t// \"podsecuritypolicy\" is the name of the plugin, \"admission.k8s.io\" is the name of the organization, \"admit-policy\" is the key name.\n    64\u2192\t// An error is returned if the format of key is invalid. When trying to overwrite annotation with a new value, an error is returned.\n    65\u2192\t// Both ValidationInterface and MutationInterface are allowed to add Annotations.\n    66\u2192\t// By default, an annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or\n    67\u2192\t// equal to Metadata.\n    68\u2192\tAddAnnotation(key, value string) error\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192\t// AddAnnotationWithLevel sets annotation according to key-value pair with additional intended audit level.\n    71\u2192\t// An Annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or equal to the\n    72\u2192\t// intended audit level.\n    73\u2192\tAddAnnotationWithLevel(key, value string, level auditinternal.Level) error\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192\t// GetReinvocationContext tracks the admission request information relevant to the re-invocation policy.\n    76\u2192\tGetReinvocationContext() ReinvocationContext\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192// ObjectInterfaces is an interface used by AdmissionController to get object interfaces\n    80\u2192// such as Converter or Defaulter. These interfaces are normally coming from Request Scope\n    81\u2192// to handle special cases like CRDs.\n    82\u2192type ObjectInterfaces interface {\n    83\u2192\t// GetObjectCreater is the ObjectCreator appropriate for the requested object.\n    84\u2192\tGetObjectCreater() runtime.ObjectCreater\n    85\u2192\t// GetObjectTyper is the ObjectTyper appropriate for the requested object.\n    86\u2192\tGetObjectTyper() runtime.ObjectTyper\n    87\u2192\t// GetObjectDefaulter is the ObjectDefaulter appropriate for the requested object.\n    88\u2192\tGetObjectDefaulter() runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n    89\u2192\t// GetObjectConvertor is the ObjectConvertor appropriate for the requested object.\n    90\u2192\tGetObjectConvertor() runtime.ObjectConvertor\n    91\u2192\t// GetEquivalentResourceMapper is the EquivalentResourceMapper appropriate for finding equivalent resources and expected kind for the requested object.\n    92\u2192\tGetEquivalentResourceMapper() runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\n    93\u2192}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192// privateAnnotationsGetter is a private interface which allows users to get annotations from Attributes.\n    96\u2192type privateAnnotationsGetter interface {\n    97\u2192\tgetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192// AnnotationsGetter allows users to get annotations from Attributes. An alternate Attribute should implement\n   101\u2192// this interface.\n   102\u2192type AnnotationsGetter interface {\n   103\u2192\tGetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192// ReinvocationContext provides access to the admission related state required to implement the re-invocation policy.\n   107\u2192type ReinvocationContext interface {\n   108\u2192\t// IsReinvoke returns true if the current admission check is a re-invocation.\n   109\u2192\tIsReinvoke() bool\n   110\u2192\t// SetIsReinvoke sets the current admission check as a re-invocation.\n   111\u2192\tSetIsReinvoke()\n   112\u2192\t// ShouldReinvoke returns true if any plugin has requested a re-invocation.\n   113\u2192\tShouldReinvoke() bool\n   114\u2192\t// SetShouldReinvoke signals that a re-invocation is desired.\n   115\u2192\tSetShouldReinvoke()\n   116\u2192\t// AddValue set a value for a plugin name, possibly overriding a previous value.\n   117\u2192\tSetValue(plugin string, v interface{})\n   118\u2192\t// Value reads a value for a webhook.\n   119\u2192\tValue(plugin string) interface{}\n   120\u2192}\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192// Interface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\n   123\u2192type Interface interface {\n   124\u2192\t// Handles returns true if this admission controller can handle the given operation\n   125\u2192\t// where operation can be one of CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, or CONNECT\n   126\u2192\tHandles(operation Operation) bool\n   127\u2192}\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192type MutationInterface interface {\n   130\u2192\tInterface\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192\t// Admit makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.\n   133\u2192\t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\n   134\u2192\tAdmit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\n   135\u2192}\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192// ValidationInterface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\n   138\u2192type ValidationInterface interface {\n   139\u2192\tInterface\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192\t// Validate makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.  It is NOT allowed to mutate\n   142\u2192\t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\n   143\u2192\tValidate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\n   144\u2192}\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192// Operation is the type of resource operation being checked for admission control\n   147\u2192type Operation string\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192// Operation constants\n   150\u2192const (\n   151\u2192\tCreate  Operation = \"CREATE\"\n   152\u2192\tUpdate  Operation = \"UPDATE\"\n   153\u2192\tDelete  Operation = \"DELETE\"\n   154\u2192\tConnect Operation = \"CONNECT\"\n   155\u2192)\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192// PluginInitializer is used for initialization of shareable resources between admission plugins.\n   158\u2192// After initialization the resources have to be set separately\n   159\u2192type PluginInitializer interface {\n   160\u2192\tInitialize(plugin Interface)\n   161\u2192}\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192// InitializationValidator holds ValidateInitialization functions, which are responsible for validation of initialized\n   164\u2192// shared resources and should be implemented on admission plugins\n   165\u2192type InitializationValidator interface {\n   166\u2192\tValidateInitialization() error\n   167\u2192}\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192// ConfigProvider provides a way to get configuration for an admission plugin based on its name\n   170\u2192type ConfigProvider interface {\n   171\u2192\tConfigFor(pluginName string) (io.Reader, error)\n   172\u2192}\n   173\u2192"

17\u2192package registry\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    23\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    24\u2192\t\"time\"\n 32\u2192\tmetainternalversion \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\"\n    33\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    38\u2192\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/validation/field\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n    43\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager\"\n    44\u2192\tgenericapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    46\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\"\n    49\u2192\tstoreerr \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/errors\"\n    50\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/metrics\"\n    51\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/dryrun\"\n    52\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    53\u2192\tflowcontrolrequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/request\"\n    54\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    57\u2192)\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192// FinishFunc is a function returned by Begin hooks to complete an operation.\n    60\u2192type FinishFunc func(ctx context.Context, success bool)\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192// AfterDeleteFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterDelete hook.\n    63\u2192type AfterDeleteFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.DeleteOptions)\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192// BeginCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginCreate hook.\n    66\u2192type BeginCreateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (FinishFunc, error)\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// AfterCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterCreate hook.\n    69\u2192type AfterCreateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions)\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192// BeginUpdateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginUpdate hook.\n    72\u2192type BeginUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (FinishFunc, error)\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192// AfterUpdateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterUpdate hook.\n    75\u2192type AfterUpdateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.UpdateOptions)\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192// GenericStore interface can be used for type assertions when we need to access the underlying strategies.\n    78\u2192type GenericStore interface {\n    79\u2192\tGetCreateStrategy() rest.RESTCreateStrategy\n    80\u2192\tGetUpdateStrategy() rest.RESTUpdateStrategy\n    81\u2192\tGetDeleteStrategy() rest.RESTDeleteStrategy\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192// Store implements k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest.StandardStorage. It's\n    85\u2192// intended to be embeddable and allows the consumer to implement any\n    86\u2192// non-generic functions that are required. This object is intended to be\n    87\u2192// copyable so that it can be used in different ways but share the same\n    88\u2192// underlying behavior.\n    89\u2192//\n    90\u2192// All fields are required unless specified.\n    91\u2192//\n    92\u2192// The intended use of this type is embedding within a Kind specific\n    93\u2192// RESTStorage implementation. This type provides CRUD semantics on a Kubelike\n    94\u2192// resource, handling details like conflict detection with ResourceVersion and\n    95\u2192// semantics. The RESTCreateStrategy, RESTUpdateStrategy, and\n    96\u2192// RESTDeleteStrategy are generic across all backends, and encapsulate logic\n    97\u2192// specific to the API.\n    98\u2192//\n    99\u2192// TODO: make the default exposed methods exactly match a generic RESTStorage\n   100\u2192type Store struct {\n   101\u2192\t// NewFunc returns a new instance of the type this registry returns for a\n   102\u2192\t// GET of a single object, e.g.:\n   103\u2192\t//\n   104\u2192\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource/name-of-object\n   105\u2192\tNewFunc func() runtime.Object\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192\t// NewListFunc returns a new list of the type this registry; it is the\n   108\u2192\t// type returned when the resource is listed, e.g.:\n   109\u2192\t//\n   110\u2192\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource\n   111\u2192\tNewListFunc func() runtime.Object\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192\t// DefaultQualifiedResource is the pluralized name of the resource.\n   114\u2192\t// This field is used if there is no request info present in the context.\n   115\u2192\t// See qualifiedResourceFromContext for details.\n   116\u2192\tDefaultQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192\t// SingularQualifiedResource is the singular name of the resource.\n   119\u2192\tSingularQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192\t// KeyRootFunc returns the root etcd key for this resource; should not\n   122\u2192\t// include trailing \"/\".  This is used for operations that work on the\n   123\u2192\t// entire collection (listing and watching).\n   124\u2192\t//\n   125\u2192\t// KeyRootFunc and KeyFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\n   126\u2192\tKeyRootFunc func(ctx context.Context) string\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192\t// KeyFunc returns the key for a specific object in the collection.\n   129\u2192\t// KeyFunc is called for Create/Update/Get/Delete. Note that 'namespace'\n   130\u2192\t// can be gotten from ctx.\n   131\u2192\t//\n   132\u2192\t// KeyFunc and KeyRootFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\n   133\u2192\tKeyFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string) (string, error)\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192\t// ObjectNameFunc returns the name of an object or an error.\n   136\u2192\tObjectNameFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error)\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192\t// TTLFunc returns the TTL (time to live) that objects should be persisted\n   139\u2192\t// with. The existing parameter is the current TTL or the default for this\n   140\u2192\t// operation. The update parameter indicates whether this is an operation\n   141\u2192\t// against an existing object.\n   142\u2192\t//\n   143\u2192\t// Objects that are persisted with a TTL are evicted once the TTL expires.\n   144\u2192\tTTLFunc func(obj runtime.Object, existing uint64, update bool) (uint64, error)\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192\t// PredicateFunc returns a matcher corresponding to the provided labels\n   147\u2192\t// and fields. The SelectionPredicate returned should return true if the\n   148\u2192\t// object matches the given field and label selectors.\n   149\u2192\tPredicateFunc func(label labels.Selector, field fields.Selector) storage.SelectionPredicate\n   150\u2192"

either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package authorizer\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    22\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n    27\u2192)\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192// Attributes is an interface used by an Authorizer to get information about a request\n    30\u2192// that is used to make an authorization decision.\n    31\u2192type Attributes interface {\n    32\u2192\t// GetUser returns the user.Info object to authorize\n    33\u2192\tGetUser() user.Info\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\t// GetVerb returns the kube verb associated with API requests (this includes get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete, deletecollection, and proxy),\n    36\u2192\t// or the lowercased HTTP verb associated with non-API requests (this includes get, put, post, patch, and delete)\n    37\u2192\tGetVerb() string\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192\t// When IsReadOnly() == true, the request has no side effects, other than\n    40\u2192\t// caching, logging, and other incidentals.\n    41\u2192\tIsReadOnly() bool\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192\t// The namespace of the object, if a request is for a REST object.\n    44\u2192\tGetNamespace() string\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\t// The kind of object, if a request is for a REST object.\n    47\u2192\tGetResource() string\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192\t// GetSubresource returns the subresource being requested, if present\n    50\u2192\tGetSubresource() string\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192\t// GetName returns the name of the object as parsed off the request.  This will not be present for all request types, but\n    53\u2192\t// will be present for: get, update, delete\n    54\u2192\tGetName() string\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192\t// The group of the resource, if a request is for a REST object.\n    57\u2192\tGetAPIGroup() string\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192\t// GetAPIVersion returns the version of the group requested, if a request is for a REST object.\n    60\u2192\tGetAPIVersion() string\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\t// IsResourceRequest returns true for requests to API resources, like /api/v1/nodes,\n    63\u2192\t// and false for non-resource endpoints like /api, /healthz\n    64\u2192\tIsResourceRequest() bool\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192\t// GetPath returns the path of the request\n    67\u2192\tGetPath() string\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192\t// ParseFieldSelector is lazy, thread-safe, and stores the parsed result and error.\n    70\u2192\t// It returns an error if the field selector cannot be parsed.\n    71\u2192\t// The returned requirements must be treated as readonly and not modified.\n    72\u2192\tGetFieldSelector() (fields.Requirements, error)\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192\t// ParseLabelSelector is lazy, thread-safe, and stores the parsed result and error.\n    75\u2192\t// It returns an error if the label selector cannot be parsed.\n    76\u2192\t// The returned requirements must be treated as readonly and not modified.\n    77\u2192\tGetLabelSelector() (labels.Requirements, error)\n    78\u2192}\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192// Authorizer makes an authorization decision based on information gained by making\n    81\u2192// zero or more calls to methods of the Attributes interface.  It returns nil when an action is\n    82\u2192// authorized, otherwise it returns an error.\n    83\u2192type Authorizer interface {\n    84\u2192\tAuthorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (authorized Decision, reason string, err error)\n    85\u2192}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192type AuthorizerFunc func(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error)\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192func (f AuthorizerFunc) Authorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error) {\n    90\u2192\treturn f(ctx, a)\n    91\u2192}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192// RuleResolver provides a mechanism for resolving the list of rules that apply to a given user within a namespace.\n    94\u2192type RuleResolver interface {\n    95\u2192\t// RulesFor get the list of cluster wide rules, the list of rules in the specific namespace, incomplete status and errors.\n    96\u2192\tRulesFor(ctx context.Context, user user.Info, namespace string) ([]ResourceRuleInfo, []NonResourceRuleInfo, bool, error)\n    97\u2192}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192// RequestAttributesGetter provides a function that extracts Attributes from an http.Request\n   100\u2192type RequestAttributesGetter interface {\n   101\u2192\tGetRequestAttributes(user.Info, *http.Request) Attributes\n   102\u2192}\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192// AttributesRecord implements Attributes interface.\n   105\u2192type AttributesRecord struct {\n   106\u2192\tUser            user.Info\n   107\u2192\tVerb            string\n   108\u2192\tNamespace       string\n   109\u2192\tAPIGroup        string\n   110\u2192\tAPIVersion      string\n   111\u2192\tResource        string\n   112\u2192\tSubresource     string\n   113\u2192\tName            string\n   114\u2192\tResourceRequest bool\n   115\u2192\tPath            string\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192\tFieldSelectorRequirements fields.Requirements\n   118\u2192\tFieldSelectorParsingErr   error\n   119\u2192\tLabelSelectorRequirements labels.Requirements\n   120\u2192\tLabelSelectorParsingErr   error\n   121\u2192}\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetUser() user.Info {\n   124\u2192\treturn a.User\n   125\u2192}\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetVerb() string {\n   128\u2192\treturn a.Verb\n   129\u2192}\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) IsReadOnly() bool {\n   132\u2192\treturn a.Verb == \"get\" || a.Verb == \"list\" || a.Verb == \"watch\"\n   133\u2192}\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetNamespace() string {\n   136\u2192\treturn a.Namespace\n   137\u2192}\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetResource() string {\n   140\u2192\treturn a.Resource\n   141\u2192}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetSubresource() string {\n   144\u2192\treturn a.Subresource\n   145\u2192}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetName() string {\n   148\u2192\treturn a.Name\n   149\u2192}\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetAPIGroup() string {\n   152\u2192\treturn a.APIGroup\n   153\u2192}\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetAPIVersion() string {\n   156\u2192\treturn a.APIVersion\n   157\u2192}\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) IsResourceRequest() bool {\n   160\u2192\treturn a.ResourceRequest\n   161\u2192}\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetPath() string {\n   164\u2192\treturn a.Path\n   165\u2192}\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetFieldSelector() (fields.Requirements, error) {\n   168\u2192\treturn a.FieldSelectorRequirements, a.FieldSelectorParsingErr\n   169\u2192}\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192func (a AttributesRecord) GetLabelSelector() (labels.Requirements, error) {\n   172\u2192\treturn a.LabelSelectorRequirements, a.LabelSelectorParsingErr\n   173\u2192}\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192type Decision int\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192const (\n   178\u2192\t// DecisionDeny means that an authorizer decided to deny the action.\n   179\u2192\tDecisionDeny Decision = iota\n   180\u2192\t// DecisionAllow means that an authorizer decided to allow the action.\n   181\u2192\tDecisionAllow\n   182\u2192\t// DecisionNoOpinion means that an authorizer has no opinion on whether\n   183\u2192\t// to allow or deny an action.\n   184\u2192\tDecisionNoOpinion\n   185\u2192)\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192func (d Decision) String() string {\n   188\u2192\tswitch d {\n   189\u2192\tcase DecisionDeny:\n   190\u2192\t\treturn \"Deny\"\n   191\u2192\tcase DecisionAllow:\n   192\u2192\t\treturn \"Allow\"\n   193\u2192\tcase DecisionNoOpinion:\n   194\u2192\t\treturn \"NoOpinion\"\n   195\u2192\tdefault:\n   196\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Unknown (%d)\", int(d))\n   197\u2192\t}\n   198\u2192}\n   199\u2192"

Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package bearertoken\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    21\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    22\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning\"\n    26\u2192)\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192const (\n    29\u2192\tinvalidTokenWithSpaceWarning = \"the provided Authorization header contains extra space before the bearer token, and is ignored\"\n    30\u2192)\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192type Authenticator struct {\n    33\u2192\tauth authenticator.Token\n    34\u2192}\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192func New(auth authenticator.Token) *Authenticator {\n    37\u2192\treturn &Authenticator{auth}\n    38\u2192}\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192var invalidToken = errors.New(\"invalid bearer token\")\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192func (a *Authenticator) AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*authenticator.Response, bool, error) {\n    43\u2192\tauth := strings.TrimSpace(req.Header.Get(\"Authorization\"))\n    44\u2192\tif auth == \"\" {\n    45\u2192\t\treturn nil, false, nil\n    46\u2192\t}\n    47\u2192\tparts := strings.SplitN(auth, \" \", 3)\n    48\u2192\tif len(parts) < 2 || strings.ToLower(parts[0]) != \"bearer\" {\n    49\u2192\t\treturn nil, false, nil\n    50\u2192\t}\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192\ttoken := parts[1]\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192\t// Empty bearer tokens aren't valid\n    55\u2192\tif len(token) == 0 {\n    56\u2192\t\t// The space before the token case\n    57\u2192\t\tif len(parts) == 3 {\n    58\u2192\t\t\twarning.AddWarning(req.Context(), \"\", invalidTokenWithSpaceWarning)\n    59\u2192\t\t}\n    60\u2192\t\treturn nil, false, nil\n    61\u2192\t}\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192\tresp, ok, err := a.auth.AuthenticateToken(req.Context(), token)\n    64\u2192\t// if we authenticated successfully, go ahead and remove the bearer token so that no one\n    65\u2192\t// is ever tempted to use it inside of the API server\n    66\u2192\tif ok {\n    67\u2192\t\treq.Header.Del(\"Authorization\")\n    68\u2192\t}\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192\t// If the token authenticator didn't error, provide a default error\n    71\u2192\tif !ok && err == nil {\n    72\u2192\t\terr = invalidToken\n    73\u2192\t}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192\treturn resp, ok, err\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192"

{
  "command": "find /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints -name \"*.go\" | grep -E \"(handlers|filter)\" | head -20",

either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package handlers\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"bytes\"\n    21\u2192\t\"context\"\n    22\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    23\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    24\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    25\u2192\t\"time\"\n    26\u2192\t\"unicode\"\n    27\u2192\t\"unicode/utf8\"\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\"\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    33\u2192\tmetainternalversionscheme \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion/scheme\"\n    34\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/validation\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/fieldmanager\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/finisher\"\n    42\u2192\trequestmetrics \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/metrics\"\n    43\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n    46\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/dryrun\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    49\u2192)\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192var namespaceGVR = schema.GroupVersionResource{Group: \"\", Version: \"v1\", Resource: \"namespaces\"}\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192func createHandler(r rest.NamedCreater, scope *RequestScope, admit admission.Interface, includeName bool) http.HandlerFunc {\n    54\u2192\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n    55\u2192\t\tctx := req.Context()\n    56\u2192\t\t// For performance tracking purposes.\n    57\u2192\t\tctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, \"Create\", traceFields(req)...)\n    58\u2192\t\treq = req.WithContext(ctx)\n    59\u2192\t\tdefer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192\t\tnamespace, name, err := scope.Namer.Name(req)\n    62\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    63\u2192\t\t\tif includeName {\n    64\u2192\t\t\t\t// name was required, return\n    65\u2192\t\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n    66\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n    67\u2192\t\t\t}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192\t\t\t// otherwise attempt to look up the namespace\n    70\u2192\t\t\tnamespace, err = scope.Namer.Namespace(req)\n    71\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n    72\u2192\t\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n    73\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n    74\u2192\t\t\t}\n    75\u2192\t\t}\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192\t\t// enforce a timeout of at most requestTimeoutUpperBound (34s) or less if the user-provided\n    78\u2192\t\t// timeout inside the parent context is lower than requestTimeoutUpperBound.\n    79\u2192\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, requestTimeoutUpperBound)\n    80\u2192\t\tdefer cancel()\n    81\u2192\t\toutputMediaType, _, err := negotiation.NegotiateOutputMediaType(req, scope.Serializer, scope)\n    82\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    83\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n    84\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    85\u2192\t\t}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\t\tgv := scope.Kind.GroupVersion()\n    88\u2192\t\ts, err := negotiation.NegotiateInputSerializer(req, false, scope.Serializer)\n    89\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    90\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n    91\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    92\u2192\t\t}\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192\t\tbody, err := limitedReadBodyWithRecordMetric(ctx, req, scope.MaxRequestBodyBytes, scope.Resource.GroupResource(), requestmetrics.Create)\n    95\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    96\u2192\t\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"limitedReadBody failed\", attribute.Int(\"len\", len(body)), attribute.String(\"err\", err.Error()))\n    97\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n    98\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    99\u2192\t\t}\n   100\u2192\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"limitedReadBody succeeded\", attribute.Int(\"len\", len(body)))\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192\t\toptions := &metav1.CreateOptions{}\n   103\u2192\t\tvalues := req.URL.Query()\n   104\u2192\t\tif err := metainternalversionscheme.ParameterCodec.DecodeParameters(values, scope.MetaGroupVersion, options); err != nil {\n   105\u2192\t\t\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\n   106\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   107\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   108\u2192\t\t}\n   109\u2192\t\tif errs := validation.ValidateCreateOptions(options); len(errs) > 0 {\n   110\u2192\t\t\terr := errors.NewInvalid(schema.GroupKind{Group: metav1.GroupName, Kind: \"CreateOptions\"}, \"\", errs)\n   111\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   112\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   113\u2192\t\t}\n   114\u2192\t\toptions.TypeMeta.SetGroupVersionKind(metav1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(\"CreateOptions\"))\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192\t\tdefaultGVK := scope.Kind\n   117\u2192\t\toriginal := r.New()\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192\t\tvalidationDirective := fieldValidation(options.FieldValidation)\n   120\u2192\t\tdecodeSerializer := s.Serializer\n   121\u2192\t\tif validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn || validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationStrict {\n   122\u2192\t\t\tdecodeSerializer = s.StrictSerializer\n   123\u2192\t\t}\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192\t\tdecoder := scope.Serializer.DecoderToVersion(decodeSerializer, scope.HubGroupVersion)\n   126\u2192\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"About to convert to expected version\")\n   127\u2192\t\tobj, gvk, err := decoder.Decode(body, &defaultGVK, original)\n   128\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   129\u2192\t\t\tstrictError, isStrictError := runtime.AsStrictDecodingError(err)\n   130\u2192\t\t\tswitch {\n   131\u2192\t\t\tcase isStrictError && obj != nil && validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationWarn:\n   132\u2192\t\t\t\taddStrictDecodingWarnings(req.Context(), strictError.Errors())\n   133\u2192\t\t\tcase isStrictError && validationDirective == metav1.FieldValidationIgnore:\n   134\u2192\t\t\t\tklog.Warningf(\"unexpected strict error when field validation is set to ignore\")\n   135\u2192\t\t\t\tfallthrough\n   136\u2192\t\t\tdefault:\n   137\u2192\t\t\t\terr = transformDecodeError(scope.Typer, err, original, gvk, body)\n   138\u2192\t\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   139\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   140\u2192\t\t\t}\n   141\u2192\t\t}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192\t\tobjGV := gvk.GroupVersion()\n   144\u2192\t\tif !scope.AcceptsGroupVersion(objGV) {\n   145\u2192\t\t\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\"the API version in the data (%s) does not match the expected API version (%v)\", objGV.String(), gv.String()))\n   146\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   147\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   148\u2192\t\t}\n   149\u2192\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"Conversion done\")\n   150\u2192"
135. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authentication.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    22\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    23\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    24\u2192\t\"time\"\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticatorfactory\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/headerrequest\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n    34\u2192\tgenericapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    35\u2192\tgenericfeatures \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    36\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    38\u2192)\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192type authenticationRecordMetricsFunc func(context.Context, *authenticator.Response, bool, error, authenticator.Audiences, time.Time, time.Time)\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192// WithAuthentication creates an http handler that tries to authenticate the given request as a user, and then\n    43\u2192// stores any such user found onto the provided context for the request. If authentication fails or returns an error\n    44\u2192// the failed handler is used. On success, \"Authorization\" header is removed from the request and handler\n    45\u2192// is invoked to serve the request.\n    46\u2192func WithAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig) http.Handler {\n    47\u2192\treturn withAuthentication(handler, auth, failed, apiAuds, requestHeaderConfig, recordAuthenticationMetrics)\n    48\u2192}\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192func withAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig, metrics authenticationRecordMetricsFunc) http.Handler {\n    51\u2192\tif auth == nil {\n    52\u2192\t\tklog.Warning(\"Authentication is disabled\")\n    53\u2192\t\treturn handler\n    54\u2192\t}\n    55\u2192\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig := &authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig{\n    56\u2192\t\tUsernameHeaders:     headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-User\"},\n    57\u2192\t\tUIDHeaders:          headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-Uid\"},\n    58\u2192\t\tGroupHeaders:        headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-Group\"},\n    59\u2192\t\tExtraHeaderPrefixes: headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-Extra-\"},\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n    62\u2192\t\tauthenticationStart := time.Now()\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192\t\tif len(apiAuds) > 0 {\n    65\u2192\t\t\treq = req.WithContext(authenticator.WithAudiences(req.Context(), apiAuds))\n    66\u2192\t\t}\n    67\u2192\t\tresp, ok, err := auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)\n    68\u2192\t\tauthenticationFinish := time.Now()\n    69\u2192\t\tgenericapirequest.TrackAuthenticationLatency(req.Context(), authenticationFinish.Sub(authenticationStart))\n    70\u2192\t\tdefer func() {\n    71\u2192\t\t\tmetrics(req.Context(), resp, ok, err, apiAuds, authenticationStart, authenticationFinish)\n    72\u2192\t\t}()\n    73\u2192\t\tif err != nil || !ok {\n    74\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n    75\u2192\t\t\t\tklog.ErrorS(err, \"Unable to authenticate the request\")\n    76\u2192\t\t\t}\n    77\u2192\t\t\tfailed.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    78\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    79\u2192\t\t}\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t\tif !audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, resp.Audiences) {\n    82\u2192\t\t\terr = fmt.Errorf(\"unable to match the audience: %v , accepted: %v\", resp.Audiences, apiAuds)\n    83\u2192\t\t\tklog.Error(err)\n    84\u2192\t\t\tfailed.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    85\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    86\u2192\t\t}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192\t\t// authorization header is not required anymore in case of a successful authentication.\n    89\u2192\t\treq.Header.Del(\"Authorization\")\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192\t\t// delete standard front proxy headers\n    92\u2192\t\theaderrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(\n    93\u2192\t\t\treq.Header,\n    94\u2192\t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,\n    95\u2192\t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,\n    96\u2192\t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,\n    97\u2192\t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,\n    98\u2192\t\t)\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192\t\t// also delete any custom front proxy headers\n   101\u2192\t\tif requestHeaderConfig != nil {\n   102\u2192\t\t\theaderrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(\n   103\u2192\t\t\t\treq.Header,\n   104\u2192\t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,\n   105\u2192\t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,\n   106\u2192\t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,\n   107\u2192\t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,\n   108\u2192\t\t\t)\n   109\u2192\t\t}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192\t\t// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,\n   112\u2192\t\t// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.\n   113\u2192\t\t// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these\n   114\u2192\t\t// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the\n   115\u2192\t\t// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).\n   116\u2192\t\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 && isAnonymousUser(resp.User) {\n   117\u2192\t\t\t// limit this connection to just this request,\n   118\u2192\t\t\t// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection\n   119\u2192\t\t\t// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c\n   120\u2192\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Connection\", \"close\")\n   121\u2192\t\t}\n   122\u2192\t\treq = req.WithContext(genericapirequest.WithUser(req.Context(), resp.User))\n   123\u2192\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n   124\u2192\t})\n   125\u2192}\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192func Unauthorized(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {\n   128\u2192\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n   129\u2192\t\t// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,\n   130\u2192\t\t// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.\n   131\u2192\t\t// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these\n   132\u2192\t\t// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the\n   133\u2192\t\t// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).\n   134\u2192\t\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 {\n   135\u2192\t\t\t// limit this connection to just this request,\n   136\u2192\t\t\t// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection\n   137\u2192\t\t\t// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c\n   138\u2192\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Connection\", \"close\")\n   139\u2192\t\t}\n   140\u2192\t\tctx := req.Context()\n   141\u2192\t\trequestInfo, found := genericapirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n   142\u2192\t\tif !found {\n   143\u2192\t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New(\"no RequestInfo found in the context\"))\n   144\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   145\u2192\t\t}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192\t\tgv := schema.GroupVersion{Group: requestInfo.APIGroup, Version: requestInfo.APIVersion}\n   148\u2192\t\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewUnauthorized(\"Unauthorized\"), s, gv, w, req)\n   149\u2192\t})\n   150\u2192}\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192func audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, responseAudiences authenticator.Audiences) bool {\n   153\u2192\tif len(apiAuds) == 0 || len(responseAudiences) == 0 {\n   154\u2192\t\treturn true\n   155\u2192\t}\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192\treturn len(apiAuds.Intersect(responseAudiences)) > 0\n   158\u2192}\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192func isAnonymousUser(u user.Info) bool {\n   161\u2192\tif u.GetName() == user.Anonymous {\n   162\u2192\t\treturn true\n   163\u2192\t}\n   164\u2192\tfor _, group := range u.GetGroups() {\n   165\u2192\t\tif group == user.AllUnauthenticated {\n   166\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n   167\u2192\t\t}\n   168\u2192\t}\n   169\u2192\treturn false\n   170\u2192}\n   171\u2192"
136. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authorization.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package filters\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    22\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    23\u2192\t\"time\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n    27\u2192\tgenericfeatures \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    28\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n    34\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    37\u2192)\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192const (\n    40\u2192\t// Annotation key names set in advanced audit\n    41\u2192\tdecisionAnnotationKey = \"authorization.k8s.io/decision\"\n    42\u2192\treasonAnnotationKey   = \"authorization.k8s.io/reason\"\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192\t// Annotation values set in advanced audit\n    45\u2192\tdecisionAllow  = \"allow\"\n    46\u2192\tdecisionForbid = \"forbid\"\n    47\u2192\treasonError    = \"internal error\"\n    48\u2192)\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192type recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc func(ctx context.Context, authorized authorizer.Decision, err error, authStart time.Time, authFinish time.Time)\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192// WithAuthorization passes all authorized requests on to handler, and returns a forbidden error otherwise.\n    53\u2192func WithAuthorization(hhandler http.Handler, auth authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {\n    54\u2192\treturn withAuthorization(hhandler, auth, s, recordAuthorizationMetrics)\n    55\u2192}\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192func withAuthorization(handler http.Handler, a authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, metrics recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc) http.Handler {\n    58\u2192\tif a == nil {\n    59\u2192\t\tklog.Warning(\"Authorization is disabled\")\n    60\u2192\t\treturn handler\n    61\u2192\t}\n    62\u2192\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n    63\u2192\t\tctx := req.Context()\n    64\u2192\t\tauthorizationStart := time.Now()\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192\t\tattributes, err := GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)\n    67\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    68\u2192\t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)\n    69\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    70\u2192\t\t}\n    71\u2192\t\tauthorized, reason, err := a.Authorize(ctx, attributes)\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\t\tauthorizationFinish := time.Now()\n    74\u2192\t\trequest.TrackAuthorizationLatency(ctx, authorizationFinish.Sub(authorizationStart))\n    75\u2192\t\tdefer func() {\n    76\u2192\t\t\tmetrics(ctx, authorized, err, authorizationStart, authorizationFinish)\n    77\u2192\t\t}()\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192\t\t// an authorizer like RBAC could encounter evaluation errors and still allow the request, so authorizer decision is checked before error here.\n    80\u2192\t\tif authorized == authorizer.DecisionAllow {\n    81\u2192\t\t\taudit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,\n    82\u2192\t\t\t\tdecisionAnnotationKey, decisionAllow,\n    83\u2192\t\t\t\treasonAnnotationKey, reason)\n    84\u2192\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n    85\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    86\u2192\t\t}\n    87\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    88\u2192\t\t\taudit.AddAuditAnnotation(ctx, reasonAnnotationKey, reasonError)\n    89\u2192\t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)\n    90\u2192\t\t\treturn\n    91\u2192\t\t}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192\t\tklog.V(4).InfoS(\"Forbidden\", \"URI\", req.RequestURI, \"reason\", reason)\n    94\u2192\t\taudit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,\n    95\u2192\t\t\tdecisionAnnotationKey, decisionForbid,\n    96\u2192\t\t\treasonAnnotationKey, reason)\n    97\u2192\t\tresponsewriters.Forbidden(attributes, w, req, reason, s)\n    98\u2192\t})\n    99\u2192}\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192func GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx context.Context) (authorizer.Attributes, error) {\n   102\u2192\tattribs := authorizer.AttributesRecord{}\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192\tuser, ok := request.UserFrom(ctx)\n   105\u2192\tif ok {\n   106\u2192\t\tattribs.User = user\n   107\u2192\t}\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192\trequestInfo, found := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n   110\u2192\tif !found {\n   111\u2192\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"no RequestInfo found in the context\")\n   112\u2192\t}\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192\t// Start with common attributes that apply to resource and non-resource requests\n   115\u2192\tattribs.ResourceRequest = requestInfo.IsResourceRequest\n   116\u2192\tattribs.Path = requestInfo.Path\n   117\u2192\tattribs.Verb = requestInfo.Verb\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192\tattribs.APIGroup = requestInfo.APIGroup\n   120\u2192\tattribs.APIVersion = requestInfo.APIVersion\n   121\u2192\tattribs.Resource = requestInfo.Resource\n   122\u2192\tattribs.Subresource = requestInfo.Subresource\n   123\u2192\tattribs.Namespace = requestInfo.Namespace\n   124\u2192\tattribs.Name = requestInfo.Name\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.AuthorizeWithSelectors) {\n   127\u2192\t\t// parsing here makes it easy to keep the AttributesRecord type value-only and avoids any mutex copies when\n   128\u2192\t\t// doing shallow copies in other steps.\n   129\u2192\t\tif len(requestInfo.FieldSelector) > 0 {\n   130\u2192\t\t\tfieldSelector, err := fields.ParseSelector(requestInfo.FieldSelector)\n   131\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   132\u2192\t\t\t\tattribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = nil, err\n   133\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   134\u2192\t\t\t\tif requirements := fieldSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {\n   135\u2192\t\t\t\t\tattribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = fieldSelector.Requirements(), nil\n   136\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   137\u2192\t\t\t}\n   138\u2192\t\t}\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192\t\tif len(requestInfo.LabelSelector) > 0 {\n   141\u2192\t\t\tlabelSelector, err := labels.Parse(requestInfo.LabelSelector)\n   142\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   143\u2192\t\t\t\tattribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = nil, err\n   144\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   145\u2192\t\t\t\tif requirements, _ /*selectable*/ := labelSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {\n   146\u2192\t\t\t\t\tattribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = requirements, nil\n   147\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   148\u2192\t\t\t}\n   149\u2192\t\t}\n   150\u2192\t}\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192\treturn &attribs, nil\n   153\u2192}\n   154\u2192"

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go",

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package server\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    22\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    23\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    24\u2192\tgpath \"path\"\n    25\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    26\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    27\u2192\t\"time\"\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\tsystemd \"github.com/coreos/go-systemd/v22/daemon\"\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\t\"golang.org/x/time/rate\"\n    32\u2192\tapidiscoveryv2 \"k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2\"\n    33\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n    34\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    35\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    36\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    37\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer\"\n    38\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer/cbor\"\n    39\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n    40\u2192\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n    43\u2192\tutilwaitgroup \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/waitgroup\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n    46\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n    47\u2192\tgenericapi \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n    49\u2192\tdiscoveryendpoint \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery/aggregated\"\n    50\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    51\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n    52\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\"\n    53\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes\"\n    54\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/statusz\"\n    55\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n    56\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    57\u2192\trestclient \"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n    58\u2192\tbasecompatibility \"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\"\n    59\u2192\t\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\"\n    60\u2192\tzpagesfeatures \"k8s.io/component-base/zpages/features\"\n    61\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    62\u2192\topenapibuilder3 \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/builder3\"\n    63\u2192\topenapicommon \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\"\n    64\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/handler\"\n    65\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/handler3\"\n    66\u2192\topenapiutil \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/util\"\n    67\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\"\n    68\u2192)\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192// Info about an API group.\n    71\u2192type APIGroupInfo struct {\n    72\u2192\tPrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion\n    73\u2192\t// Info about the resources in this group. It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\n    74\u2192\tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\n    75\u2192\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\n    76\u2192\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\n    77\u2192\t// define a version \"v1beta1\" but want to use the Kubernetes \"v1\" internal objects.\n    78\u2192\t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\n    79\u2192\t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\n    80\u2192\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n    81\u2192\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \"meta.k8s.io/v1\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\n    82\u2192\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\n    83\u2192\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\n    84\u2192\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192\t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\n    87\u2192\t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\n    88\u2192\t// TODO: replace with interfaces\n    89\u2192\tScheme *runtime.Scheme\n    90\u2192\t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\n    91\u2192\tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n    92\u2192\t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\n    93\u2192\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\n    96\u2192\t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\n    97\u2192\tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192func (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\n   101\u2192\tfor _, stores := range a.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n   102\u2192\t\tfor _, store := range stores {\n   103\u2192\t\t\tstore.Destroy()\n   104\u2192\t\t}\n   105\u2192\t}\n   106\u2192}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192// GenericAPIServer contains state for a Kubernetes cluster api server.\n   109\u2192type GenericAPIServer struct {\n   110\u2192\t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\n   111\u2192\tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n   114\u2192\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192\t// minRequestTimeout is how short the request timeout can be.  This is used to build the RESTHandler\n   117\u2192\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192\t// ShutdownTimeout is the timeout used for server shutdown. This specifies the timeout before server\n   120\u2192\t// gracefully shutdown returns.\n   121\u2192\tShutdownTimeout time.Duration\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192\t// legacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\n   124\u2192\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup\n   125\u2192\tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192\t// admissionControl is used to build the RESTStorage that backs an API Group.\n   128\u2192\tadmissionControl admission.Interface\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192\t// SecureServingInfo holds configuration of the TLS server.\n   131\u2192\tSecureServingInfo *SecureServingInfo\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192\t// ExternalAddress is the address (hostname or IP and port) that should be used in\n   134\u2192\t// external (public internet) URLs for this GenericAPIServer.\n   135\u2192\tExternalAddress string\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192\t// Serializer controls how common API objects not in a group/version prefix are serialized for this server.\n   138\u2192\t// Individual APIGroups may define their own serializers.\n   139\u2192\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192\t// \"Outputs\"\n   142\u2192\t// Handler holds the handlers being used by this API server\n   143\u2192\tHandler *APIServerHandler\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192\t// UnprotectedDebugSocket is used to serve pprof information in a unix-domain socket. This socket is\n   146\u2192\t// not protected by authentication/authorization.\n   147\u2192\tUnprotectedDebugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192\t// listedPathProvider is a lister which provides the set of paths to show at /\n   150\u2192\tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192\t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\n   153\u2192\tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\n   156\u2192\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192\t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\n   159\u2192\tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192\t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\n   162\u2192\tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\n   165\u2192\topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\n   168\u2192\topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\n   171\u2192\t// during PrepareRun.\n   172\u2192\t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\n   173\u2192\t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\n   174\u2192\tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192\t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\n   177\u2192\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n   178\u2192\tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192\t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\n   181\u2192\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n   182\u2192\tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\n   185\u2192\t// It is set during PrepareRun.\n   186\u2192\tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192\t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\n   189\u2192\t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\n   190\u2192\t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\n   191\u2192\tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\n   192\u2192\tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\n   193\u2192\tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\n   194\u2192\tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192\tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\n   197\u2192\tpreShutdownHooks       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n   198\u2192\tpreShutdownHooksCalled bool\n   199\u2192\n   200\u2192\t// healthz checks\n   201\u2192\thealthzRegistry healthCheckRegistry\n   202\u2192\treadyzRegistry  healthCheckRegistry\n   203\u2192\tlivezRegistry   healthCheckRegistry\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192\tlivezGracePeriod time.Duration\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192\t// auditing. The backend is started before the server starts listening.\n   208\u2192\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192\t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\n   211\u2192\t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\n   212\u2192\t// the create-on-update case\n   213\u2192\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192\t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\n   216\u2192\t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\n   217\u2192\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192\t// delegationTarget is the next delegate in the chain. This is never nil.\n   220\u2192\tdelegationTarget DelegationTarget\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\n   223\u2192\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\n   224\u2192\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\n   225\u2192\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\n   226\u2192\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\n   227\u2192\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\n   228\u2192\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\n   229\u2192\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\n   232\u2192\t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\n   233\u2192\t// but /readyz will return failure.\n   234\u2192\tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192\t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\n   237\u2192\t// 0 means no limit.\n   238\u2192\tmaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192\t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\n   241\u2192\tAPIServerID string\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192\t// StorageReadinessHook implements post-start-hook functionality for checking readiness\n   244\u2192\t// of underlying storage for registered resources.\n   245\u2192\tStorageReadinessHook *StorageReadinessHook\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192\t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\n   248\u2192\tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available"
138. Bash @ -
