Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package server\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"errors\"\n    22\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    23\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    24\u2192\t\"runtime/debug\"\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192\tutilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n    27\u2192\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\"\n    29\u2192\trestclient \"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    31\u2192)\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192// PostStartHookFunc is a function that is called after the server has started.\n    34\u2192// It must properly handle cases like:\n    35\u2192//  1. asynchronous start in multiple API server processes\n    36\u2192//  2. conflicts between the different processes all trying to perform the same action\n    37\u2192//  3. partially complete work (API server crashes while running your hook)\n    38\u2192//  4. API server access **BEFORE** your hook has completed\n    39\u2192//\n    40\u2192// Think of it like a mini-controller that is super privileged and gets to run in-process\n    41\u2192// If you use this feature, tag @deads2k on github who has promised to review code for anyone's PostStartHook\n    42\u2192// until it becomes easier to use.\n    43\u2192type PostStartHookFunc func(context PostStartHookContext) error\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192// PreShutdownHookFunc is a function that can be added to the shutdown logic.\n    46\u2192type PreShutdownHookFunc func() error\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192// PostStartHookContext provides information about this API server to a PostStartHookFunc\n    49\u2192type PostStartHookContext struct {\n    50\u2192\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n    51\u2192\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n    52\u2192\t// Context gets cancelled when the server stops.\n    53\u2192\tcontext.Context\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192// PostStartHookProvider is an interface in addition to provide a post start hook for the api server\n    57\u2192type PostStartHookProvider interface {\n    58\u2192\tPostStartHook() (string, PostStartHookFunc, error)\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192type postStartHookEntry struct {\n    62\u2192\thook PostStartHookFunc\n    63\u2192\t// originatingStack holds the stack that registered postStartHooks. This allows us to show a more helpful message\n    64\u2192\t// for duplicate registration.\n    65\u2192\toriginatingStack string\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\t// done will be closed when the postHook is finished\n    68\u2192\tdone chan struct{}\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192type PostStartHookConfigEntry struct {\n    72\u2192\thook PostStartHookFunc\n    73\u2192\t// originatingStack holds the stack that registered postStartHooks. This allows us to show a more helpful message\n    74\u2192\t// for duplicate registration.\n    75\u2192\toriginatingStack string\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192type preShutdownHookEntry struct {\n    79\u2192\thook PreShutdownHookFunc\n    80\u2192}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192// AddPostStartHook allows you to add a PostStartHook.\n    83\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) AddPostStartHook(name string, hook PostStartHookFunc) error {\n    84\u2192\tif len(name) == 0 {\n    85\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"missing name\")\n    86\u2192\t}\n    87\u2192\tif hook == nil {\n    88\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"hook func may not be nil: %q\", name)\n    89\u2192\t}\n    90\u2192\tif s.disabledPostStartHooks.Has(name) {\n    91\u2192\t\tklog.V(1).Infof(\"skipping %q because it was explicitly disabled\", name)\n    92\u2192\t\treturn nil\n    93\u2192\t}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\ts.postStartHookLock.Lock()\n    96\u2192\tdefer s.postStartHookLock.Unlock()\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192\tif s.postStartHooksCalled {\n    99\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to add %q because PostStartHooks have already been called\", name)\n   100\u2192\t}\n   101\u2192\tif postStartHook, exists := s.postStartHooks[name]; exists {\n   102\u2192\t\t// this is programmer error, but it can be hard to debug\n   103\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to add %q because it was already registered by: %s\", name, postStartHook.originatingStack)\n   104\u2192\t}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192\t// done is closed when the poststarthook is finished.  This is used by the health check to be able to indicate\n   107\u2192\t// that the poststarthook is finished\n   108\u2192\tdone := make(chan struct{})\n   109\u2192\tif err := s.AddBootSequenceHealthChecks(postStartHookHealthz{name: \"poststarthook/\" + name, done: done}); err != nil {\n   110\u2192\t\treturn err\n   111\u2192\t}\n   112\u2192\ts.postStartHooks[name] = postStartHookEntry{hook: hook, originatingStack: string(debug.Stack()), done: done}\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192\treturn nil\n   115\u2192}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192// AddPostStartHookOrDie allows you to add a PostStartHook, but dies on failure\n   118\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) AddPostStartHookOrDie(name string, hook PostStartHookFunc) {\n   119\u2192\tif err := s.AddPostStartHook(name, hook); err != nil {\n   120\u2192\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"Error registering PostStartHook %q: %v\", name, err)\n   121\u2192\t}\n   122\u2192}\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192// AddPreShutdownHook allows you to add a PreShutdownHook.\n   125\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) AddPreShutdownHook(name string, hook PreShutdownHookFunc) error {\n   126\u2192\tif len(name) == 0 {\n   127\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"missing name\")\n   128\u2192\t}\n   129\u2192\tif hook == nil {\n   130\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   131\u2192\t}\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192\ts.preShutdownHookLock.Lock()\n   134\u2192\tdefer s.preShutdownHookLock.Unlock()\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192\tif s.preShutdownHooksCalled {\n   137\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to add %q because PreShutdownHooks have already been called\", name)\n   138\u2192\t}\n   139\u2192\tif _, exists := s.preShutdownHooks[name]; exists {\n   140\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to add %q because it is already registered\", name)\n   141\u2192\t}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192\ts.preShutdownHooks[name] = preShutdownHookEntry{hook: hook}\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192\treturn nil\n   146\u2192}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192// AddPreShutdownHookOrDie allows you to add a PostStartHook, but dies on failure\n   149\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) AddPreShutdownHookOrDie(name string, hook PreShutdownHookFunc) {\n   150\u2192\tif err := s.AddPreShutdownHook(name, hook); err != nil {\n   151\u2192\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"Error registering PreShutdownHook %q: %v\", name, err)\n   152\u2192\t}\n   153\u2192}\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192// RunPostStartHooks runs the PostStartHooks for the server.\n   156\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) RunPostStartHooks(ctx context.Context) {\n   157\u2192\ts.postStartHookLock.Lock()\n   158\u2192\tdefer s.postStartHookLock.Unlock()\n   159\u2192\ts.postStartHooksCalled = true\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192\tcontext := PostStartHookContext{\n   162\u2192\t\tLoopbackClientConfig: s.LoopbackClientConfig,\n   163\u2192\t\tContext:              ctx,\n   164\u2192\t}\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192\tfor hookName, hookEntry := range s.postStartHooks {\n   167\u2192\t\tgo runPostStartHook(hookName, hookEntry, context)\n   168\u2192\t}\n   169\u2192}\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192// RunPreShutdownHooks runs the PreShutdownHooks for the server\n   172\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) RunPreShutdownHooks() error {\n   173\u2192\tvar errorList []error\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192\ts.preShutdownHookLock.Lock()\n   176\u2192\tdefer s.preShutdownHookLock.Unlock()\n   177\u2192\ts.preShutdownHooksCalled = true\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192\tfor hookName, hookEntry := range s.preShutdownHooks {\n   180\u2192\t\tif err := runPreShutdownHook(hookName, hookEntry); err != nil {\n   181\u2192\t\t\terrorList = append(errorList, err)\n   182\u2192\t\t}\n   183\u2192\t}\n   184\u2192\treturn utilerrors.NewAggregate(errorList)\n   185\u2192}\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192// isPostStartHookRegistered checks whether a given PostStartHook is registered\n   188\u2192func (s *GenericAPIServer) isPostStartHookRegistered(name string) bool {\n   189\u2192\ts.postStartHookLock.Lock()\n   190\u2192\tdefer s.postStartHookLock.Unlock()\n   191\u2192\t_, exists := s.postStartHooks[name]\n   192\u2192\treturn exists\n   193\u2192}\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192func runPostStartHook(name string, entry postStartHookEntry, context PostStartHookContext) {\n   196\u2192\tvar err error\n   197\u2192\tfunc() {\n   198\u2192\t\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrashWithContext(context)\n   199\u2192\t\terr = entry.hook(context)\n   200\u2192\t}()\n   201\u2192\t// if the hook intentionally wants to kill server, let it.\n   202\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   203\u2192\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"PostStartHook %q failed: %v\", name, err)\n   204\u2192\t}\n   205\u2192\tclose(entry.done)\n   206\u2192}\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192func runPreShutdownHook(name string, entry preShutdownHookEntry) error {\n   209\u2192\tvar err error\n   210\u2192\tfunc() {\n   211\u2192\t\t// don't let the hook *accidentally* panic and kill the server\n   212\u2192\t\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrash()\n   213\u2192\t\terr = entry.hook()\n   214\u2192\t}()\n   215\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   216\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"PreShutdownHook %q failed: %v\", name, err)\n   217\u2192\t}\n   218\u2192\treturn nil\n   219\u2192}\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192// postStartHookHealthz implements a healthz check for poststarthooks.  It will return a \"hookNotFinished\"\n   222\u2192// error until the poststarthook is finished.\n   223\u2192type postStartHookHealthz struct {\n   224\u2192\tname string\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192\t// done will be closed when the postStartHook is finished\n   227\u2192\tdone chan struct{}\n   228\u2192}\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192var _ healthz.HealthChecker = postStartHookHealthz{}\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192func (h postStartHookHealthz) Name() string {\n   233\u2192\treturn h.name\n   234\u2192}\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192var errHookNotFinished = errors.New(\"not finished\")\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192func (h postStartHookHealthz) Check(req *http.Request) error {\n   239\u2192\tselect {\n   240\u2192\tcase <-h.done:\n   241\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   242\u2192\tdefault:\n   243\u2192\t\treturn errHookNotFinished\n   244\u2192\t}\n   245\u2192}\n   246\u2192"

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/storage_decorator.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package generic\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    21\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\"\n    22\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/storagebackend\"\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/storagebackend/factory\"\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n    25\u2192)\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192// StorageDecorator is a function signature for producing a storage.Interface\n    28\u2192// and an associated DestroyFunc from given parameters.\n    29\u2192type StorageDecorator func(\n    30\u2192\tconfig *storagebackend.ConfigForResource,\n    31\u2192\tresourcePrefix string,\n    32\u2192\tkeyFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error),\n    33\u2192\tnewFunc func() runtime.Object,\n    34\u2192\tnewListFunc func() runtime.Object,\n    35\u2192\tgetAttrsFunc storage.AttrFunc,\n    36\u2192\ttrigger storage.IndexerFuncs,\n    37\u2192\tindexers *cache.Indexers) (storage.Interface, factory.DestroyFunc, error)\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192// UndecoratedStorage returns the given a new storage from the given config\n    40\u2192// without any decoration.\n    41\u2192func UndecoratedStorage(\n    42\u2192\tconfig *storagebackend.ConfigForResource,\n    43\u2192\tresourcePrefix string,\n    44\u2192\tkeyFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error),\n    45\u2192\tnewFunc func() runtime.Object,\n    46\u2192\tnewListFunc func() runtime.Object,\n    47\u2192\tgetAttrsFunc storage.AttrFunc,\n    48\u2192\ttrigger storage.IndexerFuncs,\n    49\u2192\tindexers *cache.Indexers) (storage.Interface, factory.DestroyFunc, error) {\n    50\u2192\treturn NewRawStorage(config, newFunc, newListFunc, resourcePrefix)\n    51\u2192}\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192// NewRawStorage creates the low level kv storage. This is a work-around for current\n    54\u2192// two layer of same storage interface.\n    55\u2192// TODO: Once cacher is enabled on all registries (event registry is special), we will remove this method.\n    56\u2192func NewRawStorage(config *storagebackend.ConfigForResource, newFunc, newListFunc func() runtime.Object, resourcePrefix string) (storage.Interface, factory.DestroyFunc, error) {\n    57\u2192\treturn factory.Create(*config, newFunc, newListFunc, resourcePrefix)\n    58\u2192}\n    59\u2192"
141. Read @ -

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go",
  "limit": 200
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package rest\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"io\"\n    22\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    23\u2192\t\"net/url\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\t\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\"\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192\tmetainternalversion \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\"\n    28\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n    32\u2192)\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192//TODO:\n    35\u2192// Storage interfaces need to be separated into two groups; those that operate\n    36\u2192// on collections and those that operate on individually named items.\n    37\u2192// Collection interfaces:\n    38\u2192// (Method: Current -> Proposed)\n    39\u2192//    GET: Lister -> CollectionGetter\n    40\u2192//    WATCH: Watcher -> CollectionWatcher\n    41\u2192//    CREATE: Creater -> CollectionCreater\n    42\u2192//    DELETE: (n/a) -> CollectionDeleter\n    43\u2192//    UPDATE: (n/a) -> CollectionUpdater\n    44\u2192//\n    45\u2192// Single item interfaces:\n    46\u2192// (Method: Current -> Proposed)\n    47\u2192//    GET: Getter -> NamedGetter\n    48\u2192//    WATCH: (n/a) -> NamedWatcher\n    49\u2192//    CREATE: (n/a) -> NamedCreater\n    50\u2192//    DELETE: Deleter -> NamedDeleter\n    51\u2192//    UPDATE: Update -> NamedUpdater\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192// Storage is a generic interface for RESTful storage services.\n    54\u2192// Resources which are exported to the RESTful API of apiserver need to implement this interface. It is expected\n    55\u2192// that objects may implement any of the below interfaces.\n    56\u2192//\n    57\u2192// Consider using StorageWithReadiness whenever possible.\n    58\u2192type Storage interface {\n    59\u2192\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create and Update after request data has been put into it.\n    60\u2192\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n    61\u2192\tNew() runtime.Object\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\n    64\u2192\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\n    65\u2192\t// for being called more than once.\n    66\u2192\tDestroy()\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192// StorageWithReadiness extends Storage interface with the readiness check.\n    70\u2192type StorageWithReadiness interface {\n    71\u2192\tStorage\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\t// ReadinessCheck allows for checking storage readiness.\n    74\u2192\tReadinessCheck() error\n    75\u2192}\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192// Scoper indicates what scope the resource is at. It must be specified.\n    78\u2192// It is usually provided automatically based on your strategy.\n    79\u2192type Scoper interface {\n    80\u2192\t// NamespaceScoped returns true if the storage is namespaced\n    81\u2192\tNamespaceScoped() bool\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192// KindProvider specifies a different kind for its API than for its internal storage.  This is necessary for external\n    85\u2192// objects that are not compiled into the api server.  For such objects, there is no in-memory representation for\n    86\u2192// the object, so they must be represented as generic objects (e.g. runtime.Unknown), but when we present the object as part of\n    87\u2192// API discovery we want to present the specific kind, not the generic internal representation.\n    88\u2192type KindProvider interface {\n    89\u2192\tKind() string\n    90\u2192}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192// ShortNamesProvider is an interface for RESTful storage services. Delivers a list of short names for a resource. The list is used by kubectl to have short names representation of resources.\n    93\u2192type ShortNamesProvider interface {\n    94\u2192\tShortNames() []string\n    95\u2192}\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192// CategoriesProvider allows a resource to specify which groups of resources (categories) it's part of. Categories can\n    98\u2192// be used by API clients to refer to a batch of resources by using a single name (e.g. \"all\" could translate to \"pod,rc,svc,...\").\n    99\u2192type CategoriesProvider interface {\n   100\u2192\tCategories() []string\n   101\u2192}\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192// SingularNameProvider returns singular name of resources. This is used by kubectl discovery to have singular\n   104\u2192// name representation of resources. In case of shortcut conflicts(with CRD shortcuts) singular name should always map to this resource.\n   105\u2192type SingularNameProvider interface {\n   106\u2192\tGetSingularName() string\n   107\u2192}\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192// GroupVersionKindProvider is used to specify a particular GroupVersionKind to discovery.  This is used for polymorphic endpoints\n   110\u2192// which generally point to foreign versions.  Scale refers to Scale.v1beta1.extensions for instance.\n   111\u2192// This trumps KindProvider since it is capable of providing the information required.\n   112\u2192// TODO KindProvider (only used by federation) should be removed and replaced with this, but that presents greater risk late in 1.8.\n   113\u2192type GroupVersionKindProvider interface {\n   114\u2192\tGroupVersionKind(containingGV schema.GroupVersion) schema.GroupVersionKind\n   115\u2192}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192// GroupVersionAcceptor is used to determine if a particular GroupVersion is acceptable to send to an endpoint.\n   118\u2192// This is used for endpoints which accept multiple versions (which is extremely rare).\n   119\u2192// The only known instance is pods/evictions which accepts policy/v1, but also policy/v1beta1 for backwards compatibility.\n   120\u2192type GroupVersionAcceptor interface {\n   121\u2192\tAcceptsGroupVersion(gv schema.GroupVersion) bool\n   122\u2192}\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192// Lister is an object that can retrieve resources that match the provided field and label criteria.\n   125\u2192type Lister interface {\n   126\u2192\t// NewList returns an empty object that can be used with the List call.\n   127\u2192\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n   128\u2192\tNewList() runtime.Object\n   129\u2192\t// List selects resources in the storage which match to the selector. 'options' can be nil.\n   130\u2192\tList(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n   131\u2192\t// TableConvertor ensures all list implementers also implement table conversion\n   132\u2192\tTableConvertor\n   133\u2192}\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192// Getter is an object that can retrieve a named RESTful resource.\n   136\u2192type Getter interface {\n   137\u2192\t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\n   138\u2192\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\n   139\u2192\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\n   140\u2192\tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options *metav1.GetOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n   141\u2192}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192// GetterWithOptions is an object that retrieve a named RESTful resource and takes\n   144\u2192// additional options on the get request. It allows a caller to also receive the\n   145\u2192// subpath of the GET request.\n   146\u2192type GetterWithOptions interface {\n   147\u2192\t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\n   148\u2192\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\n   149\u2192\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\n   150\u2192\t// The options object passed to it is of the same type returned by the NewGetOptions\n   151\u2192\t// method.\n   152\u2192\t// TODO: Pass metav1.GetOptions.\n   153\u2192\tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error)\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192\t// NewGetOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass\n   156\u2192\t// options to the Get method. It may return a bool and a string, if true, the\n   157\u2192\t// value of the request path below the object will be included as the named\n   158\u2192\t// string in the serialization of the runtime object. E.g., returning \"path\"\n   159\u2192\t// will convert the trailing request scheme value to \"path\" in the map[string][]string\n   160\u2192\t// passed to the converter.\n   161\u2192\tNewGetOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)\n   162\u2192}\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192type TableConvertor interface {\n   165\u2192\tConvertToTable(ctx context.Context, object runtime.Object, tableOptions runtime.Object) (*metav1.Table, error)\n   166\u2192}\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192// GracefulDeleter knows how to pass deletion options to allow delayed deletion of a\n   169\u2192// RESTful object.\n   170\u2192type GracefulDeleter interface {\n   171\u2192\t// Delete finds a resource in the storage and deletes it.\n   172\u2192\t// The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.\n   173\u2192\t// If options are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an invalid\n   174\u2192\t// request error.\n   175\u2192\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\n   176\u2192\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\n   177\u2192\t// Delete *may* return the object that was deleted, or a status object indicating additional\n   178\u2192\t// information about deletion.\n   179\u2192\t// It also returns a boolean which is set to true if the resource was instantly\n   180\u2192\t// deleted or false if it will be deleted asynchronously.\n   181\u2192\tDelete(ctx context.Context, name string, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\n   182\u2192}\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192// MayReturnFullObjectDeleter may return deleted object (instead of a simple status) on deletion.\n   185\u2192type MayReturnFullObjectDeleter interface {\n   186\u2192\tDeleteReturnsDeletedObject() bool\n   187\u2192}\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192// CollectionDeleter is an object that can delete a collection\n   190\u2192// of RESTful resources.\n   191\u2192type CollectionDeleter interface {\n   192\u2192\t// DeleteCollection selects all resources in the storage matching given 'listOptions'\n   193\u2192\t// and deletes them. The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.\n   194\u2192\t// If 'options' are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an\n   195\u2192\t// invalid request error.\n   196\u2192\t// DeleteCollection may not be atomic - i.e. it may delete some objects and still\n   197\u2192\t// return an error after it. On success, returns a list of deleted objects.\n   198\u2192\tDeleteCollection(ctx context.Context, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions, listOptions *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n   199\u2192}\n   200\u2192"

{
  "command": "grep -n \"admission\\|Admit\" /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/create.go | head -30",
  "description": "Find admission invocation in create handler"
}
Output
"38:\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n53:func createHandler(r rest.NamedCreater, scope *RequestScope, admit admission.Interface, includeName bool) http.HandlerFunc {\n160:\t\tadmit = admission.WithAudit(admit)\n182:\t\tadmissionAttributes := admission.NewAttributesRecord(obj, nil, scope.Kind, namespace, name, scope.Resource, scope.Subresource, admission.Create, options, dryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun), userInfo)\n188:\t\t\t\trest.AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc(admit, admissionAttributes, scope),\n202:\t\t\tif mutatingAdmission, ok := admit.(admission.MutationInterface); ok && mutatingAdmission.Handles(admission.Create) {\n203:\t\t\t\tif err := mutatingAdmission.Admit(ctx, admissionAttributes, scope); err != nil {\n207:\t\t\t// Dedup owner references again after mutating admission happens\n240:func CreateNamedResource(r rest.NamedCreater, scope *RequestScope, admission admission.Interface) http.HandlerFunc {\n241:\treturn createHandler(r, scope, admission, true)\n245:func CreateResource(r rest.Creater, scope *RequestScope, admission admission.Interface) http.HandlerFunc {\n246:\treturn createHandler(&namedCreaterAdapter{r}, scope, admission, false)"

"   150\u2192\n   151\u2192\t\t// On create, get name from new object if unset\n   152\u2192\t\tif len(name) == 0 {\n   153\u2192\t\t\t_, name, _ = scope.Namer.ObjectName(obj)\n   154\u2192\t\t}\n   155\u2192\t\tif len(namespace) == 0 && scope.Resource == namespaceGVR {\n   156\u2192\t\t\tnamespace = name\n   157\u2192\t\t}\n   158\u2192\t\tctx = request.WithNamespace(ctx, namespace)\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192\t\tadmit = admission.WithAudit(admit)\n   161\u2192\t\taudit.LogRequestObject(req.Context(), obj, objGV, scope.Resource, scope.Subresource, scope.Serializer)\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192\t\tuserInfo, _ := request.UserFrom(ctx)\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192\t\tif objectMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj); err == nil {\n   166\u2192\t\t\tpreserveObjectMetaSystemFields := false\n   167\u2192\t\t\tif c, ok := r.(rest.SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver); ok && len(scope.Subresource) > 0 {\n   168\u2192\t\t\t\tpreserveObjectMetaSystemFields = c.PreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate()\n   169\u2192\t\t\t}\n   170\u2192\t\t\tif !preserveObjectMetaSystemFields {\n   171\u2192\t\t\t\trest.WipeObjectMetaSystemFields(objectMeta)\n   172\u2192\t\t\t}\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192\t\t\t// ensure namespace on the object is correct, or error if a conflicting namespace was set in the object\n   175\u2192\t\t\tif err := rest.EnsureObjectNamespaceMatchesRequestNamespace(rest.ExpectedNamespaceForResource(namespace, scope.Resource), objectMeta); err != nil {\n   176\u2192\t\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   177\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   178\u2192\t\t\t}\n   179\u2192\t\t}\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"About to store object in database\")\n   182\u2192\t\tadmissionAttributes := admission.NewAttributesRecord(obj, nil, scope.Kind, namespace, name, scope.Resource, scope.Subresource, admission.Create, options, dryrun.IsDryRun(options.DryRun), userInfo)\n   183\u2192\t\trequestFunc := func() (runtime.Object, error) {\n   184\u2192\t\t\treturn r.Create(\n   185\u2192\t\t\t\tctx,\n   186\u2192\t\t\t\tname,\n   187\u2192\t\t\t\tobj,\n   188\u2192\t\t\t\trest.AdmissionToValidateObjectFunc(admit, admissionAttributes, scope),\n   189\u2192\t\t\t\toptions,\n   190\u2192\t\t\t)\n   191\u2192\t\t}\n   192\u2192\t\t// Dedup owner references before updating managed fields\n   193\u2192\t\tdedupOwnerReferencesAndAddWarning(obj, req.Context(), false)\n   194\u2192\t\tresult, err := finisher.FinishRequest(ctx, func() (runtime.Object, error) {\n   195\u2192\t\t\tliveObj, err := scope.Creater.New(scope.Kind)\n   196\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   197\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create new object (Create for %v): %v\", scope.Kind, err)\n   198\u2192\t\t\t}\n   199\u2192\t\t\tobj = scope.FieldManager.UpdateNoErrors(liveObj, obj, managerOrUserAgent(options.FieldManager, req.UserAgent()))\n   200\u2192\t\t\tadmit = fieldmanager.NewManagedFieldsValidatingAdmissionController(admit)\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192\t\t\tif mutatingAdmission, ok := admit.(admission.MutationInterface); ok && mutatingAdmission.Handles(admission.Create) {\n   203\u2192\t\t\t\tif err := mutatingAdmission.Admit(ctx, admissionAttributes, scope); err != nil {\n   204\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n   205\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   206\u2192\t\t\t}\n   207\u2192\t\t\t// Dedup owner references again after mutating admission happens\n   208\u2192\t\t\tdedupOwnerReferencesAndAddWarning(obj, req.Context(), true)\n   209\u2192\t\t\tresult, err := requestFunc()\n   210\u2192\t\t\t// If the object wasn't committed to storage because it's serialized size was too large,\n   211\u2192\t\t\t// it is safe to remove managedFields (which can be large) and try again.\n   212\u2192\t\t\tif isTooLargeError(err) {\n   213\u2192\t\t\t\tif accessor, accessorErr := meta.Accessor(obj); accessorErr == nil {\n   214\u2192\t\t\t\t\taccessor.SetManagedFields(nil)\n   215\u2192\t\t\t\t\tresult, err = requestFunc()\n   216\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   217\u2192\t\t\t}\n   218\u2192\t\t\treturn result, err\n   219\u2192\t\t})\n   220\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   221\u2192\t\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"Write to database call failed\", attribute.Int(\"len\", len(body)), attribute.String(\"err\", err.Error()))\n   222\u2192\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n   223\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   224\u2192\t\t}\n   225\u2192\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"Write to database call succeeded\", attribute.Int(\"len\", len(body)))\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192\t\tcode := http.StatusCreated\n   228\u2192\t\tstatus, ok := result.(*metav1.Status)\n   229\u2192\t\tif ok && status.Code == 0 {\n   230\u2192\t\t\tstatus.Code = int32(code)\n   231\u2192\t\t}\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"About to write a response\")\n   234\u2192\t\tdefer span.AddEvent(\"Writing http response done\")\n   235\u2192\t\ttransformResponseObject(ctx, scope, req, w, code, outputMediaType, result)\n   236\u2192\t}\n   237\u2192}\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192// CreateNamedResource returns a function that will handle a resource creation with name.\n   240\u2192func CreateNamedResource(r rest.NamedCreater, scope *RequestScope, admission admission.Interface) http.HandlerFunc {\n   241\u2192\treturn createHandler(r, scope, admission, true)\n   242\u2192}\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192// CreateResource returns a function that will handle a resource creation.\n   245\u2192func CreateResource(r rest.Creater, scope *RequestScope, admission admission.Interface) http.HandlerFunc {\n   246\u2192\treturn createHandler(&namedCreaterAdapter{r}, scope, admission, false)\n   247\u2192}\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192type namedCreaterAdapter struct {\n   250\u2192\trest.Creater\n   251\u2192}\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192func (c *namedCreaterAdapter) Create(ctx context.Context, name string, obj runtime.Object, createValidatingAdmission rest.ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error) {\n   254\u2192\treturn c.Creater.Create(ctx, obj, createValidatingAdmission, options)\n   255\u2192}\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192// manager is assumed to be already a valid value, we need to make\n   258\u2192// userAgent into a valid value too.\n   259\u2192func managerOrUserAgent(manager, userAgent string) string {\n   260\u2192\tif manager != \"\" {\n   261\u2192\t\treturn manager\n   262\u2192\t}\n   263\u2192\treturn prefixFromUserAgent(userAgent)\n   264\u2192}\n   265\u2192\n   266\u2192// prefixFromUserAgent takes the characters preceding the first /, quote\n   267\u2192// unprintable character and then trim what's beyond the\n   268\u2192// FieldManagerMaxLength limit.\n   269\u2192func prefixFromUserAgent(u string) string {\n   270\u2192\tm := strings.Split(u, \"/\")[0]\n   271\u2192\tbuf := bytes.NewBuffer(nil)\n   272\u2192\tfor _, r := range m {\n   273\u2192\t\t// Ignore non-printable characters\n   274\u2192\t\tif !unicode.IsPrint(r) {\n   275\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   276\u2192\t\t}\n   277\u2192\t\t// Only append if we have room for it\n   278\u2192\t\tif buf.Len()+utf8.RuneLen(r) > validation.FieldManagerMaxLength {\n   279\u2192\t\t\tbreak\n   280\u2192\t\t}\n   281\u2192\t\tbuf.WriteRune(r)\n   282\u2192\t}\n   283\u2192\treturn buf.String()\n   284\u2192}\n   285\u2192"

17\u2192package server\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"context\"\n    21\u2192\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n    22\u2192\t\"encoding/base32\"\n    23\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    24\u2192\t\"net\"\n    25\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    26\u2192\t\"os\"\n    27\u2192\tgoruntime \"runtime\"\n    28\u2192\t\"runtime/debug\"\n    29\u2192\t\"sort\"\n    30\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n    31\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    32\u2192\t\"sync/atomic\"\n    33\u2192\t\"time\"\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\t\"github.com/google/uuid\"\n    36\u2192\t\"golang.org/x/crypto/cryptobyte\"\n    37\u2192\tjsonpatch \"gopkg.in/evanphx/json-patch.v4\"\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    40\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    41\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    42\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer\"\n    43\u2192\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n    44\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    45\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/version\"\n    46\u2192\tutilwaitgroup \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/waitgroup\"\n    47\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n    48\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n    49\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\"\n 60\u2192\tapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n    61\u2192\tgenericfeatures \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n    62\u2192\tgenericregistry \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/value\"\n    72\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n    73\u2192\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    74\u2192\tutilflowcontrol \"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n    78\u2192\tbasecompatibility \"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\"\n    79\u2192\t\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\"\n    80\u2192\t\"k8s.io/component-base/logs\"\n 84\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    85\u2192\topenapicommon \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\"\n    86\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/spec3\"\n    87\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\"\n    88\u2192\t\"k8s.io/utils/clock\"\n    89\u2192\tutilsnet \"k8s.io/utils/net\"\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192\t// install apis\n    92\u2192\t_ \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver/install\"\n    93\u2192)\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192// hostnameFunc is a function to set the hostnameFunc of this apiserver.\n    96\u2192// To be used for testing purpose only, to simulate scenarios where multiple apiservers\n    97\u2192// exist. In such cases we want to ensure unique apiserver IDs which are a hash of hostnameFunc.\n    98\u2192var (\n    99\u2192\thostnameFunc = os.Hostname\n   100\u2192)\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192const (\n   103\u2192\t// DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix is where the legacy APIs will be located.\n   104\u2192\tDefaultLegacyAPIPrefix = \"/api\"\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192\t// APIGroupPrefix is where non-legacy API group will be located.\n   107\u2192\tAPIGroupPrefix = \"/apis\"\n   108\u2192)\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192// Config is a structure used to configure a GenericAPIServer.\n   111\u2192// Its members are sorted roughly in order of importance for composers.\n   112\u2192type Config struct {\n   113\u2192\t// SecureServing is required to serve https\n   114\u2192\tSecureServing *SecureServingInfo\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192\t// Authentication is the configuration for authentication\n   117\u2192\tAuthentication AuthenticationInfo\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192\t// Authorization is the configuration for authorization\n   120\u2192\tAuthorization AuthorizationInfo\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n   123\u2192\t// This is required for proper functioning of the PostStartHooks on a GenericAPIServer\n   124\u2192\t// TODO: move into SecureServing(WithLoopback) as soon as insecure serving is gone\n   125\u2192\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192\t// EgressSelector provides a lookup mechanism for dialing outbound connections.\n   128\u2192\t// It does so based on a EgressSelectorConfiguration which was read at startup.\n   129\u2192\tEgressSelector *egressselector.EgressSelector\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192\t// RuleResolver is required to get the list of rules that apply to a given user\n   132\u2192\t// in a given namespace\n   133\u2192\tRuleResolver authorizer.RuleResolver\n   134\u2192\t// AdmissionControl performs deep inspection of a given request (including content)\n   135\u2192\t// to set values and determine whether its allowed\n   136\u2192\tAdmissionControl      admission.Interface\n   137\u2192\tCorsAllowedOriginList []string\n   138\u2192\tHSTSDirectives        []string\n   139\u2192\t// FlowControl, if not nil, gives priority and fairness to request handling\n   140\u2192\tFlowControl utilflowcontrol.Interface\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192\tEnableIndex     bool\n   143\u2192\tEnableProfiling bool\n   144\u2192\tDebugSocketPath string\n   145\u2192\tEnableDiscovery bool\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192\t// Requires generic profiling enabled\n   148\u2192\tEnableContentionProfiling bool\n   149\u2192\tEnableMetrics             bool\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192\tDisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n   152\u2192\t// done values in this values for this map are ignored.\n   153\u2192\tPostStartHooks map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\n   156\u2192\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\n   157\u2192\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\n   158\u2192\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\n   159\u2192\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\n   160\u2192\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\n   161\u2192\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\n   162\u2192\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\n   163\u2192\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n   164\u2192\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\n   165\u2192\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\n   166\u2192\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\n   167\u2192\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n   168\u2192\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\n   169\u2192\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\n   170\u2192\t// AuditBackend is where audit events are sent to.\n   171\u2192\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\n   172\u2192\t// AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator makes the decision of whether and how to audit log a request.\n   173\u2192\tAuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\n   174\u2192\t// ExternalAddress is the host name to use for external (public internet) facing URLs (e.g. Swagger)\n   175\u2192\t// Will default to a value based on secure serving info and available ipv4 IPs.\n   176\u2192\tExternalAddress string\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192\t// TracerProvider can provide a tracer, which records spans for distributed tracing.\n   179\u2192\tTracerProvider tracing.TracerProvider\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192\t//===========================================================================\n   182\u2192\t// Fields you probably don't care about changing\n   183\u2192\t//===========================================================================\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192\t// BuildHandlerChainFunc allows you to build custom handler chains by decorating the apiHandler.\n   186\u2192\tBuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) (secure http.Handler)\n   187\u2192\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\n   188\u2192\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\n   189\u2192\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\n   190\u2192\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\n   191\u2192\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\n   192\u2192\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\n   193\u2192\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\n   194\u2192\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\n   195\u2192\t// DiscoveryAddresses is used to build the IPs pass to discovery. If nil, the ExternalAddress is\n   196\u2192\t// always reported\n   197\u2192\tDiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n   198\u2192\t// The default set of healthz checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\n   199\u2192\tHealthzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n   200\u2192\t// The default set of livez checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\n   201\u2192\tLivezChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n   202\u2192\t// The default set of readyz-only checks. There might be more added via AddReadyzChecks dynamically.\n   203\u2192\tReadyzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n   204\u2192\tFlagz        flagz.Reader\n   205\u2192\t// LegacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\n   206\u2192\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup. New API servers don't generally have legacy groups at all.\n   207\u2192\tLegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\n   208\u2192\t// RequestInfoResolver is used to assign attributes (used by admission and authorization) based on a request URL.\n   209\u2192\t// Use-cases that are like kubelets may need to customize this.\n   210\u2192\tRequestInfoResolver apirequest.RequestInfoResolver\n   211\u2192\t// Serializer is required and provides the interface for serializing and converting objects to and from the wire\n   212\u2192\t// The default (api.Codecs) usually works fine.\n   213\u2192\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n   214\u2192\t// OpenAPIConfig will be used in generating OpenAPI spec. This is nil by default. Use DefaultOpenAPIConfig for \"working\" defaults.\n   215\u2192\tOpenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n   216\u2192\t// OpenAPIV3Config will be used in generating OpenAPI V3 spec. This is nil by default. Use DefaultOpenAPIV3Config for \"working\" defaults.\n   217\u2192\tOpenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n   218\u2192\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation avoids installing the OpenAPI handler if set to true.\n   219\u2192\tSkipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192\t// ResourceTransformers are used to transform resources from and to etcd, e.g. encryption.\n   222\u2192\tResourceTransformers storagevalue.ResourceTransformers\n   223\u2192\t// RESTOptionsGetter is used to construct RESTStorage types via the generic registry.\n   224\u2192\tRESTOptionsGetter genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192\t// If specified, all requests except those which match the LongRunningFunc predicate will timeout\n   227\u2192\t// after this duration.\n   228\u2192\tRequestTimeout time.Duration\n   229\u2192\t// If specified, long running requests such as watch will be allocated a random timeout between this value, and\n   230\u2192\t// twice this value.  Note that it is up to the request handlers to ignore or honor this timeout. In seconds.\n   231\u2192\tMinRequestTimeout int\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192\t// StorageInitializationTimeout defines the maximum amount of time to wait for storage initialization\n   234\u2192\t// before declaring apiserver ready.\n   235\u2192\tStorageInitializationTimeout time.Duration\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192\t// This represents the maximum amount of time it should take for apiserver to complete its startup\n   238\u2192\t// sequence and become healthy. From apiserver's start time to when this amount of time has\n   239\u2192\t// elapsed, /livez will assume that unfinished post-start hooks will complete successfully and\n   240\u2192\t// therefore return true.\n   241\u2192\tLivezGracePeriod time.Duration\n   242\u2192\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\n   243\u2192\t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\n   244\u2192\t// but /readyz will return failure.\n   245\u2192\tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192\t// The limit on the total size increase all \"copy\" operations in a json\n   248\u2192\t// patch may cause.\n   249\u2192\t// This affects all places that applies json patch in the binary.\n   250\u2192\tJSONPatchMaxCopyBytes int64"
146. Read @ -
Input
{

Output
"   250\u2192\tJSONPatchMaxCopyBytes int64\n   251\u2192\t// The limit on the request size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request\n   252\u2192\t// 0 means no limit.\n   253\u2192\tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n   254\u2192\t// MaxRequestsInFlight is the maximum number of parallel non-long-running requests. Every further\n   255\u2192\t// request has to wait. Applies only to non-mutating requests.\n   256\u2192\tMaxRequestsInFlight int\n   257\u2192\t// MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight is the maximum number of parallel mutating requests. Every further\n   258\u2192\t// request has to wait.\n   259\u2192\tMaxMutatingRequestsInFlight int\n   260\u2192\t// Predicate which is true for paths of long-running http requests\n   261\u2192\tLongRunningFunc apirequest.LongRunningRequestCheck\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192\t// GoawayChance is the probability that send a GOAWAY to HTTP/2 clients. When client received\n   264\u2192\t// GOAWAY, the in-flight requests will not be affected and new requests will use\n   265\u2192\t// a new TCP connection to triggering re-balancing to another server behind the load balance.\n   266\u2192\t// Default to 0, means never send GOAWAY. Max is 0.02 to prevent break the apiserver.\n   267\u2192\tGoawayChance float64\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192\t// MergedResourceConfig indicates which groupVersion enabled and its resources enabled/disabled.\n   270\u2192\t// This is composed of genericapiserver defaultAPIResourceConfig and those parsed from flags.\n   271\u2192\t// If not specify any in flags, then genericapiserver will only enable defaultAPIResourceConfig.\n   272\u2192\tMergedResourceConfig *serverstore.ResourceConfig\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192\t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals\n   275\u2192\t// that happen during lifecycle of the apiserver.\n   276\u2192\t// it's intentionally marked private as it should never be overridden.\n   277\u2192\tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192\t// StorageObjectCountTracker is used to keep track of the total number of objects\n   280\u2192\t// in the storage per resource, so we can estimate width of incoming requests.\n   281\u2192\tStorageObjectCountTracker flowcontrolrequest.StorageObjectCountTracker\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\n   284\u2192\t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\n   285\u2192\t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\n   286\u2192\t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\n   287\u2192\t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\n   288\u2192\t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\n   289\u2192\t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\n   290\u2192\tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192\t//===========================================================================\n   293\u2192\t// values below here are targets for removal\n   294\u2192\t//===========================================================================\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192\t// PublicAddress is the IP address where members of the cluster (kubelet,\n   297\u2192\t// kube-proxy, services, etc.) can reach the GenericAPIServer.\n   298\u2192\t// If nil or 0.0.0.0, the host's default interface will be used.\n   299\u2192\tPublicAddress net.IP\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192\t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\n   302\u2192\t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\n   303\u2192\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192\t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\n   306\u2192\tAPIServerID string\n   307\u2192\n   308\u2192\t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\n   309\u2192\tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\n   312\u2192\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192\t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\n   315\u2192\t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\n   316\u2192\t// watch request(s) to drain.\n   317\u2192\t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\n   318\u2192\t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\n   319\u2192\t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\n   320\u2192\t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\n   321\u2192\t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\n   322\u2192\t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\n   323\u2192\t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\n   324\u2192\t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\n   325\u2192\t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\n   326\u2192\t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\n   327\u2192\t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\n   328\u2192\t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\n   329\u2192\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\n   330\u2192}\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192type RecommendedConfig struct {\n   333\u2192\tConfig\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192\t// SharedInformerFactory provides shared informers for Kubernetes resources. This value is set by\n   336\u2192\t// RecommendedOptions.CoreAPI.ApplyTo called by RecommendedOptions.ApplyTo. It uses an in-cluster client config\n   337\u2192\t// by default, or the kubeconfig given with kubeconfig command line flag.\n   338\u2192\tSharedInformerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192\t// ClientConfig holds the kubernetes client configuration.\n   341\u2192\t// This value is set by RecommendedOptions.CoreAPI.ApplyTo called by RecommendedOptions.ApplyTo.\n   342\u2192\t// By default in-cluster client config is used.\n   343\u2192\tClientConfig *restclient.Config\n   344\u2192}\n   345\u2192\n   346\u2192type SecureServingInfo struct {\n   347\u2192\t// Listener is the secure server network listener.\n   348\u2192\tListener net.Listener\n   349\u2192\n   350\u2192\t// Cert is the main server cert which is used if SNI does not match. Cert must be non-nil and is\n   351\u2192\t// allowed to be in SNICerts.\n   352\u2192\tCert dynamiccertificates.CertKeyContentProvider\n   353\u2192\n   354\u2192\t// SNICerts are the TLS certificates used for SNI.\n   355\u2192\tSNICerts []dynamiccertificates.SNICertKeyContentProvider\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192\t// ClientCA is the certificate bundle for all the signers that you'll recognize for incoming client certificates\n   358\u2192\tClientCA dynamiccertificates.CAContentProvider\n   359\u2192\n   360\u2192\t// MinTLSVersion optionally overrides the minimum TLS version supported.\n   361\u2192\t// Values are from tls package constants (https://golang.org/pkg/crypto/tls/#pkg-constants).\n   362\u2192\tMinTLSVersion uint16\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192\t// CipherSuites optionally overrides the list of allowed cipher suites for the server.\n   365\u2192\t// Values are from tls package constants (https://golang.org/pkg/crypto/tls/#pkg-constants).\n   366\u2192\tCipherSuites []uint16\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192\t// HTTP2MaxStreamsPerConnection is the limit that the api server imposes on each client.\n   369\u2192\t// A value of zero means to use the default provided by golang's HTTP/2 support.\n   370\u2192\tHTTP2MaxStreamsPerConnection int\n   371\u2192\n   372\u2192\t// DisableHTTP2 indicates that http2 should not be enabled.\n   373\u2192\tDisableHTTP2 bool\n   374\u2192}\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192type AuthenticationInfo struct {\n   377\u2192\t// APIAudiences is a list of identifier that the API identifies as. This is\n   378\u2192\t// used by some authenticators to validate audience bound credentials.\n   379\u2192\tAPIAudiences authenticator.Audiences\n   380\u2192\t// Authenticator determines which subject is making the request\n   381\u2192\tAuthenticator authenticator.Request\n   382\u2192\n   383\u2192\tRequestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig\n   384\u2192}\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192type AuthorizationInfo struct {\n   387\u2192\t// Authorizer determines whether the subject is allowed to make the request based only\n   388\u2192\t// on the RequestURI\n   389\u2192\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\n   390\u2192}\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192func init() {\n   393\u2192\tutilruntime.Must(features.AddFeatureGates(utilfeature.DefaultMutableFeatureGate))\n   394\u2192}\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192// NewConfig returns a Config struct with the default values\n   397\u2192func NewConfig(codecs serializer.CodecFactory) *Config {\n   398\u2192\tdefaultHealthChecks := []healthz.HealthChecker{healthz.PingHealthz, healthz.LogHealthz}\n   399\u2192\tvar id string\n   400\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerIdentity) {\n   401\u2192\t\thostname, err := hostnameFunc()\n   402\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   403\u2192\t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"error getting hostname for apiserver identity: %v\", err)\n   404\u2192\t\t}\n   405\u2192\n   406\u2192\t\t// Since the hash needs to be unique across each kube-apiserver and aggregated apiservers,\n   407\u2192\t\t// the hash used for the identity should include both the hostname and the identity value.\n   408\u2192\t\t// TODO: receive the identity value as a parameter once the apiserver identity lease controller\n   409\u2192\t\t// post start hook is moved to generic apiserver.\n   410\u2192\t\tb := cryptobyte.NewBuilder(nil)\n   411\u2192\t\tb.AddUint16LengthPrefixed(func(b *cryptobyte.Builder) {\n   412\u2192\t\t\tb.AddBytes([]byte(hostname))\n   413\u2192\t\t})\n   414\u2192\t\tb.AddUint16LengthPrefixed(func(b *cryptobyte.Builder) {\n   415\u2192\t\t\tb.AddBytes([]byte(\"kube-apiserver\"))\n   416\u2192\t\t})\n   417\u2192\t\thashData, err := b.Bytes()\n   418\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   419\u2192\t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"error building hash data for apiserver identity: %v\", err)\n   420\u2192\t\t}\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192\t\thash := sha256.Sum256(hashData)\n   423\u2192\t\tid = \"apiserver-\" + strings.ToLower(base32.StdEncoding.WithPadding(base32.NoPadding).EncodeToString(hash[:16]))\n   424\u2192\t}\n   425\u2192\tlifecycleSignals := newLifecycleSignals()\n   426\u2192\n   427\u2192\treturn &Config{\n   428\u2192\t\tSerializer:                     codecs,\n   429\u2192\t\tBuildHandlerChainFunc:          DefaultBuildHandlerChain,\n   430\u2192\t\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup: new(utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup),\n   431\u2192\t\tWatchRequestWaitGroup:          &utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup{},\n   432\u2192\t\tLegacyAPIGroupPrefixes:         sets.NewString(DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix),\n   433\u2192\t\tDisabledPostStartHooks:         sets.NewString(),\n   434\u2192\t\tPostStartHooks:                 map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry{},\n   435\u2192\t\tHealthzChecks:                  append([]healthz.HealthChecker{}, defaultHealthChecks...),\n   436\u2192\t\tReadyzChecks:                   append([]healthz.HealthChecker{}, defaultHealthChecks...),\n   437\u2192\t\tLivezChecks:                    append([]healthz.HealthChecker{}, defaultHealthChecks...),\n   438\u2192\t\tEnableIndex:                    true,\n   439\u2192\t\tEnableDiscovery:                true,\n   440\u2192\t\tEnableProfiling:                true,\n   441\u2192\t\tDebugSocketPath:                \"\",\n   442\u2192\t\tEnableMetrics:                  true,\n   443\u2192\t\tMaxRequestsInFlight:            400,\n   444\u2192\t\tMaxMutatingRequestsInFlight:    200,\n   445\u2192\t\tRequestTimeout:                 time.Duration(60) * time.Second,\n   446\u2192\t\tMinRequestTimeout:              1800,\n   447\u2192\t\tStorageInitializationTimeout:   time.Minute,\n   448\u2192\t\tLivezGracePeriod:               time.Duration(0),\n   449\u2192\t\tShutdownDelayDuration:          time.Duration(0),"

authenticated\n  1050\u2192\t// clients to influence sampling.\n  1051\u2192\tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerTracing) {\n  1052\u2192\t\thandler = genericapifilters.WithTracing(handler, c.TracerProvider)\n  1053\u2192\t}\n  1054\u2192\tfailedHandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(failedHandler)\n  1055\u2192\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n  1056\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuthentication(handler, c.Authentication.Authenticator, failedHandler, c.Authentication.APIAudiences, c.Authentication.RequestHeaderConfig)\n  1057\u2192\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"authentication\")\n  1058\u2192\n  1059\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithCORS(handler, c.CorsAllowedOriginList, nil, nil, nil, \"true\")\n  1060\u2192\n  1061\u2192\t// WithWarningRecorder must be wrapped by the timeout handler\n  1062\u2192\t// to make the addition of warning headers threadsafe\n  1063\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithWarningRecorder(handler)\n  1064\u2192\n  1065\u2192\t// WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests will call the rest of the request handling in a go-routine with the\n  1066\u2192\t// context with deadline. The go-routine can keep running, while the timeout logic will return a timeout to the client.\n  1067\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\n  1068\u2192\n  1069\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestDeadline(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator,\n  1070\u2192\t\tc.LongRunningFunc, c.Serializer, c.RequestTimeout)\n  1071\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithWaitGroup(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup)\n  1072\u2192\tif c.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod > 0 {\n  1073\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown(handler, c.lifecycleSignals, c.WatchRequestWaitGroup)\n  1074\u2192\t}\n  1075\u2192\tif c.SecureServing != nil && !c.SecureServing.DisableHTTP2 && c.GoawayChance > 0 {\n  1076\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithProbabilisticGoaway(handler, c.GoawayChance)\n  1077\u2192\t}\n  1078\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithCacheControl(handler)\n  1079\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithHSTS(handler, c.HSTSDirectives)\n  1080\u2192\tif c.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n  1081\u2192\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithRetryAfter(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest.Signaled())\n  1082\u2192\t}\n  1083\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithHTTPLogging(handler)\n  1084\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithLatencyTrackers(handler)\n  1085\u2192\t// WithRoutine will execute future handlers in a separate goroutine and serving\n  1086\u2192\t// handler in current goroutine to minimize the stack memory usage. It must be\n  1087\u2192\t// after WithPanicRecover() to be protected from panics.\n  1088\u2192\tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServingWithRoutine) {\n  1089\u2192\t\thandler = routine.WithRoutine(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\n  1090\u2192\t}\n  1091\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestInfo(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)\n  1092\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestReceivedTimestamp(handler)\n  1093\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signaled())\n  1094\u2192\thandler = genericfilters.WithPanicRecovery(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)\n  1095\u2192\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuditInit(handler)\n  1096\u2192\treturn handler\n  1097\u2192}\n s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n  1102\u2192\t}\n  1103\u2192\tif c.EnableProfiling {\n  1104\u2192\t\troutes.Profiling{}.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n  1105\u2192\t\tif c.EnableContentionProfiling {\n  1106\u2192\t\t\tgoruntime.SetBlockProfileRate(1)\n  1107\u2192\t\t}\n  1108\u2192\t\t// so far, only logging related endpoints are considered valid to add for these debug flags.\n 1112\u2192\t\ts.UnprotectedDebugSocket.InstallProfiling()\n  1113\u2192\t\ts.UnprotectedDebugSocket.InstallDebugFlag(\"v\", routes.StringFlagPutHandler(logs.GlogSetter))\n  1114\u2192\t\tif c.EnableContentionProfiling {\n  1115\u2192\t\t\tgoruntime.SetBlockProfileRate(1)\n  1116\u2192\t\t}\n  1117\u2192\t}\n  1118\u2192\n  1119\u2192\tif c.EnableMetrics {\n  1120\u2192\t\tif "   150\u2192\t\t\ti.valueWatchers[value] = watchersMap{}\n   151\u2192\t\t}\n   152\u2192\t\ti.valueWatchers[value].addWatcher(w, number)\n   153\u2192\t} else {\n   154\u2192\t\tscopedWatchers, ok := i.allWatchers[scope]\n   155\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   156\u2192\t\t\tscopedWatchers = watchersMap{}\n   157\u2192\t\t\ti.allWatchers[scope] = scopedWatchers\n   158\u2192\t\t}\n   159\u2192\t\tscopedWatchers.addWatcher(w, number)\n   160\u2192\t}\n   161\u2192}\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192func (i *indexedWatchers) deleteWatcher(number int, scope namespacedName, value string, supported bool) {\n   164\u2192\tif supported {\n   165\u2192\t\ti.valueWatchers[value].deleteWatcher(number)\n   166\u2192\t\tif len(i.valueWatchers[value]) == 0 {\n   167\u2192\t\t\tdelete(i.valueWatchers, value)\n   168\u2192\t\t}\n   169\u2192\t} else {\n   170\u2192\t\ti.allWatchers[scope].deleteWatcher(number)\n   171\u2192\t\tif len(i.allWatchers[scope]) == 0 {\n   172\u2192\t\t\tdelete(i.allWatchers, scope)\n   173\u2192\t\t}\n   174\u2192\t}\n   175\u2192}\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192func (i *indexedWatchers) terminateAll(groupResource schema.GroupResource, done func(*cacheWatcher)) {\n   178\u2192\t// note that we don't have to call setDrainInputBufferLocked method on the watchers\n   179\u2192\t// because we take advantage of the default value - stop immediately\n   180\u2192\t// also watchers that have had already its draining strategy set\n   181\u2192\t// are no longer available (they were removed from the allWatchers and the valueWatchers maps)\n   182\u2192\tif len(i.allWatchers) > 0 || len(i.valueWatchers) > 0 {\n   183\u2192\t\tklog.Warningf(\"Terminating all watchers from cacher %v\", groupResource)\n   184\u2192\t}\n   185\u2192\tfor _, watchers := range i.allWatchers {\n   186\u2192\t\twatchers.terminateAll(done)\n   187\u2192\t}\n   188\u2192\tfor _, watchers := range i.valueWatchers {\n   189\u2192\t\twatchers.terminateAll(done)\n   190\u2192\t}\n   191\u2192\ti.allWatchers = map[namespacedName]watchersMap{}\n   192\u2192\ti.valueWatchers = map[string]watchersMap{}\n   193\u2192}\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192// As we don't need a high precision here, we keep all watchers timeout within a\n   196\u2192// second in a bucket, and pop up them once at the timeout. To be more specific,\n   197\u2192// if you set fire time at X, you can get the bookmark within (X-1,X+1) period.\n   198\u2192type watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets struct {\n   199\u2192\t// the key of watcherBuckets is the number of seconds since createTime\n   200\u2192\twatchersBuckets   map[int64][]*cacheWatcher\n   201\u2192\tcreateTime        time.Time\n   202\u2192\tstartBucketID     int64\n   203\u2192\tclock             clock.Clock\n   204\u2192\tbookmarkFrequency time.Duration\n   205\u2192}\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192func newTimeBucketWatchers(clock clock.Clock, bookmarkFrequency time.Duration) *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets {\n   208\u2192\treturn &watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets{\n   209\u2192\t\twatchersBuckets:   make(map[int64][]*cacheWatcher),\n   210\u2192\t\tcreateTime:        clock.Now(),\n   211\u2192\t\tstartBucketID:     0,\n   212\u2192\t\tclock:             clock,\n   213\u2192\t\tbookmarkFrequency: bookmarkFrequency,\n   214\u2192\t}\n   215\u2192}\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192// adds a watcher to the bucket, if the deadline is before the start, it will be\n   218\u2192// added to the first one.\n   219\u2192func (t *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets) addWatcherThreadUnsafe(w *cacheWatcher) bool {\n   220\u2192\t// note that the returned time can be before t.createTime,\n   221\u2192\t// especially in cases when the nextBookmarkTime method\n   222\u2192\t// give us the zero value of type Time\n   223\u2192\t// so buckedID can hold a negative value\n   224\u2192\tnextTime, ok := w.nextBookmarkTime(t.clock.Now(), t.bookmarkFrequency)\n   225\u2192\tif !ok {\n   226\u2192\t\treturn false\n   227\u2192\t}\n   228\u2192\tbucketID := int64(nextTime.Sub(t.createTime) / time.Second)\n   229\u2192\tif bucketID < t.startBucketID {\n   230\u2192\t\tbucketID = t.startBucketID\n   231\u2192\t}\n   232\u2192\twatchers := t.watchersBuckets[bucketID]\n   233\u2192\tt.watchersBuckets[bucketID] = append(watchers, w)\n   234\u2192\treturn true\n   235\u2192}\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192func (t *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets) popExpiredWatchersThreadUnsafe() [][]*cacheWatcher {\n   238\u2192\tcurrentBucketID := int64(t.clock.Since(t.createTime) / time.Second)\n   239\u2192\t// There should be one or two elements in almost all cases\n   240\u2192\texpiredWatchers := make([][]*cacheWatcher, 0, 2)\n   241\u2192\tfor ; t.startBucketID <= currentBucketID; t.startBucketID++ {\n   242\u2192\t\tif watchers, ok := t.watchersBuckets[t.startBucketID]; ok {\n   243\u2192\t\t\tdelete(t.watchersBuckets, t.startBucketID)\n   244\u2192\t\t\texpiredWatchers = append(expiredWatchers, watchers)\n   245\u2192\t\t}\n   246\u2192\t}\n   247\u2192\treturn expiredWatchers\n   248\u2192}\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192type filterWithAttrsFunc func(key string, l labels.Set, f fields.Set) bool\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192type indexedTriggerFunc struct {\n   253\u2192\tindexName   string\n   254\u2192\tindexerFunc storage.IndexerFunc\n   255\u2192}\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192// Cacher is responsible for serving WATCH and LIST requests for a given\n   258\u2192// resource from its internal cache and updating its cache in the background\n   259\u2192// based on the underlying storage contents.\n   260\u2192// Cacher implements storage.Interface (although most of the calls are just\n   261\u2192// delegated to the underlying storage).\n   262\u2192type Cacher struct {\n   263\u2192\t// HighWaterMarks for performance debugging.\n   264\u2192\t// Important: Since HighWaterMark is using sync/atomic, it has to be at the top of the struct due to a bug on 32-bit platforms\n   265\u2192\t// See: https://golang.org/pkg/sync/atomic/ for more information\n   266\u2192\tincomingHWM storage.HighWaterMark\n   267\u2192\t// Incoming events that should be dispatched to watchers.\n   268\u2192\tincoming chan watchCacheEvent\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192\tresourcePrefix string\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192\tsync.RWMutex\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192\t// Before accessing the cacher's cache, wait for the ready to be ok.\n   275\u2192\t// This is necessary to prevent users from accessing structures that are\n   276\u2192\t// uninitialized or are being repopulated right now.\n   277\u2192\t// ready needs to be set to false when the cacher is paused or stopped.\n   278\u2192\t// ready needs to be set to true when the cacher is ready to use after\n   279\u2192\t// initialization.\n   280\u2192\tready *ready\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192\t// Underlying storage.Interface.\n   283\u2192\tstorage storage.Interface\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192\t// Expected type of objects in the underlying cache.\n   286\u2192\tobjectType reflect.Type\n   287\u2192\t// Used for logging, to disambiguate *unstructured.Unstructured (CRDs)\n   288\u2192\tgroupResource schema.GroupResource\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192\t// \"sliding window\" of recent changes of objects and the current state.\n   291\u2192\twatchCache *watchCache\n   292\u2192\treflector  *cache.Reflector\n   293\u2192\n   294\u2192\t// Versioner is used to handle resource versions.\n   295\u2192\tversioner storage.Versioner\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192\t// newFunc is a function that creates new empty object storing a object of type Type.\n   298\u2192\tnewFunc func() runtime.Object\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192\t// newListFunc is a function that creates new empty list for storing objects of type Type.\n   301\u2192\tnewListFunc func() runtime.Object\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192\t// indexedTrigger is used for optimizing amount of watchers that needs to process\n   304\u2192\t// an incoming event.\n   305\u2192\tindexedTrigger *indexedTriggerFunc\n   306\u2192\t// watchers is mapping from the value of trigger function that a\n   307\u2192\t// watcher is interested into the watchers\n   308\u2192\twatcherIdx int\n   309\u2192\twatchers   indexedWatchers\n   310\u2192\n   311\u2192\t// Defines a time budget that can be spend on waiting for not-ready watchers\n   312\u2192\t// while dispatching event before shutting them down.\n   313\u2192\tdispatchTimeoutBudget timeBudget\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192\t// Handling graceful termination.\n   316\u2192\tstopLock sync.RWMutex\n   317\u2192\tstopped  bool\n   318\u2192\tstopCh   chan struct{}\n   319\u2192\tstopWg   sync.WaitGroup\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192\tclock clock.Clock\n   322\u2192\t// timer is used to avoid unnecessary allocations in underlying watchers.\n   323\u2192\ttimer *time.Timer\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192\t// dispatching determines whether there is currently dispatching of\n   326\u2192\t// any event in flight.\n   327\u2192\tdispatching bool\n   328\u2192\t// watchersBuffer is a list of watchers potentially interested in currently\n   329\u2192\t// dispatched event.\n   330\u2192\twatchersBuffer []*cacheWatcher\n   331\u2192\t// blockedWatchers is a list of watchers whose buffer is currently full.\n   332\u2192\tblockedWatchers []*cacheWatcher\n   333\u2192\t// watchersToStop is a list of watchers that were supposed to be stopped\n   334\u2192\t// during current dispatching, but stopping was deferred to the end of\n   335\u2192\t// dispatching that event to avoid race with closing channels in watchers.\n   336\u2192\twatchersToStop []*cacheWatcher\n   337\u2192\t// Maintain a timeout queue to send the bookmark event before the watcher times out.\n   338\u2192\t// Note that this field when accessed MUST be protected by the Cacher.lock.\n   339\u2192\tbookmarkWatchers *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets\n   340\u2192\t// expiredBookmarkWatchers is a list of watchers that were expired and need to be schedule for a next bookmark event\n   341\u2192\texpiredBookmarkWatchers []*cacheWatcher\n   342\u2192\tcompactor               *compactor\n   343\u2192}\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192// NewCacherFromConfig creates a new Cacher responsible for servicing WATCH and LIST requests from\n   346\u2192// its internal cache and updating its cache in the background based on the\n   347\u2192// given configuration.\n   348\u2192func NewCacherFromConfig(config Config) (*Cacher, error) {\n   349\u2192\tstopCh := make(chan struct{})"
