"   345\u2192// NewCacherFromConfig creates a new Cacher responsible for servicing WATCH and LIST requests from\n   346\u2192// its internal cache and updating its cache in the background based on the\n   347\u2192// given configuration.\n   348\u2192func NewCacherFromConfig(config Config) (*Cacher, error) {\n   349\u2192\tstopCh := make(chan struct{})\n   350\u2192\tobj := config.NewFunc()\n   351\u2192\t// Give this error when it is constructed rather than when you get the\n   352\u2192\t// first watch item, because it's much easier to track down that way.\n   353\u2192\tif err := runtime.CheckCodec(config.Codec, obj); err != nil {\n   354\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"storage codec doesn't seem to match given type: %v\", err)\n   355\u2192\t}\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192\tvar indexedTrigger *indexedTriggerFunc\n   358\u2192\tif config.IndexerFuncs != nil {\n   359\u2192\t\t// For now, we don't support multiple trigger functions defined\n   360\u2192\t\t// for a given resource.\n   361\u2192\t\tif len(config.IndexerFuncs) > 1 {\n   362\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"cacher %s doesn't support more than one IndexerFunc: \", reflect.TypeOf(obj).String())\n   363\u2192\t\t}\n   364\u2192\t\tfor key, value := range config.IndexerFuncs {\n   365\u2192\t\t\tif value != nil {\n   366\u2192\t\t\t\tindexedTrigger = &indexedTriggerFunc{\n   367\u2192\t\t\t\t\tindexName:   key,\n   368\u2192\t\t\t\t\tindexerFunc: value,\n   369\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   370\u2192\t\t\t}\n   371\u2192\t\t}\n   372\u2192\t}\n   373\u2192\n   374\u2192\tif config.Clock == nil {\n   375\u2192\t\tconfig.Clock = clock.RealClock{}\n   376\u2192\t}\n   377\u2192\tobjType := reflect.TypeOf(obj)\n   378\u2192\tresourcePrefix := config.ResourcePrefix\n   379\u2192\tif resourcePrefix == \"\" {\n   380\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"resourcePrefix cannot be empty\")\n   381\u2192\t}\n   382\u2192\tif resourcePrefix == \"/\" {\n   383\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"resourcePrefix cannot be /\")\n   384\u2192\t}\n   385\u2192\tif !strings.HasPrefix(resourcePrefix, \"/\") {\n   386\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"resourcePrefix needs to start from /\")\n   387\u2192\t}\n   388\u2192\tcacher := &Cacher{\n   389\u2192\t\tresourcePrefix: resourcePrefix,\n   390\u2192\t\tready:          newReady(config.Clock),\n   391\u2192\t\tstorage:        config.Storage,\n   392\u2192\t\tobjectType:     objType,\n   393\u2192\t\tgroupResource:  config.GroupResource,\n   394\u2192\t\tversioner:      config.Versioner,\n   395\u2192\t\tnewFunc:        config.NewFunc,\n   396\u2192\t\tnewListFunc:    config.NewListFunc,\n   397\u2192\t\tindexedTrigger: indexedTrigger,\n   398\u2192\t\twatcherIdx:     0,\n   399\u2192\t\twatchers: indexedWatchers{\n   400\u2192\t\t\tallWatchers:   make(map[namespacedName]watchersMap),\n   401\u2192\t\t\tvalueWatchers: make(map[string]watchersMap),\n   402\u2192\t\t},\n   403\u2192\t\t// TODO: Figure out the correct value for the buffer size.\n   404\u2192\t\tincoming:              make(chan watchCacheEvent, 100),\n   405\u2192\t\tdispatchTimeoutBudget: newTimeBudget(),\n   406\u2192\t\t// We need to (potentially) stop both:\n   407\u2192\t\t// - wait.Until go-routine\n   408\u2192\t\t// - reflector.ListAndWatch\n   409\u2192\t\t// and there are no guarantees on the order that they will stop.\n   410\u2192\t\t// So we will be simply closing the channel, and synchronizing on the WaitGroup.\n   411\u2192\t\tstopCh:           stopCh,\n   412\u2192\t\tclock:            config.Clock,\n   413\u2192\t\ttimer:            time.NewTimer(time.Duration(0)),\n   414\u2192\t\tbookmarkWatchers: newTimeBucketWatchers(config.Clock, defaultBookmarkFrequency),\n   415\u2192\t}\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192\t// Ensure that timer is stopped.\n   418\u2192\tif !cacher.timer.Stop() {\n   419\u2192\t\t// Consume triggered (but not yet received) timer event\n   420\u2192\t\t// so that future reuse does not get a spurious timeout.\n   421\u2192\t\t<-cacher.timer.C\n   422\u2192\t}\n   423\u2192\tvar contextMetadata metadata.MD\n   424\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.SeparateCacheWatchRPC) {\n   425\u2192\t\t// Add grpc context metadata to watch and progress notify requests done by cacher to:\n   426\u2192\t\t// * Prevent starvation of watch opened by cacher, by moving it to separate Watch RPC than watch request that bypass cacher.\n   427\u2192\t\t// * Ensure that progress notification requests are executed on the same Watch RPC as their watch, which is required for it to work.\n   428\u2192\t\tcontextMetadata = metadata.New(map[string]string{\"source\": \"cache\"})\n   429\u2192\t}\n   430\u2192\n   431\u2192\teventFreshDuration := config.EventsHistoryWindow\n   432\u2192\tif eventFreshDuration < DefaultEventFreshDuration {\n   433\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"config.EventsHistoryWindow (%v) must not be below %v\", eventFreshDuration, DefaultEventFreshDuration)\n   434\u2192\t}\n   435\u2192\n   436\u2192\tprogressRequester := progress.NewConditionalProgressRequester(config.Storage.RequestWatchProgress, config.Clock, contextMetadata)\n   437\u2192\twatchCache := newWatchCache(\n   438\u2192\t\tconfig.KeyFunc, cacher.processEvent, config.GetAttrsFunc, config.Versioner, config.Indexers,\n   439\u2192\t\tconfig.Clock, eventFreshDuration, config.GroupResource, progressRequester, config.Storage.GetCurrentResourceVersion)\n   440\u2192\tlisterWatcher := NewListerWatcher(config.Storage, resourcePrefix, config.NewListFunc, contextMetadata)\n   441\u2192\treflectorName := \"storage/cacher.go:\" + resourcePrefix\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192\treflector := cache.NewNamedReflector(reflectorName, listerWatcher, obj, watchCache, 0)\n   444\u2192\t// Configure reflector's pager to for an appropriate pagination chunk size for fetching data from"

either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package registry\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    21\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage\"\n    29\u2192\tcacherstorage \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher\"\n    30\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/storagebackend\"\n    31\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/storagebackend/factory\"\n    32\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n    33\u2192)\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192// Creates a cacher based given storageConfig.\n    36\u2192func StorageWithCacher() generic.StorageDecorator {\n    37\u2192\treturn func(\n    38\u2192\t\tstorageConfig *storagebackend.ConfigForResource,\n    39\u2192\t\tresourcePrefix string,\n    40\u2192\t\tkeyFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error),\n    41\u2192\t\tnewFunc func() runtime.Object,\n    42\u2192\t\tnewListFunc func() runtime.Object,\n    43\u2192\t\tgetAttrsFunc storage.AttrFunc,\n    44\u2192\t\ttriggerFuncs storage.IndexerFuncs,\n    45\u2192\t\tindexers *cache.Indexers) (storage.Interface, factory.DestroyFunc, error) {\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192\t\ts, d, err := generic.NewRawStorage(storageConfig, newFunc, newListFunc, resourcePrefix)\n    48\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    49\u2192\t\t\treturn s, d, err\n    50\u2192\t\t}\n    51\u2192\t\tif klogV := klog.V(5); klogV.Enabled() {\n    52\u2192\t\t\t//nolint:logcheck // It complains about the key/value pairs because it cannot check them.\n    53\u2192\t\t\tklogV.InfoS(\"Storage caching is enabled\", objectTypeToArgs(newFunc())...)\n    54\u2192\t\t}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192\t\tcacherConfig := cacherstorage.Config{\n    57\u2192\t\t\tStorage:             s,\n    58\u2192\t\t\tVersioner:           storage.APIObjectVersioner{},\n    59\u2192\t\t\tGroupResource:       storageConfig.GroupResource,\n    60\u2192\t\t\tEventsHistoryWindow: storageConfig.EventsHistoryWindow,\n    61\u2192\t\t\tResourcePrefix:      resourcePrefix,\n    62\u2192\t\t\tKeyFunc:             keyFunc,\n    63\u2192\t\t\tNewFunc:             newFunc,\n    64\u2192\t\t\tNewListFunc:         newListFunc,\n    65\u2192\t\t\tGetAttrsFunc:        getAttrsFunc,\n    66\u2192\t\t\tIndexerFuncs:        triggerFuncs,\n    67\u2192\t\t\tIndexers:            indexers,\n    68\u2192\t\t\tCodec:               storageConfig.Codec,\n    69\u2192\t\t}\n    70\u2192\t\tcacher, err := cacherstorage.NewCacherFromConfig(cacherConfig)\n    71\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    72\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, func() {}, err\n    73\u2192\t\t}\n    74\u2192\t\tdelegator := cacherstorage.NewCacheDelegator(cacher, s)\n    75\u2192\t\tvar once sync.Once\n    76\u2192\t\tdestroyFunc := func() {\n    77\u2192\t\t\tonce.Do(func() {\n    78\u2192\t\t\t\tdelegator.Stop()\n    79\u2192\t\t\t\tcacher.Stop()\n    80\u2192\t\t\t\td()\n    81\u2192\t\t\t})\n    82\u2192\t\t}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192\t\treturn delegator, destroyFunc, nil\n    85\u2192\t}\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192func objectTypeToArgs(obj runtime.Object) []interface{} {\n    89\u2192\t// special-case unstructured objects that tell us their apiVersion/kind\n    90\u2192\tif u, isUnstructured := obj.(*unstructured.Unstructured); isUnstructured {\n    91\u2192\t\tif apiVersion, kind := u.GetAPIVersion(), u.GetKind(); len(apiVersion) > 0 && len(kind) > 0 {\n    92\u2192\t\t\treturn []interface{}{\"apiVersion\", apiVersion, \"kind\", kind}\n    93\u2192\t\t}\n    94\u2192\t}\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192\t// otherwise just return the type\n    97\u2192\treturn []interface{}{\"type\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", obj)}\n    98\u2192}\n    99\u2192"

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage/storage_factory.go",

Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\n    15\u2192*/\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192package storage\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import (\n    20\u2192\t\"crypto/tls\"\n    21\u2192\t\"crypto/x509\"\n    22\u2192\t\"os\"\n    23\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/storagebackend\"\n    29\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    30\u2192)\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192// Backend describes the storage servers, the information here should be enough\n    33\u2192// for health validations.\n    34\u2192type Backend struct {\n    35\u2192\t// the url of storage backend like: https://etcd.domain:2379\n    36\u2192\tServer string\n    37\u2192\t// the required tls config\n    38\u2192\tTLSConfig *tls.Config\n    39\u2192}\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192// StorageFactory is the interface to locate the storage for a given GroupResource\n    42\u2192type StorageFactory interface {\n    43\u2192\t// New finds the storage destination for the given group and resource. It will\n    44\u2192\t// return an error if the group has no storage destination configured.\n    45\u2192\tNewConfig(groupResource schema.GroupResource, example runtime.Object) (*storagebackend.ConfigForResource, error)\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192\t// ResourcePrefix returns the overridden resource prefix for the GroupResource\n    48\u2192\t// This allows for cohabitation of resources with different native types and provides\n    49\u2192\t// centralized control over the shape of etcd directories\n    50\u2192\tResourcePrefix(groupResource schema.GroupResource) string\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192\t// Configs gets configurations for all of registered storage destinations.\n    53\u2192\tConfigs() []storagebackend.Config\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192\t// Backends gets all backends for all registered storage destinations.\n    56\u2192\t// Used for getting all instances for health validations.\n    57\u2192\t// Deprecated: Use Configs instead\n    58\u2192\tBackends() []Backend\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192// DefaultStorageFactory takes a GroupResource and returns back its storage interface.  This result includes:\n    62\u2192// 1. Merged etcd config, including: auth, server locations, prefixes\n    63\u2192// 2. Resource encodings for storage: group,version,kind to store as\n    64\u2192// 3. Cohabitating default: some resources like hpa are exposed through multiple APIs.  They must agree on 1 and 2\n    65\u2192type DefaultStorageFactory struct {\n    66\u2192\t// StorageConfig describes how to create a storage backend in general.\n    67\u2192\t// Its authentication information will be used for every storage.Interface returned.\n    68\u2192\tStorageConfig storagebackend.Config\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192\tOverrides map[schema.GroupResource]groupResourceOverrides\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192\tDefaultResourcePrefixes map[schema.GroupResource]string\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192\t// DefaultMediaType is the media type used to store resources. If it is not set, \"application/json\" is used.\n    75\u2192\tDefaultMediaType string\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192\t// DefaultSerializer is used to create encoders and decoders for the storage.Interface.\n    78\u2192\tDefaultSerializer runtime.StorageSerializer\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192\t// ResourceEncodingConfig describes how to encode a particular GroupVersionResource\n    81\u2192\tResourceEncodingConfig ResourceEncodingConfig\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192\t// APIResourceConfigSource indicates whether the *storage* is enabled, NOT the API\n    84\u2192\t// This is discrete from resource enablement because those are separate concerns.  How this source is configured\n    85\u2192\t// is left to the caller.\n    86\u2192\tAPIResourceConfigSource APIResourceConfigSource\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192\t// newStorageCodecFn exists to be overwritten for unit testing.\n    89\u2192\tnewStorageCodecFn func(opts StorageCodecConfig) (codec runtime.Codec, encodeVersioner runtime.GroupVersioner, err error)\n    90\u2192}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192type groupResourceOverrides struct {\n    93\u2192\t// etcdLocation contains the list of \"special\" locations that are used for particular GroupResources\n    94\u2192\t// These are merged on top of the StorageConfig when requesting the storage.Interface for a given GroupResource\n    95\u2192\tetcdLocation []string\n    96\u2192\t// etcdPrefix is the base location for a GroupResource.\n    97\u2192\tetcdPrefix string\n    98\u2192\t// etcdResourcePrefix is the location to use to store a particular type under the `etcdPrefix` location\n    99\u2192\t// If empty, the default mapping is used.  If the default mapping doesn't contain an entry, it will use\n   100\u2192\t// the ToLowered name of the resource, not including the group.\n   101\u2192\tetcdResourcePrefix string\n   102\u2192\t// mediaType is the desired serializer to choose. If empty, the default is chosen.\n   103\u2192\tmediaType string\n   104\u2192\t// serializer contains the list of \"special\" serializers for a GroupResource.  Resource=* means for the entire group\n   105\u2192\tserializer runtime.StorageSerializer\n   106\u2192\t// cohabitatingResources keeps track of which resources must be stored together.  This happens when we have multiple ways\n   107\u2192\t// of exposing one set of concepts.  autoscaling.HPA and extensions.HPA as a for instance\n   108\u2192\t// The order of the slice matters!  It is the priority order of lookup for finding a storage location\n   109\u2192\tcohabitatingResources []schema.GroupResource\n   110\u2192\t// encoderDecoratorFn is optional and may wrap the provided encoder prior to being serialized.\n   111\u2192\tencoderDecoratorFn func(runtime.Encoder) runtime.Encoder\n   112\u2192\t// decoderDecoratorFn is optional and may wrap the provided decoders (can add new decoders). The order of\n   113\u2192\t// returned decoders will be priority for attempt to decode.\n   114\u2192\tdecoderDecoratorFn func([]runtime.Decoder) []runtime.Decoder\n   115\u2192}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192// Apply overrides the provided config and options if the override has a value in that position\n   118\u2192func (o groupResourceOverrides) Apply(config *storagebackend.Config, options *StorageCodecConfig) {\n   119\u2192\tif len(o.etcdLocation) > 0 {\n   120\u2192\t\tconfig.Transport.ServerList = o.etcdLocation\n   121\u2192\t}\n   122\u2192\tif len(o.etcdPrefix) > 0 {\n   123\u2192\t\tconfig.Prefix = o.etcdPrefix\n   124\u2192\t}\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192\tif len(o.mediaType) > 0 {\n   127\u2192\t\toptions.StorageMediaType = o.mediaType\n   128\u2192\t}\n   129\u2192\tif o.serializer != nil {\n   130\u2192\t\toptions.StorageSerializer = o.serializer\n   131\u2192\t}\n   132\u2192\tif o.encoderDecoratorFn != nil {\n   133\u2192\t\toptions.EncoderDecoratorFn = o.encoderDecoratorFn\n   134\u2192\t}\n   135\u2192\tif o.decoderDecoratorFn != nil {\n   136\u2192\t\toptions.DecoderDecoratorFn = o.decoderDecoratorFn\n   137\u2192\t}\n   138\u2192}\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192var _ StorageFactory = &DefaultStorageFactory{}\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192const AllResources = \"*\"\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192func NewDefaultStorageFactory(\n   145\u2192\tconfig storagebackend.Config,\n   146\u2192\tdefaultMediaType string,\n   147\u2192\tdefaultSerializer runtime.StorageSerializer,\n   148\u2192\tresourceEncodingConfig ResourceEncodingConfig,\n   149\u2192\tresourceConfig APIResourceConfigSource,\n   150\u2192\tspecialDefaultResourcePrefixes map[schema.GroupResource]string,\n   151\u2192) *DefaultStorageFactory {\n   152\u2192\tif len(defaultMediaType) == 0 {\n   153\u2192\t\tdefaultMediaType = runtime.ContentTypeJSON\n   154\u2192\t}\n   155\u2192\treturn &DefaultStorageFactory{\n   156\u2192\t\tStorageConfig:           config,\n   157\u2192\t\tOverrides:               map[schema.GroupResource]groupResourceOverrides{},\n   158\u2192\t\tDefaultMediaType:        defaultMediaType,\n   159\u2192\t\tDefaultSerializer:       defaultSerializer,\n   160\u2192\t\tResourceEncodingConfig:  resourceEncodingConfig,\n   161\u2192\t\tAPIResourceConfigSource: resourceConfig,\n   162\u2192\t\tDefaultResourcePrefixes: specialDefaultResourcePrefixes,\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192\t\tnewStorageCodecFn: NewStorageCodec,\n   165\u2192\t}\n   166\u2192}\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192func (s *DefaultStorageFactory) SetEtcdLocation(groupResource schema.GroupResource, location []string) {\n   169\u2192\toverrides := s.Overrides[groupResource]\n   170\u2192\toverrides.etcdLocation = location\n   171\u2192\ts.Overrides[groupResource] = overrides\n   172\u2192}\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192func (s *DefaultStorageFactory) SetEtcdPrefix(groupResource schema.GroupResource, prefix string) {\n   175\u2192\toverrides := s.Overrides[groupResource]\n   176\u2192\toverrides.etcdPrefix = prefix\n   177\u2192\ts.Overrides[groupResource] = overrides\n   178\u2192}\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192// SetResourceEtcdPrefix sets the prefix for a resource, but not the base-dir.  You'll end up in `etcdPrefix/resourceEtcdPrefix`.\n   181\u2192func (s *DefaultStorageFactory) SetResourceEtcdPrefix(groupResource schema.GroupResource, prefix string) {\n   182\u2192\toverrides := s.Overrides[groupResource]\n   183\u2192\toverrides.etcdResourcePrefix = prefix\n   184\u2192\ts.Overrides[groupResource] = overrides\n   185\u2192}\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192func (s *DefaultStorageFactory) SetSerializer(groupResource schema.GroupResource, mediaType string, serializer runtime.StorageSerializer) {\n   188\u2192\toverrides := s.Overrides[groupResource]\n   189\u2192\toverrides.mediaType = mediaType\n   190\u2192\toverrides.serializer = serializer\n   191\u2192\ts.Overrides[groupResource] = overrides\n   192\u2192}\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192// AddCohabitatingResources links resources together the order of the slice matters!  its the priority order of lookup for finding a storage location\n   195\u2192func (s *DefaultStorageFactory) AddCohabitatingResources(groupResources ...schema.GroupResource) {\n   196\u2192\tfor _, groupResource := range groupResources {\n   197\u2192\t\toverrides := s.Overrides[groupResource]\n   198\u2192\t\toverrides.cohabitatingResources = groupResources\n   199\u2192\t\ts.Overrides[groupResource] = overrides\n   200\u2192\t}"

39\u2192\tStorageObjectCountTracker flowcontrolrequest.StorageObjectCountTracker\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192// Implement RESTOptionsGetter so that RESTOptions can directly be used when available (i.e. tests)\n    43\u2192func (opts RESTOptions) GetRESTOptions(schema.GroupResource, runtime.Object) (RESTOptions, error) {\n    44\u2192\treturn opts, nil\n    45\u2192}\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192type RESTOptionsGetter interface {\n    48\u2192\t// GetRESTOptions returns the RESTOptions for the given resource and example object.\n    49\u2192\t// The example object is used to determine the storage version for the resource.\n    50\u2192\t// If the example object is nil, the storage version will be determined by the resource's default storage version.\n    51\u2192\tGetRESTOptions(resource schema.GroupResource, example runtime.Object) (RESTOptions, error)\n    52\u2192}\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192// StoreOptions is set of configuration options used to complete generic registries.\n    55\u2192type StoreOptions struct {\n    56\u2192\tRESTOptions RESTOptionsGetter\n    57\u2192\tTriggerFunc storage.IndexerFuncs\n    58\u2192\tAttrFunc    storage.AttrFunc\n    59\u2192\tIndexers    *cache.Indexers\n    60\u2192}\n    61\u2192"

\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n    53\u2192\tversioninfo \"k8s.io/component-base/version\"\n    54\u2192)\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192const (\n    57\u2192\tRouteMetaGVK              = \"x-kubernetes-group-version-kind\"\n    58\u2192\tRouteMetaSelectableFields = \"x-kubernetes-selectable-fields\"\n    59\u2192\tRouteMetaAction           = \"x-kubernetes-action\"\n    60\u2192)\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192type APIInstaller struct {\n    63\u2192\tgroup             *APIGroupVersion\n    64\u2192\tprefix            string // Path prefix where API resources are to be registered.\n    65\u2192\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n    66\u2192}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// Struct capturing information about an action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\n    69\u2192type action struct {\n    70\u2192\tVerb          string               // Verb identifying the action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\n    71\u2192\tPath          string               // The path of the action\n    72\u2192\tParams        []*restful.Parameter // List of parameters associated with the action.\n    73\u2192\tNamer         handlers.ScopeNamer\n    74\u2192\tAllNamespaces bool // true iff the action is namespaced but works on aggregate result for all namespaces\n    75\u2192}\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192func ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(list []metav1.APIResource) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, error) {\n    78\u2192\tvar apiResourceList []apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery\n    79\u2192\tparentResources := make(map[string]int)\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t// Loop through all top-level resources\n    82\u2192\tfor _, r := range list {\n    83\u2192\t\tif strings.Contains(r.Name, \"/\") {\n    84\u2192\t\t\t// Skip subresources for now so we can get the list of resources\n    85\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    86\u2192\t\t}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192\t\tvar scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope\n    89\u2192\t\tif r.Namespaced {\n    90\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace\n    91\u2192\t\t} else {\n    92\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\n    93\u2192\t\t}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\t\tresource := apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{\n    96\u2192\t\t\tResource: r.Name,\n    97\u2192\t\t\tScope:    scope,\n    98\u2192\t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{\n    99\u2192\t\t\t\tGroup:   r.Group,\n   100\u2192\t\t\t\tVersion: r.Version,\n   101\u2192\t\t\t\tKind:    r.Kind,\n   102\u2192\t\t\t},\n   103\u2192\t\t\tVerbs:            r.Verbs,\n   104\u2192\t\t\tShortNames:       r.ShortNames,\n   105\u2192\t\t\tCategories:       r.Categories,\n   106\u2192\t\t\tSingularResource: r.SingularName,\n   107\u2192\t\t}\n   108\u2192\t\tapiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, resource)\n   109\u2192\t\tparentResources[r.Name] = len(apiResourceList) - 1\n   110\u2192\t}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192\t// Loop through all subresources\n   113\u2192\tfor _, r := range list {\n   114\u2192\t\t// Split resource name and subresource name\n   115\u2192\t\tsplit := strings.SplitN(r.Name, \"/\", 2)\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192\t\tif len(split) != 2 {\n   118\u2192\t\t\t// Skip parent resources\n   119\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   120\u2192\t\t}\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192\t\tvar scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope\n   123\u2192\t\tif r.Namespaced {\n   124\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace\n   125\u2192\t\t} else {\n   126\u2192\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\n   127\u2192\t\t}\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192\t\tparentidx, exists := parentResources[split[0]]\n   130\u2192\t\tif !exists {\n   131\u2192\t\t\t// If a subresource exists without a parent, create a parent\n   132\u2192\t\t\tapiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{\n   133\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: split[0],\n   134\u2192\t\t\t\tScope:    scope,\n   135\u2192\t\t\t\t// avoid nil panics in v0.26.0-v0.26.3 client-go clients\n   136\u2192\t\t\t\t// see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/118361\n   137\u2192\t\t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{},\n   138\u2192\t\t\t})\n   139\u2192\t\t\tparentidx = len(apiResourceList) - 1\n   140\u2192\t\t\tparentResources[split[0]] = parentidx\n   141\u2192\t\t}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192\t\tif apiResourceList[parentidx].Scope != scope {\n   144\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Error: Parent %s (scope: %s) and subresource %s (scope: %s) scope do not match\", split[0], apiResourceList[parentidx].Scope, split[1], scope)\n   145\u2192\t\t\t//\n   146\u2192\t\t}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192\t\tsubresource := apidiscoveryv2.APISubresourceDiscovery{\n   149\u2192\t\t\tSubresource: split[1],\n   150\u2192\t\t\tVerbs:       r.Verbs,\n   151\u2192\t\t\t// avoid nil panics in v0.26.0-v0.26.3 client-go clients\n   152\u2192\t\t\t// see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/118361\n   153\u2192\t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{},\n   154\u2192\t\t}\n   155\u2192\t\tif r.Kind != \"\" {\n   156\u2192\t\t\tsubresource.ResponseKind = &metav1.GroupVersionKind{\n   157\u2192\t\t\t\tGroup:   r.Group,\n   158\u2192\t\t\t\tVersion: r.Version,\n   159\u2192\t\t\t\tKind:    r.Kind,\n   160\u2192\t\t\t}\n   161\u2192\t\t}\n   162\u2192\t\tapiResourceList[parentidx].Subresources = append(apiResourceList[parentidx].Subresources, subresource)\n   163\u2192\t}\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192\treturn apiResourceList, nil\n   166\u2192}\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192// An interface to see if one storage supports override its default verb for monitoring\n   169\u2192type StorageMetricsOverride interface {\n   170\u2192\t// OverrideMetricsVerb gives a storage object an opportunity to override the verb reported to the metrics endpoint\n   171\u2192\tOverrideMetricsVerb(oldVerb string) (newVerb string)\n   172\u2192}\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192// An interface to see if an object supports swagger documentation as a method\n   175\u2192type documentable interface {\n   176\u2192\tSwaggerDoc() map[string]string\n   177\u2192}\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192// toDiscoveryKubeVerb maps an action.Verb to the logical kube verb, used for discovery\n   180\u2192var toDiscoveryKubeVerb = map[string]string{\n   181\u2192\trequest.MethodConnect:          \"\", // do not list in discovery.\n   182\u2192\trequest.MethodDelete:           \"delete\",\n   183\u2192\trequest.MethodDeleteCollection: \"deletecollection\",\n   184\u2192\trequest.MethodGet:              \"get\",\n   185\u2192\trequest.MethodList:             \"list\",\n   186\u2192\trequest.MethodPatch:            \"patch\",\n   187\u2192\trequest.MethodPost:             \"create\",\n   188\u2192\trequest.MethodProxy:            \"proxy\",\n   189\u2192\trequest.MethodPut:              \"update\",\n   190\u2192\trequest.MethodWatch:            \"watch\",\n   191\u2192\trequest.MethodWatchList:        \"watch\",\n   192\u2192}\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192// Install handlers for API resources.\n   195\u2192func (a *APIInstaller) Install() ([]metav1.APIResource, []*storageversion.ResourceInfo, *restful.WebService, []error) {\n   196\u2192\tvar apiResources []metav1.APIResource\n   197\u2192\tvar resourceInfos []*storageversion.ResourceInfo\n   198\u2192\tvar errors []error\n   199\u2192\tws := a.newWebService()\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192\t// Register the paths in a deterministic (sorted) order to get a deterministic swagger spec.\n   202\u2192\tpaths := make([]string, len(a.group.Storage))\n   203\u2192\tvar i int = 0\n   204\u2192\tfor path := range a.group.Storage {\n   205\u2192\t\tpaths[i] = path\n   206\u2192\t\ti++\n   207\u2192\t}\n   208\u2192\tsort.Strings(paths)\n   209\u2192\tfor _, path := range paths {\n   210\u2192\t\tapiResource, resourceInfo, err := a.registerResourceHandlers(path, a.group.Storage[path], ws)\n   211\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   212\u2192\t\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Errorf(\"error in registering resource: %s, %v\", path, err))\n   213\u2192\t\t}\n   214\u2192\t\tif apiResource != nil {\n   215\u2192\t\t\tapiResources = append(apiResources, *apiResource)\n   216\u2192\t\t}\n   217\u2192\t\tif resourceInfo != nil {\n   218\u2192\t\t\tresourceInfos = append(resourceInfos, resourceInfo)\n   219\u2192\t\t}\n   220\u2192\t}\n   221\u2192\treturn apiResources, resourceInfos, ws, errors\n   222\u2192}\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192// newWebService creates a new restful webservice with the api installer's prefix and version.\n   225\u2192func (a *APIInstaller) newWebService() *restful.WebService {\n   226\u2192\tws := new(restful.WebService)\n   227\u2192\tws.Path(a.prefix)\n   228\u2192\t// a.prefix contains \"prefix/group/version\"\n   229\u2192\tws.Doc(\"API at \" + a.prefix)\n   230\u2192\t// Backwards compatibility, we accepted objects with empty content-type at V1.\n   231\u2192\t// If we stop using go-restful, we can default empty content-type to application/json on an\n   232\u2192\t// endpoint by endpoint basis\n   233\u2192\tws.Consumes(\"*/*\")\n   234\u2192\tmediaTypes, streamMediaTypes := negotiation.MediaTypesForSerializer(a.group.Serializer)\n   235\u2192\tws.Produces(append(mediaTypes, streamMediaTypes...)...)\n   236\u2192\tws.ApiVersion(a.group.GroupVersion.String())\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192\treturn ws\n   239\u2192}\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192// calculate the storage gvk, the gvk objects are converted to before persisted to the etcd.\n   242\u2192func getStorageVersionKind(storageVersioner runtime.GroupVersioner, storage rest.Storage, typer runtime.ObjectTyper) (schema.GroupVersionKind, error) {\n   243\u2192\tobject := storage.New()\n   244\u2192\tfqKinds, _, err := typer.ObjectKinds(object)\n   245\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   246\u2192\t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, err\n   247\u2192\t}\n   248\u2192\tgvk, ok := storageVersioner.KindForGroupVersionKinds(fqKinds)\n   249\u2192\tif !ok {\n   250\u2192\t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot find the storage version kind for %v\", reflect.TypeOf(object))"

Output
"   250\u2192\t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot find the storage version kind for %v\", reflect.TypeOf(object))\n   251\u2192\t}\n   252\u2192\treturn gvk, nil\n   253\u2192}\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192// GetResourceKind returns the external group version kind registered for the given storage\n   256\u2192// object. If the storage object is a subresource and has an override supplied for it, it returns\n   257\u2192// the group version kind supplied in the override.\n   258\u2192func GetResourceKind(groupVersion schema.GroupVersion, storage rest.Storage, typer runtime.ObjectTyper) (schema.GroupVersionKind, error) {\n   259\u2192\t// Let the storage tell us exactly what GVK it has\n   260\u2192\tif gvkProvider, ok := storage.(rest.GroupVersionKindProvider); ok {\n   261\u2192\t\treturn gvkProvider.GroupVersionKind(groupVersion), nil\n   262\u2192\t}\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192\tobject := storage.New()\n   265\u2192\tfqKinds, _, err := typer.ObjectKinds(object)\n   266\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   267\u2192\t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, err\n   268\u2192\t}\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192\t// a given go type can have multiple potential fully qualified kinds.  Find the one that corresponds with the group\n   271\u2192\t// we're trying to register here\n   272\u2192\tfqKindToRegister := schema.GroupVersionKind{}\n   273\u2192\tfor _, fqKind := range fqKinds {\n   274\u2192\t\tif fqKind.Group == groupVersion.Group {\n   275\u2192\t\t\tfqKindToRegister = groupVersion.WithKind(fqKind.Kind)\n   276\u2192\t\t\tbreak\n   277\u2192\t\t}\n   278\u2192\t}\n   279\u2192\tif fqKindToRegister.Empty() {\n   280\u2192\t\treturn schema.GroupVersionKind{}, fmt.Errorf(\"unable to locate fully qualified kind for %v: found %v when registering for %v\", reflect.TypeOf(object), fqKinds, groupVersion)\n   281\u2192\t}\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192\t// group is guaranteed to match based on the check above\n   284\u2192\treturn fqKindToRegister, nil\n   285\u2192}\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192func (a *APIInstaller) registerResourceHandlers(path string, storage rest.Storage, ws *restful.WebService) (*metav1.APIResource, *storageversion.ResourceInfo, error) {\n   288\u2192\tadmit := a.group.Admit\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192\toptionsExternalVersion := a.group.GroupVersion\n   291\u2192\tif a.group.OptionsExternalVersion != nil {\n   292\u2192\t\toptionsExternalVersion = *a.group.OptionsExternalVersion\n   293\u2192\t}\n   294\u2192\n   295\u2192\tresource, subresource, err := splitSubresource(path)\n   296\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   297\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   298\u2192\t}\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192\tgroup, version := a.group.GroupVersion.Group, a.group.GroupVersion.Version\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192\tfqKindToRegister, err := GetResourceKind(a.group.GroupVersion, storage, a.group.Typer)\n   303\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   304\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   305\u2192\t}\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192\tversionedPtr, err := a.group.Creater.New(fqKindToRegister)\n   308\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   309\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   310\u2192\t}\n   311\u2192\tdefaultVersionedObject := indirectArbitraryPointer(versionedPtr)\n   312\u2192\tkind := fqKindToRegister.Kind\n   313\u2192\tisSubresource := len(subresource) > 0\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192\t// If there is a subresource, namespace scoping is defined by the parent resource\n   316\u2192\tvar namespaceScoped bool\n   317\u2192\tif isSubresource {\n   318\u2192\t\tparentStorage, ok := a.group.Storage[resource]\n   319\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   320\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"missing parent storage: %q\", resource)\n   321\u2192\t\t}\n   322\u2192\t\tscoper, ok := parentStorage.(rest.Scoper)\n   323\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   324\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%q must implement scoper\", resource)\n   325\u2192\t\t}\n   326\u2192\t\tnamespaceScoped = scoper.NamespaceScoped()\n   327\u2192\n   328\u2192\t} else {\n   329\u2192\t\tscoper, ok := storage.(rest.Scoper)\n   330\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   331\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%q must implement scoper\", resource)\n   332\u2192\t\t}\n   333\u2192\t\tnamespaceScoped = scoper.NamespaceScoped()\n   334\u2192\t}\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192\t// what verbs are supported by the storage, used to know what verbs we support per path\n   337\u2192\tcreater, isCreater := storage.(rest.Creater)\n   338\u2192\tnamedCreater, isNamedCreater := storage.(rest.NamedCreater)\n   339\u2192\tlister, isLister := storage.(rest.Lister)\n   340\u2192\tgetter, isGetter := storage.(rest.Getter)\n   341\u2192\tgetterWithOptions, isGetterWithOptions := storage.(rest.GetterWithOptions)\n   342\u2192\tgracefulDeleter, isGracefulDeleter := storage.(rest.GracefulDeleter)\n   343\u2192\tcollectionDeleter, isCollectionDeleter := storage.(rest.CollectionDeleter)\n   344\u2192\tupdater, isUpdater := storage.(rest.Updater)\n   345\u2192\tpatcher, isPatcher := storage.(rest.Patcher)\n   346\u2192\twatcher, isWatcher := storage.(rest.Watcher)\n   347\u2192\tconnecter, isConnecter := storage.(rest.Connecter)\n   348\u2192\tstorageMeta, isMetadata := storage.(rest.StorageMetadata)\n   349\u2192\tstorageVersionProvider, isStorageVersionProvider := storage.(rest.StorageVersionProvider)\n   350\u2192\tgvAcceptor, _ := storage.(rest.GroupVersionAcceptor)\n   351\u2192\tif !isMetadata {\n   352\u2192\t\tstorageMeta = defaultStorageMetadata{}\n   353\u2192\t}\n   354\u2192\n   355\u2192\tif isNamedCreater {\n   356\u2192\t\tisCreater = true\n   357\u2192\t}\n   358\u2192\n   359\u2192\tvar versionedList interface{}\n   360\u2192\tif isLister {\n   361\u2192\t\tlist := lister.NewList()\n   362\u2192\t\tlistGVKs, _, err := a.group.Typer.ObjectKinds(list)\n   363\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   364\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   365\u2192\t\t}\n   366\u2192\t\tversionedListPtr, err := a.group.Creater.New(a.group.GroupVersion.WithKind(listGVKs[0].Kind))\n   367\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   368\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   369\u2192\t\t}\n   370\u2192\t\tversionedList = indirectArbitraryPointer(versionedListPtr)\n   371\u2192\t}\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192\tversionedListOptions, err := a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"ListOptions\"))\n   374\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   375\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   376\u2192\t}\n   377\u2192\tversionedCreateOptions, err := a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"CreateOptions\"))\n   378\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   379\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   380\u2192\t}\n   381\u2192\tversionedPatchOptions, err := a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"PatchOptions\"))\n   382\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   383\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   384\u2192\t}\n   385\u2192\tversionedUpdateOptions, err := a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"UpdateOptions\"))\n   386\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   387\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   388\u2192\t}\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192\tvar versionedDeleteOptions runtime.Object\n   391\u2192\tvar versionedDeleterObject interface{}\n   392\u2192\tdeleteReturnsDeletedObject := false\n   393\u2192\tif isGracefulDeleter {\n   394\u2192\t\tversionedDeleteOptions, err = a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"DeleteOptions\"))\n   395\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   396\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   397\u2192\t\t}\n   398\u2192\t\tversionedDeleterObject = indirectArbitraryPointer(versionedDeleteOptions)\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192\t\tif mayReturnFullObjectDeleter, ok := storage.(rest.MayReturnFullObjectDeleter); ok {\n   401\u2192\t\t\tdeleteReturnsDeletedObject = mayReturnFullObjectDeleter.DeleteReturnsDeletedObject()\n   402\u2192\t\t}\n   403\u2192\t}\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192\tversionedStatusPtr, err := a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(\"Status\"))\n   406\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   407\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   408\u2192\t}\n   409\u2192\tversionedStatus := indirectArbitraryPointer(versionedStatusPtr)\n   410\u2192\tvar (\n   411\u2192\t\tgetOptions             runtime.Object\n   412\u2192\t\tversionedGetOptions    runtime.Object\n   413\u2192\t\tgetOptionsInternalKind schema.GroupVersionKind\n   414\u2192\t\tgetSubpath             bool\n   415\u2192\t)\n   416\u2192\tif isGetterWithOptions {\n   417\u2192\t\tgetOptions, getSubpath, _ = getterWithOptions.NewGetOptions()\n   418\u2192\t\tgetOptionsInternalKinds, _, err := a.group.Typer.ObjectKinds(getOptions)\n   419\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   420\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   421\u2192\t\t}\n   422\u2192\t\tgetOptionsInternalKind = getOptionsInternalKinds[0]\n   423\u2192\t\tversionedGetOptions, err = a.group.Creater.New(a.group.GroupVersion.WithKind(getOptionsInternalKind.Kind))\n   424\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   425\u2192\t\t\tversionedGetOptions, err = a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(getOptionsInternalKind.Kind))\n   426\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   427\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   428\u2192\t\t\t}\n   429\u2192\t\t}\n   430\u2192\t\tisGetter = true\n   431\u2192\t}\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192\tvar versionedWatchEvent interface{}\n   434\u2192\tif isWatcher {\n   435\u2192\t\tversionedWatchEventPtr, err := a.group.Creater.New(a.group.GroupVersion.WithKind(\"WatchEvent\"))\n   436\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   437\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   438\u2192\t\t}\n   439\u2192\t\tversionedWatchEvent = indirectArbitraryPointer(versionedWatchEventPtr)\n   440\u2192\t}\n   441\u2192\n   442\u2192\tvar (\n   443\u2192\t\tconnectOptions             runtime.Object\n   444\u2192\t\tversionedConnectOptions    runtime.Object\n   445\u2192\t\tconnectOptionsInternalKind schema.GroupVersionKind\n   446\u2192\t\tconnectSubpath             bool\n   447\u2192\t)\n   448\u2192\tif isConnecter {\n   449\u2192\t\tconnectOptions, connectSubpath, _ = connecter.NewConnectOptions()\n   450\u2192\t\tif connectOptions != nil {\n   451\u2192\t\t\tconnectOptionsInternalKinds, _, err := a.group.Typer.ObjectKinds(connectOptions)\n   452\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   453\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   454\u2192\t\t\t}\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192\t\t\tconnectOptionsInternalKind = connectOptionsInternalKinds[0]\n   457\u2192\t\t\tversionedConnectOptions, err = a.group.Creater.New(a.group.GroupVersion.WithKind(connectOptionsInternalKind.Kind))\n   458\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   459\u2192\t\t\t\tversionedConnectOptions, err = a.group.Creater.New(optionsExternalVersion.WithKind(connectOptionsInternalKind.Kind))\n   460\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   461\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   462\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   463\u2192\t\t\t}\n   464\u2192\t\t}\n   465\u2192\t}\n   466\u2192\n   467\u2192\tallowWatchList := isWatcher && isLister // watching on lists is allowed only for kinds that support both watch and list.\n   468\u2192\tnameParam := ws.PathParameter(\"name\", \"name of the \"+kind).DataType(\"string\")\n   469\u2192\tpathParam := ws.PathParameter(\"path\", \"path to the resource\").DataType(\"string\")\n   470\u2192\n   471\u2192\tparams := []*restful.Parameter{}\n   472\u2192\tactions := []action{}\n   473\u2192\n   474\u2192\tvar resourceKind string\n   475\u2192\tkindProvider, ok := storage.(rest.KindProvider)\n   476\u2192\tif ok {\n   477\u2192\t\tresourceKind = kindProvider.Kind()\n   478\u2192\t} else {\n   479\u2192\t\tresourceKind = kind\n   480\u2192\t}\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192\ttableProvider, isTableProvider := storage.(rest.TableConvertor)\n   483\u2192\tif isLister && !isTableProvider {\n   484\u2192\t\t// All listers must implement TableProvider\n   485\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%q must implement TableConvertor\", resource)\n   486\u2192\t}\n   487\u2192\n   488\u2192\tvar apiResource metav1.APIResource\n   489\u2192\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.StorageVersionHash) &&\n   490\u2192\t\tisStorageVersionProvider &&\n   491\u2192\t\tstorageVersionProvider.StorageVersion() != nil {\n   492\u2192\t\tversioner := storageVersionProvider.StorageVersion()\n   493\u2192\t\tgvk, err := getStorageVersionKind(versioner, storage, a.group.Typer)\n   494\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   495\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n   496\u2192\t\t}\n   497\u2192\t\tapiResource.StorageVersionHash = discovery.StorageVersionHash(gvk.Group, gvk.Version, gvk.Kind)\n   498\u2192\t}\n   499\u2192\n   500\u2192\t// Get the list of actions for the given scope.\n   501\u2192\tswitch {\n   502\u2192\tcase !namespaceScoped:\n   503\u2192\t\t// Handle non-namespace scoped resources like nodes.\n   504\u2192\t\tresourcePath := resource\n   505\u2192\t\tresourceParams := params\n   506\u2192\t\titemPath := resourcePath + \"/{name}\"\n   507\u2192\t\tnameParams := append(params, nameParam)\n   508\u2192\t\tproxyParams := append(nameParams, pathParam)\n   509\u2192\t\tsuffix := \"\"\n   510\u2192\t\tif isSubresource {\n   511\u2192\t\t\tsuffix = \"/\" + subresource\n   512\u2192\t\t\titemPath = itemPath + suffix\n   513\u2192\t\t\tresourcePath = itemPath\n   514\u2192\t\t\tresourceParams = nameParams\n   515\u2192\t\t}\n   516\u2192\t\tapiResource.Name = path\n   517\u2192\t\tapiResource.Namespaced = false\n   518\u2192\t\tapiResource.Kind = resourceKind\n   519\u2192\t\tnamer := handlers.ContextBasedNaming{\n   520\u2192\t\t\tNamer:         a.group.Namer,\n   521\u2192\t\t\tClusterScoped: true,\n   522\u2192\t\t}\n   523\u2192\n   524\u2192\t\t// Handler for standard REST verbs (GET, PUT, POST and DELETE).\n   525\u2192\t\t// Add actions at the resource path: /api/apiVersion/resource\n   526\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodList, resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, isLister)\n   527\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodPost, resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, isCreater)\n   528\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodDeleteCollection, resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, isCollectionDeleter)\n   529\u2192\t\t// DEPRECATED in 1.11\n   530\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodWatchList, \"watch/\" + resourcePath, resourceParams, namer, false}, allowWatchList)\n   531\u2192\n   532\u2192\t\t// Add actions at the item path: /api/apiVersion/resource/{name}\n   533\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodGet, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isGetter)\n   534\u2192\t\tif getSubpath {\n   535\u2192\t\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodGet, itemPath + \"/{path:*}\", proxyParams, namer, false}, isGetter)\n   536\u2192\t\t}\n   537\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodPut, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isUpdater)\n   538\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodPatch, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isPatcher)\n   539\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodDelete, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isGracefulDeleter)\n   540\u2192\t\t// DEPRECATED in 1.11\n   541\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodWatch, \"watch/\" + itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isWatcher)\n   542\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodConnect, itemPath, nameParams, namer, false}, isConnecter)\n   543\u2192\t\tactions = appendIf(actions, action{request.MethodConnect, itemPath + \"/{path:*}\", proxyParams, namer, false}, isConnecter && connectSubpath)\n   544\u2192\tdefault:\n   545\u2192\t\tnamespaceParamName := \"namespaces\"\n   546\u2192\t\t// Handler for standard REST verbs (GET, PUT, POST and DELETE).\n   547\u2192\t\tnamespaceParam := ws.PathParameter(\"namespace\", \"object name and auth scope, such as for teams and projects\").DataType(\"string\")\n   548\u2192\t\tnamespacedPath := namespaceParamName + \"/{namespace}/\" + resource\n   549\u2192\t\tnamespaceParams := []*restful.Parameter{namespaceParam}"

165. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go",
  "limit": 90,
  "offset": 1015
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "func DefaultBuildHandlerChain(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) http.Handler {\n\thandler := apiHandler\n\n\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuthorization(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)\n\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"authorization\")\n\n\tif c.FlowControl != nil {\n\t\tworkEstimatorCfg := flowcontrolrequest.DefaultWorkEstimatorConfig()\n\t\trequestWorkEstimator := flowcontrolrequest.NewWorkEstimator(\n\t\t\tc.StorageObjectCountTracker.Get, c.FlowControl.GetInterestedWatchCount, workEstimatorCfg, c.FlowControl.GetMaxSeats)\n\t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithPriorityAndFairness(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.FlowControl, requestWorkEstimator, c.RequestTimeout/4)\n\t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"priorityandfairness\")\n\t} else {\n\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithMaxInFlightLimit(handler, c.MaxRequestsInFlight, c.MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight, c.LongRunningFunc)\n\t}\n\n\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n\tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.ConstrainedImpersonation) {\n\t\thandler = impersonation.WithConstrainedImpersonation(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)\n\t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"constrainedimpersonation\")\n\t} else {\n\t\thandler = impersonation.WithImpersonation(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)\n\t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"impersonation\")\n\t}\n\n\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAudit(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator, c.LongRunningFunc)\n\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"audit\")\n\n\tfailedHandler := genericapifilters.Unauthorized(c.Serializer)\n\tfailedHandler = genericapifilters.WithFailedAuthenticationAudit(failedHandler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator)\n\n\t// WithTracing comes after authentication so we can allow authenticated\n\t// clients to influence sampling.\n\tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerTracing) {\n\t\thandler = genericapifilters.WithTracing(handler, c.TracerProvider)\n\t}\n\tfailedHandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(failedHandler)\n\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuthentication(handler, c.Authentication.Authenticator, failedHandler, c.Authentication.APIAudiences, c.Authentication.RequestHeaderConfig)\n\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"authentication\")\n\n\thandler = genericfilters.WithCORS(handler, c.CorsAllowedOriginList, nil, nil, nil, \"true\")\n\n\t// WithWarningRecorder must be wrapped by the timeout handler\n\t// to make the addition of warning headers threadsafe\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithWarningRecorder(handler)\n\n\t// WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests will call the rest of the request handling in a go-routine with the\n\t// context with deadline. The go-routine can keep running, while the timeout logic will return a timeout to the client.\n\thandler = genericfilters.WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\n\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestDeadline(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator,\n\t\tc.LongRunningFunc, c.Serializer, c.RequestTimeout)\n\thandler = genericfilters.WithWaitGroup(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup)\n\tif c.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod > 0 {\n\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown(handler, c.lifecycleSignals, c.WatchRequestWaitGroup)\n\t}\n\tif c.SecureServing != nil && !c.SecureServing.DisableHTTP2 && c.GoawayChance > 0 {\n\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithProbabilisticGoaway(handler, c.GoawayChance)\n\t}\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithCacheControl(handler)\n\thandler = genericfilters.WithHSTS(handler, c.HSTSDirectives)\n\tif c.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n\t\thandler = genericfilters.WithRetryAfter(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest.Signaled())\n\t}\n\thandler = genericfilters.WithHTTPLogging(handler)\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithLatencyTrackers(handler)\n\t// WithRoutine will execute future handlers in a separate goroutine and serving\n\t// handler in current goroutine to minimize the stack memory usage. It must be\n\t// after WithPanicRecover() to be protected from panics.\n\tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServingWithRoutine) {\n\t\thandler = routine.WithRoutine(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\n\t}\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestInfo(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestReceivedTimestamp(handler)\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signaled())\n\thandler = genericfilters.WithPanicRecovery(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)\n\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuditInit(handler)\n\treturn handler\n}\n\nfunc installAPI(name string, s *GenericAPIServer, c *Config) {\n\tif c.EnableIndex {\n\t\troutes.Index{}.Install(s.listedPathProvider, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n\t}\n\tif c.EnableProfiling {\n\t\troutes.Profiling{}.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)",

    "numLines": 90,

    "totalLines": 1208

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go",

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\nCopyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\trt \"runtime\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\n\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/mux\"\n)\n\n// APIServerHandlers holds the different http.Handlers used by the API server.\n// This includes the full handler chain, the director (which chooses between gorestful and nonGoRestful,\n// the gorestful handler (used for the API) which falls through to the nonGoRestful handler on unregistered paths,\n// and the nonGoRestful handler (which can contain a fallthrough of its own)\n// FullHandlerChain -> Director -> {GoRestfulContainer,NonGoRestfulMux} based on inspection of registered web services\ntype APIServerHandler struct {\n\t// FullHandlerChain is the one that is eventually served with.  It should include the full filter\n\t// chain and then call the Director.\n\tFullHandlerChain http.Handler\n\t// The registered APIs.  InstallAPIs uses this.  Other servers probably shouldn't access this directly.\n\tGoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n\t// NonGoRestfulMux is the final HTTP handler in the chain.\n\t// It comes after all filters and the API handling\n\t// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.\n\tNonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\n\n\t// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.\n\t// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have\n\t// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)\n\t// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to\n\t// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding\n\t// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints\n\t// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the\n\t//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.\n\t// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to\n\t// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in\n\t// order to handle \"normal\" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think\n\t// we should consider completely removing gorestful.\n\t// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.\n\tDirector http.Handler\n}\n\n// HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.\n// It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization\ntype HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler\n\nfunc NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {\n\tnonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)\n\tif notFoundHandler != nil {\n\t\tnonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)\n\t}\n\n\tgorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()\n\tgorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}\n\tgorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {\n\t\tlogStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)\n\t})\n\tgorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {\n\t\tserviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)\n\t})\n\n\tdirector := director{\n\t\tname:               name,\n\t\tgoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n\t\tnonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n\t}\n\n\treturn &APIServerHandler{\n\t\tFullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),\n\t\tGoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n\t\tNonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n\t\tDirector:           director,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ListedPaths returns the paths that should be shown under /\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ListedPaths() []string {\n\tvar handledPaths []string\n\t// Extract the paths handled using restful.WebService\n\tfor _, ws := range a.GoRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n\t\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, ws.RootPath())\n\t}\n\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, a.NonGoRestfulMux.ListedPaths()...)\n\tsort.Strings(handledPaths)\n\n\treturn handledPaths\n}\n\ntype director struct {\n\tname               string\n\tgoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n\tnonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux\n}\n\nfunc (d director) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\tpath := req.URL.Path\n\n\t// check to see if our webservices want to claim this path\n\tfor _, ws := range d.goRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase ws.RootPath() == \"/apis\":\n\t\t\t// if we are exactly /apis or /apis/, then we need special handling in loop.\n\t\t\t// normally these are passed to the nonGoRestfulMux, but if discovery is enabled, it will go directly.\n\t\t\t// We can't rely on a prefix match since /apis matches everything (see the big comment on Director above)\n\t\t\tif path == \"/apis\" || path == \"/apis/\" {\n\t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n\t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n\t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n\t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tcase strings.HasPrefix(path, ws.RootPath()):\n\t\t\t// ensure an exact match or a path boundary match\n\t\t\tif len(path) == len(ws.RootPath()) || path[len(ws.RootPath())] == '/' {\n\t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n\t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n\t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n\t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// if we didn't find a match, then we just skip gorestful altogether\n\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by nonGoRestful\", d.name, req.Method, path)\n\td.nonGoRestfulMux.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n}\n\n// TODO: Unify with RecoverPanics?\nfunc logStackOnRecover(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, panicReason interface{}, w http.ResponseWriter) {\n\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\n\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"recover from panic situation: - %v\\r\\n\", panicReason))\n\tfor i := 2; ; i++ {\n\t\t_, file, line, ok := rt.Caller(i)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    %s:%d\\r\\n\", file, line))\n\t}\n\tklog.Errorln(buffer.String())\n\n\theaders := http.Header{}\n\tif ct := w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\"); len(ct) > 0 {\n\t\theaders.Set(\"Accept\", ct)\n\t}\n\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusInternalServerError, \"\", schema.GroupResource{}, \"\", \"\", 0, false), s, schema.GroupVersion{}, w, &http.Request{Header: headers})\n}\n\nfunc serviceErrorHandler(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, resp *restful.Response) {\n\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(\n\t\tapierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(serviceErr.Code, \"\", schema.GroupResource{}, \"\", serviceErr.Message, 0, false),\n\t\ts,\n\t\tschema.GroupVersion{},\n\t\tresp,\n\t\trequest.Request,\n\t)\n}\n\n// ServeHTTP makes it an http.Handler\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\ta.FullHandlerChain.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go",
    "numLines": 190,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 190
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\tzpagesfeatures \"k8s.io/component-base/zpages/features\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\topenapibuilder3 \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/builder3\"\n\topenapicommon \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/handler\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/handler3\"\n\topenapiutil \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/util\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\"\n)\n\n// Info about an API group.\ntype APIGroupInfo struct {\n\tPrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion\n\t// Info about the resources in this group. It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\n\tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\n\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\n\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\n\t// define a version \"v1beta1\" but want to use the Kubernetes \"v1\" internal objects.\n\t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\n\t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\n\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \"meta.k8s.io/v1\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\n\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\n\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\n\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n\n\t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\n\t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\n\t// TODO: replace with interfaces\n\tScheme *runtime.Scheme\n\t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\n\tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n\t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\n\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\n\n\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\n\t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\n\tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\n}\n\nfunc (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\n\tfor _, stores := range a.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n\t\tfor _, store := range stores {\n\t\t\tstore.Destroy()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// GenericAPIServer contains state for a Kubernetes cluster api server.\ntype GenericAPIServer struct {\n\t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\n\tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n\n\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n\n\t// minRequestTimeout is how short the request timeout can be.  This is used to build the RESTHandler\n\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n\n\t// ShutdownTimeout is the timeout used for server shutdown. This specifies the timeout before server\n\t// gracefully shutdown returns.\n\tShutdownTimeout time.Duration\n\n\t// legacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\n\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup\n\tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\n\n\t// admissionControl is used to build the RESTStorage that backs an API Group.\n\tadmissionControl admission.Interface\n\n\t// SecureServingInfo holds configuration of the TLS server.\n\tSecureServingInfo *SecureServingInfo\n\n\t// ExternalAddress is the address (hostname or IP and port) that should be used in\n\t// external (public internet) URLs for this GenericAPIServer.\n\tExternalAddress string\n\n\t// Serializer controls how common API objects not in a group/version prefix are serialized for this server.\n\t// Individual APIGroups may define their own serializers.\n\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n\n\t// \"Outputs\"\n\t// Handler holds the handlers being used by this API server\n\tHandler *APIServerHandler\n\n\t// UnprotectedDebugSocket is used to serve pprof information in a unix-domain socket. This socket is\n\t// not protected by authentication/authorization.\n\tUnprotectedDebugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\n\n\t// listedPathProvider is a lister which provides the set of paths to show at /\n\tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\n\n\t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\n\tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\n\n\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\n\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n\n\t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\n\tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go",
    "numLines": 100,

}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\n\t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\n\tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n\n\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\n\topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n\n\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\n\topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n\n\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\n\t// during PrepareRun.\n\t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\n\t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\n\tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n\n\t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\n\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n\tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\n\n\t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\n\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n\tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\n\n\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\n\t// It is set during PrepareRun.\n\tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\n\n\t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\n\t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\n\t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\n\tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\n\tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\n\tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\n\tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n\n\tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\n\tpreShutdownHooks       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n\tpreShutdownHooksCalled bool\n\n\t// healthz checks\n\thealthzRegistry healthCheckRegistry\n\treadyzRegistry  healthCheckRegistry\n\tlivezRegistry   healthCheckRegistry\n\n\tlivezGracePeriod time.Duration\n\n\t// auditing. The backend is started before the server starts listening.\n\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\n\n\t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\n\t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\n\t// the create-on-update case\n\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\n\n\t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\n\t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\n\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\n\n\t// delegationTarget is the next delegate in the chain. This is never nil.\n\tdelegationTarget DelegationTarget\n\n\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\n\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\n\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\n\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\n\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\n\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\n\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\n\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\n\n\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\n\t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\n\t// but /readyz will return failure.\n\tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\n\t// 0 means no limit.\n\tmaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n\n\t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\n\tAPIServerID string\n\n\t// StorageReadinessHook implements post-start-hook functionality for checking readiness\n\t// of underlying storage for registered resources.\n\tStorageReadinessHook *StorageReadinessHook\n\n\t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\n\tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\n\n\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\n\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\n\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\n\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\n\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\n\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\n\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\n\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\n\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\n\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\n\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\n\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n\n\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\n\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\n\n\t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals that happen during the life cycle of the apiserver.\n\tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\n\n\t// destroyFns contains a list of functions that should be called on shutdown to clean up resources.\n\tdestroyFns []func()\n\n\t// muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals holds signals that indicate all known HTTP paths have been registered.\n\t// it exists primarily to avoid returning a 404 response when a resource actually exists but we haven't installed the path to a handler.\n\t// it is exposed for easier composition of the individual servers.\n\t// the primary users of this field are the WithMuxCompleteProtection filter and the NotFoundHandler\n\tmuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals map[string]<-chan struct{}\n\n\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\n\t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\n\t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\n\t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\n\t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\n\t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\n\t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\n\tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\n\n\t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\n\t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go",
    "numLines": 130,
    "startLine": 160,
    "totalLines": 1091
  },
  "type": "text"
}
