Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cacher.go",
  "limit": 100,
  "offset": 257
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "// Cacher is responsible for serving WATCH and LIST requests for a given\n// resource from its internal cache and updating its cache in the background\n// based on the underlying storage contents.\n// Cacher implements storage.Interface (although most of the calls are just\n// delegated to the underlying storage).\ntype Cacher struct {\n\t// HighWaterMarks for performance debugging.\n\t// Important: Since HighWaterMark is using sync/atomic, it has to be at the top of the struct due to a bug on 32-bit platforms\n\t// See: https://golang.org/pkg/sync/atomic/ for more information\n\tincomingHWM storage.HighWaterMark\n\t// Incoming events that should be dispatched to watchers.\n\tincoming chan watchCacheEvent\n\n\tresourcePrefix string\n\n\tsync.RWMutex\n\n\t// Before accessing the cacher's cache, wait for the ready to be ok.\n\t// This is necessary to prevent users from accessing structures that are\n\t// uninitialized or are being repopulated right now.\n\t// ready needs to be set to false when the cacher is paused or stopped.\n\t// ready needs to be set to true when the cacher is ready to use after\n\t// initialization.\n\tready *ready\n\n\t// Underlying storage.Interface.\n\tstorage storage.Interface\n\n\t// Expected type of objects in the underlying cache.\n\tobjectType reflect.Type\n\t// Used for logging, to disambiguate *unstructured.Unstructured (CRDs)\n\tgroupResource schema.GroupResource\n\n\t// \"sliding window\" of recent changes of objects and the current state.\n\twatchCache *watchCache\n\treflector  *cache.Reflector\n\n\t// Versioner is used to handle resource versions.\n\tversioner storage.Versioner\n\n\t// newFunc is a function that creates new empty object storing a object of type Type.\n\tnewFunc func() runtime.Object\n\n\t// newListFunc is a function that creates new empty list for storing objects of type Type.\n\tnewListFunc func() runtime.Object\n\n\t// indexedTrigger is used for optimizing amount of watchers that needs to process\n\t// an incoming event.\n\tindexedTrigger *indexedTriggerFunc\n\t// watchers is mapping from the value of trigger function that a\n\t// watcher is interested into the watchers\n\twatcherIdx int\n\twatchers   indexedWatchers\n\n\t// Defines a time budget that can be spend on waiting for not-ready watchers\n\t// while dispatching event before shutting them down.\n\tdispatchTimeoutBudget timeBudget\n\n\t// Handling graceful termination.\n\tstopLock sync.RWMutex\n\tstopped  bool\n\tstopCh   chan struct{}\n\tstopWg   sync.WaitGroup\n\n\tclock clock.Clock\n\t// timer is used to avoid unnecessary allocations in underlying watchers.\n\ttimer *time.Timer\n\n\t// dispatching determines whether there is currently dispatching of\n\t// any event in flight.\n\tdispatching bool\n\t// watchersBuffer is a list of watchers potentially interested in currently\n\t// dispatched event.\n\twatchersBuffer []*cacheWatcher\n\t// blockedWatchers is a list of watchers whose buffer is currently full.\n\tblockedWatchers []*cacheWatcher\n\t// watchersToStop is a list of watchers that were supposed to be stopped\n\t// during current dispatching, but stopping was deferred to the end of\n\t// dispatching that event to avoid race with closing channels in watchers.\n\twatchersToStop []*cacheWatcher\n\t// Maintain a timeout queue to send the bookmark event before the watcher times out.\n\t// Note that this field when accessed MUST be protected by the Cacher.lock.\n\tbookmarkWatchers *watcherBookmarkTimeBuckets\n\t// expiredBookmarkWatchers is a list of watchers that were expired and need to be schedule for a next bookmark event\n\texpiredBookmarkWatchers []*cacheWatcher\n\tcompactor               *compactor\n}\n\n// NewCacherFromConfig creates a new Cacher responsible for servicing WATCH and LIST requests from\n// its internal cache and updating its cache in the background based on the\n// given configuration.\nfunc NewCacherFromConfig(config Config) (*Cacher, error) {\n\tstopCh := make(chan struct{})\n\tobj := config.NewFunc()\n\t// Give this error when it is constructed rather than when you get the\n\t// first watch item, because it's much easier to track down that way.\n\tif err := runtime.CheckCodec(config.Codec, obj); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"storage codec doesn't seem to match given type: %v\", err)\n\t}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/cacher.go",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 257,
    "totalLines": 1440
  },
  "type": "text"
}
170. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go",
  "limit": 100,
  "offset": 870
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\treturn nil\n}\n\n// InstallAPIGroups exposes given api groups in the API.\n// The <apiGroupInfos> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the\n// underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfos ...*APIGroupInfo) error {\n\tfor _, apiGroupInfo := range apiGroupInfos {\n\t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions) == 0 {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no version priority set for %#v\", *apiGroupInfo)\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Do not register empty group or empty version.  Doing so claims /apis/ for the wrong entity to be returned.\n\t\t// Catching these here places the error  much closer to its origin\n\t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group) == 0 {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"cannot register handler with an empty group for %#v\", *apiGroupInfo)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Version) == 0 {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"cannot register handler with an empty version for %#v\", *apiGroupInfo)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\topenAPIModels, err := s.getOpenAPIModels(APIGroupPrefix, apiGroupInfos...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to get openapi models: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, apiGroupInfo := range apiGroupInfos {\n\t\tif err := s.installAPIResources(APIGroupPrefix, apiGroupInfo, openAPIModels); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to install api resources: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// setup discovery\n\t\t// Install the version handler.\n\t\t// Add a handler at /apis/<groupName> to enumerate all versions supported by this group.\n\t\tapiVersionsForDiscovery := []metav1.GroupVersionForDiscovery{}\n\t\tfor _, groupVersion := range apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions {\n\t\t\t// Check the config to make sure that we elide versions that don't have any resources\n\t\t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap[groupVersion.Version]) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tapiVersionsForDiscovery = append(apiVersionsForDiscovery, metav1.GroupVersionForDiscovery{\n\t\t\t\tGroupVersion: groupVersion.String(),\n\t\t\t\tVersion:      groupVersion.Version,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t\tpreferredVersionForDiscovery := metav1.GroupVersionForDiscovery{\n\t\t\tGroupVersion: apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].String(),\n\t\t\tVersion:      apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Version,\n\t\t}\n\t\tapiGroup := metav1.APIGroup{\n\t\t\tName:             apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group,\n\t\t\tVersions:         apiVersionsForDiscovery,\n\t\t\tPreferredVersion: preferredVersionForDiscovery,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ts.DiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroup(apiGroup)\n\t\ts.Handler.GoRestfulContainer.Add(discovery.NewAPIGroupHandler(s.Serializer, apiGroup).WebService())\n\t\ts.registerStorageReadinessCheck(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group, apiGroupInfo)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// registerStorageReadinessCheck registers the readiness checks for all underlying storages\n// for a given APIGroup.\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) registerStorageReadinessCheck(groupName string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) {\n\tfor version, storageMap := range apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n\t\tfor resource, storage := range storageMap {\n\t\t\tif withReadiness, ok := storage.(rest.StorageWithReadiness); ok {\n\t\t\t\tgvr := metav1.GroupVersionResource{\n\t\t\t\t\tGroup:    groupName,\n\t\t\t\t\tVersion:  version,\n\t\t\t\t\tResource: resource,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\ts.StorageReadinessHook.RegisterStorage(gvr, withReadiness)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// InstallAPIGroup exposes the given api group in the API.\n// The <apiGroupInfo> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the\n// underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroup(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) error {\n\treturn s.InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfo)\n}\n\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) getAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, groupVersion schema.GroupVersion, apiPrefix string) (*genericapi.APIGroupVersion, error) {\n\tstorage := make(map[string]rest.Storage)\n\tfor k, v := range apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap[groupVersion.Version] {\n\t\tif strings.ToLower(k) != k {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"resource names must be lowercase only, not %q\", k)\n\t\t}\n\t\tstorage[k] = v\n\t}\n\tversion := s.newAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo, groupVersion)\n\tversion.Root = apiPrefix\n\tversion.Storage = storage\n\treturn version, nil\n}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 870,
    "totalLines": 1091
  },
  "type": "text"
}
171. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go",
  "limit": 120,
  "offset": 90
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "// All fields are required unless specified.\n//\n// The intended use of this type is embedding within a Kind specific\n// RESTStorage implementation. This type provides CRUD semantics on a Kubelike\n// resource, handling details like conflict detection with ResourceVersion and\n// semantics. The RESTCreateStrategy, RESTUpdateStrategy, and\n// RESTDeleteStrategy are generic across all backends, and encapsulate logic\n// specific to the API.\n//\n// TODO: make the default exposed methods exactly match a generic RESTStorage\ntype Store struct {\n\t// NewFunc returns a new instance of the type this registry returns for a\n\t// GET of a single object, e.g.:\n\t//\n\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource/name-of-object\n\tNewFunc func() runtime.Object\n\n\t// NewListFunc returns a new list of the type this registry; it is the\n\t// type returned when the resource is listed, e.g.:\n\t//\n\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource\n\tNewListFunc func() runtime.Object\n\n\t// DefaultQualifiedResource is the pluralized name of the resource.\n\t// This field is used if there is no request info present in the context.\n\t// See qualifiedResourceFromContext for details.\n\tDefaultQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\n\n\t// SingularQualifiedResource is the singular name of the resource.\n\tSingularQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\n\n\t// KeyRootFunc returns the root etcd key for this resource; should not\n\t// include trailing \"/\".  This is used for operations that work on the\n\t// entire collection (listing and watching).\n\t//\n\t// KeyRootFunc and KeyFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\n\tKeyRootFunc func(ctx context.Context) string\n\n\t// KeyFunc returns the key for a specific object in the collection.\n\t// KeyFunc is called for Create/Update/Get/Delete. Note that 'namespace'\n\t// can be gotten from ctx.\n\t//\n\t// KeyFunc and KeyRootFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\n\tKeyFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string) (string, error)\n\n\t// ObjectNameFunc returns the name of an object or an error.\n\tObjectNameFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error)\n\n\t// TTLFunc returns the TTL (time to live) that objects should be persisted\n\t// with. The existing parameter is the current TTL or the default for this\n\t// operation. The update parameter indicates whether this is an operation\n\t// against an existing object.\n\t//\n\t// Objects that are persisted with a TTL are evicted once the TTL expires.\n\tTTLFunc func(obj runtime.Object, existing uint64, update bool) (uint64, error)\n\n\t// PredicateFunc returns a matcher corresponding to the provided labels\n\t// and fields. The SelectionPredicate returned should return true if the\n\t// object matches the given field and label selectors.\n\tPredicateFunc func(label labels.Selector, field fields.Selector) storage.SelectionPredicate\n\n\t// EnableGarbageCollection affects the handling of Update and Delete\n\t// requests. Enabling garbage collection allows finalizers to do work to\n\t// finalize this object before the store deletes it.\n\t//\n\t// If any store has garbage collection enabled, it must also be enabled in\n\t// the kube-controller-manager.\n\tEnableGarbageCollection bool\n\n\t// DeleteCollectionWorkers is the maximum number of workers in a single\n\t// DeleteCollection call. Delete requests for the items in a collection\n\t// are issued in parallel.\n\tDeleteCollectionWorkers int\n\n\t// Decorator is an optional exit hook on an object returned from the\n\t// underlying storage. The returned object could be an individual object\n\t// (e.g. Pod) or a list type (e.g. PodList). Decorator is intended for\n\t// integrations that are above storage and should only be used for\n\t// specific cases where storage of the value is not appropriate, since\n\t// they cannot be watched.\n\tDecorator func(runtime.Object)\n\n\t// CreateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during creation.\n\tCreateStrategy rest.RESTCreateStrategy\n\t// BeginCreate is an optional hook that returns a \"transaction-like\"\n\t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\n\t// but before AfterCreate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\n\t// whether the operation succeeded.  If this returns an error, the function\n\t// is not called.  Almost nobody should use this hook.\n\tBeginCreate BeginCreateFunc\n\t// AfterCreate implements a further operation to run after a resource is\n\t// created and before it is decorated, optional.\n\tAfterCreate AfterCreateFunc\n\n\t// UpdateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during updates.\n\tUpdateStrategy rest.RESTUpdateStrategy\n\t// BeginUpdate is an optional hook that returns a \"transaction-like\"\n\t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\n\t// but before AfterUpdate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\n\t// whether the operation succeeded.  If this returns an error, the function\n\t// is not called.  Almost nobody should use this hook.\n\tBeginUpdate BeginUpdateFunc\n\t// AfterUpdate implements a further operation to run after a resource is\n\t// updated and before it is decorated, optional.\n\tAfterUpdate AfterUpdateFunc\n\n\t// DeleteStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during deletion.\n\tDeleteStrategy rest.RESTDeleteStrategy\n\t// AfterDelete implements a further operation to run after a resource is\n\t// deleted and before it is decorated, optional.\n\tAfterDelete AfterDeleteFunc\n\t// ReturnDeletedObject determines whether the Store returns the object\n\t// that was deleted. Otherwise, return a generic success status response.\n\tReturnDeletedObject bool\n\t// ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate is an optional function to determine whether\n\t// an update from existing to obj should result in a delete.\n\t// If specified, this is checked in addition to standard finalizer,\n\t// deletionTimestamp, and deletionGracePeriodSeconds checks.\n\tShouldDeleteDuringUpdate func(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, existing runtime.Object) bool\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go",
    "numLines": 120,
    "startLine": 90,
    "totalLines": 1722
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go",
  "limit": 130,
  "offset": 160
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\t\t\t*p.ResourceVersion,\n\t\t\tobjMeta.GetResourceVersion())\n\t\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Interface offers a common interface for object marshaling/unmarshaling operations and\n// hides all the storage-related operations behind it.\ntype Interface interface {\n\t// Returns Versioner associated with this interface.\n\tVersioner() Versioner\n\n\t// Create adds a new object at a key unless it already exists. 'ttl' is time-to-live\n\t// in seconds (0 means forever). If no error is returned and out is not nil, out will be\n\t// set to the read value from database.\n\tCreate(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error\n\n\t// Delete removes the specified key and returns the value that existed at that spot.\n\t// If key didn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error.\n\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\n\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\n\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\n\tDelete(\n\t\tctx context.Context, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions,\n\t\tvalidateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error\n\n\t// Watch begins watching the specified key. Events are decoded into API objects,\n\t// and any items selected by 'p' are sent down to returned watch.Interface.\n\t// resourceVersion may be used to specify what version to begin watching,\n\t// which should be the current resourceVersion, and no longer rv+1\n\t// (e.g. reconnecting without missing any updates).\n\t// If resource version is \"0\", this interface will get current object at given key\n\t// and send it in an \"ADDED\" event, before watch starts.\n\tWatch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\n\n\t// Get unmarshals object found at key into objPtr. On a not found error, will either\n\t// return a zero object of the requested type, or an error, depending on 'opts.ignoreNotFound'.\n\t// Treats empty responses and nil response nodes exactly like a not found error.\n\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\n\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\n\tGet(ctx context.Context, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error\n\n\t// GetList unmarshalls objects found at key into a *List api object (an object\n\t// that satisfies runtime.IsList definition).\n\t// If 'opts.Recursive' is false, 'key' is used as an exact match. If `opts.Recursive'\n\t// is true, 'key' is used as a prefix.\n\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\n\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\n\tGetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error\n\n\t// GuaranteedUpdate keeps calling 'tryUpdate()' to update key 'key' (of type 'destination')\n\t// retrying the update until success if there is index conflict.\n\t// Note that object passed to tryUpdate may change across invocations of tryUpdate() if\n\t// other writers are simultaneously updating it, so tryUpdate() needs to take into account\n\t// the current contents of the object when deciding how the update object should look.\n\t// If the key doesn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error if ignoreNotFound=false\n\t// else `destination` will be set to the zero value of it's type.\n\t// If the eventual successful invocation of `tryUpdate` returns an output with the same serialized\n\t// contents as the input, it won't perform any update, but instead set `destination` to an object with those\n\t// contents.\n\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\n\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\n\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\n\t//\n\t// Example:\n\t//\n\t// s := /* implementation of Interface */\n\t// err := s.GuaranteedUpdate(\n\t//     \"myKey\", &MyType{}, true, preconditions,\n\t//     func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {\n\t//       // Before each invocation of the user defined function, \"input\" is reset to\n\t//       // current contents for \"myKey\" in database.\n\t//       curr := input.(*MyType)  // Guaranteed to succeed.\n\t//\n\t//       // Make the modification\n\t//       curr.Counter++\n\t//\n\t//       // Return the modified object - return an error to stop iterating. Return\n\t//       // a uint64 to alter the TTL on the object, or nil to keep it the same value.\n\t//       return cur, nil, nil\n\t//    }, cachedExistingObject\n\t// )\n\tGuaranteedUpdate(\n\t\tctx context.Context, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,\n\t\tpreconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error\n\n\t// Stats returns storage stats.\n\tStats(ctx context.Context) (Stats, error)\n\n\t// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\n\tReadinessCheck() error\n\n\t// RequestWatchProgress requests the a watch stream progress status be sent in the\n\t// watch response stream as soon as possible.\n\t// Used for monitor watch progress even if watching resources with no changes.\n\t//\n\t// If watch is lagging, progress status might:\n\t// * be pointing to stale resource version. Use etcd KV request to get linearizable resource version.\n\t// * not be delivered at all. It's recommended to poll request progress periodically.\n\t//\n\t// Note: Only watches with matching context grpc metadata will be notified.\n\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/9325a57125e8502941d1b0c7379c4bb80a678d5c/vendor/go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/watch.go#L1037-L1042\n\t//\n\t// TODO: Remove when storage.Interface will be separate from etc3.store.\n\t// Deprecated: Added temporarily to simplify exposing RequestProgress for watch cache.\n\tRequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error\n\n\t// GetCurrentResourceVersion gets the current resource version from etcd.\n\t// This method issues an empty list request and reads only the ResourceVersion from the object metadata\n\tGetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)\n\n\t// EnableResourceSizeEstimation enables estimating resource size by providing function get keys from storage.\n\tEnableResourceSizeEstimation(KeysFunc) error\n\n\t// CompactRevision returns latest observed revision that was compacted.\n\t// Without ListFromCacheSnapshot enabled only locally executed compaction will be observed.\n\t// Returns 0 if no compaction was yet observed.\n\tCompactRevision() int64\n}\n\n// KeysFunc is a function prototype to fetch keys from storage.\ntype KeysFunc func(context.Context) ([]string, error)\n\n// GetOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage get operations.\ntype GetOptions struct {\n\t// IgnoreNotFound determines what is returned if the requested object is not found. If\n\t// true, a zero object is returned. If false, an error is returned.\n\tIgnoreNotFound bool\n\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the get operation",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go",
    "numLines": 130,
    "startLine": 160,
    "totalLines": 423
  },
  "type": "text"
}

174. Read @ -

{

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\tPrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer\n\n\t// MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals exposes registered signals that indicate if all known HTTP paths have been installed.\n\tMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{}\n\n\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\n\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\n\t// for being called more than once.\n\tDestroy()\n}\n\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) UnprotectedHandler() http.Handler {\n\t// when we delegate, we need the server we're delegating to choose whether or not to use gorestful\n\treturn s.Handler.Director\n}\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) PostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry {\n\treturn s.postStartHooks\n}\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) PreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry {\n\treturn s.preShutdownHooks\n}\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) HealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker {\n\treturn s.healthzRegistry.checks\n}\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) ListedPaths() []string {\n\treturn s.listedPathProvider.ListedPaths()\n}\n\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) NextDelegate() DelegationTarget {\n\treturn s.delegationTarget\n}\n\n// RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal registers the given signal that will be used to determine if all known\n// HTTP paths have been registered. It is okay to call this method after instantiating the generic server but before running.\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal(signalName string, signal <-chan struct{}) error {\n\tif _, exists := s.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals[signalName]; exists {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s already registered\", signalName)\n\t}\n\ts.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals[signalName] = signal\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{} {\n\treturn s.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals\n}\n\n// RegisterDestroyFunc registers a function that will be called during Destroy().\n// The function have to be idempotent and prepared to be called more than once.\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) RegisterDestroyFunc(destroyFn func()) {\n\ts.destroyFns = append(s.destroyFns, destroyFn)",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 328,
    "totalLines": 1091
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) RunWithContext(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tstopCh := ctx.Done()\n\tdelayedStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.AfterShutdownDelayDuration\n\tshutdownInitiatedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated\n\n\t// Clean up resources on shutdown.\n\tdefer s.Destroy()\n\n\t// If UDS profiling is enabled, start a local http server listening on that socket\n\tif s.UnprotectedDebugSocket != nil {\n\t\tgo func() {\n\t\t\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrashWithContext(ctx)\n\t\t\tklog.Error(s.UnprotectedDebugSocket.RunWithContext(ctx))\n\t\t}()\n\t}\n\n\t// spawn a new goroutine for closing the MuxAndDiscoveryComplete signal\n\t// registration happens during construction of the generic api server\n\t// the last server in the chain aggregates signals from the previous instances\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tfor _, muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal := range s.GenericAPIServer.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal:\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n\t\t\t\tklog.V(1).Infof(\"haven't completed %s, stop requested\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signal()\n\t\tklog.V(1).Infof(\"%s has all endpoints registered and discovery information is complete\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\n\t}()\n\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tdefer delayedStopCh.Signal()\n\t\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", delayedStopCh.Name())\n\n\t\t<-stopCh\n\n\t\t// As soon as shutdown is initiated, /readyz should start returning failure.\n\t\t// This gives the load balancer a window defined by ShutdownDelayDuration to detect that /readyz is red\n\t\t// and stop sending traffic to this server.\n\t\tshutdownInitiatedCh.Signal()\n\t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", shutdownInitiatedCh.Name())\n\n\t\ttime.Sleep(s.ShutdownDelayDuration)\n\t}()\n\n\t// close socket after delayed stopCh\n\tshutdownTimeout := s.ShutdownTimeout\n\tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n\t\t// when this mode is enabled, we do the following:\n\t\t// - the server will continue to listen until all existing requests in flight\n\t\t//   (not including active long running requests) have been drained.\n\t\t// - once drained, http Server Shutdown is invoked with a timeout of 2s,\n\t\t//   net/http waits for 1s for the peer to respond to a GO_AWAY frame, so\n\t\t//   we should wait for a minimum of 2s\n\t\tshutdownTimeout = 2 * time.Second\n\t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] using HTTP Server shutdown timeout\", \"shutdownTimeout\", shutdownTimeout)\n\t}\n\n\tnotAcceptingNewRequestCh := s.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest\n\tdrainedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained\n\t// Canceling the parent context does not immediately cancel the HTTP server.\n\t// We only inherit context values here and deal with cancellation ourselves.\n\tstopHTTPServerCtx, stopHTTPServer := context.WithCancelCause(context.WithoutCancel(ctx))\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tdefer stopHTTPServer(errors.New(\"time to stop HTTP server\"))\n\n\t\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh := notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\n\t\tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n\t\t\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh = drainedCh.Signaled()\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t<-timeToStopHttpServerCh\n\t}()\n\n\t// Start the audit backend before any request comes in. This means we must call Backend.Run\n\t// before http server start serving. Otherwise the Backend.ProcessEvents call might block.\n\t// AuditBackend.Run will stop as soon as all in-flight requests are drained.\n\tif s.AuditBackend != nil {\n\t\tif err := s.AuditBackend.Run(drainedCh.Signaled()); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to run the audit backend: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tstoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err := s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(stopHTTPServerCtx, shutdownTimeout)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\thttpServerStoppedListeningCh := s.lifecycleSignals.HTTPServerStoppedListening\n\tgo func() {\n\t\t<-listenerStoppedCh\n\t\thttpServerStoppedListeningCh.Signal()\n\t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", httpServerStoppedListeningCh.Name())\n\t}()\n\n\t// we don't accept new request as soon as both ShutdownDelayDuration has\n\t// elapsed and preshutdown hooks have completed.",
    "filePath": "/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 525,
    "totalLines": 1091
  },
  "type": "text"
}
